title: Prepare v0.8.1 release
description: Prepare the v0.8.1 patch release by synchronizing app release metadata and adding release notes.

close_reason: Prepared and published Canopi v0.8.1. Bumped synchronized release metadata to 0.8.1, added docs/release-notes/v0.8.1.md, pushed main at 9e1b05f, ran Release Candidate workflow 27917118081 successfully for Linux, macOS Apple Silicon, macOS Intel, and Windows, promoted the run to GitHub Release v0.8.1, verified checksums during promotion, and published the release. Local checks: npm test -- about-canopi-dialog.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; npm run build; local release-version preflight parser.

title: Preserve broader FTS candidates in indexed Species Search
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix indexed Species Catalog relevance search so generated name-entry candidates do not replace broader FTS candidates from non-name text fields.

## User Value
Searches that match documented broader Species text still return relevant Species while retaining the selected-language Common Name ranking improvements.

## Out of Scope
Do not add compatibility work for older unpackaged DB assets, change the active-search length policy, or reintroduce English Common Name fallback for non-English locales.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Preserve broader FTS candidates when the generated Species Search name-entry index is available.

**Current behavior:**
When the generated name-entry search index is present, relevance searches with tokenized text take the indexed search path. That path ranks Common Name, canonical, and taxonomy candidates from name-entry tables, but it no longer includes broader FTS matches from documented text columns such as uses text or other descriptive text.

**Desired behavior:**
Indexed relevance search should keep selected-language Common Name candidates and their ranking tiers, while also returning broader FTS-only matches behind those stronger name candidates. Count and list queries should describe the same candidate universe. The fix should keep non-English searches selected-language-only for Common Name ranking and display fallback.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Search request and result contract.
- Relevance-ordered Species Catalog list and count planning.
- FTS text matching for broader Species text fields.
- Selected-language Common Name ranking and matched-name display behavior.

**Out of scope:**
- Compatibility with older unpackaged plant DB assets.
- Changing the two-character active-search UX.
- Reintroducing English Common Name fallback for non-English locales.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A relevance search that matches only broader FTS text fields still returns matching Species when the generated name-entry index is available.
- [ ] Indexed list and count queries agree on the candidate set for broader FTS-only matches.
- [ ] Selected-language Common Name matches still outrank broader FTS-only matches.
- [ ] Existing apple/appl and French melis/melisse relevance expectations remain covered.
- [ ] No compatibility work is added for older unpackaged DB assets.
close_reason: Restored broader FTS-only candidates in generated-index Species Catalog relevance search while preserving selected-language Common Name ranking. Added regression coverage through search() for a uses_text-only match with list and total count. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog_read::search::tests::indexed_active_search_preserves_broader_fts_only_matches; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog_read::search::tests::relevance; cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::query_builder::tests; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace.

title: Avoid interrupting unrelated plant DB reads
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix active Species Catalog Search cancellation so superseded search work cannot interrupt unrelated plant DB statements that happen to own the shared connection.

## User Value
Typing in the Species Catalog search field should cancel stale searches without causing detail, favorites, filter, or other plant DB reads to fail with SQLite interrupted errors.

## Out of Scope
Do not change search relevance, minimum query length, or frontend stale-result handling.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Prevent active Species Search cancellation from interrupting unrelated plant DB reads.

**Current behavior:**
Active Species Catalog Search cancellation stores a connection-wide SQLite interrupt handle. If a stale search is queued behind another plant DB read, starting a newer active search can interrupt whichever statement currently owns the shared plant DB connection, including unrelated detail, favorites, filter, or browse reads.

**Desired behavior:**
Superseding an active search should make stale search work stop promptly without interrupting unrelated plant DB statements. Cancellation should only affect a stale search once that stale search owns the plant DB connection, or use an equivalent isolation mechanism. Stale cancellation remains an internal behavior, not a user-facing error.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Search IPC/service boundary.
- Plant DB connection ownership and SQLite interrupt semantics.
- Active search cancellation coordinator.
- Other plant DB read workflows that share the bundled plant DB connection.

**Out of scope:**
- Relevance ranking changes.
- Changing the active-search minimum length.
- Frontend stale-result generation handling.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Starting a newer active search cannot interrupt an unrelated plant DB read that currently owns the shared connection.
- [ ] A stale active search that has not yet started DB work is rejected before acquiring/running the search query.
- [ ] A stale active search that owns the DB read path is still cancelled or interrupted promptly enough to avoid blocking newer active searches.
- [ ] Cancelled stale searches remain internal and do not create a user-facing search error state.
- [ ] Existing search, favorites hydration, and selected-language Common Name tests continue to pass.
close_reason: Scoped active Species Search interruption to searches that have marked themselves running while owning the plant DB read. Queued stale searches are rejected without interrupting unrelated plant DB statements, and a running guard clears interrupt eligibility before favorite hydration. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::plant_browser::tests; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog_read::search::tests::indexed_active_search_preserves_broader_fts_only_matches; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog_read::search::tests::relevance; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Stage short-prefix Species Catalog relevance before weaker matches
description:
## Slice Summary
Optimize short-prefix active Species Catalog Search so selected-language Common Name tiers are satisfied before weaker Canonical Name, family, genus, or broad text matches are ranked.

## User Value
Two-character searches keep the same UX and relevance expectations, but broad prefixes avoid unnecessary candidate ranking that causes first-search stalls.

## Out of Scope
Do not raise the minimum query length, reintroduce English Common Name fallback, or change the Species Catalog Workbench UI behavior in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Optimize short-prefix relevance by staging stronger selected-language matches first.

**Current behavior:**
Broad short-prefix active searches can materialize, group, and sort thousands of candidate Species before returning the first page. This preserves relevance but makes the first real backend search expensive, especially when the query has only two normalized characters.

**Desired behavior:**
Short-prefix active search should keep the same visible ranking rules while doing less work. Selected-language displayed Common Names and Matched Common Names should be satisfied before weaker Canonical Name, family, genus, or broad text matches are ranked. Canonical and taxonomy matches remain language-neutral fallback search behavior, but they should not force the first page to rank a huge weak candidate set when enough stronger selected-language Common Name results exist.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Search relevance ordering.
- Selected-language Common Name and Matched Common Name display behavior.
- Species Catalog read projection list query.
- Search-performance harness for broad first-active prefixes.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the active-search minimum length.
- Reintroducing English Common Name fallback in non-English locales.
- Moving backend work to an async command boundary.
- Cancelling obsolete searches.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `apple`, `appl`, and representative `ap` searches still put `Malus domestica` first in English.
- [ ] French `melisse` and `melis` searches still prioritize selected-language Mélisse Common Name matches, including expected higher placement for Mélisse alpine and Mélisse des champs ahead of weaker matches.
- [ ] Non-English searches continue to ignore English Common Name fallback for ranking, matching, and display.
- [ ] Broad first-active prefixes such as `en-ap`, `fr-me`, and `fr-po` show a clear first-page list-latency improvement in the local harness, with before/after timings recorded in the bead or PR.
- [ ] Any generated DB asset or schema-index change is explicitly justified, regenerated, and documented; otherwise the change remains runtime-query-only.
close_reason: Implemented runtime-query-only short-prefix staging for unfiltered indexed Species Catalog relevance searches. Two-character active prefixes now rank selected-language Common Name candidates before canonical/taxonomy fallback work. Regression coverage added for staged short-prefix planning, conservative filtered planning, and longer-query broad planning. Bundled latency harness before -> after cold sample: en-ap 2527ms -> 279ms, fr-me 2487ms -> 261ms, fr-po 4023ms -> 556ms; warm after sample: en-ap 14ms, fr-me 10ms, fr-po 24ms. Bundled relevance harness still puts Malus domestica first for apple/appl and French Mélisse, Mélisse alpine, Mélisse des champs before weaker matches. No DB asset or schema/index regeneration was needed.

title: Add first-active Species Catalog Search latency coverage
description:
## Slice Summary
Add regression coverage or a manual harness update for the first real active Species Catalog Search prefixes that users hit after typing the second character.

## User Value
Reviewers can verify search changes against the prefixes that currently cause the side panel to slow down, without changing the Species Catalog Workbench UX.

## Out of Scope
Do not optimize SQL, change the two-character active-search policy, or change Common Name relevance in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Add search-performance coverage for first-active Species Catalog Search prefixes.

**Current behavior:**
The manual Species Catalog latency harness covers several relevance examples, but it does not directly cover the broad two-character prefixes that become the first real backend search after the user types the second normalized character. Production active text search skips exact totals, so coverage should distinguish first-page list cost from count cost.

**Desired behavior:**
There is reviewable latency coverage for representative first-active prefixes and known relevance examples. The coverage should make performance changes easy to compare without making normal CI brittle. It should preserve selected-language Common Name behavior and avoid changing the Species Catalog Workbench UX.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Search request contract, especially text, locale, sort, limit, and include-total behavior.
- Species Catalog read projection list query and relevance examples.
- Manual or focused backend performance harness output.

**Out of scope:**
- Optimizing SQL shape.
- Moving backend work to an async boundary.
- Changing the minimum active search length.
- Changing selected-language Common Name ranking or display behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Coverage includes broad first-active prefixes such as `en-ap`, `fr-me`, and `fr-po`.
- [ ] Coverage includes known relevance examples such as `en-apple`, `en-appl`, `fr-melis`, and `fr-melisse`.
- [ ] Coverage reports first-page list latency separately from total-count latency, and active-search coverage reflects that production active text search does not need an exact first-page count.
- [ ] The coverage is useful for local review without making normal CI fail on machine-dependent timing noise.
- [ ] Existing selected-language-only Common Name relevance expectations remain covered.
close_reason: Added first-active Species Catalog Search latency coverage for broad two-character prefixes, active no-count timing output, and a focused harness configuration regression. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog_read::search::tests::latency_harness_covers_first_active_prefixes_without_counts; ignored bundled latency harness with --ignored --nocapture.

title: Run active Species Catalog Search behind an async backend boundary
description:
## Slice Summary
Move active Species Catalog Search work off the synchronous Tauri invoke path while keeping the existing search request and result contract intact.

## User Value
Typing the second character should no longer make the app feel frozen while the first backend search resolves.

## Out of Scope
Do not add cancellation, rewrite relevance ranking, or change the bundled Species Catalog schema in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Move active Species Catalog Search off the synchronous Tauri invoke path.

**Current behavior:**
The first active Species Catalog Search starts when the user enters the second normalized character. That backend call performs SQLite read work through a synchronous command path and a shared plant DB read connection. Broad short-prefix queries can therefore make the app feel frozen while the command resolves.

**Desired behavior:**
The Species Catalog Workbench keeps the same two-character active-search UX and the same request/result contract, but active search SQLite work runs behind an async or background backend boundary. A slow first-page search should not block synchronous invoke handling. Existing list hydration, favorite marking, and selected-language Common Name behavior must stay intact.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Search IPC command and typed request/response contract.
- Species Catalog read projection for first-page search results.
- Plant DB and user DB lock ordering.
- Species Catalog Workbench search session.

**Out of scope:**
- Cancelling obsolete searches.
- Rewriting relevance SQL.
- Changing Common Name fallback rules.
- Changing the bundled Species Catalog schema or generated DB asset.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Active text search still starts at two normalized characters; one-character text remains a local short-query state.
- [ ] The frontend `searchSpecies` request/response shape remains compatible.
- [ ] Active Species Catalog Search SQLite list work no longer runs on the synchronous Tauri command path.
- [ ] Plant DB and user DB lock ordering remains explicit and avoids holding both locks at once.
- [ ] Existing search, favorites hydration, and selected-language Common Name regression tests pass or are updated with equivalent coverage.
close_reason: Moved Species Catalog Search through an async backend boundary using the existing run_blocking helper and cloneable single-connection DB handles, preserving the IPC request/response contract and lock ordering. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::plant_browser; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace.

title: Cancel obsolete active Species Catalog Search reads
description:
## Slice Summary
Stop obsolete active Species Catalog Search reads when newer input supersedes them, so broad two-character searches do not hold the plant DB read path after the user keeps typing.

## User Value
Fast typing should lead to the latest query becoming responsive instead of waiting behind an old broad prefix query.

## Out of Scope
Do not change relevance ranking, minimum search length, or Common Name fallback behavior in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Cancel obsolete active Species Catalog Search reads.

**Current behavior:**
The Species Catalog Workbench ignores stale frontend responses by generation, but an obsolete backend search can continue running after the user types a newer query. A broad two-character search can hold the plant DB read path and delay the newer query that the user actually wants.

**Desired behavior:**
When a newer active text search supersedes an older one, the older backend read should be cancelled or interrupted promptly. Stale cancellation should be internal behavior, not a user-facing error. The latest query should be able to complete without waiting for an obsolete broad-prefix read to finish.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench search session generation/stale-result behavior.
- Species Catalog Search IPC boundary.
- Backend plant DB read execution and SQLite interruption or equivalent cancellation mechanism.

**Out of scope:**
- Relevance-ranking changes.
- Raising the minimum active search length.
- English Common Name fallback.
- New generated search-index schema unless a separate bead explicitly adds it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Starting a newer active text search invalidates and stops an older in-flight active text search promptly.
- [ ] Cancelled stale searches do not show a user-facing error state.
- [ ] A regression test or controlled harness demonstrates that a newer search is not forced to wait for an obsolete broad-prefix search to finish.
- [ ] Cancellation releases backend resources and plant DB access cleanly.
- [ ] Browse, filter, and next-page search behavior remain compatible with existing UX.
close_reason: Added active Species Catalog Search cancellation with a shared cancellation coordinator, SQLite interrupt handles that do not require the PlantDb mutex, stale queued-search rejection, and active two-character policy tests. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::plant_browser::tests; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace.

title: Use indexed candidates for active Species Catalog Search
description:
## Slice Summary
Route active text Species Catalog Search through the generated candidate index when it is available, falling back to the legacy query plan for older database assets.

## User Value
Typing in the Species Catalog Workbench keeps the same result ordering and selected-language behavior, but first-page active searches avoid second-scale SQLite work.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the SpeciesSearchRequest contract, reintroduce user-facing sort controls, add typo/fuzzy matching, or remove compatibility with older bundled databases.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Use generated search-entry candidates for active Species Catalog Search when available.

**Current behavior:**
Active text Species Catalog Search builds one broad SQL query that combines FTS matches and selected-language Common Name token predicates. On the real Species Catalog database, short active queries can take around a second or more for the first page, causing visible pauses while users type.

**Desired behavior:**
When the generated search-entry table is available, active text search should first identify and rank candidate Species through that index, then hydrate the final first page through the existing SpeciesListItem read projection. Result ordering and display behavior should remain coherent with the selected app language. When the table is absent, runtime search should fall back to the legacy plan so older database assets still work.

**Key interfaces:**
- SpeciesSearchRequest
- Species Catalog Search read projection
- SpeciesListItem hydration
- selected-language Common Name and Matched Common Name display
- Species Catalog latency/relevance harnesses

**Out of scope:**
- User-facing sort controls
- Changing one-character search policy
- Fuzzy typo matching
- Removing support for older database assets
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Active text search uses indexed candidates when the generated search-entry table exists.
- [ ] Older database assets without the generated table continue to return active search results through a fallback path.
- [ ] Existing relevance examples remain true, including English `apple/appl` and French `melisse/melis` ordering.
- [ ] Selected-language-only Common Name matching remains enforced.
- [ ] First-page active text list latency on a regenerated bundled Species Catalog database is materially below the previous second-scale behavior.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover indexed and fallback active search paths.
close_reason: Implemented generated-index active Species Catalog Search with legacy fallback, selected-language-only Common Name matching, canonical/taxonomy indexed matches, idx_species_id-backed hydration, focused regressions, clean regenerated DB relevance checks, and real-data latency harness showing 2+ character active searches below second-scale. Gates: python3 -m unittest discover -s scripts -p 'test_*.py'; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; regenerated /tmp/canopi-core-indexed.db plus ignored relevance/latency harnesses.

title: Generate Species Catalog Search entry index
description:
## Slice Summary
Build a generated Species Catalog Search entry index during database preparation so selected-language Common Name search can query pre-normalized candidate rows instead of recomputing match data from raw Common Name tables at runtime.

## User Value
Future generated Species Catalog database assets carry the data shape needed for fast active text search while preserving selected-language-only Common Name behavior.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the visible Species Catalog Workbench behavior, expose new user controls, or route runtime search through the new index in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Generate a purpose-built Species Catalog Search entry index for selected-language active text search.

**Current behavior:**
Species Catalog Search uses raw Species, Common Name, token, and FTS tables at runtime to find and rank active text results. The runtime query recomputes normalized Common Name match facts and must scan/sort more rows than the first page needs.

**Desired behavior:**
Database preparation should create a generated search-entry table that contains one or more pre-normalized candidate rows per Species and language. The index should include enough stable fields for runtime search to find selected-language Common Name candidates, rank displayed and alternate Common Name matches, and explain a Matched Common Name without reading every raw Common Name row first. Older database assets without the generated table must remain valid for the app through runtime fallback work in a dependent slice.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog database preparation
- Species Catalog schema contract/versioning
- selected-language Common Name normalization
- Species Catalog latency/relevance harnesses

**Out of scope:**
- Runtime routing through the generated index
- Changing SpeciesSearchRequest
- Fuzzy typo matching
- User-facing Species Catalog Workbench changes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Database preparation creates a generated Species Catalog Search entry table for active text candidate lookup.
- [ ] Search entries are selected-language scoped and do not introduce English Common Name fallback rows for non-English locales.
- [ ] Search entries carry normalized prefix-match data and enough display/matched-name fields to preserve current relevance behavior.
- [ ] Schema/version checks recognize the new generated table without breaking older database assets.
- [ ] Focused tests or generation checks cover diacritic-folded Common Name prefixes such as French `melis` matching `Mélisse`.
close_reason: Generated Species Catalog Search name-entry and entry-token indexes during database preparation, bumped plant DB schema contract to v10, and covered selected-language diacritic-folded entry generation plus schema-version tests.

title: Fix Design Notebook direct drag interactions
description: Design Notebook section drag is missing and Design row dragging is unreliable after removing explicit handles. Restore file-view-style direct drag behavior for Notebook Sections and Design rows without reintroducing handles, search, pinned views, or overflow actions.
design: Use pointer-based direct drag handling instead of native HTML drag/drop so section headers and row bodies behave consistently inside the Tauri WebView. Keep action buttons and rename inputs out of drag initiation.
acceptance_criteria: Notebook Sections can be reordered by dragging the section title/header directly. Design rows can be reordered within a section and moved to another section by dragging the row body directly. Dragging does not accidentally open the Design row. Existing direct delete and double-click section rename behavior still work. Focused regression tests cover section drag and row drag.
notes: Fixed Design Notebook direct drag interactions by replacing native HTML drag/drop with pointer-based row and section-title drag handling. Added regression coverage for section reordering, row reordering, cross-section moves, and post-drag click suppression. Tests: npm test -- design-notebook-panel.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

No agent-doc updates were needed.

title: Let pinned plant name legend grow before scrolling
description: Pinned Plant Name legend currently gets a scrollbar instead of growing when there are more pinned species and the canvas has room. Diagnose and fix the legend so it uses available canvas space before overflow scrolling.
acceptance_criteria: Pinned Plant Name legend expands to fit more entries when viewport/canvas height allows; it only scrolls after reaching a viewport-safe maximum; regression tests cover small and large available-height cases; relevant frontend checks pass.
close_reason: Fixed pinned plant name legend sizing by removing the fixed 200px legend cap and applying a canvas-relative max height that reserves the scale-bar bottom band plus top ruler-safe space before overflow scrolling. Added a regression test with many pinned-name entries. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- display-legend.test.tsx; cd desktop/web && npm test -- wave4-coherence.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Fix plant name label selection behavior
description: Pinned Plant Names should render at their default per-plant position without overlap nudging. Transient Selection Labels should render only for a whole-canvas selection of exactly one unpinned placed plant; multi-selection and mixed selection should render no transient plant names.
acceptance_criteria: Pinned Plant Names are not overlap-nudged; transient Selection Labels appear only for exactly one selected unpinned placed plant; multi-plant, mixed-object, and selected pinned-plant cases render no Selection Label; focused renderer/unit tests cover the behavior; relevant frontend checks pass.
close_reason: Fixed plant name label selection behavior. Pinned Plant Names now render at their default per-plant position without overlap nudging; transient Selection Labels render only for exactly one selected unpinned Placed Plant, including viewport-only redraws that carry full selection identity. Updated CONTEXT.md and docs/agent/canvas-runtime.md to record the Selection Label boundary. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- selection-labels.test.ts canvas2d-scene.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts -t 'pinned plant name labels'; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

description: Prepare and publish Canopi v0.9.0 from main. Exclude the design report branch from release contents.
acceptance_criteria: Version metadata is synchronized at 0.9.0; docs/release-notes/v0.9.0.md exists; release candidate is run from main; promoted GitHub release uses those notes.

Added docs/release-notes/v0.9.0.md, synchronized Tauri/Rust/npm version metadata to 0.9.0, pushed main, ran Release Candidate workflow 28128584233 successfully for Linux, macOS Apple Silicon, macOS Intel, and Windows, promoted the run to GitHub Release v0.9.0 with checksum verification, uploaded six packaged artifacts plus SHA256SUMS.txt and release-metadata.json, and published the release. No agent-doc updates were needed.

title: Fit Design Report canvas image to printable frame
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the first Design Report page so the canvas image fits the printable frame, preserves the full visible design, keeps the frame border intact, and makes report-scale labels readable.

## User Value
A user can print or share the first page without a broken border, tiny design, or unreadable Measurement Guide and Annotation labels.

## Out of Scope
Do not change app canvas interaction, saved design geometry, Timeline, Budget, Consortium, or map-layer composition in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make the first Design Report page fit the canvas image cleanly inside its printable frame.

**Current behavior:**
The report canvas image can cover or interrupt the frame border, and representative designs may render tiny inside a large white frame. Report-scale Annotation and Measurement Guide labels can become too small to read.

**Desired behavior:**
The first page chooses an orientation and frame that fit the design bounds, renders a continuous frame border, and scales the visible design so it uses the available report area without clipping. The output must preserve the full visible design and keep report labels legible on common desktop PDF viewers and printed pages.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report export command and structured report input.
- Canvas Runtime Surface report/export image generation.
- Design Report page fitting, image placement, border drawing, and label scaling.
- Measurement Guide and Annotation report presentation.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing saved design geometry or canvas editing behavior.
- Adding map or terrain layer composition; that is covered by the visible map Layers bead.
- Reworking Timeline, Budget, or Consortium sections.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The first-page canvas frame border is continuous on all sides after the canvas image is rendered.
- [ ] The exported canvas image and PDF placement use the actual report frame aspect and usable area, not only the full page printable size.
- [ ] A representative dense design fills the report canvas frame without excessive empty margins while preserving all visible design bounds.
- [ ] Annotation and Measurement Guide labels remain readable in rasterized PDF checks at print scale.
- [ ] Landscape and portrait first-page designs both choose a fitting orientation and do not clip visible content.
- [ ] Tests are deterministic across Linux, macOS, and Windows and avoid brittle full-page golden snapshots.
close_reason: Fixed Design Report first-page canvas frame rendering: the embedded canvas image now paints before the frame border, and image-backed frames fit wide and tall canvas image aspects instead of leaving a large mismatched blank frame. Tests: CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_ -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Improve Consortium chart wrapping and pagination
description:
## Slice Summary
Improve Consortium chart and table report layout so localized names and phase labels do not split awkwardly, and continuation pages are only added when content genuinely needs them.

## User Value
Consortium report pages remain readable and efficient for dense multilingual designs.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Consortium planning data, ecological phases, or user-facing Consortium edit behavior in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Improve Consortium report chart wrapping and continuation-page density.

**Current behavior:**
Consortium chart labels and localized plant names can split mid-word, and the report can add a continuation page with only a few rows and large unused space.

**Desired behavior:**
Consortium chart and table pages should keep localized names readable, avoid awkward mid-word splits for normal labels, and paginate based on the actual rendered content height closely enough that sparse continuation pages are avoided unless content genuinely requires them.

**Key interfaces:**
- Structured Design Report Consortium input.
- Consortium phase/stratum chart layout, wrapping, swatches, and table pagination.
- Locale resources for Consortium labels.
- Report page measurement and page-break logic.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Consortium planning semantics, phases, strata, or edit workflows.
- Removing required Consortium information to make the page shorter.
- Replacing the shared report renderer.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Consortium chart labels avoid awkward mid-word splits for normal French and localized common-name text.
- [ ] Phase labels such as "Secondaire 1" fit cleanly through abbreviation, wrapping, font sizing, legend treatment, or another readable report layout.
- [ ] Chart cells keep stable alignment and swatches.
- [ ] Table pagination uses rendered row and chart heights closely enough to avoid unnecessary mostly blank continuation pages.
- [ ] A representative ten-row Consortium design fits without a sparse continuation page unless content genuinely requires it.
- [ ] Tests cover all strata, all phases, long localized names, dense cells, and continuation-page behavior with CI-stable assertions.
close_reason: Improved Consortium Design Report layout: dense ten-row multilingual chart/table sections no longer create sparse continuation pages, normal French/common-name chart words avoid mid-word splits, and dense pagination coverage still verifies repeated headers. Tests: CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_keeps_ -- --nocapture; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_ -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Make visible map and terrain layers honest in Design Reports
description:
## Slice Summary
Ensure Design Reports print the visible map-driven Layer content they claim to include, such as base map, terrain, and contour presentation, while still using the report's white-background ink-saving behavior.

## User Value
When a user leaves Layers visible, the Design Report output matches what the visible Layer list says was included.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new map styling controls, new Layer types, or non-report map features in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make Design Report output match visible map-driven Layers.

**Current behavior:**
The Design Report can list map-related Layers such as base or contours as visible while the printed canvas image does not visibly include those Layers.

**Desired behavior:**
If map-driven Layers are visible in the design, the report includes a printable representation of that visible content while preserving the report's white-background, ink-saving intent. Hidden map-driven Layers stay omitted. The visible Layer list must describe what the report actually rendered.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Layer Presentation for visible Layer labels and visibility.
- Canvas Map Surface or equivalent printable map/terrain/contour report projection.
- Design Report canvas image generation and white-background export behavior.
- Locale resources for Layer labels.

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new map provider settings or user-facing map style controls.
- Printing dark app chrome or non-design UI.
- Changing non-report map interaction behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Visible base map, terrain, contour, or other map-driven Layers have a printable representation in the Design Report or a deliberate documented print-safe fallback that is reflected in the visible Layer list.
- [ ] Hidden map-driven Layers remain omitted from both the report image and the visible Layer list.
- [ ] The visible Layer list matches the Layers actually represented in the report output.
- [ ] White-background ink-saving export remains available and does not reintroduce dark app or map chrome.
- [ ] Tests cover visible and hidden map-driven Layers plus normal scene Layers with deterministic CI assertions across supported operating systems.
- [ ] Locale changes still localize visible Layer labels.
close_reason: Shipped deterministic print-safe map underlays for visible base, contour, and hillshade layers in Design Reports; hidden map layers stay out of the image request and visible scene layers remain localized. Added report request coverage and Canvas2D underlay ordering/state-boundary coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- design-report.test.ts canvas2d-scene.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Render Timeline report rows as print-native tables
description:
## Slice Summary
Replace remaining pipe-delimited Timeline text in Design Reports with structured, wrapped table content and verify localized labels for all supported UI languages.

## User Value
Timeline content is readable in print and does not look like raw debug text or a failed table export.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign Timeline editing, scheduling rules, or Timeline data storage in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Render Timeline report content as structured tables in every supported UI language.

**Current behavior:**
Timeline report pages still contain pipe-delimited row and header text, and the French start-date header can appear without its accent.

**Desired behavior:**
Timeline sections render as print-native, wrapped table content with stable columns, repeated headers across page breaks, and localized labels for all supported UI languages. Optional empty fields should be omitted or represented consistently without raw separators.

**Key interfaces:**
- Structured Design Report Timeline input.
- Design Report table measurement, wrapping, and pagination.
- Locale resources for report Timeline labels.
- All-locale report input/test fixtures.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Timeline editing or scheduling data semantics.
- Adding new Timeline fields.
- Rewriting unrelated report sections.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Timeline headers and rows no longer render pipe-delimited text in exported PDFs or extracted report text.
- [ ] Long targets, descriptions, dependencies, and localized labels wrap inside stable columns without overlap.
- [ ] Dense Timeline sections paginate with repeated headers.
- [ ] Empty optional Timeline fields remain omitted or localized consistently.
- [ ] The French start-date label renders as "Début" and all supported locale resources remain complete.
- [ ] Tests cover French, all-locale label completeness, long targets, and sparse scheduled or unscheduled Timeline actions.
close_reason: Rendered Timeline Design Report rows as wrapped print-native columns, removed pipe-delimited Timeline row/detail text, and corrected the French start-date label to Début. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts; npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_ -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Fix Unicode and font rendering in Design Reports
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix Design Report text rendering so non-ASCII text, currency symbols, and supported UI-language scripts render correctly in generated reports.

## User Value
Designers can export reports in any supported language without mojibake or missing glyphs, independent of operating system or installed fonts.

## Out of Scope
Changing report layout, canvas fidelity, planning-section semantics, or adding export-time customization.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make Design Report text render correctly for every supported UI language on every supported desktop platform.

**Current behavior:**
Design Reports can render valid UTF-8 Design content as mojibake, for example French accented text and Euro symbols appear corrupted. The renderer also relies on built-in PDF font behavior that cannot cover Latin accents, Cyrillic, Japanese, Korean, and Chinese deterministically.

**Desired behavior:**
Design Reports embed bundled report fonts or an equivalent deterministic font resource strategy so all supported UI languages render readable glyphs on Linux, macOS, and Windows without depending on system fonts. Text extraction should preserve readable Unicode text for representative report strings. PDF metadata should not use placeholder epoch timestamps.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report structured read model
- Shared Rust Design Report renderer
- Bundled report font/resource loading
- PDF serialization and metadata
- Locale coverage for all supported UI languages

**Out of scope:**
- Reworking canvas image fidelity
- Changing Timeline, Budget, or Consortium semantics
- Adding user-selectable fonts or export customization
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Design Reports render accented Latin, Cyrillic, Japanese, Korean, Chinese, and currency symbols without mojibake or replacement glyphs.
- [ ] Report font behavior is deterministic on Linux, macOS, and Windows and does not rely on locally installed system fonts.
- [ ] A focused renderer test exercises representative strings from every supported UI language.
- [ ] PDF text extraction for representative non-ASCII report text returns readable Unicode text.
- [ ] Generated PDF metadata no longer uses placeholder epoch creation/modification dates.
- [ ] Existing empty-section omission and pagination behavior remains intact.
close_reason: Embedded deterministic report fonts, fixed Unicode PDF text extraction and non-epoch metadata, and preserved pagination/empty-section behavior. Tests: CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Render first Design Report page from faithful white canvas image
description:
## Slice Summary
Replace the current reconstructed first-page drawing with a faithful print-ready canvas image that respects visible Layers and forces a white report background.

## User Value
Designers can inspect the exported design itself without dense debug labels, black text blobs, missing fills, or marker mismatches.

## Out of Scope
Changing the interactive canvas renderer, adding export controls, or redesigning non-canvas report sections.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Render the first Design Report page from a faithful white-background canvas image.

**Current behavior:**
The first Design Report page reconstructs Design objects as rough PDF primitives. It labels every Zone, Placed Plant, and Annotation with names, raw IDs, symbols, colors, and debug-like details, creating unreadable black text blobs for dense Designs. Plant symbols, colors, Zone fills, and visual footprint behavior do not match the canvas.

**Desired behavior:**
The first Design Report page should use a print-ready canvas image generated from the current Design presentation with a forced white background. It should respect visible Layers, omit app chrome, preserve visible Design object appearance, and avoid general per-plant labels except explicit Pinned Plant Names and intentional report legends.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report canvas page read model
- Canvas Runtime Surface or canvas document surface serialization
- Layer visibility and presentation state
- Plant Symbol, color, Zone fill, Annotation, Measurement Guide, and Pinned Plant Name presentation
- Cross-platform image embedding in the shared report renderer

**Out of scope:**
- Changing interactive canvas editing behavior
- Redesigning Timeline, Budget, or Consortium pages
- Adding export-time customization controls
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dense Designs no longer render unreadable general per-plant label blobs on the first report page.
- [ ] The first page uses a faithful white-background canvas image rather than raw per-object debug labels.
- [ ] Visible Layers are respected; hidden Layer contents are omitted from the report image.
- [ ] Plant Symbols, plant colors, Zone fills, Annotations, Measurement Guides, and Pinned Plant Names match the intended canvas presentation.
- [ ] Internal IDs, UUIDs, raw Zone types, raw symbol keys, and raw color hex strings do not appear as canvas labels unless explicitly part of user-authored text.
- [ ] The first page still adapts to portrait or landscape based on Design bounds.
- [ ] Tests cover a dense multi-layer Design with pinned names, hidden Layers, Zones, Annotations, and Measurement Guides.
close_reason: Implemented first-page Design Report canvas image export using the Canvas2D scene renderer on a white background and backend PDF image embedding, with dense object labels omitted from PDF text. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts canvas2d-scene.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Make Budget report rows print-native and localized
description:
## Slice Summary
Polish Budget report rendering for localized categories, currencies, zero-value rows, sparse budgets, and clean printable table layout.

## User Value
Designers can print budget information without corrupted currency text, raw categories, or awkward sparse pages.

## Out of Scope
Changing Budget Item editing, pricing workflows, CSV export, or adding new budget fields.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make Budget report rows print-native, localized, and readable for sparse or zero-value budgets.

**Current behavior:**
Budget report rows can expose raw category keys and corrupted currency text when font handling is incomplete. Sparse budgets can produce a mostly empty page that still feels unfinished or awkward.

**Desired behavior:**
Budget report output should use localized category labels, stable currency formatting, clean totals, and a polished layout for both dense and sparse Budget Items. Zero-value rows should be represented intentionally and not look like a rendering failure.

**Key interfaces:**
- Budget Item read model
- Budget currency formatting
- Target label resolution
- Design Report Budget rows and totals
- PDF Budget pagination

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Budget Item editing or pricing behavior
- Changing CSV export
- Adding new Budget Item fields
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Budget categories are localized user-facing labels.
- [ ] Currency amounts and symbols render correctly after the report font work is complete.
- [ ] Zero-value Budget Items and zero totals are displayed intentionally and readably.
- [ ] Sparse Budget sections use polished spacing and do not look like accidental blank pages.
- [ ] Dense Budget sections still paginate with repeated headers and complete totals.
- [ ] Tests cover localized categories, multiple currencies where supported by current data, zero totals, sparse rows, and dense pagination.
close_reason: Implemented print-native Budget report rows with fixed PDF columns, trimmed empty budget descriptions, represented zero-value rows and multi-currency zero totals intentionally, and suppressed empty table headers for totals-only budgets. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Replace Consortium pipe-text dump with real print chart
description:
## Slice Summary
Render the Consortium section as a real print-native Stratum by Succession Phase chart plus complete localized table instead of wrapped pipe-delimited text.

## User Value
Designers can read Consortium planning at print scale, including species, phases, counts, and colors without broken alignment.

## Out of Scope
Changing Consortium editing behavior, auto-sync defaults, or the Consortium Time Model.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Replace the Consortium report pipe-text dump with a print-native chart and complete table.

**Current behavior:**
The Consortium report chart is rendered as wrapped pipe-delimited text. Dense cells break alignment, color information appears as raw hex text, and the chart is difficult to read at print scale.

**Desired behavior:**
The Consortium report should render a real Stratum by Succession Phase chart with stable columns, readable wrapped cell content, and visual color swatches or another print-native color cue. The complete localized table should remain present and paginated when needed.

**Key interfaces:**
- Consortium Planning Projection
- Consortium Time Model and Stratum labels
- Design Report Consortium chart rows and table rows
- PDF chart/table layout and pagination
- Localized Common Names and Canonical Names

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Consortium editing behavior
- Changing auto-created default Consortium behavior
- Adding new Consortium lane concepts
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Consortium chart renders with stable Stratum rows and Succession Phase columns instead of pipe-delimited text.
- [ ] Dense chart cells wrap without breaking column alignment or overlapping adjacent content.
- [ ] Species colors are represented with swatches or another polished visual cue rather than raw hex-only text.
- [ ] The complete active Consortium table remains present with localized Common Name, Canonical Name where helpful, Stratum, start/end phases, and counts.
- [ ] Pagination preserves headings, repeated table headers, and readable spacing for dense Consortium data.
- [ ] Tests cover dense cells, long localized names, all Strata, all Succession Phases, and table completeness.
close_reason: Replaced the Consortium report pipe-text chart/table rendering with print-native PDF grid/table cells, wrapped dense phase content, and color swatches without raw hex text. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Make Timeline report summaries honest for sparse data
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix Timeline report summaries and rows so mixed scheduled and unscheduled actions, empty descriptions, dependencies, and recurrence values print clearly.

## User Value
Designers can trust the Timeline section and do not mistake a partial date range for all actions being scheduled.

## Out of Scope
Changing Timeline Action editing behavior, recurrence modeling, or target selection workflows.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make Timeline report summaries and rows accurate when Timeline Actions have sparse data.

**Current behavior:**
Timeline report overview rows can summarize a date range from only scheduled actions while counting unscheduled actions in the same type. Rows with empty descriptions print blank Description lines, which looks incomplete.

**Desired behavior:**
Timeline report summaries should clearly represent mixed scheduled and unscheduled Timeline Actions. Rows should omit empty optional fields or use localized empty values consistently. The section should remain readable, localized, and complete without implying data that is not present.

**Key interfaces:**
- Timeline Action read model
- Planning Projection target labels
- Design Report Timeline overview rows
- Localized date and empty-value formatting
- PDF Timeline pagination and repeated headers

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Timeline Action editing behavior
- Changing recurrence data modeling
- Adding new Timeline fields
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Timeline overview rows distinguish scheduled, unscheduled, and mixed action groups without misleading date ranges.
- [ ] Timeline rows with empty descriptions do not render blank Description lines.
- [ ] Empty recurrence, dependency, target, and date values use localized report behavior consistently.
- [ ] Timeline action labels, dates, and status text remain localized.
- [ ] Pagination and repeated table headers still work for dense Timeline sections.
- [ ] Focused tests cover mixed scheduled/unscheduled actions, empty descriptions, multiple targets, and all relevant localized text.
close_reason: Implemented honest Timeline report summaries for mixed scheduled/unscheduled action groups, trimmed empty action descriptions, localized mixed-summary status in all UI languages, and omitted blank Description lines in PDF rows. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Sanitize and localize Design Report chrome and planning values
description:
## Slice Summary
Remove raw implementation values from Design Report text and replace them with localized, user-facing labels and print-native visual cues.

## User Value
Reports look intentional in every supported language and do not expose enum keys, Layer internals, raw color strings, or debug IDs.

## Out of Scope
Changing the meaning of Timeline Actions, Budget Items, Consortium entries, or the canvas image renderer.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Remove raw implementation values from Design Report chrome and replace them with localized user-facing labels.

**Current behavior:**
Design Reports can print raw or internal values such as Layer keys, action type codes, Budget category keys, raw color strings, debug IDs, and English-only page number text in localized reports.

**Desired behavior:**
All Design Report chrome and non-user-authored planning labels should use localized, user-facing text. Raw implementation keys should be converted to labels or print-native visuals such as swatches. Page numbers, Layer labels, Timeline action types, Budget categories, and common report labels should be localized for every supported UI language.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report read model labels
- App i18n resources for all supported UI languages
- Layer presentation labels
- Timeline Action type labels
- Budget Item category labels
- Report legends and page numbering

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the meaning of planning entries
- Reworking the first-page canvas image
- Redesigning Consortium chart geometry
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Page number text is localized and works for every supported UI language.
- [ ] Visible Layer names in the report use user-facing localized labels, not raw Layer keys.
- [ ] Timeline action types print localized labels without raw enum codes unless explicitly needed for debugging outside user reports.
- [ ] Budget categories print localized labels instead of raw category keys.
- [ ] Report legends use swatches or polished labels instead of raw debug-style color strings where color is visual information.
- [ ] Internal IDs, UUIDs, and implementation keys are absent from normal report output.
- [ ] i18n completeness tests cover every new report label in all supported locales.
close_reason: Shipped localized Design Report chrome: localized page numbers/layer names/budget categories/dependency counts, sanitized raw enum/id/color text from report renderer, and covered new labels across all locales. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Use release version authority for About dialog
description: The About Canopi dialog currently hard-codes an older app version while release metadata is 0.8.0. Make the About version follow the release-version authority and document the release step so future releases do not drift.
acceptance_criteria: About dialog displays the release version from the app release authority; release process documents updating/validating the About-visible version; focused tests/type checks pass.
close_reason: About dialog now reads the app release version from desktop/tauri.conf.json, release-candidate preflight validates Cargo/npm/package-lock metadata drift against that authority, and release docs describe the version authority. Verified with npm test -- about-canopi-dialog.test.tsx, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run build, and a local run of the preflight version parser.

title: Make Saved Stamp reorder preview respond sooner
description: Saved Stamp reorder now stays active, but the preview feels behind the pointer when dragging downward because insertion waits too far into the target row. Adjust the pointer threshold so the reordered preview moves a little sooner while keeping preview and commit aligned.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a Saved Stamp downward updates the preview before the pointer reaches the target row midpoint, commits the same order on pointerup, and existing upward/downward/end reorder regressions continue to pass.
close_reason: Made Saved Stamp reorder feel less sticky by biasing the insertion threshold in the active drag direction: downward previews cross at 40% of the target row and upward previews at 60%. Added a regression that verifies downward preview updates just before the old midpoint and still commits the previewed order; verified with focused FavoritesPanel Vitest and TypeScript check.

title: Keep Saved Stamp downward reorder session alive
description: Dragging a Saved Stamp downward can revert to the original order or behave like it released after one row. The pointer reorder grip likely loses pointer capture when the dragged row reflows downward, and the current lostpointercapture handler cancels the session. The reorder session should survive capture loss and continue from document-level pointermove/pointerup so downward drags stay active until real pointerup/cancel.
acceptance_criteria: A downward Saved Stamp reorder that loses pointer capture after the first preview continues to update on document pointermove and commits the final previewed order on pointerup. Pointercancel still aborts. Upward reorder and stamp-body drag-to-canvas remain covered.
close_reason: Kept Saved Stamp downward reorder active after row reflow by moving pointermove/pointerup ownership to document listeners and no longer treating lost pointer capture as cancel. Pointercancel still aborts. Added a regression covering capture loss during downward drag; verified with focused FavoritesPanel Vitest and TypeScript check.

title: Replace Saved Stamp reorder with pointer session
description: Saved Stamp reorder preview can still diverge from the released result because native drag/drop computes preview from dragover targets and commit from later drop/dragend targets after the row list has already reflowed. The reorder grip should use a pointer-captured reorder session so preview and commit share one ordered-id state and one geometry model. Stamp body drag-to-canvas should keep native drag/drop.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a Saved Stamp reorder grip previews from pointer position and pointerup commits the same ids that were last previewed. Moving a stamp above, below, or to the end works without relying on DataTransfer. Dragging the stamp body to the canvas still writes Saved Object Stamp drag data. Regression coverage exercises pointer-based reorder and asserts preview order equals committed order.
close_reason: Replaced Saved Stamp grip reordering with a pointer-captured reorder session so preview and release share the same latest ordered ids. Removed native DataTransfer-based reorder handling from the grip while keeping stamp-body drag-to-canvas native. Added pointer-session regressions for before, after, after-last, and preview-equals-commit behavior; verified with focused FavoritesPanel Vitest and TypeScript check.

title: Keep Saved Stamp reorder stable after release
description: Saved Stamp reorder preview is correct during drag, but after releasing the mouse the row can snap back to its original position. The commit path should keep the intended order visible through async persistence and not clear the preview until the Saved Stamp library reflects that order or the persistence call fails.
acceptance_criteria: After dropping a Saved Stamp while reorder persistence is still pending, the visible list keeps the dropped order instead of reverting. Once persistence resolves with the same order, the preview can clear without a visual jump. Regression coverage should simulate pending persistence.
close_reason: Fixed release-time Saved Stamp reorder snapback by keeping the committed preview active through the post-drop dragend event until the Saved Stamp library order catches up, and reverting only on persistence failure. Added a regression that dispatches drop followed by dragend and verifies the dropped order remains visible; verified with focused FavoritesPanel Vitest and TypeScript check.

title: Improve Saved Stamp reorder drop targets
description: Saved Stamp reordering feels unlike modern playlist reordering: dragging over a row always inserts before it, so dragging below the last stamp has no after-last destination and lower-half row hovers feel delayed or wrong. Reorder preview and drop should use row midpoint intent, including inserting after the hovered row and therefore after the last row.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a Saved Stamp reorder grip over the upper half of a row previews/drops before that row. Dragging over the lower half previews/drops after that row. Dragging onto the lower half of the bottom row can move a stamp to the end. Persistence still happens once on drop.
close_reason: Fixed Saved Stamp reorder feel by switching row drops from before-only insertion to midpoint-based before/after insertion, and by making the list space below the final row commit the current after-last preview. Added regressions for lower-half row drops and bottom-space drops; verified with focused FavoritesPanel Vitest and TypeScript check.

title: Fix live Saved Stamp reorder preview
description: Saved Stamp reordering in the Favorites panel updates only after drop in real browser drag sessions. The live reorder preview should update during dragover even when browser-protected DataTransfer payload data is unavailable until drop.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a Saved Stamp reorder grip over another stamp changes the visible row order before drop. Persistence still happens once on drop. Regression coverage simulates protected dragover data where DataTransfer types are visible but getData returns an empty string until drop.
close_reason: Fixed live Saved Stamp reorder preview by keeping the active reorder source id in the Favorites panel during the drag session, because browser dragover exposes DataTransfer types but not custom payload data. Added a protected-dragover regression in favorites-panel.test.tsx and verified with focused Vitest plus TypeScript check.

title: Improve Saved Object Stamp UI and placement preview
description: Saved Object Stamp rows and drag/placement previews are visually inconsistent with the field-notebook interface. The placement ghost must match the created plants, zones, annotations, and captured groups exactly enough that users can trust what will be placed.
acceptance_criteria: Saved Stamps panel rows follow the app design system; dragging a stamp body still starts placement while the reorder grip remains panel-only; stamp drag/drop and armed placement ghosts preserve relative geometry, annotation scale, and zone rotation; focused frontend tests cover the corrected behavior.
close_reason: Improved Saved Object Stamp panel rows to match the field-notebook chrome and replaced mismatch-prone DOM rectangle ghosts with SVG previews built from the same placement entities and geometry helpers used by the runtime. Tests: npm test -- --run src/__tests__/favorites-panel.test.tsx; npm test -- --run src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Activate canvas after Species drop
description: After dropping a Species from the Species Catalog Workbench or Favorites panel, selecting the new Placed Plant and focusing the interaction host still does not make the next click-drag work for users. Diagnose the real canvas activation behavior, implement the equivalent of an activation click at the correct seam after successful drop, and cover the real post-drop first drag behavior with regression tests.
acceptance_criteria: After a successful Species drag/drop, users can immediately drag the dropped Placed Plant without an extra canvas click. The fix activates the canvas at the runtime/app seam rather than relying only on DOM focus. Regression coverage fails before the fix and passes after it, and existing drag/drop locked-layer behavior remains unchanged.
close_reason: Fixed the post-drop activation gap by having real canvas pointerdowns synchronously claim the interaction host: ignored UI targets still return, then canvas gestures prevent the browser default activation behavior and focus the runtime host before pan/tool/select routing. The regression now simulates drag-source focus returning after native drop and verifies the first drag pointerdown is claimed and moves the dropped Placed Plant. Tests: npm test -- --run src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'returns to Select and reactivates the canvas after a native plant drop'; npm test -- --run src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Fix first drag after Species drop
description: After dropping a Species from the Species Catalog Workbench or Favorites panel, the new Placed Plant is selected but users still cannot immediately click-drag on the canvas; the first click appears to activate or reset the canvas instead. Reproduce the missing first post-drop drag behavior, fix the drop/gesture/focus ordering, and add regression coverage that exercises the real post-drop first drag.
acceptance_criteria: A successful Species drag/drop leaves the canvas ready for an immediate first click-drag of the selected Placed Plant without an extra activation click. Regression coverage fails before the fix and passes after it, covering the drop path and first post-drop drag gesture.
close_reason: Fixed first-drag friction after Species drops by focusing the canvas interaction host after a successful native drop. The failing regression now covers side-panel focus handoff plus immediate first drag of the selected dropped Placed Plant. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'returns to Select'; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Fix extra canvas activation click after dragging Species
description: Dragging a Species from the Species Catalog Workbench or Favorites panel leaves the canvas in a state where the next click only activates the canvas instead of interacting with Design Objects. Diagnose the drag/drop focus or interaction ordering, add a focused regression test, and restore immediate canvas interaction after the drop.
acceptance_criteria: After dragging a Species from catalog or favorites, the canvas can select or drag existing Design Objects on the first subsequent click. Regression coverage exercises the drag/drop path and verifies no extra activation click is required.
close_reason: Fixed native Species drag/drop so successful drops select the placed plant and return the canvas runtime to Select, letting the next canvas gesture edit Design Objects immediately. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'returns to Select'; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Fix release promotion release-note printf bullets
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the release promotion path so release-note generation does not abort when emitting literal bullet or heading lines that begin with a hyphen.

## User Value
Release maintainers can run promotion without bash printf treating release-note text as command options and terminating the script under strict error handling.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign the release workflow, change promotion semantics, or modify unrelated release-note content generation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Prevent release promotion from aborting while generating release-note lines that start with hyphens.

**Current behavior:**
The promotion script emits release-note headings or bullet lines through `printf` using literal format strings that begin with `-`. Under bash, `printf` can interpret such a format string as an option. With strict error handling enabled, the script aborts while generating release notes.

**Desired behavior:**
Release-note generation must safely emit literal text that starts with `-` without bash option parsing failures. The promotion script should continue to use strict error handling, and the fix should apply consistently to every release-note `printf` call in the affected block that can start with a hyphen.

**Key interfaces:**
- Release promotion shell script strict-mode behavior
- Bash `printf` invocation semantics for literal strings beginning with `-`
- Release-note generation output contract

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning the release promotion flow
- Changing release-note content beyond the escaping or formatting needed for safe emission
- Running an actual release promotion as validation unless explicitly requested
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Every release-note `printf` call in the affected generation block that can begin with `-` is made safe with `printf --`, a `%s` format, or an equivalent bash-safe pattern.
- [ ] The script retains strict error handling and does not mask failures globally to work around the issue.
- [ ] A focused validation path is documented or run that demonstrates the script no longer fails solely because release-note output begins with hyphenated headings or bullets.
- [ ] Unrelated dirty/user-owned changes in the release script are preserved and not reverted.
close_reason: Fixed release-note generation so bash printf treats hyphen-leading headings and bullets as literal formats via printf --. Validated with focused strict-shell printf coverage and bash -n scripts/promote-release.sh.

title: Preserve Plant Symbol species defaults through document normalization
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the document normalization/load/save path so v3 Plant Symbol species defaults survive opening and saving a Design.

## User Value
Users who set Plant Symbol defaults for species do not lose those defaults when a Design is reopened or saved again.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the Plant Symbol picker UX or introduce new symbol IDs.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Preserve Plant Symbol species defaults through document normalization and save composition.

**Current behavior:**
Plant Symbol species defaults can be represented in the Design file and canvas scene state, but the document normalization layer does not preserve the top-level `plant_species_symbols` field. Loading or composing a Design can drop non-empty species symbol defaults, causing them to be saved back as empty or missing.

**Desired behavior:**
The normalized document and composed save output preserve `plant_species_symbols` the same way other scene-owned known fields are preserved. Opening a file with species symbol defaults, saving it, and reopening it should keep those defaults intact.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design file normalization and known-field handling
- Document load/save composition
- Scene-owned file fields and generated field ownership metadata
- File format round-trip tests

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new Plant Symbol IDs
- Changing Plant Symbol renderer behavior
- Changing the Plant Symbol picker UX
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `plant_species_symbols` is included in document normalization so known-field filtering does not drop it.
- [ ] Save composition preserves non-empty Plant Symbol species defaults from the canvas scene output.
- [ ] Loading and saving a Design with `plant_species_symbols` round-trips the field unchanged.
- [ ] Focused tests fail before the fix and pass after it, covering both load normalization and save composition or the nearest existing round-trip contract.
- [ ] Required frontend gates pass, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Preserved Plant Symbol species defaults through document normalization and save composition by normalizing plant_species_symbols with focused document contract coverage and TypeScript check passing.

title: Restore plant drag-and-drop onto canvas
description: After the latest pushed frontend interaction changes, dragging plant rows/cards from the Plant Database or Favorites side panel onto the canvas no longer creates plant placements. Diagnose the event routing and restore drop behavior without regressing active pointer gestures across runtime overlays.
acceptance_criteria: Plant drag data dropped from Plant Database or Favorites creates a plant on the canvas; invalid or locked-layer drops are still rejected; regression coverage exercises the real event/listener path instead of only private drop handlers.
close_reason: Fixed plant drag-and-drop by accepting protected browser dragover events via DataTransfer MIME types and keeping payload parsing on drop. Tests: npm test -- plant-stamp-source.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Exclude locked targets from selection drag capture
description:
## Slice Summary
Mixed locked and unlocked canvas selections must not let locked Design Objects participate in drag movement.

## User Value
A user can select a locked object for the Unlock affordance without accidentally moving it when dragging other selected editable objects.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not redesign mixed selection semantics, lock toolbar UX, grouping, or layer-lock behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Mixed locked and unlocked canvas selections must not move locked Design Objects during drag.

**Current behavior:**
A directly locked Design Object can be selected for the Unlock affordance. When that locked target is added to an existing unlocked selection and the user drags an editable selected object, the drag capture can include the raw mixed selection and move the locked target.

**Desired behavior:**
Locked Design Objects may remain selected for Unlock, but drag movement must capture only editable targets. If a selection contains both editable and locked targets, dragging an editable object moves only the editable targets and leaves locked targets unchanged. If no editable target is available, no movement transaction should be created.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas selection read model for editable versus locked Design Object targets
- Shared selection drag gesture handling
- Scene Edit transaction boundary for movement
- Design Object lock state on Plants, Zones, Annotations, and Object Groups

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning locked-object selection or Unlock toolbar behavior
- Changing layer-lock selection semantics
- Changing grouping or rotation behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Shift-selecting a locked object into an unlocked selection does not let that locked object move during drag.
- [ ] Mixed selections still move editable selected objects normally.
- [ ] Locked-only selections do not start movement transactions.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover the mixed locked/unlocked drag path.
- [ ] Existing Unlock selection behavior for directly locked objects remains intact.
close_reason: Fixed mixed locked/unlocked selection drags so drag capture uses editable Design Object targets only; locked selected targets remain selected for Unlock but do not move. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'drags only editable objects from a mixed locked and unlocked selection'; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime/mutations.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts serializer-plant-species-colors.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: Run MapLibre Host destroy hooks after post-create failures
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the MapLibre Host post-create failure path so adapter cleanup hooks run before the failed map is removed and the initialization error is reported.

## User Value
Map-backed adapters cannot leave stale listeners, refs, or overlays behind when setup fails after the host has published a map context. Failed initialization cleans up both host-owned and adapter-owned resources.

## Out of Scope
Do not change successful map creation, normal destroy semantics, preserved view behavior, or product behavior for map-backed surfaces.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Run MapLibre Host adapter cleanup hooks when post-create setup fails.

**Current behavior:**
If post-create setup fails after the MapLibre Host has published a context, the host removes the MapLibre instance and clears host-owned state, but it can bypass the request's destroy cleanup hook. Adapters that attached listeners, refs, overlays, or other surface-owned resources during partial setup can therefore retain stale state after the map is removed.

**Desired behavior:**
Any failure after a MapLibre Host context is published uses the same adapter cleanup guarantee as normal destroy: the request cleanup hook runs and the MapLibre instance is removed in a finally-style path. The host still clears the current context, disconnects resize observation, and reports the create error after cleanup. Cleanup-hook failures should not prevent map removal.

**Key interfaces:**
- MapLibre Host lifecycle and current context semantics
- Request post-create and destroy cleanup hooks
- Map-backed surface adapter resource ownership
- MapLibre Host regression tests

**Out of scope:**
