- Changing successful map creation, key rebuild, resize, or preserved view behavior
- Moving adapter-owned listener/ref/overlay logic into the shared host
- Changing product behavior for Canvas Map Surface, Location, or Design Template maps
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Regression coverage simulates post-create setup throwing after adapter-owned setup has partially run.
- [ ] The failed create path invokes the request destroy cleanup hook before/while removing the failed map.
- [ ] The failed create path still disconnects resize observation, clears the current context, removes the MapLibre instance, and reports the create error.
- [ ] If the destroy cleanup hook throws, the failed map is still removed and the initialization error path remains deterministic.
- [ ] Focused MapLibre Host tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` pass.
close_reason: Fixed MapLibre Host failed-create cleanup to run adapter onDestroy hooks before removing failed maps, while still removing maps and reporting create errors when cleanup throws. Tests: npm test -- maplibre-host.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Clean up MapLibre Host after post-create setup failures
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the MapLibre Host failure path so a map that fails during caller post-create setup is fully torn down before the initialization error is reported.

## User Value
Map-backed surfaces cannot show an initialization failure while retaining a reusable partially initialized MapLibre instance. Retry and same-key requests start from a clean resource state.

## Out of Scope
Do not change successful map creation, basemap style semantics, preserved view behavior, or product behavior for map-backed surfaces.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Clean up MapLibre Host resources when caller post-create setup fails.

**Current behavior:**
If caller setup after MapLibre map creation throws after the host has installed its map context and resize observer, the host reports an initialization error but can leave the partially initialized map/context alive. A caller can then see an error while `current()` still exposes a live map, and a same-key update can reuse the failed context.

**Desired behavior:**
Any failure during map creation or post-create setup leaves no live MapLibre context, map instance, resize observer, or stale initialization state in the host. The host reports the create error only after tearing down the partial context, and a retry starts from a clean map creation path.

**Key interfaces:**
- MapLibre Host lifecycle and `current()` behavior
- Caller post-create setup callback
- Resize observer ownership
- Map-backed surface host adapter tests

**Out of scope:**
- Changing successful map creation, basemap style rebuild, or preserved view semantics
- Changing Canvas Map Surface, Location, or Design Template product behavior beyond the failure cleanup path
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Regression coverage simulates caller post-create setup throwing after the map context and resize observer are installed.
- [ ] The failed create path removes the MapLibre instance, disconnects the resize observer, clears the current host context, and does not leave `current()` exposing the failed map.
- [ ] A same-key retry after the failure creates a fresh MapLibre instance instead of reusing the failed context.
- [ ] Existing successful MapLibre Host create, rebuild, preserved view, resize, and destroy behavior remains covered.
- [ ] Focused MapLibre Host tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` pass.
close_reason: Fixed MapLibre Host post-create failure cleanup so failed contexts are removed before reporting errors, with regression coverage for observer disconnect, map removal, current() clearing, same-key retry, plus npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Use latest saved Location during lazy map creation
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix Location map editing so lazy MapLibre creation reads the latest saved Location at actual map creation time instead of using the Location captured when the effect started.

## User Value
If the Design Location changes while MapLibre is still loading, the map opens centered and zoomed on the current saved Location, keeping the initial view consistent with pin projection and commit behavior.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Location draft authority, geocoding search lifecycle, MapLibre Host behavior outside the Location adapter, or Canvas Map Surface behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Read the current saved Location when Location map creation actually runs after lazy loading.

**Current behavior:**
Location map editing can capture the saved Location at effect start. If MapLibre is still lazy-loading and the Design Location changes before the map is actually created, the initial map center and zoom can use stale saved-location data while pin projection and commit behavior use the current saved-location ref.

**Desired behavior:**
The Location map creation callback reads the latest saved Location at the time the map is created. Initial center/zoom, saved pin projection, pending search-result preview, drag clearing, and map-center commit behavior stay consistent with the current Design Location after lazy loading completes.

**Key interfaces:**
- Location map editing host
- MapLibre Host `createMap` callback
- Saved Location presentation/read model
- Location map editing tests

**Out of scope:**
- Moving saved Location authority out of the Location Workbench
- Changing geocoding search lifecycle or pending search-result semantics
- Changing Canvas Map Surface or Design Template world map behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Regression coverage simulates saved Location changing while MapLibre is lazy-loading before the Location map is created.
- [ ] The Location map initial center and zoom use the latest saved Location at creation time.
- [ ] Saved pin projection, pending search-result preview, drag clearing, initialization failure state, and map-center commit behavior remain unchanged.
- [ ] Focused Location map tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` pass.
close_reason: Fixed Location map lazy creation to read the latest saved Location from the ref at create time, with regression coverage for location changes during lazy MapLibre load plus focused Location/MapLibre tests and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Introduce Planning Canvas Interaction Frame through Timeline Action Canvas
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame as the shared planning-surface interaction lifetime seam and route Timeline Action Canvas through it as the tracer bullet.

## User Value
Timeline Action Canvas keeps its current interaction behavior while document listeners, drag cleanup, stale selection cleanup, and Target Presentation write ordering move behind a reusable planning-specific interface.

## Out of Scope
Do not migrate Consortium planning in this slice. Do not change Timeline Action geometry, renderer output, popover fields, or Design Edit command semantics.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Introduce Planning Canvas Interaction Frame through Timeline Action Canvas.

**Current behavior:**
Timeline Action Canvas owns a broad interaction stack that includes document listeners, drag preview/commit/abort, popover ordering, hover cleanup, stale selection cleanup, and Target Presentation writes. Similar planning-canvas lifetime concerns also exist in Consortium planning.

**Desired behavior:**
Planning Canvas Interaction Frame exists as a planning-specific seam and Timeline Action Canvas uses it as the tracer bullet. Timeline Action Canvas keeps its geometry, rendering, popover details, Timeline Action Workbench commands, and Design Edit behavior while shared interaction ordering moves behind the frame.

**Key interfaces:**
- Planning Canvas Interaction Frame interface for pointer/document listener lifetime, drag lifecycle, hover cleanup, stale selection cleanup, and Target Presentation write ordering
- Timeline Action Canvas host model and controller behavior
- Timeline Action Workbench and Design Edit seams
- Timeline interaction-frame tests

**Out of scope:**
- Migrating Consortium planning
- Changing Timeline Action geometry, renderer output, popover fields, or Design Edit command semantics
- Changing Scene Interaction Frame for canvas runtime tools
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A Planning Canvas Interaction Frame seam exists under app planning code and is specific to planning surfaces.
- [ ] Timeline Action Canvas routes shared interaction lifetime through the frame while preserving panning, zooming, moving, resizing, popover open/save/delete/cancel, keyboard delete, hover, selection, and Target Presentation behavior.
- [ ] Timeline Action geometry, renderer output, Timeline Action Workbench commands, and Design Edit transaction semantics remain owned by their existing seams.
- [ ] Focused tests cover the frame-driven Timeline Action Canvas path, including cleanup and stale selection behavior.
- [ ] Frontend type checks and focused Timeline Action Canvas tests pass.
close_reason: Implemented Planning Canvas Interaction Frame, routed Timeline Action Canvas listener lifetime and target cleanup through it, and verified with focused Vitest coverage plus npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Migrate Location Workbench map to MapLibre Host
description:
## Slice Summary
Route Location Workbench map editing through the MapLibre Host while preserving saved pin projection, search-result preview, drag clearing, and map-center commit behavior.

## User Value
Location editing keeps the same user-facing behavior while sharing MapLibre resource lifetime with other map-backed surfaces.

## Out of Scope
Do not move Location draft state, saved Location authority, geocoding search lifecycle, or Canvas Map Surface behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Migrate Location Workbench map editing to the MapLibre Host.

**Current behavior:**
Location Workbench map editing directly owns MapLibre creation, event binding, resize observation, preserved view state, failure state, and teardown even though MapLibre resource lifetime is becoming a shared host seam.

**Desired behavior:**
Location Workbench map editing uses a MapLibre Host adapter. The adapter keeps saved pin projection, pending search-result preview, drag clearing, resize-triggered pin updates, initialization failure presentation, and map-center commit behavior under Location Workbench authority.

**Key interfaces:**
- MapLibre Host interface for interactive map lifetime
- Location Workbench saved Location presentation and draft/search behavior
- Location map editing host model consumed by Location UI
- Location map editing tests

**Out of scope:**
- Moving saved Location authority out of Location Workbench
- Changing geocoding search lifecycle
- Changing Canvas Map Surface or Design Template map behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Location Workbench map editing no longer directly imports or constructs MapLibre outside the MapLibre Host adapter path.
- [ ] Saved pin projection, pending search-result preview, map drag clearing, resize behavior, initialization failure state, and map-center commit behavior are preserved.
- [ ] Location map editing tests cover the host adapter behavior and stale/preserved view cases that the previous implementation owned locally.
- [ ] Focused source guard coverage prevents direct production MapLibre ownership from returning to Location Workbench map editing.
- [ ] Frontend type checks and focused Location map tests pass.
close_reason: Shipped the Location Workbench map migration to MapLibre Host: Location map editing now requests map lifetime through maplibre/host.ts, with MapLibre construction isolated in maplibre/location-map.ts; preserved saved pin projection, pending search preview, drag clearing, resize-triggered pin updates, initialization failure state, map-center commits, altitude preservation, and preserved view across basemap rebuilds; updated Location source guards and MapLibre/frontend agent docs. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- location-route-boundary.test.ts location-map-editing-host.test.tsx location-tab-map-failure.test.tsx maplibre-host.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Migrate Consortium planning to Planning Canvas Interaction Frame
description:
## Slice Summary
Route Consortium canvas hover and drag interaction through the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame while preserving Consortium Time Model geometry, drag preview/commit behavior, and Target Presentation behavior.

## User Value
Consortium planning keeps the same user-facing interaction behavior while sharing planning canvas interaction lifetime with Timeline Action Canvas.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Consortium Time Model facts, renderer geometry, Succession Phase or Stratum semantics, or Design Edit transaction behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Migrate Consortium planning to the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame.

**Current behavior:**
Consortium planning owns its own document listeners, hover cleanup, drag preview/commit, and Target Presentation writes outside the new planning-specific interaction frame.

**Desired behavior:**
Consortium planning uses a Planning Canvas Interaction Frame adapter while preserving Consortium Time Model geometry, renderer output, drag preview/commit semantics, hover behavior, and Target Presentation behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Planning Canvas Interaction Frame interface
- Consortium Workbench interaction behavior
- Consortium interaction math and Design Edit transaction behavior
- Consortium Time Model and renderer geometry

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Consortium Time Model facts
- Changing Succession Phase or Stratum semantics
- Changing renderer geometry or visual design beyond what the adapter requires
- Changing Timeline Action Canvas behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Consortium planning routes document listener lifetime, hover cleanup, drag preview/commit/abort ordering, and Target Presentation writes through a Planning Canvas Interaction Frame adapter.
- [ ] Consortium Time Model facts, renderer geometry, hover behavior, drag preview/commit behavior, and Design Edit transaction semantics are preserved.
- [ ] Focused tests cover Consortium planning through the frame adapter, including drag cleanup and stale hover/selection cleanup.
- [ ] Consortium-specific logic remains in Consortium adapters rather than moving into the shared frame.
- [ ] Frontend type checks and focused Consortium planning tests pass.
close_reason: Migrated Consortium planning hover, stale cleanup, and document listener lifetime through the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame while preserving Consortium interaction math; verified focused Vitest coverage and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Introduce MapLibre Host through Canvas Map Surface
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the MapLibre Host as the shared MapLibre resource lifetime seam and route the Canvas Map Surface through it as the tracer bullet.

## User Value
The in-canvas map keeps its current behavior while MapLibre creation, resize, rebuild, teardown, preserved view state, and initialization failure handling move behind one reusable interface.

## Out of Scope
Do not migrate the Location Workbench map or Design Template world map in this slice. Do not change Canvas Map Surface camera sync, terrain semantics, Target Presentation overlays, layer authority, or Location authority.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Introduce the MapLibre Host seam and route Canvas Map Surface through it as the tracer bullet.

**Current behavior:**
Canvas Map Surface owns a mature MapLibre lifecycle path, while other map-backed surfaces still own MapLibre resource lifetime directly. The app has no single MapLibre Host seam for lazy loading, creation, resize observation, basemap style rebuilds, preserved view state, initialization failure state, and teardown.

**Desired behavior:**
MapLibre Host exists as a reusable resource-lifetime module that does not import app-owned authorities. Canvas Map Surface uses a host adapter while preserving its current camera sync, terrain, layer visibility, Target Presentation overlays, diagnostics, and readiness behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- MapLibre Host interface for attach, update, destroy, failure state, and preserved view state
- Canvas Map Surface snapshot and reconciliation behavior
- MapLibre loader and basemap style creation
- Canvas Map Surface lifecycle tests and source ownership guards

**Out of scope:**
- Migrating Location Workbench map editing
- Migrating Design Template world map
- Changing map/canvas projection, terrain semantics, Target Presentation overlays, or Location authority
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A MapLibre Host seam exists under the maplibre module and owns MapLibre lazy loading, map creation, resize observation, basemap style rebuild, preserved view state, initialization failure state, and teardown.
- [ ] Canvas Map Surface renders through a MapLibre Host adapter with no user-visible behavior regression in activation, camera sync, terrain, layer presentation, Target Presentation overlays, readiness state, or diagnostics.
- [ ] The MapLibre Host module does not import app-owned modules or own Design, Location, Target Presentation, terrain interpretation, or canvas authority.
- [ ] Focused tests cover host lifecycle behavior and the Canvas Map Surface adapter path.
- [ ] Existing frontend type checks and focused map tests pass.
close_reason: Shipped the MapLibre Host tracer bullet: added maplibre/host.ts for lazy loading, keyed creation/rebuild teardown, resize observation, preserved view state, stale-load cancellation, and removal; routed Canvas Map Surface lifecycle through the host while keeping camera sync, terrain, overlays, readiness, diagnostics, and event handling in the Canvas Map Surface adapter; added source guards and updated docs/agent/maplibre.md. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- maplibre-host.test.ts canvas-map-surface-lifecycle.test.ts maplibre-canvas-surface.test.tsx canvas-map-surface-snapshot.test.ts canvas-map-surface-reconciliation.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Consolidate Species Catalog Read Projection
description: Make services/species_catalog_read the real owner of Species Catalog read projection implementation, not just the caller seam. Move caller-oriented SQL shape, row mapping, localized Common Name hydration, and projection tests behind the Species Catalog Read Projection while keeping db::plant_db focused on storage/schema primitives.
acceptance_criteria: Backend callers continue to cross SpeciesCatalogRead. Projection implementation and tests live under services/species_catalog_read or a clearly named sibling behind that seam. db::plant_db no longer exposes caller-oriented read projection functions that duplicate the service seam. Existing Species Catalog search/detail/filter/media/common-name behavior is preserved. Rust fmt, clippy/check, and focused tests pass.
close_reason: Shipped Species Catalog Read projection consolidation: moved search/list/detail/detail projection row mapping/filter/flower/media/relationship/Common Name implementations and projection tests under services/species_catalog_read, reduced db::plant_db to a storage namespace with no caller projection pub uses, added service boundary guards and detail integration coverage, and updated docs/agent/database.md. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog_read --no-default-features; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Finish Canvas Runtime Surface migration
description: Complete the Canvas Runtime Surface migration by removing the old flat SceneCanvasRuntime compatibility interface from production and runtime-level tests. App code and tests should cross the commands, queries, and documents seams, with low-level SceneStore behavior tested directly in SceneStore modules or through existing interaction harnesses.
acceptance_criteria: SceneCanvasRuntime no longer exposes flat command/query/document compatibility methods or getSceneStore as a public test surface. Runtime and app-facing tests use CanvasRuntimeSurfaces or narrower command/query/document surfaces. Existing canvas runtime behavior remains covered by focused tests. Frontend typecheck and focused Vitest coverage pass.
notes: Implemented Canvas Runtime Surface migration: removed public flat SceneCanvasRuntime compatibility methods/getSceneStore, migrated runtime integration tests to command/query/document surfaces, tightened MapLibre camera to a query-surface slice, and added source guards. Verified with cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.
close_reason: Shipped the completed Canvas Runtime Surface migration: removed public flat SceneCanvasRuntime command/query/document methods and getSceneStore, migrated runtime tests to role surfaces and interaction harnesses, narrowed MapLibre to a CanvasQuerySurface slice, and added source guards. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Deepen Design Edit behind Design Session
description: Refactor non-canvas Design mutation so Location, Budget Item, Timeline Action, and Consortium workbenches call a deep Design Edit module at the Design Session seam instead of sharing dirty-state and preview semantics across callers.
acceptance_criteria: Design Edit owns non-canvas Design mutation no-op detection, dirty marking, and preview/commit/abort semantics; migrated callers no longer import the shallow document controller; focused tests cover the new interface; relevant agent docs are updated or explicitly confirmed current.
close_reason: Shipped the Design Edit seam behind Design Session: added app/design-edit for non-canvas Design edits, moved Location/Budget/Timeline/Consortium writes and preview transactions behind it, deleted shallow pass-through controllers, added boundary tests and updated CONTEXT/agent docs. Tests: npm test -- document-controller document-edit-transaction timeline-action-editing timeline-controller budget-actions consortium-actions frontend-boundaries-sources; npm test -- budget-actions; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Make Scene Interaction Frame the tool seam
description: Refactor canvas interaction so SceneInteractionController shrinks toward a composition root, Scene Interaction Frame owns pointer and keyboard lifetime ordering, and tool modules expose focused event-harness test surfaces instead of routing tool-specific coverage through the broad controller.
acceptance_criteria: Scene Interaction Frame owns shared pointer and keyboard lifecycle ordering; at least Plant Spacing is migrated as a tracer bullet; broad scene-interaction coverage is split toward tool-focused tests; canvas runtime docs and boundary tests are updated where needed.
close_reason: Refactored Scene Interaction Frame to own generic pointer capture, cached pointer bounds, tool pointer-drag state, and shared Space-key lifecycle; split representative Plant Spacing interval dependency coverage into a focused tool adapter test; updated canvas runtime agent docs and boundary guards. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction-frame.test.ts scene-interaction-tool-boundary.test.ts plant-spacing-tool.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: Finish Canvas Runtime Surface
description: Complete the Canvas Runtime Surface refactor so command, query, and Design Session document surfaces are the only external runtime seam. Remove fallback-to-raw-runtime composition and migrate app-facing tests/helpers away from SceneCanvasRuntime public pass-through methods and getSceneStore access where they should behave like callers.
design: Long-term decision: SceneCanvasRuntime may remain an internal implementation constructed by the host, but raw runtime methods and SceneStore access must not remain a parallel app/test interface.
acceptance_criteria: CanvasRuntimeSurfaces composition requires explicit role surfaces with no fallback casts to raw SceneCanvasRuntime; production callers remain on role-specific surfaces; app-facing tests use surface adapters instead of raw runtime pass-through methods; internal runtime tests are narrowed to module-owned seams; frontend boundary tests guard the finished seam; required frontend checks pass.
close_reason: Shipped explicit Canvas Runtime Surface composition: createCanvasRuntimeSurfaces now requires command/query/document role getters with no raw-runtime fallback casts; app-facing session/runtime tests use CanvasRuntimeSurfaces or a test runtime host instead of SceneCanvasRuntime pass-through methods/getSceneStore; frontend boundary tests guard the seam; updated docs/agent/canvas-runtime.md. Tests: npm test -- src/__tests__/canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts src/__tests__/canvas-session.test.ts src/__tests__/canvas-session-selection.test.ts src/__tests__/scene-runtime.test.ts src/__tests__/serializer-plant-species-colors.test.ts src/__tests__/use-canvas-document-session.test.tsx src/__tests__/frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Deepen Species Catalog Read Projection
description: Refactor backend Species Catalog reads so Species Catalog Read Projection is the sole seam for Species search, list hydration, detail, filters, media, Common Name, flower-color, compatibility, and replacement reads. Keep favorites and recently viewed as workflow adapters over UserDb behavior. Preserve existing IPC contracts and frontend Workbench behavior while concentrating SQL/storage details behind the projection.
design: Long-term decision: prefer one deep Species Catalog Read Projection seam over command-facing helper sprawl. UserDb favorites/recently viewed remain adapters outside the projection because they are not Species Catalog storage.
acceptance_criteria: All Species Catalog read paths cross SpeciesCatalogRead or a focused projection module behind it; plant_browser/species_catalog no longer call db::plant_db read helpers directly except through the projection seam; existing IPC contract shapes remain stable; focused Rust tests cover search/list hydration/detail/filter/media/common-name paths through the projection seam; required Rust checks pass.
close_reason: Shipped SpeciesCatalogRead as the backend Species read seam for search, list hydration, detail, filters, media, common names, relationships, flower colors, compatibility, and replacement reads. plant_browser/species_catalog now call the projection seam; favorites/recently viewed remain UserDb workflow adapters. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog_read; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::plant_browser; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::species_catalog; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Refactor Canvas Map Surface reconciliation
description: Deepen the Canvas Map Surface implementation behind the existing attach/update/destroy seam. The lifecycle currently owns map lifetime, basemap readiness, camera sync, terrain generation, Target overlays, diagnostics, resize, and async generation checks directly. Introduce a pure reconciliation step that decides what should happen before the lifecycle touches the MapLibre adapter, preserving existing behaviour while improving locality and testability.
design: Keep the external Canvas Map Surface interface as attach/update/destroy. Add an internal pure reconciliation module that decides inactive/create/reuse/rebuild/sync/destroy from the latest Canvas Map Surface snapshot plus current lifecycle state. Keep one lifecycle owner for MapLibre resources; the reconciler decides and the lifecycle executes cleanup/sync. Keep terrain, overlays, camera, basemap readiness, and diagnostics coordinated behind Canvas Map Surface rather than external seams.
acceptance_criteria: Existing Canvas Map Surface behaviour is preserved. Pure reconciliation table tests cover inactive inputs, visible map creation, reuse, basemap rebuild, and destroy cases. Existing lifecycle tests still cover MapLibre event ordering and cleanup. Frontend typecheck and focused tests pass.
notes: Implemented Canvas Map Surface reconciliation as a pure module with explicit inactive/destroy/create/sync/rebuild outcomes. Lifecycle remains the sole MapLibre resource owner and now executes reconciler decisions. Added focused reconciliation tests and updated docs/agent/maplibre.md for the new ownership boundary. Gates: npm test -- canvas-map-surface-reconciliation.test.ts canvas-map-surface-lifecycle.test.ts canvas-map-surface-snapshot.test.ts maplibre-canvas-surface.test.ts canvas-map-surface-overlays.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.
close_reason: Shipped pure Canvas Map Surface reconciliation behind the existing lifecycle seam, preserved MapLibre resource ownership in lifecycle, added focused reconciliation coverage, updated MapLibre agent docs, and passed focused Vitest, npx tsc --noEmit, and full npm test.

title: Restore Plant DB shortcut from welcome state
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the App Command Graph navigation regression where the Plant Database shortcut no longer opens the side panel when no Design is open.

## User Value
Users can press Ctrl+2 from the welcome/no-Design state and reach the Plant Database as before.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the visual panel layout, shortcut definitions, or Design-dependent availability for unrelated panels.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Restore the Plant Database panel shortcut behavior from the welcome/no-Design state.

**Current behavior:**
The App Command Graph disables the Plant Database navigation command when no Design is open. As a result, pressing the Plant Database shortcut from the welcome state leaves the side panel closed, breaking the existing shortcut contract.

**Desired behavior:**
The Plant Database navigation command remains available from the no-Design state through keyboard shortcut dispatch and command execution. The command should still route through the App Command Graph, and any Design-dependent panel availability should remain deliberate and covered by tests.

**Key interfaces:**
- App Command Graph navigation commands
- Keyboard shortcut dispatch for panel navigation
- Shell panel and side-panel navigation state
- PanelBar disabled-state projection

**Out of scope:**
- Changing shortcut key assignments
- Changing the set of panels
- Reworking welcome-screen or shell layout
- Broadly changing Design-dependent availability beyond this regression
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pressing the Plant Database shortcut from the no-Design state opens the Plant Database side panel.
- [ ] `runAppCommand()` preserves the intended Plant Database navigation behavior from the no-Design state.
- [ ] Existing shortcut contract coverage passes, including the panel shortcut test that exercises Ctrl+2.
- [ ] Focused frontend regression tests cover the no-Design Plant Database shortcut/command behavior.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Restored no-design Plant Database command and shortcut behavior while preserving PanelBar disabled projection. Focused frontend tests and npx tsc --noEmit passed.

title: Prepare v0.6.0 release
description: Bump Canopi release metadata to 0.6.0, add release notes, run the release-candidate workflow, and promote v0.6.0 after CI artifacts are available.
acceptance_criteria: Version metadata is 0.6.0; release notes are committed; release candidate workflow is run from main; v0.6.0 GitHub release is promoted with notes.
notes: Release prep validation passed: cargo metadata --locked --format-version=1 --no-deps; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; git diff --check.
close_reason: Shipped Canopi 0.6.0: bumped workspace, Tauri, and web package metadata to 0.6.0; added docs/release-notes/v0.6.0.md; pushed release-prep commit 82d84bc; release candidate run 26912527397 passed all platform package jobs and manifest; promoted and published GitHub release v0.6.0 with notes and provenance. Local validation: cargo metadata --locked --format-version=1 --no-deps; cargo fmt --all -- --check; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; git diff --check.

title: Cap Plant Spacing preview materialization
description:
## Slice Summary
Bound Plant Spacing preview work so very small valid intervals or long spacing guides cannot eagerly allocate and render an unbounded number of preview placed plants during pointer movement.

## User Value
A designer can enter physically valid dense intervals without freezing the interface while previewing a long spacing guide.

## Out of Scope
Do not block physically valid Plant Spacing Intervals outright, change the dense-confirmation threshold, or change final commit behavior beyond what is needed to keep preview work bounded.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Bound Plant Spacing preview generation before preview placed plants are materialized.

**Current behavior:**
Plant Spacing preview generation can eagerly compute every generated position on pointer movement before dense confirmation can protect the interface. A very small physically valid Plant Spacing Interval over a long spacing guide can allocate many positions and render one preview marker per generated placed plant.

**Desired behavior:**
Preview computation must derive the generated count without unbounded allocation and must cap or summarize preview markers before materializing them. Dense valid inputs remain allowed, but the live preview must stay responsive and communicate the dense count/warning rather than rendering unbounded ghost markers.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Spacing sequence math
- Plant Spacing preview overlay
- Plant Spacing dense warning and dense confirmation
- Scene interaction pointer-move preview path

**Out of scope:**
- Blocking physically valid Plant Spacing Intervals solely because they are dense
- Changing the 100 generated-plant dense confirmation threshold
- Changing final dense commit behavior beyond any bounded-count helper needed by preview
- Adding new UX copy unless existing dense-warning copy is insufficient
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Live Plant Spacing preview does not allocate or render unbounded generated positions for very small valid intervals over long spacing guides.
- [ ] The preview still reports the generated count and dense warning for dense valid intervals.
- [ ] Normal previews at or below the safe preview cap still render generated ghost markers as before.
- [ ] Dense commits still require confirmation when generated count is greater than 100.
- [ ] Focused tests cover a tiny valid interval over a long spacing guide without materializing all generated positions.
close_reason: Bounded Plant Spacing live preview materialization with constant-time generated counts and capped ghost positions, deferred full dense position materialization until explicit confirmation, added focused preview-cap coverage, and verified with Plant Spacing sequence tests, SceneInteraction tests, and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Block Plant Spacing HUD clicks from canvas commits
description:
## Slice Summary
Prevent Plant Spacing HUD pointer interactions from being interpreted as spacing guide endpoint clicks by the canvas.

## User Value
A designer can edit the Plant Spacing Interval or press Cancel/Confirm without accidentally creating placed plants or changing the current spacing guide.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Plant Spacing generation math, dense-count thresholds, keyboard shortcuts, or general canvas pointer handling outside Plant Spacing HUD interactions.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Prevent Plant Spacing HUD pointer interactions from becoming canvas spacing guide endpoint clicks.

**Current behavior:**
When Plant Spacing has a sampled placed plant, pointer interactions from the HUD can bubble into the canvas pointerdown handler. Editing the Plant Spacing Interval or pressing HUD actions can be treated as a spacing guide endpoint click before the control action runs.

**Desired behavior:**
Pointerdown interactions that originate inside the Plant Spacing HUD or its editable controls are consumed as HUD interactions only. They must not commit, update, or replace a Plant Spacing spacing guide endpoint. Canvas pointerdown interactions outside the HUD continue to drive Plant Spacing normally.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Spacing Tool HUD
- Canvas pointer interaction handling
- Plant Spacing Interval input
- Plant Spacing cancel and dense-confirmation controls

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Plant Spacing sequence math
- Changing dense-count confirmation behavior
- Changing non-Plant Spacing canvas tools
- Reworking general overlay architecture
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Clicking or pointerdowning the Plant Spacing Interval input does not create placed plants or commit a spacing guide endpoint.
- [ ] Clicking Plant Spacing HUD Cancel does not first commit generated placed plants.
- [ ] Clicking dense Confirm or dense Cancel is handled once by the HUD and is not also interpreted as a canvas endpoint click.
- [ ] Canvas clicks outside the Plant Spacing HUD still sample a source placed plant or commit a spacing guide endpoint as before.
- [ ] Focused SceneInteraction or overlay tests cover HUD-origin pointer events.
close_reason: Stopped Plant Spacing HUD pointerdowns from bubbling into canvas endpoint commits, added focused SceneInteraction regression coverage for interval-input pointerdowns, and verified with the focused SceneInteraction test file plus npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Handle Location map initialization failures
description: LocationPanel currently constructs a MapLibre map without a component-level failure path. A macOS user hit a MapLibre stack when opening the location feature, likely from WebGL or style initialization failure. Add a regression test and keep the location panel usable when map creation fails.
acceptance_criteria: Opening the location panel does not trip the global ErrorBoundary when MapLibre map initialization throws. The panel shows a localized degraded-map message while search and navigation controls remain mounted. Focused frontend regression tests cover the failure path.
close_reason: Fixed the macOS location-panel crash path by allowing MapLibre blob workers through Tauri WebKit CSP fallback, adding LocationTab degraded-map handling for MapLibre initialization failures, and covering both paths with frontend regression tests. Verified with npx tsc --noEmit and npm test.

title: Deepen canvas query and design file composition seams
description: Improve long-term architecture health by moving canvas scene freshness behind the Canvas Query Surface and driving Design file save composition from generated field ownership instead of handwritten duplicate field knowledge.
design: Keep runtime interfaces as small as practical; expose framework-specific reactivity through a thin app/runtime adapter where needed. Use generated Design field ownership as the source for composition, with explicit shared-extra handling for guides.
acceptance_criteria: Production app code no longer imports runtime mirror revision signals directly; Canvas Query Surface exposes scene read freshness through its seam; Design file composition uses generated ownership metadata to merge document-owned, scene-owned, and shared fields; source-boundary and focused regression tests cover the new invariants.
close_reason: Shipped Canvas Query Surface revision signals for scene, plant-name, and viewport freshness; moved app callers off runtime mirror revisions; changed Design file save composition to use generated field ownership with explicit shared extra ownership; added source-boundary and regression coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test -- document-format-contract frontend-boundaries-sources planning-projection canvas-runtime-surfaces maplibre-canvas-surface canvas-map-surface-lifecycle; cd desktop/web && npm test -- src/canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts src/canvas/runtime/scene-runtime/transactions.test.ts consortium-sync-workflow budget-workbench budget-tab-hover budget-tab-export consortium-chart-hover; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Deepen Scene Edit canvas mutation seam
description: Move canvas-owned persisted state changes behind a runtime-owned Scene Edit module so plants, zones, annotations, object groups, Layers, plant species colors, and guides share one mutation path for undo/redo, dirty state, mirror projections, revisions, and render invalidation.
design: Long-term health preference: make Scene Edit the only canvas mutation module even if this requires moving more code and tests.
acceptance_criteria: Scene-owned Layer and guide writes enter through the canvas runtime command/edit seam; signal-backed Layer and guide state is projection or transient UI state; focused tests cover undo/redo, dirty state, save composition, and render invalidation; agent docs reflect the new seam.
close_reason: Scene-owned layer and guide mutations now flow through the runtime Scene Edit seam; projections no longer author document state. Verified with npx tsc --noEmit and full desktop/web npm test.

title: Deepen app command, template import, and species read modules
description: Refactor three selected architecture review candidates: deepen the app command graph shared by menu/palette/shortcuts, move Design Template import coordination into a workflow module, and deepen Species Catalog read behavior used by Site Adaptation.
design: Favor long-term locality and leverage over minimum diff. Preserve existing behavior while reducing leakage through small adapter seams.
acceptance_criteria: App command metadata and dispatch are centralized behind one module; Design Template import is coordinated outside Community catalog state and core Design Session actions; Site Adaptation no longer owns raw Species Catalog SQL/storage facts for compatibility and replacement reads; focused tests cover each seam; relevant agent docs are updated or explicitly confirmed unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented App Command Graph, Design Template import workflow, and Species Catalog Read refactors; ran frontend typecheck, full frontend tests, rustfmt check, clippy, cargo check, and cargo test.

title: Deepen Species Catalog Workbench seam
description: Long-term architecture refactor from the architecture review: make the Species Catalog Workbench the public read/write module for Species Catalog UI behavior. The current Workbench protects components from some state imports, but its interface still mirrors raw search, favorites, dynamic option, detail selection, and view-mode implementation state.
design: Move Species Catalog UI state ownership into the Workbench implementation. Keep Preact ergonomics by exposing read-only reactive projections and commands from the Workbench. Stop treating app/plant-browser/state.ts as public surface; migrate components and tests away from raw writable signals except focused Workbench implementation tests.
acceptance_criteria: Species Catalog UI modules and broad tests use the Workbench interface rather than raw state exports. Workbench owns search intent/results, filter state, dynamic filter options, favorites, detail selection, view mode, and lifecycle. Source-boundary tests prevent plant-browser raw state from leaking into UI modules. Existing search, filtering, pagination, favorites, and detail-selection behavior is preserved. Required frontend typecheck and focused Vitest coverage pass.
close_reason: Implemented the Species Catalog Workbench as the public read/write seam, folded raw UI state and controller behavior into the Workbench factory/singleton, removed the old plant-browser state/controller public modules, migrated UI tests to Workbench interfaces, added boundary coverage, updated frontend agent docs, and verified with npx tsc --noEmit plus full frontend Vitest.

title: Deepen Design Session store seam
description: Long-term architecture refactor from the architecture review: make the Design Session module the read/write seam for active Design identity, dirty baselines, pending loads, saved markers, and attached/detached lifecycle state. Current Design Session behavior is deep, but tests and implementation still know too much about global signals in state/design.ts.
design: Introduce a Design Session store interface with a signal-backed adapter for production and a memory adapter for tests. Move writes for currentDesign/designPath/designName, dirty baselines, pending loads, detached canvas dirty, autosave failure, and saved markers behind the Design Session module. Preserve reactive read ergonomics through read-only projections instead of public writable globals.
acceptance_criteria: Design Session state-machine and persistence tests use a memory adapter rather than mutating global design signals directly. Production callers still get read-only reactive Design projections where needed. Existing document lifecycle behavior is preserved for attached and detached transitions, save/save-as, autosave, queued loads, and teardown snapshots. Boundary tests prevent new direct write access to Design Session internals. Required frontend typecheck and focused Vitest coverage pass.
close_reason: Implemented the Design Session store seam with signal-backed and memory adapters, migrated production callers off raw state/design imports, added a production boundary guard, updated document lifecycle agent docs, and verified with npx tsc --noEmit plus focused Vitest suites for Design Session, persistence, dirty state, document actions, and canvas document session integration.

title: Deepen Species Catalog SQL Planning
description: Architecture review found shallow Species Catalog SQL planning modules. The query builder splits useful concepts into modules, but the interface still leaks mutable parameter order, placeholder numbering, join alias ordering, count/list predicate convention, and string assembly details. Deepen Species Catalog SQL planning around a typed internal builder that owns placeholders, joins, predicate fragments, sort fragments, pagination, and shared count/list behavior.
design: Use a typed internal builder inside desktop/src/db/query_builder rather than ad hoc SQL strings passed through every module. Preserve the public SpeciesSearchRequest and SpeciesSearchPlan interface unless implementation proves a small compatibility facade is needed.
acceptance_criteria: SpeciesSearchPlan exposes the same search behavior while internal SQL assembly uses a typed plan/builder seam for placeholders, joins, predicates, order, limit, and count/list reuse. Existing backend search/filter/relevance tests pass or are strengthened. No runtime dependency is added. Rust formatting, clippy/check scope relevant to changed backend code is run or explicitly documented if blocked.
close_reason: Shipped typed query_builder SqlBuilder seam for Species Catalog SQL placeholder allocation, routed predicates/relevance/pagination/fixed filters through it, added dense-placeholder regression coverage, updated database agent guide, and passed cargo fmt --all -- --check, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::query_builder, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Deepen Planning Canvas Workbenches
description: Architecture review found shallow planning canvas modules. Timeline Action and Consortium canvas workbenches currently require callers to understand renderer geometry, pointer ordering, local view state, Target presentation, popover lifecycle, and document edit transactions. Deepen the workbench modules so canvas hosts forward events and snapshots through a smaller seam while interaction implementation owns hover, selection presentation, drag preview and commit, popup orchestration, cleanup, and local view-state ordering.
design: Use separate deep modules first. Do not create a generic planning canvas abstraction until Timeline and Consortium prove the same interface. The deep modules own local view state involved in interaction correctness, including scroll, hover, pending click, popup state, drag lifecycle, auto-scroll, and cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: InteractiveTimeline delegates full Timeline Action interaction/view-state ordering to a deep app timeline workbench module. ConsortiumChart delegates full Consortium drag/hover ordering to a deep app consortium workbench module. Existing behavior is preserved with focused tests for hover, selection, drag commit/cleanup, popover open/save/delete, and no-op cleanup. Frontend typecheck and focused Vitest coverage pass.
close_reason: Deepened Planning Canvas Workbenches by moving Timeline and Consortium interaction ordering into app-layer workbench seams, updated boundary tests and agent docs, and verified with npx tsc --noEmit plus focused and full frontend Vitest suites.

title: Deepen Species Catalog search
description: Architecture review selected this as the second long-term-health refactor. Species Catalog search policy is currently spread across frontend search-session intent, IPC argument shapes, Tauri command arguments, plant_browser orchestration, user favorite hydration, plant_db search execution, and query_builder planning. Deepen this into a structured search module with flat transports as adapters.
acceptance_criteria: A structured Species Catalog search request/result contract replaces the internal flat argument chain; frontend search-session and IPC use adapter seams; Rust command/service/search modules consume the structured contract; favorite hydration and count policy are tested through the deeper interface; generated bindings are refreshed if shared contracts change; agent docs are updated only if commands or architecture guidance change.
close_reason: Deepened Species Catalog search: common-types now owns SpeciesSearchRequest, frontend search-session and IPC use a structured request with flat Tauri adapter, Rust command/service/plant DB/query planner consume the request object, favorite hydration and count-policy tests pass, generated bindings and agent docs were updated, and CI-parity gates passed.

title: Deepen Design file contract
description: Architecture review selected this as the top long-term-health refactor. The Design file contract is currently encoded across Rust shared types, generated TypeScript known keys, frontend field ownership metadata, scene serialization placeholders, and backend migration/default behavior. Deepen this into one authoritative contract so adding a Design field is no longer a cross-module memory test.
acceptance_criteria: common-types owns budget_currency and Design file defaults; generated frontend metadata includes known keys and field ownership; save/load composition tests cover ownership, defaults, and unknown fields; generated bindings are refreshed; agent docs are updated only if workflow commands or architecture guidance change.
close_reason: Deepened the Design file contract: common-types now owns budget_currency/defaults and field ownership metadata, generated frontend contract metadata is consumed by save composition, generated bindings were refreshed, tests and CI-parity gates passed.

title: Prepare 0.5.0 release
description: Bump app/package versions to 0.5.0, trigger the release candidate workflow, and leave release notes for after the GitHub release is created.
close_reason: Released Canopi 0.5.0 from RC run 26666890609, published release notes, and verified public GitHub release assets.

title: Fix macOS canvas object creation without randomUUID
description: Users on macOS 0.4.0 report they cannot add any canvas object. Older WKWebView builds may lack crypto.randomUUID, which is used by shared canvas object-creation paths. Add a deterministic repro and replace direct randomUUID calls with a compatible frontend ID helper.
close_reason: Fixed macOS canvas object creation by replacing direct frontend crypto.randomUUID calls with a createUuid helper that falls back to getRandomValues; added a SceneInteractionController regression for missing native randomUUID; verified npx tsc --noEmit and full npm test.

title: Collapse Timeline Action workbench
description:
Refactor the frontend Timeline Action workbench so InteractiveTimeline delegates add/edit/delete popover orchestration, selection cleanup, and document mutations through an app/timeline seam instead of owning those workflows inline. Preserve existing hover, drag, and document authority behavior.

## Required Skills
craft,improve-codebase-architecture
design: Create a deep app/timeline workbench module that coordinates Timeline Action form data, controller calls, popover mode, selection targets, and action mutations behind a small interface. Keep InteractiveTimeline responsible for canvas rendering, hit testing, pointer geometry, and refs.
acceptance_criteria: InteractiveTimeline no longer imports timeline controller mutations or form mapping helpers directly; Timeline Action add/edit/delete flows preserve behavior; boundary tests document the workbench seam; focused Timeline tests plus frontend typecheck pass.
close_reason: Collapsed Timeline Action workbench orchestration into app/timeline/workbench.ts; InteractiveTimeline now delegates popover action mutations and target cleanup through that seam; frontend typecheck and full Vitest suite passed.

title: Refactor Design Session into explicit state machine
description: Refactor the frontend Design Session lifecycle so guarded document transitions, attached/detached apply paths, queued loads, save composition, dirty baseline resets, autosave, teardown snapshots, and workflow installation are coordinated through an explicit state-machine module. Preserve existing document authority behavior and regression coverage.
design: Create a deep app/document-session state-machine seam. Keep DOM runtime ownership in lifecycle.ts and user action loading in actions.ts, but move transition ordering and session state into a single module with explicit state/status values and injected adapters for persistence/workflows/dialog/IPC.
acceptance_criteria: Design Session transitions preserve existing open/new/template/queued/mount behavior; attached and detached save paths preserve canvas/non-canvas authority; autosave and teardown use the state-machine surface; focused Vitest coverage documents state transitions and cancellation/error paths; docs/agent guidance is updated if the seam changes.
close_reason: Implemented explicit Design Session state machine; lifecycle now delegates start, autosave, and teardown through it; frontend typecheck and full Vitest suite passed.

title: Generate Species Catalog Filter adapter
description: Deepen the Species Catalog Filter module by moving repeated frontend/backend fixed-filter adapter behavior into generated metadata or generated adapter code, reducing parallel manual behavior across plant-filter-fields.json, TypeScript filter model code, Rust query_builder fixed-filter code, and shared SpeciesFilter request values.
acceptance_criteria: Fixed Species Catalog Filter behavior is generated or schema-driven on both TypeScript and Rust sides; request-value and SQL predicate behavior remain covered; generated bindings are current; frontend and Rust filter tests pass.
close_reason: Generated more Species Catalog Filter adapter behavior from common-types/plant-filter-fields.json: categorical strip fields now carry strip/chip UI metadata, frontend strip/chip catalogs consume generated behavior, and the Rust fixed SpeciesFilter request-value adapter is generated beside fixed-filter predicates. Verified generated bindings, focused filter tests, full frontend Vitest, TypeScript check, Rust format/check/clippy, and Rust workspace tests.

title: Deepen Design Session lifecycle
description: Refactor the Design Session lifecycle so attach, transition, save, autosave, teardown snapshot, dirty baselines, queued loads, and workflow installation are owned by a deeper app/document-session module instead of leaking ordering across the CanvasPanel hook, transition, persistence, and state modules.
acceptance_criteria: Design Session lifecycle has a single app-facing seam for canvas mount and autosave; existing document transition, persistence, dirty-state, queued-load, and canvas-session tests pass or are strengthened; no document authority rules are weakened.
close_reason: Deepened Design Session lifecycle by moving canvas runtime attachment, mount transition, autosave scheduling, queued-load installation, settings flush, teardown snapshot, persistence disposal, and canvas-session publication behind app/document-session/lifecycle.ts. Verified with focused lifecycle/boundary Vitest, full frontend Vitest, TypeScript check, Rust format/check/clippy, and Rust workspace tests.

title: Deepen Species Catalog Filter catalog
description: Why: Species Catalog Filter behavior is partly data-driven, but fixed-filter defaults, activity rules, presentation metadata, and SQL dispatch still require duplicated caller knowledge across JSON, generated TypeScript/Rust metadata, frontend model code, and backend query builder code. What: derive fixed-filter defaults, activity, UI behavior, and predicate dispatch from a single catalog seam while preserving the flat SpeciesFilter request shape unless a later bead deliberately changes IPC contracts.
design: Keep SpeciesFilter flat for compatibility. Deepen the catalog around it first, then decide separately whether the request contract should change.
acceptance_criteria: Fixed Species Catalog Filter behavior has one catalog interface for frontend defaults/activity/presentation and backend predicate dispatch; generated bindings remain source-controlled; focused frontend and backend tests cover catalog-derived behavior.
notes: Follow-up to closed canopi-9go. That bead moved fixed Species Catalog Filter presentation and Rust predicate behavior into generated metadata; this bead should focus only on remaining catalog shallowness such as frontend defaults/activity and any backend dispatch knowledge still duplicated after that work.
close_reason: Deepened the Species Catalog Filter catalog so frontend fixed-filter defaults and backend SQL dispatch derive from generated fixed-filter behavior, with focused frontend tests, query-builder coverage, full Rust workspace tests, typecheck, format, check, and clippy passing.

title: Deepen Design Session persistence module
description: Why: Design Session save, autosave, attached/detached persistence, teardown snapshotting, and field ownership composition are spread across several modules, so callers must preserve ordering knowledge. What: introduce a deeper Design Session persistence seam that centralizes persisted Design content production and snapshot behavior while preserving existing transition and dirty-state semantics.
design: Keep transition flow intact for this slice. Deepen the seam around producing persisted Design content and snapshotting canvas state, then update callers to consume that module instead of scattered runtime helpers.
acceptance_criteria: Persisted Design content for save/autosave/teardown goes through one Design Session persistence module; existing document transition, autosave, attached/detached snapshot, and field ownership behavior remains covered by focused tests; no unrelated architecture changes are included.
close_reason: Introduced app/document-session/persistence as the Design Session persistence seam, moved save/autosave/teardown callers to it, removed the old runtime helper, updated agent docs, and verified with focused Vitest, full frontend Vitest, and TypeScript checks.

title: Fix review-found correctness bugs
description: Fix the bugs found during static review: settings persistence must not mark failed saves as clean and close must flush settings; unknown .canopi top-level fields must round-trip through frontend saves; plant search cursor pagination must handle nullable sort values; document file actions must work when the canvas panel is unmounted; redo shortcut handling must accept shifted key events.
acceptance_criteria: Focused regression tests cover each fixed bug. Frontend typecheck passes. Rust workspace check passes for backend query changes.
notes: Implemented fixes for review-found correctness bugs: durable settings persistence/close flush, top-level .canopi unknown field preservation, nullable sort cursor pagination, detached document actions, and shifted redo shortcut. Verification passed: focused Vitest suite, full npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, cargo test query_builder, cargo test height_pagination_continues_after_null_sort_value, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace, cargo test --workspace.
close_reason: Fixed all review-found correctness bugs and verified with focused/full frontend tests, TypeScript check, Rust workspace check, and Rust workspace tests.

title: Integrate DuckDB-WASM Species Catalog Workbench
description:
## Slice Summary
Implement the browser Species Catalog read adapter and connect it to the shared Species Catalog Workbench for v1 search, filters, favorites, and recently viewed behavior.

## User Value
A designer can search and filter Species in the Web Edition with Canonical Name/Common Name text, climate zone, habit or growth form, life cycle, and localized Common Name display/search.

## Out of Scope
Do not add desktop-only detail fields, edibility, hardiness, height, stratum, Site Adaptation, or desktop DuckDB migration.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Integrate the DuckDB-WASM Species Catalog adapter with the shared Species Catalog Workbench.

**Current behavior:**
Species Catalog Workbench behavior currently reads through desktop storage and IPC-shaped adapters. Web Edition needs a browser-native read adapter over reduced DuckDB-WASM assets.

**Desired behavior:**
The Web Edition Species Catalog Workbench can browse, search, filter, paginate, favorite, and show recently viewed Species using the browser DuckDB-WASM read adapter. Search supports Canonical Name/Common Name text and selected-language Common Name display/search for all 11 UI languages. Filters are limited to climate zone, habit or growth form, and life cycle.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench
- Species Catalog Read Projection
- DuckDB-WASM browser adapter
- Browser-local favorites and recently viewed app data
- Species search/filter request behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Edibility, hardiness, height, stratum, Site Adaptation, or replacement suggestions
- Full desktop Species detail payload
- Desktop migration away from SQLite
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Web Edition can browse Species through the reduced DuckDB-WASM catalog.
- [ ] Text search works for Canonical Name and selected-language Common Names.
- [ ] Climate zone, habit or growth form, and life cycle filters work in the web catalog.
- [ ] Pagination or cursor behavior works without relying on a desktop IPC path.
- [ ] Favorites and recently viewed Species use browser-local app data.
- [ ] The adapter does not expose excluded fields or Site Adaptation behavior.
- [ ] Focused tests cover search, filters, locale behavior, pagination, and app-data integration.

title: Add Browser Drafts and browser-local app data
description:
## Slice Summary
Persist Browser Drafts, autosave state, settings, Species favorites, recently viewed Species, and Saved Object Stamps in browser-local app data behind caller-shaped seams.

## User Value
A designer can resume browser work and personal app data on the same browser profile without accounts, sync, or a backend.

## Out of Scope
Do not add Design Notebook behavior, Notebook Sections, saved filesystem paths, stamp import/export, cloud sync, or durable storage promises beyond explicit `.canopi` download.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add Browser Drafts and browser-local personal app data behind app-data seams.

**Current behavior:**
Desktop settings, Recent Designs, Design Notebook, favorites, recently viewed Species, and Saved Object Stamps are native/user-database shaped. Web Edition v1 needs browser-local convenience state without accounts, sync, paths, or notebook organization.

**Desired behavior:**
The Web Edition persists Browser Drafts, autosave state, a simple Drafts list, settings, Species favorites, recently viewed Species, and Saved Object Stamps in browser storage behind caller-shaped app-data seams. Browser Drafts are clearly convenience state and remain distinct from downloaded `.canopi` files and from the desktop Design Notebook.

**Key interfaces:**
- Browser storage adapter
- Design Session autosave/persistence seam
- Settings projection
- Species favorites and recently viewed app data
- Saved Object Stamp workbench

**Out of scope:**
- Accounts, sync, or backend persistence
- Design Notebook, Notebook Sections, saved filesystem paths, file reveal actions, or Recent Design path semantics
- Saved Object Stamp import/export
- Durable-storage promises beyond explicit `.canopi` download
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Browser Drafts autosave and reopen/resume work in the same browser profile.
- [ ] The Drafts list has no Notebook Sections, saved paths, file reveal actions, or notebook-style organization.
- [ ] UI copy or shell state clearly distinguishes Browser Drafts from downloaded `.canopi` files.
- [ ] Settings persist through browser-local app data.
- [ ] Species favorites and recently viewed Species persist through browser-local app data.
- [ ] Saved Object Stamps persist browser-locally and do not expose import/export in web.
- [ ] Storage failures or unavailable storage are handled without corrupting the active Design Session.

title: Package Web Edition static artifact for website handoff
description:
## Slice Summary
Produce a versioned static Web Edition artifact with the correct `/app/` base path, SPA fallback expectations, and Cloudflare Pages asset-limit checks.

## User Value
The website can publish Web Edition as static assets without importing app source or committing generated `/app` output long term.

## Out of Scope
Do not build feature-complete browser app behavior beyond what is needed to prove artifact handoff and guardrails.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Package the Web Edition as a versioned static artifact for website handoff.

**Current behavior:**
The accepted architecture says the Canopi app repository owns Web Edition source and the website publishes a built artifact under `/app/`, but the artifact packaging and handoff checks are not implemented.

**Desired behavior:**
The Web Edition build produces a versioned static artifact with a `/app/`-compatible base path, documented SPA fallback expectations, and automated Cloudflare Pages per-asset limit checks. The website handoff follows the selected artifact source decision and does not require importing Canopi app source or committing generated `/app` assets long term.

**Key interfaces:**
- Web Edition build artifact
- Website `/app/` route contract
- Cloudflare Pages asset-limit check
- Release artifact metadata

**Out of scope:**
- Feature-complete Web Edition behavior
- Changing the website into an app source consumer
- Adding service workers, PWA install, or offline-first cache behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A command or workflow produces a versioned static Web Edition artifact.
- [ ] The artifact is built with a `/app/`-compatible base path.
- [ ] The expected website SPA fallback for `/app/*` is documented or implemented in the relevant handoff.
- [ ] Asset-size checks cover app chunks, workers, WASM, catalog shards, templates, and image-metadata assets.
- [ ] The handoff does not require committed generated `/app` assets in the website repository.
- [ ] No service worker, PWA install flow, or app-managed precache is introduced.
close_reason: Added npm run package:web to build the Web Edition at /app/, scan browser chunks, package a versioned artifact directory plus tar.gz, write manifest/checksums and SPA fallback metadata, enforce Cloudflare Pages asset/file limits, ignore generated outputs, and document the handoff. Verified with npm test -- web-edition-packaging.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts, npm run package:web, npm run build, and git diff --check.

title: Ship reduced Web Edition Plant Detail
description:
## Slice Summary
Render the reduced Web Edition Species detail view with lazy remote hero image metadata, attribution/source handling, and only v1-approved fields.

## User Value
A designer gets essential Species detail in the browser without loading the full desktop detail payload or image binaries.

## Out of Scope
Do not add galleries, bundled image binaries, image proxying, desktop image cache, dimensions, hardiness, uses, soil, ecology, propagation, risk, related Species, or extended taxonomy.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Ship the reduced Web Edition Species detail view with lazy hero image metadata.

**Current behavior:**
Desktop Species detail includes a large detail payload and native image cache assumptions. Web Edition v1 needs a reduced detail view backed by the lightweight catalog.

**Desired behavior:**
Web Edition Species detail renders only hero image, Canonical Name, Common Names, climate zone, habit or growth form, and life cycle. Hero images are loaded lazily from remote metadata when available, include source/credit/license access, and fail gracefully when missing or unavailable.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species detail controller/read projection
- Plant Detail UI
- Hero image metadata
- Species Catalog Workbench selection

**Out of scope:**
- Image galleries
- Bundled image binaries
- Backend image proxy
- Desktop native image cache
- Dimensions, hardiness, uses, soil, ecology, propagation, risk, related Species, or extended taxonomy
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Web Plant Detail renders only v1-approved fields.
- [ ] Hero image metadata loads lazily when available.
- [ ] Missing or failed remote images render a clean fallback.
- [ ] Source, credit, or license information is reachable where metadata provides it.
- [ ] Desktop-only detail sections are hidden rather than shown as empty shells.
- [ ] Tests cover successful image metadata, missing image metadata, failed image load, attribution/source display, and omitted desktop sections.

title: Implement Web Edition Location and street map scope
description:
## Slice Summary
Support Web Edition Location editing through manual coordinates and map click or pin placement with street-basemap-only MapLibre behavior and graceful network failure handling.

## User Value
A designer can set a site Location in the browser and see street map context when network resources are available.

## Out of Scope
Do not add geocoding, satellite basemaps, terrain contours, hillshade, offline tile download, service-worker tile precache, or offline map promises.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Implement Web Edition Location editing and street-basemap-only map behavior.

**Current behavior:**
Desktop Location and map flows include native geocoding and broader map/terrain assumptions. Web Edition v1 needs manual Location editing and normal interactive street basemap behavior only.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can set or edit Location through manual latitude/longitude and map click or pin placement. MapLibre street basemap behavior works when network resources are available, includes required attribution behavior, and handles map load failures gracefully. Web Edition does not expose geocoding, satellite basemaps, terrain contours, hillshade, offline tile download, tile precache, or offline map promises.

**Key interfaces:**
- Location Workbench
- Location map editing host
- MapLibre Host
- MapLibre Surface Adapter
- Canvas Map Surface and Canvas Layer Presentation where relevant

**Out of scope:**
- Address search/geocoding
- Satellite or terrain layers
- Offline maps or tile prefetch
- Backend map proxy
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Manual latitude/longitude Location editing works in Web Edition.
- [ ] Map click or pin placement can set the saved Location.
- [ ] Street basemap behavior works for normal interactive viewing when network resources load.
- [ ] Map failures are presented gracefully without corrupting Location state.
- [ ] Geocoding, satellite, terrain, offline, and tile-precache controls are absent from Web Edition.
- [ ] Tests cover manual edit, map click/pin commit, geocoding absence, street-basemap-only scope, and failure handling.

title: Support static Web Edition Design Template imports
description:
## Slice Summary
Allow Web Edition to import known static `.canopi` Design Templates through the normal Design Session template-open flow, and hide the template map when no static template set is configured.

## User Value
A designer can start from curated static examples in the browser without arbitrary remote downloads or backend template storage.

## Out of Scope
Do not add arbitrary remote template URLs, native temp-file download assumptions, backend template storage, Site Adaptation review, compatibility checks, or replacement suggestions.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Support static Web Edition Design Template imports.

**Current behavior:**
Template import behavior has native/download assumptions and Site Adaptation-adjacent behavior that do not fit Web Edition v1 static hosting.

**Desired behavior:**
Web Edition can present static Design Templates only when known `.canopi` template assets are configured. Selecting a template fetches the static asset and routes it through the normal Design Session template-open flow. If no static template set is configured, the template map is hidden. Web templates import as-is with no Site Adaptation review, compatibility checks, or replacement suggestions.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Template catalog/config
- Design Template import workflow
- Design Session template-open flow
- World map surface when templates are configured

**Out of scope:**
- Arbitrary remote template URLs
- Native temp-file download behavior
- Backend template storage
- Site Adaptation or replacement suggestions
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Known static `.canopi` template assets can be configured for Web Edition.
- [ ] Selecting a static template opens it through the normal Design Session template-open flow.
- [ ] The template map or entry point is hidden when no static template set is configured.
- [ ] Static web templates import as-is without Site Adaptation review.
- [ ] Native temp-file download and arbitrary remote URL assumptions are absent.
- [ ] Tests cover configured template import, no-template hiding behavior, and absence of Site Adaptation.

title: Verify Web Edition v1 end-to-end release readiness
description:
## Slice Summary
Run the final Web Edition v1 acceptance pass across the static bundle, website handoff, browser workflows, catalog behavior, omitted surfaces, and Cloudflare Pages constraints.

## User Value
Maintainers have one release-readiness gate proving the Web Edition works as a static browser app and honors the documented v1 scope.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new features during this verification slice; create follow-up beads for defects or scope changes found during acceptance.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Verify Web Edition v1 end-to-end release readiness.

**Current behavior:**
Individual Web Edition slices will land separately. A final acceptance gate is needed to prove the combined static browser app satisfies the PRD and documented scope.

**Desired behavior:**
The final acceptance pass verifies the static `/app/` bundle, website artifact handoff, browser app shell, `.canopi` lifecycle, Browser Drafts, reduced Species Catalog, reduced Plant Detail, canvas workspace, Location/map scope, optional static templates, omitted surfaces, asset-size checks, and Cloudflare Pages compatibility. Defects or scope gaps become follow-up beads rather than being silently fixed in this verification slice.

**Key interfaces:**
- Web Edition static build artifact
- Website `/app/` route
- Browser App Shell
- Design Session and `.canopi` lifecycle
- Browser app-data adapters
- DuckDB-WASM Species Catalog
- MapLibre web surfaces

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new product features
- Reopening v1 scope decisions
- Implementing deferred features such as geocoding, Site Adaptation, broad exports, or offline/PWA behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A fresh browser session can launch Web Edition from the static `/app/` route.
- [ ] A user can create a Design, edit canvas content, save a Browser Draft, reopen the draft, and download a valid `.canopi` file.
- [ ] A user can open an existing `.canopi`, preserve unsupported sections where possible, edit, and download a valid `.canopi`.
- [ ] Reduced Species Catalog search, filters, 11-language Common Name behavior, favorites, recently viewed, and reduced Plant Detail work in the built web app.
- [ ] Browser-local settings and Saved Object Stamps work without accounts or backend persistence.
- [ ] Location and street map behavior work within v1 scope and handle network failures cleanly.
- [ ] Static Design Templates work when configured or are hidden when not configured.
- [ ] Omitted surfaces are absent: Design Notebook, Timeline, Budget, Consortium, Problem Report, geocoding, Site Adaptation, broad exports, Display by, and Color by.
- [ ] Build and artifact checks pass, including no Tauri/native web imports and Cloudflare Pages per-asset limits.

title: Mount web canvas workspace with v1 scope pruning
description:
## Slice Summary
Expose the shared canvas workspace in Web Edition while removing or hiding desktop-only and deferred v1 surfaces from web navigation and commands.

## User Value
A designer can edit real canvas Design Objects in the browser without encountering unavailable Timeline, Budget, Consortium, Design Notebook, Problem Report, geocoding, native export, Display by, or Color by surfaces.

## Out of Scope
Do not reimplement planning-heavy bottom tabs, Site Adaptation, broad export formats, or desktop-native chrome.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Mount the shared canvas workspace in Web Edition with v1 scope pruning.

**Current behavior:**
The desktop app includes canvas editing plus planning-heavy and desktop-only surfaces. Web Edition v1 should keep the real canvas workspace but avoid unavailable or deferred desktop/planning features.

**Desired behavior:**
The Web Edition exposes real canvas editing for Design Objects and preserves existing canvas authority through the Canvas Runtime Surface and Canvas Runtime App Adapter. Web navigation and commands omit Timeline, Budget, Consortium, Design Notebook, Problem Report, geocoding entry points, native export surfaces, Display by, and Color by.
