Manual Plant Color, Plant Symbol, grouping, locks, zones, annotations, measurement guides, and browser-local Saved Object Stamps remain usable where their dependencies are available.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Runtime Surface
- Canvas Runtime App Adapter
- Browser App Shell
- App Command Graph
- Panel chrome
- Saved Object Stamp workbench

**Out of scope:**
- Reimplementing Timeline, Budget, or Consortium in web v1
- Site Adaptation
- Broad export formats
- Desktop-native chrome or file behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Web Edition mounts a real canvas workspace backed by the shared canvas runtime seams.
- [ ] Users can create or edit at least placed plants, zones, annotations, measurement guides, groups, locks, Plant Colors, and Plant Symbols where supported by shared runtime behavior.
- [ ] Timeline, Budget, Consortium, Design Notebook, Problem Report, geocoding entry points, native exports, Display by, and Color by are absent from web navigation and commands.
- [ ] Browser-local Saved Object Stamps are available without stamp import/export.
- [ ] Focused tests verify omitted surfaces are not mounted or command-accessible in the web build.

title: Decide Web Edition artifact source for website deploy
description:
## Slice Summary
Choose the source and release flow the website deploy will use to obtain the versioned Web Edition static artifact.

## User Value
Maintainers know whether website deployment downloads from CI/release storage or uses another approved artifact source before automation is built.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the build or deploy automation in this decision slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Decide where the website deploy obtains the versioned Web Edition build artifact.

**Current behavior:**
The PRD and ADRs define artifact handoff, but not the exact release source. The website should publish a versioned Web Edition artifact without importing app source or committing generated `/app` assets long term.

**Desired behavior:**
A maintainer chooses the deployment artifact source and release flow, such as CI artifact storage, release assets, or another durable source. The decision should define versioning, retention, access, local preview behavior, and the contract the website deploy depends on.

**Key interfaces:**
- Web Edition build artifact
- Website deploy flow
- Release/versioning process
- Local preview handoff

**Out of scope:**
- Implementing build automation
- Implementing website deployment changes
- Changing the artifact handoff model already accepted by the PRD
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A concrete artifact source for website deployment is selected.
- [ ] The decision covers versioning, retention, access, and local preview behavior.
- [ ] The decision states how the website deploy locates and verifies the artifact.
- [ ] Follow-up implementation beads are adjusted if the chosen source changes their scope.
close_reason: Selected public Canopi app release assets as the production Web Edition artifact source. The website deploy will download the release-tagged artifact, verify the included manifest and SHA-256 checksums, and publish it under /app/; local sibling-checkout copy is allowed only for preview/dev.

title: Establish Web Edition build and compile-time adapters
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the browser build entry and compile-time platform adapter seam so Web Edition code can use browser adapters without runtime web-vs-Tauri branching.

## User Value
Future Web Edition slices have a safe browser target that can share platform-neutral app modules without bundling desktop-only code.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the full Browser App Shell, real browser persistence, or DuckDB-WASM catalog behavior in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Establish the Web Edition browser build entry and compile-time platform adapter seam.

**Current behavior:**
The frontend is currently shaped around the desktop/Tauri build. Browser-specific behavior for persistence, settings, app data, catalog reads, file import/export, and shell commands does not yet have a compile-time selected adapter path.

**Desired behavior:**
The project has a browser Web Edition build entry that selects browser adapters at compile time while desktop continues to select Tauri/native adapters. Shared app modules consume small caller-shaped interfaces and do not branch on runtime web-vs-Tauri flags. Build or source-boundary checks catch accidental browser imports of Tauri-only adapters, desktop IPC modules, native file path behavior, or desktop-only command surfaces.

**Key interfaces:**
- Browser Vite entry/build
- Desktop Vite entry/build
- Platform adapter seams for document persistence, settings, app data, catalog reads, shell commands, and file import/export
- Build-boundary checks

**Out of scope:**
- Full Browser App Shell UI
- Real Browser Draft persistence
- DuckDB-WASM catalog behavior
- Website artifact publication
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A browser Web Edition build entry exists and can build without Tauri runtime assumptions.
- [ ] Desktop and browser builds select platform adapters at compile time.
- [ ] Shared app modules do not contain runtime web-vs-Tauri adapter selection.
- [ ] Boundary checks fail if web chunks or browser entry code import Tauri-only or desktop IPC surfaces.
- [ ] Existing desktop build behavior remains intact.
close_reason: Added a separate Web Edition Vite build entry, compile-time #platform adapter alias, browser/desktop platform adapters, web build artifact scanner, ignored dist-web output, and command docs. Verified with npm test -- frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts, npm run build:web, npm run build, and git diff --check.

title: Generate reduced DuckDB-WASM catalog assets
description:
## Slice Summary
Add generation for the reduced Web Edition Species Catalog assets from canopi-data export lineage, including locale/search shards and hero image metadata.

## User Value
The Web Edition can ship a static, size-checked Species Catalog dataset that supports the v1 search and detail experience without bundling the full desktop database.

## Out of Scope
Do not integrate the catalog into the browser UI or migrate desktop storage away from SQLite.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Generate reduced DuckDB-WASM Species Catalog assets for Web Edition v1.

**Current behavior:**
The desktop app uses the full SQLite Species Catalog. The Web Edition needs reduced static assets generated from canopi-data export lineage and sized for Cloudflare Pages.

**Desired behavior:**
A generator emits deterministic DuckDB-compatible reduced catalog assets, locale/search shards, and image metadata artifacts. The generated assets include only v1-approved fields, support 11 UI languages for Common Name display/search, include at most one remote hero image metadata record per Species where available, and fail size checks when any artifact exceeds Cloudflare Pages per-asset limits.

**Key interfaces:**
- canopi-data export input
- Reduced Species Catalog schema
- Locale/Common Name search shards
- Hero image metadata artifact
- Asset-size checks

**Out of scope:**
- Browser UI integration
- Desktop SQLite replacement
- Site Adaptation fields
- Bundled image binaries
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A repeatable generator creates reduced Web Edition catalog assets from canopi-data export lineage.
- [ ] Generated assets include Canonical Name, Common Names, climate zone, habit or growth form, life cycle, and allowed image metadata.
- [ ] Generated assets exclude edibility, hardiness, height, stratum, and desktop-only detail payloads.
- [ ] Locale/search assets support all 11 current UI languages.
- [ ] Generated asset names are deterministic.
- [ ] Asset-size checks fail when any web catalog, shard, worker-adjacent, or image-metadata artifact exceeds the Cloudflare Pages per-asset limit.
- [ ] Tests cover schema contents, excluded fields, locale shard behavior, and image metadata shape.

title: Ship Browser App Shell tracer
description:
## Slice Summary
Mount a browser-specific shell around the shared Canopi app core with the v1 command set and without desktop title-bar, menu, native window, or native file-management promises.

## User Value
A user can open the Web Edition workspace and see browser-appropriate Canopi app chrome instead of desktop chrome that implies unsupported capabilities.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement full `.canopi` browser persistence or the reduced catalog in this shell tracer.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Ship a minimal Browser App Shell around the shared Canopi app core.

**Current behavior:**
The mounted app shell is desktop/Tauri-shaped and includes chrome or command assumptions that imply native window controls, desktop menus, native file flows, or desktop-only surfaces.

**Desired behavior:**
The Web Edition mounts a Browser App Shell with the v1 command set: New, Open `.canopi`, Download `.canopi`, Drafts, language/theme, and panel toggles. The shell routes through the App Command Graph and app seams rather than importing browser storage or file APIs directly in generic components. Desktop title bar, desktop menu bar, native window controls, native Recent Design paths, reveal-in-file-manager, updater, Problem Report, native save/save-as, direct save-back, and desktop-style exports are absent from the web shell.

**Key interfaces:**
- Browser App Shell
- App Command Graph
- Design Session actions
- Settings and panel chrome projections

**Out of scope:**
- Full browser `.canopi` persistence
- Browser Draft storage
- Reduced Species Catalog integration
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Web Edition renders browser app chrome instead of desktop title/menu/window chrome.
- [ ] The visible shell command set is limited to New, Open `.canopi`, Download `.canopi`, Drafts, language/theme, and panel toggles.
- [ ] Web shell commands route through the established app command and document/session seams.
- [ ] Desktop-only commands and affordances are absent from the web shell.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover the visible command set and omitted desktop surfaces.
close_reason: Added a browser-specific WebApp shell with only the v1 command set, omitted desktop title/menu/window/problem-report/notebook/native save affordances, routed file/draft commands through an injectable shell handler seam, and routed language/theme/panel controls through browser-safe app state. Verified with npm test -- web-app-shell.test.tsx frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts, npm run build:web, npm run package:web, and git diff --check.

title: Implement browser `.canopi` Design lifecycle
description:
## Slice Summary
Implement browser create, open, edit, and download flows for real `.canopi` Designs through the normal Design Session and save-composition seams.

## User Value
A designer can create or open a Design in the browser, make a small edit, and download a valid `.canopi` file that remains interoperable with desktop.

## Out of Scope
Do not add Browser Drafts, direct save-back to imported files, broad export formats, or desktop-native file behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Implement browser create, open, edit, and download flows for real `.canopi` Designs.

**Current behavior:**
Desktop `.canopi` lifecycle flows rely on native dialogs, Tauri IPC, and desktop save semantics. The Web Edition needs browser file adapters while preserving the normal Design Session and save-composition seams.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can create a new Design, open an existing `.canopi`, edit it in the Web Edition, and download a valid `.canopi` file. Browser adapters feed the normal Design Session lifecycle and save composition. Loaded unsupported sections are preserved where possible, and new web-created Designs emit valid empty unsupported sections.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Session actions and state machine
- Design file composition and `.canopi` format contracts
- Browser file import and download adapters
- Canvas document surface

**Out of scope:**
- Browser Drafts and autosave
- Direct save-back to imported files
- PNG, SVG, PDF, CSV, report, diagnostic bundle, or native-style exports
- Desktop-native file dialogs or path references
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A user can create a new Design in the browser through the Design Session seam.
- [ ] A user can open a `.canopi` file from browser file input or equivalent browser adapter.
- [ ] A user can make a small Design edit and download a valid `.canopi` file.
- [ ] Web-created `.canopi` files load in the existing format round-trip tests or equivalent verification.
- [ ] Loaded unsupported sections are preserved where possible.
- [ ] New web-created Designs emit valid empty unsupported sections.
- [ ] Direct save-back and non-`.canopi` export formats are not exposed.
close_reason: Added browser .canopi lifecycle handlers for new/open/download through a browser-safe Design Session controller, using the Design Session store and save composition, preserving loaded future fields, emitting valid empty sections for new web Designs, keeping opened files detached with no direct save-back, and wiring the Browser App Shell file commands to the controller. Verified with npm test -- web-design-session.test.ts web-app-shell.test.tsx frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts document-format-contract.test.ts file-format-round-trip.test.ts, npm run package:web, and git diff --check.

title: PRD: Web Edition v1
description:
## Problem Statement

Canopi needs a lightweight Web Edition that users can open from the public website without installing the desktop app or relying on a backend. The website is hosted as a static Cloudflare Pages site, so the Web Edition must fit static asset limits, avoid server-side persistence, and not turn the website into an account-backed application.

The current desktop app already has the real Canopi Design model, canvas runtime, Species Catalog Workbench, Location and map flows, favorites, Saved Object Stamps, and `.canopi` save/load behavior. The problem is to make a browser-accessible Canopi experience that reuses those proven seams while removing desktop-only promises such as native windows, filesystem paths, native save dialogs, broad export surfaces, desktop user databases, and full bundled Species Catalog data.

## Solution

Build Web Edition v1 as a real Canopi app delivered as a static browser bundle under the website's `/app/` route. Users can create, open, edit, draft, and download real `.canopi` Designs in the browser. The Web Edition uses a Browser App Shell around the shared Canopi app core, browser-local Drafts and personal app data, a reduced DuckDB-WASM Species Catalog, and explicit `.canopi` download/export as the durable portable boundary.

The Canopi app repository owns the Web Edition source and build. The website publishes a versioned static artifact only; it does not import app modules or commit generated `/app` assets long term. The Web Edition remains compatible with Cloudflare Pages by keeping all app, worker, WASM, catalog, template, and image-metadata assets under the per-asset limit and by avoiding backend services, service workers, PWA install flows, and offline-first cache promises in v1.

## Success Criteria

- A user can launch Web Edition v1 from the static website, create a Design, edit the canvas, use the reduced Species Catalog, save a Browser Draft, reopen it in the same browser profile, and download a valid `.canopi` file.
- A user can open a `.canopi` file from desktop or another browser session, preserve unsupported loaded Design sections where possible, and download a valid `.canopi` file after web edits.
- The Web Edition bundle is served as static assets under `/app/` without a backend, accounts, sync, server-side persistence, service worker, PWA install flow, or app-managed offline cache.
- The website deployment consumes a versioned Web Edition build artifact and does not import Canopi app source or commit generated `/app` assets long term.
- The web build uses browser adapters selected at compile time and has automated checks that prevent Tauri/native adapters or desktop-only command surfaces from entering web chunks.
- All generated app, WASM, worker, catalog, template, and image-metadata assets stay within Cloudflare Pages per-asset limits.
- The reduced DuckDB-WASM Species Catalog supports the v1 filters, 11-language Common Name display/search, reduced Plant Detail, and lazy hero image metadata without bundling image binaries.
- Web Edition v1 clearly omits desktop-only and deferred surfaces: Design Notebook, native file management, direct save-back, broad export formats, Problem Report, geocoding, Site Adaptation, satellite/terrain/offline maps, Timeline, Budget, Consortium, Display by, and Color by.

## User Stories

1. As a website visitor, I want to launch Canopi in my browser, so that I can try real design workflows without installing the desktop app.
2. As a designer, I want to create a new Design in the Web Edition, so that I can start planning directly from the website.
3. As a designer, I want to open an existing `.canopi` Design in the browser, so that I can inspect or continue work created elsewhere.
4. As a designer, I want to download my current Design as a `.canopi` file, so that I own a durable portable artifact that can move to the desktop app.
5. As a designer, I want browser-local Drafts and autosave, so that I can resume recent browser work on the same device/profile without manually downloading after every edit.
6. As a designer, I want the Web Edition to make the difference between a browser draft and a downloaded `.canopi` file clear, so that I do not mistake convenience storage for durable cloud sync.
7. As a designer, I want to place plants, zones, annotations, measurement guides, groups, locks, Plant Colors, and Plant Symbols in the browser, so that the Web Edition feels like Canopi rather than a static catalog demo.
8. As a designer, I want the Species Catalog to support text search by Canonical Name and Common Name, plus climate zone, habit or growth form, and life cycle filters, so that I can find usable plants from the lightweight catalog.
9. As a multilingual user, I want Common Name display and search in the current 11 UI languages, so that the Web Edition works consistently with the existing localized app experience.
10. As a designer, I want a reduced Species detail view with a hero image when available, Canonical Name, Common Names, climate zone, habit or growth form, and life cycle, so that I get the essential planning information quickly.
11. As a designer, I want Species favorites, recently viewed Species, and Saved Object Stamps to persist in my browser, so that my web workspace remains useful across visits without an account.
12. As a designer, I want Location to work through manual latitude/longitude and map click or pin placement, so that I can set a site without relying on a geocoding backend.
13. As a designer, I want the Web Edition map to show a street basemap where network access is available, so that location context remains useful in the browser.
14. As a designer, I want static Design Templates to open as real Designs when bundled for web, so that I can start from examples without arbitrary remote downloads.
15. As a website maintainer, I want the website to publish a versioned Web Edition artifact, so that the website stays a static Astro/Cloudflare site and does not own app internals.
16. As a maintainer, I want the web build to fail when browser chunks import desktop-only adapters or exceed Cloudflare Pages asset limits, so that static deployment problems are caught before release.

## Implementation Decisions

- Web Edition v1 is a real Canopi app, not a static catalog, sketch format, iframe widget, or website-only demo.
- The Web Edition creates, opens, edits, drafts, and exports real `.canopi` Designs. Unsupported web UI sections must be omitted while preserving loaded Design data where possible and emitting valid empty sections for new web-created Designs.
- The Browser App Shell replaces the desktop title bar, desktop menu bar, native window controls, native file menu, native Recent Design path menus, reveal-in-file-manager actions, updater affordances, Problem Report entry points, native save/save-as flows, direct save-back, and desktop-style export commands.
- The Browser App Shell v1 command set is New, Open `.canopi`, Download `.canopi`, Drafts, language/theme, and panel toggles.
- Web Edition source stays in the Canopi app repository as a browser Vite entry/build that shares platform-neutral app modules with desktop. The website publishes the built artifact only.
- The website should not import Canopi app source as an Astro component package, workspace dependency, package import, submodule, or copied component tree.
- Generated `/app` assets should not be committed to the website repository long term. Deployment should copy or download a versioned Web Edition build artifact; local development may copy from a sibling checkout for preview only.
- Desktop and Web Edition entries select platform adapters at compile time. Shared modules must not use runtime `isWeb` or `isTauri` branches to choose IPC, browser storage, file, catalog, or shell behavior.
- Shared app modules should depend on small caller-shaped interfaces for Design Session persistence, settings, personal app data, Species Catalog reads, shell commands, and file import/export.
- CI or build checks should reject Web Edition chunks that import Tauri-only adapters, desktop IPC modules, native file path behavior, or desktop-only command surfaces.
- Web Edition build checks should fail when any generated app, WASM, worker, catalog, template, or image-metadata asset exceeds the Cloudflare Pages per-asset limit.
- Web Edition v1 uses DuckDB-WASM with generated reduced catalog assets from canopi-data export lineage. The reduced catalog is not hand-maintained in the website repository.
- The reduced catalog includes the v1 search/filter fields: Canonical Name/Common Name text search, climate zone, habit or growth form, and life cycle.
- The reduced catalog excludes edibility, hardiness, height, and stratum in v1. Web v1 Placed Plant presentation uses explicit design colors/symbols and generic fallbacks, not stratum metadata.
- Common Name display and search should support the current 11 UI languages with language-specific reduced/search shards and Canonical Name plus English fallback data where needed.
- Web Edition image support is metadata-only and lazy: at most one remote hero image per Species when available, with source/credit/license fields. It must not bundle image binaries, prefetch galleries, add a backend image proxy, or port the desktop native image cache.
- Plant detail in Web Edition v1 is reduced to hero image, Canonical Name, Common Names, climate zone, habit or growth form, and life cycle.
- Browser Drafts and autosave are the active browser save target. Explicit `.canopi` download/export remains the durable portable save path.
- The Drafts list is not the Design Notebook. It has no Notebook Sections, saved filesystem paths, file reveal actions, notebook ordering model, or notebook-style organization.
- Browser-local app data may include Design drafts and autosave state, Drafts list, settings, favorites, recently viewed Species, and Saved Object Stamps. It is convenience state that can be lost if site data is cleared or evicted.
- Web Edition v1 keeps Saved Object Stamps browser-local only and does not expose stamp import/export.
- Web Edition v1 exports `.canopi` only. PNG, SVG, PDF, CSV, report, diagnostic bundle, native-style exports, and Saved Object Stamp import/export are out of scope.
- Web Edition v1 omits Problem Report, Diagnostic Bundle generation, native report-folder creation, and folder reveal behavior.
- Web Edition v1 omits address search/geocoding. Location remains editable through manual latitude/longitude entry and map click or pin placement.
- Web Edition v1 keeps street basemap behavior only. It omits satellite basemaps, terrain contours, hillshade, offline tile download, service-worker tile precache, and offline map promises.
- Web Edition v1 may keep the Community/Design Template world map only for templates shipped as known static `.canopi` assets. If no static template set is prepared, the template map should be hidden.
- Static web templates import as Designs without Site Adaptation review, compatibility checks, or replacement suggestions.
- Web Edition v1 is not offline-first. It should not add service workers, PWA install prompts, offline-first caches, or app-managed precache behavior.
- The deepest module seams to preserve are Design Session, Canvas Runtime Surface, Canvas Runtime App Adapter, App Command Graph, Species Catalog Workbench, Species Catalog Read Projection, Design Edit, MapLibre Host, MapLibre Surface Adapter, Location Workbench, Canvas Layer Presentation, settings projection, and browser/desktop platform adapters.
- Delivery should be sliced so the static build/adapter boundary lands before the full catalog and app-data work. The reduced DuckDB-WASM catalog and browser document persistence are independent prerequisites for a useful Web Edition but can be validated separately.

## Testing Decisions

- Tests should verify behavior through the same seams production callers use, not by reaching around module interfaces or asserting implementation details.
- Add build-boundary tests that prove the Web Edition bundle imports browser adapters only and does not include Tauri-only imports, desktop IPC modules, native file-path behavior, or desktop-only command surfaces.
- Add artifact-size checks for Web Edition build outputs, DuckDB-WASM worker/WASM files, reduced catalog shards, static templates, and image-metadata assets against Cloudflare Pages per-asset limits.
- Add Design Session adapter tests for browser create/open/edit/draft/download flows using the normal Design Session lifecycle and save-composition seams.
- Add `.canopi` round-trip tests proving Web-created Designs are valid, loaded unsupported sections are preserved where possible, and new web-created Designs emit valid empty unsupported sections.
- Add Browser Draft tests that cover autosave, reopening a draft, distinguishing browser-local draft state from downloaded `.canopi`, and avoiding Design Notebook semantics.
- Add browser-local app-data adapter tests for settings, favorites, recently viewed Species, and Saved Object Stamps, including storage unavailable or eviction-like failure handling where practical.
- Add Species Catalog read adapter tests for the reduced DuckDB-WASM catalog: browse/search, Canonical Name/Common Name matching, locale-specific Common Name display/search, v1 filters, pagination/cursors, and reduced Plant Detail hydration.
- Add catalog generator tests that prove the generated assets contain only approved v1 fields, exclude hardiness/height/stratum/edibility, include image metadata shape, and produce deterministic asset names with size checks.
- Add Plant Detail UI tests for missing/failed remote hero images, attribution/source display, and hiding desktop-only detail sections.
- Add Browser App Shell tests for visible command set, absence of desktop/native commands, and correct routing through command/document/settings seams.
- Add Location and map tests for manual coordinates, map click/pin placement, street-basemap-only scope, geocoding absence, and graceful behavior when map resources fail.
- Add static Design Template tests for known static asset import through normal Design Session template-open flow and hiding the template map when no static template set is configured.
- Add boundary tests comparable to the existing frontend boundary/source tests, command registry tests, document session tests, MapLibre Host tests, Species Catalog search/session tests, settings projection tests, and file-format round-trip tests.

## Out of Scope

- Backend services, accounts, sync, cloud libraries, server-side persistence, or any requirement that turns the website into an account-backed app.
- Importing Canopi app source into the website repository or committing generated `/app` assets to the website repository long term.
- Direct save-back to an originally imported file.
- Desktop title bar, desktop menu bar, native window controls, native File menu, desktop Recent Design path menus, saved path references, reveal-in-file-manager actions, native save/save-as flows, and desktop updater/reporting affordances.
- Design Notebook, Notebook Sections, notebook-style Design organization, and desktop Recent Design behavior in the Web Edition.
- Timeline, Budget, Consortium, Display by, Color by, Design Report PDF export, and native-style export surfaces in Web Edition v1.
- PNG, SVG, PDF, CSV, report, diagnostic bundle, Saved Object Stamp import/export, or other browser export formats beyond `.canopi`.
- Problem Report, Diagnostic Bundle generation, native report-folder creation, and folder reveal behavior.
- Address search/geocoding.
- Site Adaptation, compatibility checks, replacement suggestions, and reduced-catalog fields needed only for those workflows.
- Edibility, hardiness, height, and stratum fields or filters in the Web Edition v1 catalog.
- Satellite basemaps, terrain contours, hillshade, offline tile download, offline maps, service-worker tile precache, and offline map/image promises.
- Bundled image binaries, image galleries, backend image proxying, and desktop native image caching in the Web Edition.
- Desktop migration from SQLite to DuckDB-WASM. Desktop storage migration requires separate measured evidence.
- Service worker, PWA install flow, offline-first cache, or app-managed precache strategy in v1.

## Further Notes

- Major delivery slices likely include: static web build and artifact handoff; compile-time adapter seams and boundary checks; Browser App Shell; browser file/draft/settings/app-data adapters; reduced DuckDB-WASM catalog generator and adapter; reduced Plant Detail with hero image metadata; canvas/design workflow pruning for web; Location/map web scope; optional static Design Templates; and CI/deployment checks.
- Cloudflare Pages compatibility is a hard constraint. Current documented limit is 25 MiB per single asset, and the generated build should keep file count conservative.
- Web Edition v1 should tolerate network failures for map tiles and remote Species hero images without implying offline support.
- The reduced catalog measurements from the grill session suggest the v1 data shape is feasible when compressed/sharded: core reduced Species fields were roughly 6.8 MiB gzipped, all 11-language Common Names roughly 8.4 MiB gzipped, and first-image-per-species metadata roughly 4.6 MiB gzipped. Implementation should remeasure using the actual generator output.
- The website deployment handoff still needs an exact implementation plan: whether it downloads an artifact from CI/release storage or copies from a local sibling checkout for preview. The PRD decision is artifact handoff, not the final release automation mechanism.
- Later enhancements such as geocoding, offline/PWA behavior, additional export formats, Site Adaptation, satellite/terrain maps, stamp import/export, or desktop DuckDB migration require separate product/architecture decisions.

title: Remove design report and plant display modes
description: Remove Design Report PDF export and plant display/color-by feature surfaces from Canopi. This includes command/menu wiring, frontend UI, runtime presentation hooks, Rust report service exposure, tests/docs that describe the removed features, and any generated or dependency cleanup required by the removal.
design: Keep manual plant color/symbol behavior. Remove feature surfaces cleanly instead of hiding unavailable buttons. Preserve .canopi compatibility for existing fields where needed.
acceptance_criteria: Design Report PDF export is no longer exposed or implemented; plant display/color-by controls and color-by runtime behavior are removed; affected tests and docs are updated; required frontend/Rust quality gates pass or exact failures are recorded.
close_reason: Removed Design Report PDF export command/service/frontend report builder and removed plant Display by/Color by mode APIs/UI; kept manual plant colors/symbols and pinned-name legend; updated docs/ADRs/locales/tests. Gates: npm run check:types, npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, cargo fmt --all -- --check, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test --workspace.

title: Deduplicate prefix Common Name relevance rows
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix Species Catalog Search so selected-language Common Name prefix relevance joins cannot emit duplicate Species rows when one Species has multiple matching tokens for the same query prefix.

## User Value
Users see each Species once in active relevance search results, and pagination does not skip or underfill pages because duplicate rows consumed result slots.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the selected-language-only search policy, add fuzzy matching, or change the user-facing ranking tiers beyond preserving the existing first-token-position ordering without duplicates.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Prevent duplicate Species rows during selected-language Common Name prefix relevance search.

**Current behavior:**
Active Species Catalog Search relevance joins selected-language Common Name tokens by query prefix. If one Species has multiple token rows in the selected language that match the same prefix, the list query can emit that Species more than once before LIMIT/OFFSET pagination is applied.

**Desired behavior:**
Each Species appears at most once in active relevance search results. Prefix token ranking should still use the best first token position for each query token, and pagination should continue to operate on unique Species rows.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Search list query planning
- selected-language Common Name token relevance joins
- active text search pagination

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the selected-language-only Common Name search policy
- Adding fuzzy typo tolerance
- Reworking the public search request contract
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A Species with multiple selected-language token rows matching the same query prefix appears only once in search results.
- [ ] Prefix relevance still orders by the earliest matching token position for each query token.
- [ ] Active text search pagination works over unique Species rows.
- [ ] Regression tests cover duplicate-token prefix behavior.
close_reason: Implemented grouped selected-language Common Name token relevance joins so prefix searches return each Species once; covered by focused regression, query-builder tests, workspace tests, bundled relevance examples, and latency harness.

title: Suppress detail English Common Name fallback
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix Species detail hydration so a non-English selected language with no localized Common Name does not fall back to the English storage Common Name.

## User Value
Detail pages stay coherent with list, favorites, and recently viewed surfaces: untranslated Species show Canonical Name rather than an unexplained English Common Name.

## Out of Scope
Do not hide untranslated Species, add synthetic translations, or change English-locale fallback behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Keep Species detail Common Name display selected-language-only.

**Current behavior:**
List-style Species Catalog surfaces no longer fall back to English Common Names in non-English locales, but Species detail hydration can still use the storage Common Name when the selected language has no localized Common Name.

**Desired behavior:**
Species detail should return no Common Name for a non-English locale when the selected language has no Common Name, letting callers display Canonical Name consistently with list, favorites, and recently viewed rows. English locale behavior may continue to use the English storage Common Name fallback.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog detail read projection
- Common Name lookup service
- Species detail caller contract

**Out of scope:**
- Hiding untranslated Species
- Adding machine-translated or synthetic Common Names
- Changing English-locale detail behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A non-English Species detail request with no selected-language Common Name returns no Common Name instead of the English storage Common Name.
- [ ] English Species detail requests still return the available English Common Name.
- [ ] List-style selected-language Common Name behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Regression tests cover the detail fallback behavior.
close_reason: Removed detail English storage Common Name fallback for non-English locales while preserving English locale behavior; covered by focused detail regression, species catalog read tests, workspace tests, and review grep.

title: Explain alternate Common Name search matches
description:
## Slice Summary
Expose and render a selected-language Matched Common Name when an active Species Catalog Search result matched an alternate Common Name that is not already displayed.

## User Value
Rows such as Clochette d'Irlande can explain why they appeared for mélisse by showing the matched alternate name without making every result row show extra names.

## Out of Scope
Do not permanently expand browse rows to three Common Names or show matched names from another language.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Show a selected-language Matched Common Name when it explains an active search result.

**Current behavior:**
Rows can appear because of an alternate Common Name that is not displayed, making results feel strange. The existing secondary Common Name display is not explicitly query-aware.

**Desired behavior:**
Search results may include an optional Matched Common Name that explains why the row matched the active query. The UI should show it coherently as secondary/explanatory text only when it is useful and selected-language-local. Browse rows should remain compact and should not permanently show three Common Names.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species list item shared contract
- Species Catalog Search result projection
- Species Catalog row/card rendering
- Locale-aware result display tests

**Out of scope:**
- Showing matched names from another language
- Expanding every browse row to show all known Common Names
- Changing detail-card secondary-name behavior except where needed to stay coherent
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Search results can carry an optional selected-language Matched Common Name distinct from the displayed Common Name.
- [ ] When a row matched an alternate selected-language Common Name, the Species Catalog row/card displays that matched name as search explanation.
- [ ] Browse mode and non-search lists do not show extra matched-name explanations.
- [ ] The UI does not show duplicate names when the matched name is already the displayed Common Name or existing secondary name.
- [ ] Shared type bindings and frontend/backend tests are updated for the new result contract.
close_reason: Added matched_common_name to SpeciesListItem, populated it from selected-language alternate Common Names only when the displayed name does not already explain the active search, and rendered it coherently in list/card results while browse/favorites stay compact. Verified with backend and frontend regressions plus generated bindings and full gates.

title: Rank displayed Common Name prefixes first
description:
## Slice Summary
Improve relevance ordering so exact and prefix matches on the displayed selected-language Common Name outrank weaker Common Name, alternate-name, and broader text matches.

## User Value
Searches such as apple/appl in English and mélisse/melis in French return the species users expect at the top of the list.

## Out of Scope
Do not introduce fuzzy typo tolerance or substring-first behavior for incomplete tokens.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Rank exact and prefix matches on displayed selected-language Common Names ahead of weaker matches.

**Current behavior:**
Common Name token matches can tie many rows together, so BM25 may rank longer or hidden-name matches above the expected displayed Common Name result. Incomplete prefixes such as appl and melis do not receive the same Common Name priority as full tokens.

**Desired behavior:**
Relevance ordering should prioritize selected-language displayed Common Names before weaker matches. Exact displayed Common Name matches rank first. Displayed Common Names whose tokens start with the query tokens rank before displayed names containing the query later. Alternate selected-language Common Name matches rank below displayed-name matches. Broader BM25 matches for Canonical Name, taxonomy, uses, or other text follow after Common Name tiers.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog relevance ordering
- Common Name token normalization and prefix matching
- Species list projection for displayed Common Name
- Active text search pagination

**Out of scope:**
- Substring-first ranking inside tokens
- Typo correction or fuzzy search
- Ranking English Common Names in non-English locales
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] English apple and appl searches rank Malus domestica first.
- [ ] French mélisse and melis searches rank the displayed Mélisse result first and rank Mélisse alpine / Mélisse des champs ahead of weaker or hidden-name matches.
- [ ] Violette à feuilles de mélisse ranks below displayed names starting with Mélisse but above broader non-Common-Name matches.
- [ ] Alternate-name matches such as Clochette d'Irlande via Mélisse des Moluques do not outrank displayed-name matches.
- [ ] Backend regression tests assert the ordered examples and prefix behavior.
close_reason: Implemented selected-language Common Name relevance tiers with exact/displayed prefix/displayed contains/alternate match ordering, prefix token matching, and short-name tie breaking. Added apple/appl and melis/melisse regression coverage and verified against the bundled DB ordering harness plus full gates.

title: Verify bundled DB asset supports selected-language search
description:
## Slice Summary
Validate whether the existing bundled Species Catalog database asset supports the new selected-language relevance behavior without regeneration, and document any required asset work if it does not.

## User Value
Release preparation has a clear answer on whether the next release is code-only or requires regenerating and uploading the plant database asset.

## Out of Scope
Do not regenerate or upload a new database asset unless verification proves it is necessary and the release workflow is explicitly invoked.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Verify whether the current bundled Species Catalog DB asset supports the selected-language search implementation.

**Current behavior:**
The planned search behavior appears likely to be implementable with existing Common Name and token tables, but release risk remains unless this is checked against the bundled database and representative latency.

**Desired behavior:**
After the selected-language matching and ranking changes land, verify the bundled database asset has the tables and indexes needed for correctness and acceptable first-page latency. Record whether the next release can ship code-only or whether a database asset regeneration/upload is required.

**Key interfaces:**
- Bundled Species Catalog database schema version
- Common Name token indexes and selected-language query plans
- Species Catalog latency harness or equivalent release-facing validation
- Build/release agent guidance

**Out of scope:**
- Regenerating, uploading, or promoting a new database asset unless verification proves it is necessary and release work explicitly requests it
- Changing product search behavior
- Broad canopi-data import changes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The bundled database schema/tables/indexes are checked against the selected-language search implementation.
- [ ] Representative searches such as apple, appl, mélisse, and melis are verified against the bundled database.
- [ ] First-page latency is measured or otherwise bounded using the repo's Species Catalog latency harness or an equivalent documented method.
- [ ] The bead records a clear release answer: code-only is sufficient, or a regenerated database asset is required.
- [ ] Agent/release docs are updated if the asset requirement or validation command changes.
close_reason: Verified the existing bundled canopi-core.db supports the selected-language search implementation without regeneration: the manual relevance harness shows Malus domestica first for apple/appl and French melisse/melis ordering as expected, including Moluccella matched_name; the latency harness ran against desktop/resources/canopi-core.db. Release answer: code-only is sufficient; no DB asset reupload required.

title: Restrict Common Name search to selected language
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Species Catalog Search match Common Names only in the selected app language while preserving language-neutral Canonical Name and botanical taxonomy search.

## User Value
A French search no longer pulls species only because they have English Common Names; search behavior is coherent with the selected language.

## Out of Scope
Do not remove language-neutral Canonical Name, genus, family, or other non-Common-Name search matching.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Restrict Species Catalog Common Name matching to the selected app language.

**Current behavior:**
Species Catalog Search can match against Common Names aggregated across languages or fall back to English Common Names for relevance. This can make non-English searches surface rows for language reasons users cannot see.

**Desired behavior:**
Common Name matching uses only Common Names in the selected app language. Canonical Name, genus, family, and other language-neutral botanical fields remain searchable. Non-English searches should not match a Species solely because of an English Common Name.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Search request contract
- Species Catalog Read Projection list/count planning
- Common Name token normalization and selected-language matching
- Search pagination and first-page/next-page behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Typo tolerance or fuzzy edit-distance search
- Removing Canonical Name or taxonomy search
- Database asset regeneration unless a follow-up verification bead proves it is needed
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] In French, an English-only Common Name does not cause a Species to match a Common Name search.
- [ ] Canonical Name, genus, and family searches still work regardless of selected UI language.
- [ ] Count and list queries use coherent selected-language search predicates.
- [ ] Pagination remains stable for active text search results.
- [ ] Regression tests cover selected-language-only matching and absence of English Common Name fallback matching.
close_reason: Restricted Common Name search predicates and relevance joins to the selected language while keeping Canonical Name, genus, family, and other FTS fields language-neutral. Verified selected-language-only and no-English-fallback regressions plus full frontend/Rust gates.

title: Use selected-language Common Names only
description:
## Slice Summary
Ensure Species Catalog list-style projections and related species display surfaces use Common Names only when they exist in the selected app language.

## User Value
Users in French, Spanish, and other non-English languages do not see unexplained English Common Name fallbacks; untranslated species remain usable through Canonical Name.

## Out of Scope
Do not hide Species solely because they lack a Common Name in the selected language.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Stop displaying Common Names from another language as fallbacks across Species display surfaces.

**Current behavior:**
Species list projections and related species-name lookups may fall back to English Common Names when the selected app language has no Common Name.

**Desired behavior:**
Common Name display is selected-language-only. If a Species lacks a Common Name in the selected app language, callers should display Canonical Name instead of an English Common Name. Species should remain visible in browse, favorites, recently viewed, detail, placement, and planning surfaces.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species list item read projection
- Common Name lookup services for batches and details
- Species detail header and secondary-name projection
- Placed plant and planning species-name projections

**Out of scope:**
- Hiding untranslated Species from the catalog
- Changing Canonical Name identity behavior
- Adding machine translation or synthetic Common Names
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] In a non-English locale, Species without a selected-language Common Name display Canonical Name instead of an English Common Name.
- [ ] Favorites, recently viewed Species, detail headers, placed-plant labels/lookups, and planning surfaces follow the selected-language-only Common Name rule.
- [ ] Species remain visible in browse and filter results even when they lack a selected-language Common Name.
- [ ] Backend and frontend tests cover selected-language display and no-English-fallback behavior.
- [ ] Generated bindings are updated if the shared Species display contract changes.
close_reason: Changed Species list projections and Common Name lookup services to use selected-language Common Names only, with English storage fallback only for English locale. Verified with backend projection/search regressions, full Rust workspace tests, frontend tests/typecheck, cargo fmt/check/clippy, and git diff --check.

title: Remove Species Catalog sort controls
description:
## Slice Summary
Remove user-facing sort choices from the Species Catalog Workbench while preserving deterministic browse ordering and relevance ordering for active text search.

## User Value
Users no longer need to choose a sort mode to get expected search results; the catalog behaves like a normal search surface by default.

## Out of Scope
Do not remove backend sort variants or IPC compatibility unless a later cleanup explicitly asks for contract removal.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Remove user-facing Species Catalog sort controls while keeping deterministic browse and relevance search behavior.

**Current behavior:**
The Species Catalog has internal support for multiple sort modes, and stale UI/test surfaces still model explicit sort selection. Active text search can be overridden away from relevance.

**Desired behavior:**
The Species Catalog Workbench should not expose sort controls. Empty browse requests use deterministic Canonical Name ordering. Active text searches always request relevance ordering. Existing backend sort variants may remain for compatibility, but the user-facing workflow should not offer them.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog Workbench search intent and result lifecycle
- Species Catalog Search request contract
- Species Catalog panel controls and tests

**Out of scope:**
- Removing backend sort enum variants or IPC compatibility
- Changing filter behavior
- Changing result relevance tiers beyond enforcing the default relevance path
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Species Catalog panel renders no user-facing sort selector or sort option control.
- [ ] Empty Species Catalog browse requests use Canonical Name ordering.
- [ ] Active text searches always use relevance ordering and cannot be overridden through Workbench UI commands.
- [ ] Frontend tests cover browse ordering intent and active-text relevance intent after sort control removal.
- [ ] Obsolete sort-selector component tests are removed or replaced with behavior-level tests.
close_reason: Removed the Species Catalog sort selector and set browse to Canonical Name order while active text search always requests relevance. Verified with focused Vitest, full npm test, TypeScript, Rust query/search tests, cargo fmt/check/clippy, cargo test --workspace, and git diff --check.

title: Refresh Open Recent after Design saves and opens
description:
## Slice Summary
File menu Open Recent should reflect Recent Designs recorded by later Design opens and saves without requiring an app reload.

## User Value
Users see current Recent Designs in the File menu, including a newly saved Design and the latest ordering after reopening an older Design.

## Out of Scope
Do not add palette commands, shortcuts, or new Recent Design UI outside the existing menu and workbench.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** File menu Open Recent should refresh after later Design opens and saves.

**Current behavior:**
Recent Designs are loaded when the menu component mounts. Later Design opens and saves recorded by the backend leave Open Recent stale or disabled until app reload.

**Desired behavior:**
The Open Recent menu refreshes from the Design Notebook Workbench at an interaction boundary, such as when the File menu opens, so it reflects Recent Designs recorded after mount. Opening a recent Design should also allow the next menu open to show the updated ordering.

**Key interfaces:**
- MenuBar File menu projection
- Design Notebook Workbench recent entries
- Design Session open/save actions that record Recent Designs

**Out of scope:**
- New Recent Design commands, shortcuts, or palette entries
- Changing the five-entry cap or submenu UX
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] File > Open Recent can refresh recent entries after the menu component has mounted.
- [ ] A newly available Recent Design appears in the menu without app reload.
- [ ] Opening a Recent Design does not rely on stale menu state for the next File menu open.
- [ ] Focused frontend regression coverage verifies the refresh behavior through the menu/workbench seam.
notes: Closed after TDD fixes for the review findings. Shipped durable notebook removal by removing save/load notebook side effects and adding explicit notebook-add behavior; empty Notebook Sections render without Design rows; Open Recent refreshes on File menu open with stale-refresh protection; Add Current ignores deleted Notebook Section ids and records post-save Design content. title: Clear stale Add Current Notebook Section selections
description:
## Slice Summary
The Design Notebook Add Current Design action must not submit a deleted Notebook Section id after the section list changes.

## User Value
Adding the current Design remains predictable after deleting a section selected in the Add Current dropdown.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Notebook Section deletion semantics or add a confirmation flow.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Add Current Design must not target a deleted Notebook Section id.

**Current behavior:**
The Add Current dropdown can retain a selected section id after that section is deleted. The trigger falls back visually to No section, but the Add action can still submit the deleted id.

**Desired behavior:**
When the section list no longer contains the selected Add Current target, the selection is cleared or validated at submit time so Add Current uses no section instead of a stale id.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook panel Add Current controls
- Design Notebook Workbench add-current command
- Notebook Section deletion behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Changing section deletion confirmation or behavior
- Adding new section move/dropdown behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] After a selected Add Current section is deleted, clicking Add Current does not pass the deleted section id.
- [ ] The visual fallback to No section matches the submitted value.
- [ ] Existing Add Current behavior still passes a valid selected section id.
- [ ] Focused frontend regression coverage covers stale section selection clearing or validation.
notes: Closed after TDD fixes for the review findings. Shipped durable notebook removal by removing save/load notebook side effects and adding explicit notebook-add behavior; empty Notebook Sections render without Design rows; Open Recent refreshes on File menu open with stale-refresh protection; Add Current ignores deleted Notebook Section ids and records post-save Design content. title: Keep removed Design Notebook entries removed
description:
## Slice Summary
Saving or reopening a Design must not automatically recreate a Design Notebook reference that the user removed.

## User Value
Remove from Notebook becomes durable: a saved Design can stay out of the Design Notebook until the user explicitly adds it again.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Recent Design recording. Do not add notebook archive or undo behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** A removed Design Notebook reference must not be recreated by normal Design save/load activity.

**Current behavior:**
Saving or reopening a saved Design can automatically insert a Design Notebook reference, even after the user removed that Design from the notebook.

**Desired behavior:**
Remove from Notebook is durable. Normal Design save/load and Recent Design recording may continue, but a Design Notebook reference is created only through explicit notebook-add behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design file save/load service behavior
- Recent Design recording
- Design Notebook reference persistence

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Recent Design behavior
- Adding restore, archive, or undo behavior for removed notebook entries
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Saving a currently open Design after removing it from the Design Notebook does not recreate the notebook reference.
- [ ] Reopening a saved Design after removing it from the Design Notebook does not recreate the notebook reference.
- [ ] Recent Design recording still happens for save/load flows.
- [ ] Regression coverage exercises the public save/load behavior that previously recreated the notebook reference.
notes: Closed after TDD fixes for the review findings. Shipped durable notebook removal by removing save/load notebook side effects and adding explicit notebook-add behavior; empty Notebook Sections render without Design rows; Open Recent refreshes on File menu open with stale-refresh protection; Add Current ignores deleted Notebook Section ids and records post-save Design content. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_files::tests::save_and_load_design_do_not_record_design_notebook_entry; npm test -- design-notebook-workbench.test.ts design-notebook-panel.test.tsx menu-bar-recent-designs.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; git diff --check.

title: Render Notebook Sections when no Designs are listed
description:
## Slice Summary
The Design Notebook panel must still render user-owned Notebook Sections when there are zero Design rows.

## User Value
A user can create, rename, reorder, and delete Notebook Sections even before adding Designs or after removing the last Design.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the empty-state copy beyond the minimum required to keep sections visible.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** User-owned Notebook Sections must stay visible when the notebook has zero Design rows.

**Current behavior:**
When the Design Notebook has Notebook Sections but no Design rows, the panel shows the global empty state and skips the sectioned ledger, hiding the sections.

**Desired behavior:**
The panel shows Notebook Sections whenever any section exists. Empty section rows remain available for rename/delete/reorder behavior even when there are no Designs in the notebook. The global empty state is reserved for the case where there are no sections and no Design rows.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook panel view model
- Notebook Section rendering and actions

**Out of scope:**
- New empty-state copy or onboarding UI
- Changing Notebook Section persistence semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A notebook snapshot with sections and zero entries renders those Notebook Sections.
- [ ] Empty rendered sections still expose the existing delete and double-click rename controls.
- [ ] The global empty state appears only when both sections and entries are empty.
- [ ] Focused frontend regression coverage covers the section-with-zero-entries case.
notes: Closed after TDD fixes for the review findings. Shipped durable notebook removal by removing save/load notebook side effects and adding explicit notebook-add behavior; empty Notebook Sections render without Design rows; Open Recent refreshes on File menu open with stale-refresh protection; Add Current ignores deleted Notebook Section ids and records post-save Design content. Tests: cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_files::tests::save_and_load_design_do_not_record_design_notebook_entry; npm test -- design-notebook-workbench.test.ts design-notebook-panel.test.tsx menu-bar-recent-designs.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace; git diff --check.

title: Simplify Design Notebook organization
description: Remove pinned/all/search notebook concepts and make the side panel a minimal sectioned Design switcher with direct row deletion, section-title double-click rename, and file-explorer-style drag/drop across sections. Remove pinned state from the user DB schema and update docs so Design Notebook vocabulary matches the simplified product model.
design: Treat Design Notebook as a single sectioned ledger of saved Design references. Recent Designs remain a File menu and Welcome Screen concept. Existing user DB compatibility is intentionally not preserved for pinned metadata per product decision.
acceptance_criteria: Pinned Designs, All Designs, search, pin controls, row overflow, row drag handles, and section rename buttons are removed from the notebook UI. Design rows can be reordered within a section and moved to other sections by dragging rows directly. Section names can be renamed by double-clicking the title. Design rows expose a direct delete/remove action. User DB migrations no longer create or preserve a pinned column for design_notebook_entries. Docs describe the simplified notebook model.
close_reason: Shipped simplified Design Notebook ledger: removed pinned/all/search UI and pinned backend/schema field, added direct design delete, section-title double-click rename, row drag/drop reorder and cross-section moves, updated docs/locales/tests. Verified with focused Vitest, full npm test, tsc, check:types, cargo fmt, cargo check, clippy, and cargo test.

title: Replace inline Recent Designs with Open Recent submenu
description:
## Slice Summary
Change the File menu so Recent Designs appear under a standard Open Recent submenu instead of as an inline dynamic section.

## User Value
Users get familiar desktop menu behavior: Open Recent remains visible, is disabled when empty, and exposes at most five Recent Designs through a submenu.

## Out of Scope
Adding Recent Designs to the command palette, adding shortcuts for individual recent entries, or changing how Recent Designs are recorded.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Replace inline Recent Designs with a standard Open Recent submenu.

**Current behavior:**
The File menu inserts Recent Designs as an inline dynamic section below Open Design when recent entries exist.

**Desired behavior:**
The File menu shows a normal Open Recent submenu entry below Open Design. The parent entry is visible but disabled when there are no Recent Designs. When entries exist, the parent opens a submenu on hover, focus, or ArrowRight and lists at most five Recent Designs. The parent item does not open a Design directly. Individual Recent Design entries open through the normal Design Session switching seam.

**Key interfaces:**
- App Command Graph menu projection
- MenuBar keyboard and hover behavior
- Design Notebook Workbench Recent Design projection
- Design Session open-from-path action seam

**Out of scope:**
- Adding Recent Designs to the command palette
- Adding shortcuts for individual Recent Designs
- Changing how Recent Designs are recorded or pruned
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] File menu shows Open Recent below Open Design.
- [ ] Open Recent is visible but disabled when there are no Recent Designs.
- [ ] Open Recent opens a submenu on hover, focus, and ArrowRight when entries exist.
- [ ] The submenu lists at most five Recent Designs.
- [ ] Clicking a Recent Design submenu item opens that saved Design through the Design Session seam.
- [ ] The Open Recent parent does not open a Design directly.
- [ ] Focused tests cover empty, populated, hover/focus/keyboard, cap, and open behavior.
close_reason: Replaced inline Recent Designs with disabled/populated Open Recent submenu capped to five entries and opening through the Design Session seam; verified with menu-bar tests, full frontend tests, tsc, and Rust gates.

title: Cohere Design Notebook command header and ledger
description:
## Slice Summary
Refine the Design Notebook side panel into a command header plus a single ledger: remove the redundant All Designs subtitle, move add/create actions into compact header chrome, keep search and view controls together, and leave the ledger for sections and rows.

## User Value
Users see one coherent professional side panel instead of a collection of separate strips, with the most important notebook commands in predictable places.

## Out of Scope
Changing Design Notebook persistence, changing row ordering behavior, or adding new notebook organization concepts.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Refine the Design Notebook side panel into a command header plus one ledger.

**Current behavior:**
The Design Notebook side panel presents separate stacked strips: title plus redundant All Designs subtitle, search, view pills, add-current strip, section creation form, then the ledger.

**Desired behavior:**
The side panel should feel like one coherent professional tool surface. Remove the All Designs subtitle under Design Notebook while keeping the count badge. Use a compact command header for title/count and notebook-level actions such as Add Current Design and New Section. Keep search and Notebook View selection in a compact control band. The ledger should contain Notebook Section headers and dense Design rows only.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook side panel
- Design Notebook Workbench view projection and commands
- Existing i18n string coverage
- Panel CSS module and design-system tokens

**Out of scope:**
- Changing persistence or the saved Design reference model
- Adding new Notebook Views or section concepts
- Changing row reorder behavior
- Implementing Remove from Notebook
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The subtitle All Designs under Design Notebook is removed.
- [ ] The count badge remains visible and accessible.
- [ ] Add Current Design and New Section become compact notebook-level actions in the command header.
- [ ] Search and All/Pinned view selection read as one compact control band.
- [ ] The ledger area contains only Notebook Section headers, empty section states, and Design rows.
- [ ] Styling follows the field-notebook design system without green UI chrome, nested cards, or raw CSS values.
- [ ] Focused panel tests cover the revised layout and existing add-current/create-section behavior.
close_reason: Refined Design Notebook into command header plus search/view control band and ledger; removed duplicate All Designs subtitle; verified with panel tests, full frontend tests, tsc, and Rust gates.

title: Add Remove from Notebook row overflow
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a row overflow action that removes a saved Design reference from the Design Notebook without deleting the underlying .canopi Design file.

## User Value
Users can clean up notebook entries directly from the side panel while keeping the Design file safe on disk.

## Out of Scope
Deleting .canopi files, adding Reveal in File Manager, adding undo toast behavior, or moving Pin/Move to Section into the overflow.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add Remove from Notebook as a row overflow action.

**Current behavior:**
Users can delete Notebook Sections, but they cannot remove an individual saved Design reference from the Design Notebook side panel.

**Desired behavior:**
Each Design Notebook row should expose a row overflow menu with Remove from Notebook. The action removes the saved Design reference from the Design Notebook immediately, without a modal confirmation, and must not delete the underlying .canopi Design file. Pin/Unpin and Add/Move to Section remain visible row actions rather than moving into the overflow.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook side panel row actions
- Design Notebook Workbench saved-reference removal command
- User DB saved Design reference removal behavior
- Design Session opening and active-path projection

**Out of scope:**
- Deleting .canopi files
- Adding Reveal in File Manager
- Adding undo toast behavior
- Moving Pin/Unpin or Add/Move to Section into the overflow
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Each Design Notebook row exposes an overflow action for Remove from Notebook.
- [ ] Remove from Notebook removes the saved Design reference from the notebook immediately.
- [ ] The underlying .canopi file is not deleted or modified.
- [ ] Pin/Unpin and Add/Move to Section remain visible row actions.
- [ ] Removing the active Design reference does not close or mutate the active Design Session.
- [ ] Focused backend/workbench/panel tests cover reference removal and file preservation.
close_reason: Added Remove from Notebook row overflow backed by a new reference-removal IPC command that preserves the .canopi file; verified with workbench, panel, backend tests, full frontend tests, tsc, and Rust gates.

title: Prioritize Design Notebook in the right rail
description:
## Slice Summary
Move the Design Notebook entry before plant-library side panels in the right rail while preserving Canvas and Location as the primary design workflow entries.

## User Value
Users can reach the app-level Design switcher before plant search and favorites surfaces, matching the Design Notebook's role as a workspace organizer.

## Out of Scope
Changing panel availability rules, changing icons, or redesigning the Design Notebook panel itself.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Move Design Notebook ahead of plant-library side panels in the right rail.

**Current behavior:**
The right rail groups Canvas and Location first, then shows plant-oriented side panels before the Design Notebook.

**Desired behavior:**
The right rail keeps Canvas and Location as the primary design workflow entries, then shows Design Notebook before Plant Database and Favorites. Design Notebook remains available from the Welcome Screen and retains its existing command graph behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- App Command Graph panel projection
- PanelBar right-rail projection
- Navigation command availability and active-state behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Changing PanelBar icons
- Changing panel availability rules
- Redesigning the Design Notebook side panel
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Right rail order is Canvas, Location, then Design Notebook, Plant Database, Favorites.
- [ ] Design Notebook remains enabled on the Welcome Screen.
- [ ] Existing navigation active states and disabled states still behave correctly.
- [ ] Focused tests cover the panel projection/order.
close_reason: Implemented right rail order as Canvas, Location, Design Notebook, Plant Database, Favorites; verified with panel-bar test, full frontend tests, tsc, and Rust gates.

title: Add Notebook Sections and one-section membership
description:
## Slice Summary
Let users create, rename, and delete Notebook Sections, and add or move a saved Design reference into at most one section.

## User Value
Users can organize saved Designs in their own notebook structure without moving or modifying the underlying .canopi files.

## Out of Scope
Many-to-many tags, filesystem folders, manual section or row ordering, and deleting underlying Design files.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add Notebook Sections with one-section membership for saved Design references.

**Current behavior:**
The Design Notebook can expose saved Design references, but users cannot create user-named Notebook Sections or place saved Design references into a section.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can create, rename, and delete Notebook Sections. A saved Design reference can belong to at most one user section at a time. Users can add an unsectioned or recent saved Design reference to a section, or move a sectioned reference to another section. Deleting a section leaves the underlying saved Design files alone and moves its entries to an unsectioned state that remains visible in All Designs and automatic views where applicable. Section names are user-owned editable text; Design names still come from the saved Design file.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook Workbench section CRUD and membership commands
- User DB persistence for Notebook Sections and one-section membership
- Notebook ledger section headers and row actions
- i18n for section actions, empty states, and inline confirmation copy

**Out of scope:**
- Many-to-many tags
- Manual ordering of sections or rows
- Deleting `.canopi` files
- Folder import or reveal-in-file-manager
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Users can create, rename, and delete Notebook Sections.
- [ ] Users can add or move a saved Design reference into at most one Notebook Section.
- [ ] Moving a reference to a new section removes it from the previous section.
- [ ] Deleting a section does not delete saved `.canopi` files and does not remove the saved Design references from All Designs.
- [ ] Section names are editable; Design names shown in rows still come from the saved Design file or reference metadata, not from a separate notebook label.
- [ ] Recent or unsectioned saved Design references can be added to a section without auto-creating sections.
- [ ] Focused tests cover section CRUD, membership cardinality, section deletion behavior, and localized user-visible copy.
close_reason: Shipped Notebook Sections with v5 user DB tables, one-section saved Design membership, snapshot IPC/workbench state, section create/rename/delete UI, move-to-section row controls, generated bindings, and focused Rust/frontend tests.

title: Add pinned Design references and Pinned notebook view
description:
## Slice Summary
Let users pin and unpin saved Design references and view pinned entries through a built-in Pinned notebook view.

## User Value
Users can keep important saved Designs one click away without relying on recency or section organization.

## Out of Scope
Notebook Sections, manual ordering, filesystem folder import, and separate notebook labels.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add pinned saved Design references and a Pinned notebook view.

**Current behavior:**
The Design Notebook can list saved Design references, but users cannot mark important Designs as pinned or switch to a focused Pinned notebook view.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can pin or unpin a saved Design reference from notebook rows. Pinned is a built-in Notebook View, not a Notebook Section. The pinned state persists in the user DB, survives restart, and does not modify the saved `.canopi` Design file. Pinned rows still open by row click through the normal Design Session action seam and active-path highlighting remains consistent across All Designs and Pinned views.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook Workbench pinned-state commands and projections
- User DB persistence for saved Design reference metadata
- Notebook view controls
- Design Session action seam for row opening

**Out of scope:**
- Notebook Sections and section membership
- Manual ordering of pinned rows
- Separate notebook labels
- File-system folders or tags
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Users can pin and unpin saved Design references from notebook rows.
- [ ] The panel exposes a built-in Pinned view that lists only pinned saved Design references.
- [ ] Pinned state persists across app restart and does not modify `.canopi` Design content.
- [ ] Row click opens a pinned Design through the same Design Session action seam as All Designs.
- [ ] Active-row highlighting works in both All Designs and Pinned views by saved path.
- [ ] Missing-path filtering and pruning semantics remain consistent for pinned entries.
- [ ] Focused tests cover pin/unpin persistence, Pinned view filtering, row opening, and missing-path behavior.
close_reason: Shipped pinned Design references with v6 user DB persistence, snapshot pinned field, pin/unpin IPC/workbench command, Pinned notebook view, row pin controls, generated bindings, and focused Rust/frontend tests.

title: Add inline Recent Designs to File menu
description:
## Slice Summary
Expose a small inline Recent Designs section in the File menu using the shared Design Notebook workbench state and the normal Design Session open-by-path flow.

## User Value
Users can quickly reopen one of their recent saved Designs from the top-bar File menu.

## Out of Scope
Submenus, command-palette recent entries, notebook organization controls, and changes to the native Open Design dialog.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add inline Recent Designs to the File menu.

**Current behavior:**
Recent Designs appear on the Welcome Screen, but the top-bar File menu only exposes New, Open, Save, Save As, and Exit. Users must go back to the Welcome Screen or native file picker to reopen a recent saved Design from app chrome.

**Desired behavior:**
The File menu includes an inline dynamic Recent Designs section directly below Open Design and before Save. It shows at most five Recent Designs and omits the section entirely when there are none. Selecting an entry opens that saved Design through the normal Design Session action seam and respects dirty-design confirmation. Recent Design menu entries are menu-only dynamic actions; they do not become command-palette entries or shortcut targets.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook Workbench Recent Designs projection
- App Command Graph menu/chrome projection
- MenuBar keyboard and click behavior
- Design Session action seam for open-by-path

**Out of scope:**
- Submenus or nested menu navigation
- Command Palette recent entries
- Notebook Sections, pinning, or manual ordering
- Native Open Design dialog behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] File menu renders up to five Recent Designs inline below Open Design and above the Save section.
- [ ] The Recent Designs section is omitted when the recent list is empty.
- [ ] Selecting a Recent Design opens it through the Design Session open-by-path action and preserves dirty-design confirmation.
- [ ] Recent Design entries are not included in the command palette and do not receive shortcuts.
- [ ] Menu keyboard navigation remains usable across static and dynamic entries.
- [ ] User-visible copy uses "Recent Designs" language and is localized across supported UI locales.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover populated, empty, click, disabled/dirty guard, and menu projection behavior.
