close_reason: Shipped inline File menu Recent Designs loaded from the Design Notebook workbench recent projection, capped to five, omitted when empty, opening through openDesignFromPath, with focused MenuBar/workbench/command/i18n tests.

title: Persist manual notebook ordering
description:
## Slice Summary
Add handle-based manual ordering for Notebook Sections and notebook rows, with persisted order that survives restart.

## User Value
Users can arrange their notebook in the order they expect instead of being forced into recency, alphabetical, or creation-time order.

## Out of Scope
Changing row click semantics, drag-to-canvas behavior, many-to-many tags, and file-system ordering.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Persist handle-based manual ordering for notebook sections and rows.

**Current behavior:**
Notebook Sections and rows can exist, but there is no user-controlled order that survives restart. Ordering is implicit or automatic.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can reorder Notebook Sections and saved Design rows using explicit handles. The manual order persists in the user DB and survives restart. Row click remains the primary open/switch action; drag handles are the only reorder affordance so navigation and organization do not conflict. Ordering applies to user-owned organization surfaces and should not imply file-system ordering or change `.canopi` Design content.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook Workbench reorder commands and ordered projections
- User DB order fields and transactional reorder updates
- Notebook ledger drag/reorder interaction
- Accessibility and keyboard/focus behavior for reorder handles where applicable

**Out of scope:**
- Dragging notebook rows onto the canvas
- Changing row click-to-open behavior
- Many-to-many tags or file-system ordering
- Reordering automatic Recent Designs by hand
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Users can reorder Notebook Sections with explicit handles.
- [ ] Users can reorder saved Design rows within user-owned organization surfaces with explicit handles.
- [ ] Manual order persists across app restart.
- [ ] Row body click still opens the saved Design; reorder drag starts only from a handle.
- [ ] Reorder updates are persisted atomically and do not modify `.canopi` Design files.
- [ ] Automatic Recent Designs remain recency-ordered unless explicitly represented in a user-owned section/order context.
- [ ] Focused tests cover persisted ordering, handle-only drag start, restart/readback ordering, and row click behavior.
close_reason: Added persisted manual ordering for Design Notebook sections and rows with handle-only panel reordering, user DB v7 migration, IPC/workbench support, generated bindings, and focused plus full quality gates.

title: Add Save current Design to Notebook affordance
description:
## Slice Summary
When the active Design is unsaved or not yet represented in the notebook, offer a compact panel affordance that saves it when needed and adds the saved Design reference to the notebook.

## User Value
Users can get the current Design into the notebook without manually finding it later, while unsaved Designs still never become non-reopenable notebook entries.

## Out of Scope
Autosave recovery, folder import, reveal-in-file-manager, and separate notebook labels.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add an affordance to save the current Design into the Design Notebook.

**Current behavior:**
Unsaved Designs have no saved location and therefore cannot be safely represented as notebook entries. Users must save manually and then rely on later notebook discovery or recent-list behavior.

**Desired behavior:**
When the current Design is unsaved or not represented in the notebook, the Design Notebook panel offers a compact affordance to add it. If the Design has no saved path, the flow runs Save As first; only after a successful save is a saved Design reference added to the notebook or selected Notebook Section. If the user cancels Save As, no notebook entry is created. The affordance should make the unsaved state clear without listing non-reopenable entries.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook Workbench add-current-design intent
- Design Session save/save-as action seam and current Design identity projection
- Notebook Section membership commands
- Panel header or compact callout UI

**Out of scope:**
- Autosave recovery
- Folder import
- Reveal-in-file-manager
- Separate notebook labels
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Unsaved Designs do not appear as notebook entries.
- [ ] The notebook panel shows a compact affordance when the active Design can be added to the notebook.
- [ ] If the active Design has no saved path, the flow runs Save As before adding a notebook reference.
- [ ] Cancelling Save As creates no notebook entry and leaves existing notebook state unchanged.
- [ ] A successfully saved Design can be added to the notebook and optionally placed into a selected Notebook Section.
- [ ] The affordance disappears or updates once the active saved path is represented in the notebook.
- [ ] Focused tests cover unsaved, save-cancelled, save-succeeded, already-added, and add-to-section flows.
close_reason: Added the current Design notebook affordance with save-before-add behavior and focused workbench/panel/i18n coverage.

title: Surface app-level Design Notebook with All Designs
description:
## Slice Summary
Add the first end-to-end Design Notebook path: an app-level right-bar panel that lists saved Design references in a searchable All Designs ledger and opens a clicked row through the normal Design Session replacement flow.

## User Value
Users can open the notebook from the right bar, including on the Welcome Screen, and switch between saved Designs without using the native file dialog.

## Out of Scope
Pinned Designs, Notebook Sections, manual ordering, File-menu Recent Designs, folder import, reveal-in-file-manager, and separate notebook labels.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the first app-level Design Notebook path with an All Designs ledger that opens saved Designs.

**Current behavior:**
Users can open saved Designs through the native Open Design flow and can reopen Recent Designs from the Welcome Screen. There is no app-level Design Notebook side panel, and saved Design organization is not represented in the right-bar panel model.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can open a right-bar Design Notebook panel even when no Design Session is active. The first version shows a searchable, dense All Designs ledger of saved Design references only. Clicking a row opens that Design through the normal Design Session replacement flow, keeps the notebook panel open, and highlights the active row by saved path. Missing-path handling matches Recent Designs: definitely stale paths are pruned or hidden, ambiguous or permission-blocked paths are hidden without deletion, and Notebook Sections are never deleted as a side effect. The UI follows the field-notebook design system: parchment/ochre chrome, ruled separators, tokenized spacing/type, no green UI chrome, and no two-pane file-manager layout.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Notebook Workbench as the frontend owner for saved Design references, search, active-path projection, refresh state, and open-by-path intent
- User DB persistence for saved Design references that survive app updates
- App Command Graph and PanelBar projection for app-level notebook navigation
- Design Session action seam for opening a saved Design by path

**Out of scope:**
- Pinned Designs and the Pinned view
- Notebook Sections and section membership
- Manual ordering
- File-menu Recent Designs
- Folder import, reveal-in-file-manager, and separate notebook labels
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The right bar exposes a Design Notebook button that is enabled on the Welcome Screen and while a Design Session is active.
- [ ] Opening the button shows a single searchable All Designs ledger of saved Design references, not a two-pane file-manager UI.
- [ ] Clicking a notebook row opens the saved Design through the Design Session action seam, preserving dirty-design confirmation behavior.
- [ ] The notebook remains open after switching Designs and highlights the active row by saved path.
- [ ] Unsaved Designs are not listed as notebook entries.
- [ ] Definitely missing saved paths are pruned or hidden; ambiguous or permission-blocked paths are hidden for the read without deleting user organization.
- [ ] The panel follows `.interface-design/system.md` tokens and avoids green UI chrome.
- [ ] Focused frontend and backend tests cover listing, missing-path handling, app-level panel availability, row click switching, and active-row projection.
close_reason: Shipped app-level Design Notebook All Designs panel with user DB saved-design references, missing-path pruning, command graph/PanelBar navigation, searchable ledger, row switching through openDesignFromPath, active-path highlight, and focused Rust/frontend tests.

title: Prune unavailable Recent Designs
description: On the Welcome Screen, Recent Designs should not show entries whose saved .canopi path is definitely no longer available. Missing paths should be removed from the persisted Recent Designs list; ambiguous filesystem errors should not delete the reference.
acceptance_criteria: Opening the app only displays Recent Designs whose saved path is available; definitely missing paths are pruned from the user DB; inaccessible/ambiguous filesystem errors are hidden without deletion; focused backend tests cover pruning and retention behavior; relevant checks pass.
close_reason: Implemented Recent Design pruning on the backend read path. get_recent_files now returns only available saved design paths, prunes definitely stale paths from the user DB, and hides ambiguous filesystem states without deleting them. Added the Recent Design glossary term and document-lifecycle guide note. Tests: CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_files::tests::recent_design -- --nocapture; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop db::recent_files::tests -- --nocapture; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_files::tests -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings.

title: Show Canopi title in Welcome Screen state
description: When the app opens with no active Design Session, the title bar should show the app title Canopi instead of the document fallback Untitled Design. After the user creates a new design, the title bar should show the editable localized Untitled Design name as it does for active designs.
acceptance_criteria: No active Design / Welcome Screen state displays Canopi in the title bar and does not expose design-name rename input. New Design creates an active Design Session whose title-bar name remains editable and displays localized Untitled Design for the internal Untitled fallback. Existing rename behavior and dirty-state behavior remain unchanged.
close_reason: Updated the title bar so the Welcome Screen/no-Design state shows non-editable Canopi while active Design Sessions still show editable localized Untitled Design. Added the Welcome Screen glossary term and focused title-bar coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- title-bar.test.tsx; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Hide clean ready Location Notice coordinates
description: Remove the always-on coordinate summary from the canvas Location Notice. A saved Location should still activate map-backed canvas presentation, but the Location Notice should only appear for map loading, errors, terrain degradation, or precision warnings.
acceptance_criteria: Canvas does not show a Location Notice for a ready map with no terrain error or precision warning. Loading, basemap error, terrain error, and precision warning notices still render. Saved Location data, Location Workbench coordinate fields, compass, map pin, and map activation remain unchanged.
close_reason: Removed steady-state coordinate summaries from the canvas Location Notice while preserving map activation and actionable loading/error/warning notices. Updated Location Notice glossary wording. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- location-notice-read-model.test.ts canvas-panel-feedback.test.tsx; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Align Measurement Guide labels using drafting convention
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Measurement Guide distance values follow architectural drafting dimension conventions: centered on the guide, aligned with the guide direction, upright for readability, and spaced cleanly away from the dashed dimension line.

## User Value
A designer placing Measurement Guides at any angle can read the distance immediately without awkward horizontal labels, visual crowding, or inconsistent spacing between interactive canvas and print/export renderers.

## Out of Scope
Do not add collision avoidance between multiple Measurement Guide labels, user settings for label orientation, endpoint or tick style changes, or persistence contract changes.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Render Measurement Guide distance labels using drafting-style aligned dimension placement.

**Current behavior:**
Measurement Guide distance values are always horizontal and positioned near the midpoint with a small fixed perpendicular offset. At angled guide orientations this does not match drafting conventions and the text can feel visually crowded against the guide.

**Desired behavior:**
Measurement Guide distance values should behave like architectural drafting dimension text. The value is centered on the Measurement Guide, aligned parallel to the guide direction, flipped when necessary so it remains upright/readable, and offset from the dashed line by a consistent screen-space clearance that accounts for label height. The same placement model should drive interactive canvas rendering and print/export rendering paths so Measurement Guides look coherent across renderers.

**Key interfaces:**
- Measurement Guide presentation model
- Canvas renderer Measurement Guide drawing
- Print/export Measurement Guide drawing path
- Renderer geometry and visual regression tests for Measurement Guides

**Out of scope:**
- Label collision avoidance between multiple Measurement Guides
- User-configurable label orientation or spacing
- Changes to Measurement Guide endpoint marks, dash style, layer behavior, selection behavior, or persistence
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Measurement Guide distance labels are centered at the guide midpoint and rotate parallel to the guide direction.
- [ ] Labels are flipped as needed so text is never upside down for ordinary left-to-right reading.
- [ ] Label spacing uses a stable screen-space clearance from the dashed guide line after accounting for label height, instead of crowding the line with a small center offset.
- [ ] Pixi and Canvas2D Measurement Guide renderers consume the same placement model and produce matching orientation/spacing behavior.
- [ ] Print/export canvas output uses the same aligned label convention as the interactive canvas.
- [ ] Focused tests cover horizontal, vertical, diagonal, and reversed-angle Measurement Guides, including upright flipping and spacing invariants.
- [ ] Existing Measurement Guide creation, selection, endpoint editing, layer visibility, and persistence behavior remain unchanged.
close_reason: Implemented drafting-style Measurement Guide labels: shared presentation now centers labels at the guide midpoint, rotates them parallel to the guide, flips reversed angles upright, and spaces labels by font height plus clearance; Canvas2D and Pixi renderers both consume that placement. Verified with npm test -- --run src/canvas/runtime/measurement-guides.test.ts src/__tests__/canvas2d-scene.test.ts src/__tests__/pixi-scene.test.ts, npm test, and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Refresh pin and unpin toolbar actions
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Selection Action Toolbar so the Pin Plant Name and Unpin Plant Name controls have distinct action identity and the visible button refreshes when selected plant pin state changes while the toolbar stays open.

## User Value
After pinning or unpinning selected plant names, the toolbar immediately presents the correct opposite action with the correct icon and localized label.

## Out of Scope
Do not change pinned-name persistence, renderer label placement, legend behavior, or the pin/unpin product rules.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Give Pin Plant Name and Unpin Plant Name toolbar actions distinct runtime identity.

**Current behavior:**
The Selection Action Toolbar can stay mounted while a selected plant's pinned-name state changes. Pin and unpin use the same action identity, so toolbar refresh logic can decide the visible button list has not changed and leave the old button, icon, or localized label in place.

**Desired behavior:**
The pin and unpin controls must have distinct action identities so a toolbar refresh swaps the DOM button to the currently available action whenever selected plant pin state changes. The user should see Pin when at least one selected placed plant is unpinned and Unpin when all selected placed plants are pinned.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection Action Toolbar action identity and refresh/reconciliation logic
- Pinned Plant Name selection command surface
- Localized action labels and button tooltips
- Tests that exercise toolbar updates without unmounting the toolbar

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the pin/unpin toggle rules
- Changing pinned-name persistence or renderer label behavior
- Changing the pinned-name legend or Design Report behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pin Plant Name and Unpin Plant Name use distinct Selection Action Toolbar action IDs.
- [ ] When selected plant pinned-name state changes while the toolbar stays open, the visible button swaps to the correct opposite action.
- [ ] The refreshed button uses the correct icon and localized label/tooltip.
- [ ] Existing pin/unpin behavior for mixed plant selections is preserved.
- [ ] Focused tests cover pin-to-unpin and unpin-to-pin refresh without requiring toolbar unmount/remount.
close_reason: Fixed Selection Action Toolbar pin/unpin refresh by giving Unpin Plant Name a distinct action id so mounted toolbar reconciliation swaps the visible button, label, and tooltip as selected plant pin state changes. Verified with npm test -- --run src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'refreshes the Selection Action Toolbar plant-name pin action', npm test -- --run src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts, and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Select Measurement Guide after creation drag
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix Measurement Guide creation so a guide created by dragging becomes the active canvas selection immediately after it is committed.

## User Value
After drawing a Measurement Guide, the user can immediately delete it, use Selection Action Toolbar affordances that apply to it, and see/edit its endpoint controls after returning to Select without first manually reselecting it.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new Measurement Guide editing behaviors, new toolbar controls, or persistence contract changes.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Select newly created Measurement Guides when creation commits.

**Current behavior:**
Creating a Measurement Guide by dragging adds the guide to the scene, but any previous canvas selection can remain active. Follow-up delete or toolbar actions may still target the stale selected object, and the newly created guide is not immediately ready for Select-tool endpoint editing.

**Desired behavior:**
When a Measurement Guide creation drag commits a real guide, the new guide becomes the active Design Object selection. If the drag is too small, blocked by Layer state, or otherwise produces no guide, selection should not change.

**Key interfaces:**
- Measurement Guide creation tool and Scene Edit transaction boundary
- Scene selection model for Design Objects
- Selection Action Toolbar and Measurement Guide endpoint-control presentation
- Undo/redo and dirty-state behavior for creation edits

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new Measurement Guide editing gestures
- Changing Measurement Guide persistence or layer semantics
- Changing selection behavior for other creation tools unless required for consistency tests
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Creating a Measurement Guide by drag selects the newly created guide after commit.
- [ ] If another object was selected before the drag, that stale selection is replaced only when a guide is actually created.
- [ ] No-op or blocked Measurement Guide creation attempts do not clear or replace selection.
- [ ] Delete and Selection Action Toolbar behavior after creation targets the new guide.
- [ ] Focused tests cover creation over an existing selection and the no-op/blocked creation path.
close_reason: Fixed Measurement Guide creation selection: created guides become the active selection only when a valid guide is committed; no-op and blocked attempts preserve the existing selection. Verified with npm test -- --run src/canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts -t 'Measurement Guide', npm test -- --run src/canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts, and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Fix French saved stamp action label
description: French selection action toolbar and canvas context menu currently say 'Enregistrer comme tampon' for saving a selected arrangement as a Saved Object Stamp. French product terminology for Saved Object Stamps should use planche/Planches; tampon is reserved for Plant Stamp/Object Stamp tools.
acceptance_criteria: French canvas.selectionActions.saveObjectStamp and canvas.contextMenu.saveObjectStamp say 'Enregistrer comme planche'; Plant Stamp and Object Stamp tool labels still use Tampon; focused i18n regression test covers the distinction.
close_reason: Fixed French Saved Object Stamp action terminology by changing selection-toolbar and context-menu labels from 'Enregistrer comme tampon' to 'Enregistrer comme planche' while keeping Plant Stamp/Object Stamp tools as Tampon. Added focused i18n regression coverage. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- i18n-completeness.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add artifact-based PDF visual checks for Design Report polish
description:
## Slice Summary
Add representative artifact-based regression checks for Design Report output so frame, scale, table, Unicode, page-density, and language invariants are protected across supported operating systems and computers.

## User Value
Future report changes are less likely to regress printing, localization, or layout polish on another platform.

## Out of Scope
Do not replace focused unit tests with full-page golden snapshots or require absolute paths to local user downloads.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Add durable Design Report PDF output checks for cross-platform, multilingual polish.

**Current behavior:**
Several report regressions were only found by inspecting a generated artifact: broken frame border, tiny canvas content, pipe-delimited Timeline text, sparse pages, awkward Consortium wrapping, and prior mojibake risk.

**Desired behavior:**
The test suite should include representative artifact-style checks that protect Design Report layout, Unicode text, all-locale labels, and page-density invariants across supported operating systems and computers without relying on brittle full-page golden snapshots or absolute local paths.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report export service and test fixtures.
- PDF text extraction or structured PDF inspection.
- PDF rasterization or bounded pixel/geometry checks where visual invariants are required.
- Locale coverage for supported UI languages.

**Out of scope:**
- Replacing all focused report unit tests.
- Requiring `/home/daylon/Downloads` fixtures or user-specific paths.
- Introducing broad new runtime dependencies unless justified by CI-portable report testing needs.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tests inspect or rasterize generated PDFs using tooling available in normal CI or a clearly documented CI subset.
- [ ] Checks cover first-page border continuity, white background, canvas content fill, and absence of excessive empty margins.
- [ ] Checks cover absence of pipe-delimited Timeline or Consortium table text.
- [ ] Checks cover absence of mojibake, replacement characters, raw translation keys, raw UUIDs, and raw hex color text in user-visible report sections.
- [ ] Checks cover page-density invariants so representative sparse sections do not create mostly blank generated pages.
- [ ] Coverage includes all supported UI locales or combines all-locale frontend input coverage with backend Unicode/render invariants and documents the rationale.
- [ ] The representative fixture is checked into the test suite or generated deterministically, without relying on absolute paths in a user's Downloads directory.
- [ ] Failure messages identify the broken invariant and report page or section.
close_reason: Added deterministic PDF artifact regression checks for Design Report polish: parsed PDF page density, first-page frame/image geometry, white-background no full-page paint, section text, pipe-dump absence, mojibake/raw-key/raw-color leaks, and page-specific failure messages. Tests: CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_preserves_representative_pdf_artifact_invariants_without_snapshots -- --nocapture; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_ -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Polish sparse Budget report pages
description:
## Slice Summary
Make sparse or zero-value Budget sections render as intentional, readable report content instead of mostly blank pages that look incomplete.

## User Value
A user with a small or preliminary Budget can export a Design Report that still looks polished and does not suggest missing information.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Budget item semantics, currency math, or Budget editing workflows in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make sparse Budget report sections look intentional and complete.

**Current behavior:**
A Budget section with one zero-value row can produce a page that is mostly blank, making the report look incomplete even though the Budget data is present.

**Desired behavior:**
Sparse Budget content should render as a compact, readable report section with clear totals and rows. Zero quantity, unit cost, and line totals should remain explicit without visually implying a failed export or missing data.

**Key interfaces:**
- Structured Design Report Budget input.
- Budget table layout, totals, currency formatting, and pagination.
- Locale resources for Budget labels and empty/sparse states.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Budget math, Budget editing behavior, or currency conversion.
- Hiding user-entered zero-value Budget rows.
- Combining unrelated report sections unless existing page composition already supports it cleanly.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A one-row zero-total Budget renders as an intentional summary/table rather than a mostly blank accidental-looking page.
- [ ] Zero quantity, unit cost, line total, and total are clearly represented.
- [ ] User-entered descriptions and Canonical Names remain present without awkward duplication or overemphasis.
- [ ] Dense Budget sections still paginate correctly with repeated headers.
- [ ] Tests cover one-row zero-total, totals-only, and multi-currency Budget cases in at least English and French.
- [ ] Layout assertions are deterministic across operating systems and font rendering differences.
close_reason: Polished sparse Budget Design Report pages by rendering zero-value rows inside ruled table output while preserving explicit zero values, descriptions, Canonical Names, and totals. Dense Budget pagination remains covered. Tests: CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_prints_zero_budget_rows_without_pipe_dump_or_blank_description -- --nocapture; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report::tests::renderer_ -- --nocapture; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Add cross-platform all-locale Design Report regression coverage
description:
## Slice Summary
Add regression coverage for representative Design Reports across all supported UI locales and platform-independent rendering invariants.

## User Value
Future report changes catch mojibake, missing glyphs, clipped text, blank pages, missing sections, and non-deterministic report resources before release.

## Out of Scope
Building a visual design-review approval workflow or adding screenshot baselines that cannot run reliably in CI.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Add cross-platform-oriented regression coverage for representative Design Reports across all supported UI languages.

**Current behavior:**
Existing report tests do not fully exercise the real failure modes seen in a dense multilingual Design Report: mojibake, missing glyphs, unreadable first-page canvas output, raw implementation values, sparse planning rows, and dense section pagination.

**Desired behavior:**
The test suite should include representative report fixtures and assertions that are portable in CI and meaningful across Linux, macOS, and Windows. Coverage should run through all supported UI languages and detect text corruption, missing report sections, internal-value leaks, blank pages, non-white first-page background, and layout regressions that can be checked deterministically.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report read model generation
- Shared Rust Design Report renderer
- Locale resources for all supported UI languages
- Representative dense Design fixture
- Portable PDF validation helpers

**Out of scope:**
- Pixel-perfect visual snapshot approval
- Manual human review workflow
- OS-specific renderer implementations
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A representative dense Design Report fixture exercises metadata, canvas image, legends, Timeline, Budget, Consortium, visible Layers, hidden Layers, and sparse planning data.
- [ ] Regression coverage runs against every supported UI language.
- [ ] Tests detect mojibake, missing glyphs, internal IDs/keys in user-facing output, blank pages, missing sections, and page-number failures.
- [ ] Tests verify first-page white-background behavior and absence of app chrome using deterministic assertions suitable for CI.
- [ ] Tests avoid brittle pixel-perfect snapshots unless they are stable across Linux, macOS, and Windows.
- [ ] The coverage runs in normal project gates or is clearly documented if a narrower CI-compatible subset is required.
close_reason: Added representative all-locale Design Report input coverage and backend PDF invariant coverage for nonblank pages, page numbers, embedded first-page canvas image, section presence, Unicode/mojibake checks, internal-value leaks, and non-snapshot chart/table rendering. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo test --workspace.

title: Include canvas legends and persistent aids in Design Report
description:
## Slice Summary
Ensure Design Report canvas output includes Pinned Plant Names, Measurement Guides, and the correct structured canvas legend behavior.

## User Value
The exported canvas page matches the persistent design presentation that the user intentionally configured.

## Out of Scope
Core report command creation and planning-section report layouts.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Include persistent canvas aids and legends in Design Report canvas output.

**Current behavior:**
After earlier slices, Pinned Plant Names, the pinned-name legend, and Measurement Guides exist in the canvas runtime, while the Design Report foundation exports a fitted canvas page.

**Desired behavior:**
The Design Report canvas page includes Pinned Plant Names and Measurement Guides as part of the visible design presentation. If Display is Default, Color by is None, and at least one pinned visible placed plant exists, the report includes the grouped pinned-name legend as structured report content near the canvas. If Color by is active, the report uses the Color by legend instead and does not also show the pinned-name legend. All legend and name text uses the active app locale at export time. The PDF is a snapshot; changing app language after export does not affect already-exported files.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report read model and PDF renderer boundary.
- Canvas print snapshot/render data.
- Pinned Plant Name and Measurement Guide query surfaces.
- Plant color/symbol presentation and localized common-name cache.

**Out of scope:**
- Adding a full pinned-plants table.
- Adding export-time layer or legend override controls.
- Timeline, Budget, and Consortium sections.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Design Report canvas output includes visible Pinned Plant Names.
- [ ] Design Report canvas output includes visible Measurement Guides with distance labels.
- [ ] The report includes the pinned-name legend under the same conditions as the canvas.
- [ ] The report uses the Color by legend instead of the pinned-name legend when Color by is active.
- [ ] Legend and plant-name text uses the active app locale at export time.
- [ ] Focused tests cover pinned labels, Measurement Guides, legend selection rules, locale snapshot behavior, and visible-layer interaction.
close_reason: Shipped Design Report canvas aids: localized pinned plant labels, visible Measurement Guides with distance labels, pinned-name legend selection, Color by legend precedence, locale snapshot behavior, and visible-layer filtering. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts display-legend.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Add Consortium section to Design Report
description:
## Slice Summary
Render Consortium information in the Design Report only when the user has changed entries from auto-created defaults, using a chart and complete table.

## User Value
A designer can print meaningful Consortium planning without noisy untouched defaults.

## Out of Scope
Timeline, Budget, Consortium editing changes, and changing auto-sync behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a print-native Consortium section to Design Reports.

**Current behavior:**
Consortium entries are auto-created for active species without marking the Design dirty. Untouched defaults should not make a report section appear.

**Desired behavior:**
The Design Report includes a Consortium section only when at least one active Consortium Entry differs from the default auto-created state. When included, the section shows the complete current active Consortium: a readable chart by Stratum and Succession Phase plus a complete table of active entries. The table includes localized plant name, canonical name where helpful, Stratum, start Succession Phase, end Succession Phase, and count where available. Labels use the active app locale. Untouched auto-created defaults do not count as user input, but once the section is included, all active entries are represented.

**Key interfaces:**
- Consortium Entry contract and Consortium Time Model.
- Consortium auto-sync defaults.
- Planning Projection for active entries, localized names, species colors, Strata, and Succession Phases.
- Design Report chart/table layout and pagination.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Consortium editing or auto-sync behavior.
- Budget and Timeline report sections.
- Adding user-managed Consortium lanes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Consortium section is omitted when all active entries are untouched auto-created defaults.
- [ ] The Consortium section appears when at least one active entry differs from the default state.
- [ ] When included, the section represents all active Consortium Entries, not only changed entries.
- [ ] The section includes a readable chart by Stratum and Succession Phase.
- [ ] The section includes a complete table with localized name, canonical name where helpful, Stratum, start/end phases, and counts where available.
- [ ] Labels use the active app locale.
- [ ] Focused tests cover default omission, changed-entry inclusion, complete active-entry coverage, chart/table data, and localization.
close_reason: Implemented Consortium Design Report section with default omission, complete active-entry chart/table payload, localized labels, PDF pagination, and focused frontend/Rust tests. Gates: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Polish complete Design Report output
description:
## Slice Summary
Audit the combined Design Report for pagination, repeated headers, localization, date/currency formatting, and no missing information across sections.

## User Value
A designer receives a clean, readable, polished PDF suitable for printing or sharing.

## Out of Scope
Adding new report sections or changing the already-approved feature scope.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Polish the complete Design Report output across sections.

**Current behavior:**
After the feature slices, Design Reports have the core canvas, metadata, persistent aids, Timeline, Budget, and Consortium sections, but cross-section polish still needs final verification.

**Desired behavior:**
The complete Design Report is clean, readable, localized, and suitable for printing or sharing. Section headings are consistent, table headers repeat after page breaks, page numbers are present, long text wraps without overlap, dates/currencies/numbers use the active locale, and no included section omits information. Empty sections stay omitted. The report respects visible layers, uses a white page/background, and suppresses UI chrome. Regression coverage should exercise a representative multi-section Design Report with dense enough content to force pagination.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report read model and PDF renderer boundary.
- Canvas page, metadata block, legends, Timeline, Budget, and Consortium sections.
- Active locale formatting and all added i18n keys.
- Report pagination and visual regression/test harness.

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new report sections.
- Changing the product scope of existing slices.
- Adding export-time customization controls.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A representative multi-section Design Report renders with no overlapping or clipped text.
- [ ] Section headings, page numbers, repeated table headers, and page breaks are consistent.
- [ ] Empty sections remain omitted while included sections contain all relevant information.
- [ ] Date, currency, number, section, table, action-type, Stratum, Succession Phase, and plant-name text uses the active locale.
- [ ] The report keeps white page/background behavior, respects visible layers, and suppresses UI chrome.
- [ ] Focused regression coverage exercises dense Timeline, Budget, Consortium, legends, metadata, and canvas content across multiple pages.
close_reason: Polished complete Design Report output with localized renderer labels, dense multi-section pagination regression coverage, printable text-position/line-length assertions, and long-token wrapping. Gates: npm test; npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Add Budget section to Design Report
description:
## Slice Summary
Render user-entered Budget information in the Design Report with readable tables, currency formatting, and totals.

## User Value
A designer can print or share entered Budget information without including empty/default-only sections.

## Out of Scope
Timeline, Consortium, Budget editing changes, and CSV export changes.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a print-native Budget section to Design Reports.

**Current behavior:**
Budget UI derives plant rows from the canvas and lets users enter Budget Items/prices. A derived zero-price plant table can exist even when the user has not entered Budget information.

**Desired behavior:**
The Design Report includes a Budget section only when the user has entered at least one Budget Item or budget value that differs from the default. The section uses a print-native table with readable species/manual target names, descriptions, quantities, unit costs, line totals, currencies, and grand totals. Long text wraps; totals and currencies are formatted with the active locale. The report must not include an auto-generated zero-price plant count table when the user has not entered Budget information.

**Key interfaces:**
- Budget Item contract and Budget Item Workbench/Planning Projection semantics.
- Target display and localized plant names.
- Active locale currency and number formatting.
- Design Report pagination and table layout.

**Out of scope:**
- Budget editing behavior.
- Budget CSV export changes.
- Timeline and Consortium report sections.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Budget section is omitted when the Design has no user-entered Budget information.
- [ ] The Budget section appears when at least one Budget Item or non-default budget value exists.
- [ ] The section includes all user-entered Budget rows and required totals.
- [ ] Species/manual target names, descriptions, quantities, unit costs, line totals, currencies, and grand totals are readable and not clipped.
- [ ] Currency and number formatting uses the active app locale.
- [ ] Focused tests cover empty omission, user-entered inclusion, totals, wrapping, and locale formatting.
close_reason: Shipped Design Report Budget section with empty/default omission, user-entered Budget Item rows, localized target names, locale-aware number/currency formatting, per-currency totals, repeated table headers, and paginated PDF rows. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Export Design Report foundation
description:
## Slice Summary
Add the File menu Design Report PDF export path with a fitted canvas page, metadata, white page/background, visible layers respected, and current in-memory Design Session state.

## User Value
A designer can export a printable Design Report even when there are unsaved edits, without losing visible canvas information.

## Out of Scope
Timeline, Budget, Consortium, pinned-name legend, and Measurement Guide report refinements.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the foundational File menu Design Report PDF export.

**Current behavior:**
The app has file actions and a platform-shaped canvas PDF export path, but there is no user-facing Design Report export that uses current Design Session state and structured report output.

**Desired behavior:**
The File menu exposes an Export Design Report PDF action. Export uses the current in-memory Design Session, including unsaved edits, and does not mutate or mark the Design clean. The initial report includes a fitted canvas page and compact metadata: Design name always, plus description and location when present. The first page orientation adapts to the fitted design bounds. The canvas page forces the page/background to white, suppresses UI chrome such as rulers, grid, editing handles, selections, hover states, and toolbars, and respects visible design and map layers. The report has section headings and quiet page numbers.

**Key interfaces:**
- App Command Graph and File menu.
- Design Session current-state composition.
- Structured Design Report read model.
- Chosen cross-platform PDF renderer boundary.
- Canvas export snapshot or print-render surface.

**Out of scope:**
- Timeline, Budget, and Consortium report sections.
- Pinned-name legend and Measurement Guide-specific report refinements.
- Export-time layer override controls.
- Saving the Design before export.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The File menu exposes an Export Design Report PDF action only when a Design can be exported.
- [ ] Export uses current in-memory Design Session state, including unsaved edits, without clearing dirty state.
- [ ] The report includes Design name and includes description/location only when present.
- [ ] The canvas page is fitted to the whole visible Design and chooses portrait or landscape based on design bounds.
- [ ] The canvas page has a white page/background, suppresses UI chrome, and respects visible design/map layers.
- [ ] The report includes section headings and page numbers.
- [ ] Focused tests cover command availability, current-state export input, metadata omission, orientation choice, visible-layer behavior, and dirty-state preservation.
close_reason: Shipped File menu Design Report PDF export using current Design Session state, structured report input, printpdf renderer, white fitted canvas page, visible-layer handling, metadata omission, headings/page numbers, and focused frontend/Rust coverage. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts commands-registry.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Add Timeline section to Design Report
description:
## Slice Summary
Render Timeline Actions in the Design Report with a print-native overview chart and authoritative wrapped table.

## User Value
A designer can print or share Timeline information without clipped descriptions, hidden actions, or scroll-dependent context.

## Out of Scope
Budget, Consortium, canvas export, and changing Timeline editing behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a print-native Timeline section to Design Reports.

**Current behavior:**
Timeline is presented as an interactive canvas in the app. That view can clip and scroll, which is unsuitable as direct PDF content.

**Desired behavior:**
When the Design has at least one Timeline Action, the Design Report includes a Timeline section. The section uses a print-native layout: a compact overview chart grouped by action type for scanning sequence/overlap, followed by an authoritative table containing every Timeline Action. Table rows wrap long text and include action type, description, start date, end date, recurrence, target, dependencies, and completed status. Page breaks are allowed between actions, repeated table headers preserve context, and no Timeline information is clipped or hidden. If the Design has no Timeline Actions, omit the Timeline section entirely.

**Key interfaces:**
- Timeline Action contract and Planning Projection read models.
- Target display and localized plant names.
- Active locale date formatting and action-type labels.
- Design Report pagination and table layout.

**Out of scope:**
- Timeline editing behavior.
- Budget and Consortium report sections.
- Changing Timeline data semantics.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Timeline section appears only when at least one Timeline Action exists.
- [ ] The section includes a compact overview chart grouped by action type.
- [ ] The section includes a complete table with every Timeline Action.
- [ ] Description, target, recurrence, dependency, and status text wraps without clipping.
- [ ] Date and action-type labels use the active app locale.
- [ ] Repeated table headers and page breaks preserve readability.
- [ ] Focused tests cover omission when empty, complete field coverage, wrapping/pagination behavior, and locale formatting.
close_reason: Shipped Design Report Timeline section with empty omission, localized report input rows, action-type overview rows, target/date/status/dependency fields, portrait Timeline PDF pages, row wrapping estimates, repeated table headers, and pagination across report pages. Tests: npm test -- design-report.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo test -p canopi-desktop services::design_report; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Edit and operate on Measurement Guides
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Measurement Guides behave as practical design objects for editing, layer visibility, locking, duplicate, and copy/paste.

## User Value
A designer can adjust and reuse Measurement Guides after creation while keeping them out of planning targets and stamp workflows.

## Out of Scope
Grouping, Object Stamp, Saved Object Stamp, Timeline targets, Budget targets, and Consortium participation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Make Measurement Guides editable and reusable as ordinary measuring aids.

**Current behavior:**
After the creation slice, Measurement Guides can be created and saved, but advanced post-creation operations are not yet complete.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can select a visible editable Measurement Guide, move the whole guide, drag either endpoint, delete it, duplicate it, and copy/paste it. Endpoint drags update the distance label live using snapped preview geometry when snapping is enabled. Layer visibility and locking block selection and mutation consistently with other design objects. Measurement Guides do not show a rotation handle in this slice because endpoint dragging provides the needed orientation control. They do not participate in Object Groups, Object Stamp, Saved Object Stamps, Timeline targets, Budget targets, or Consortium planning.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas selection and hit testing for design objects.
- Scene edit transactions, history, and mutation eligibility.
- Endpoint handle overlay and measurement-label rendering.
- Clipboard, duplicate, delete, and layer-lock behavior.

**Out of scope:**
- Grouping, stamping, saved stamp thumbnails, or planning targets for Measurement Guides.
- Design Report export.
- Adding rotation-handle support.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Users can select, move, delete, duplicate, and copy/paste Measurement Guides.
- [ ] Users can drag either endpoint, and the distance label updates live during the drag.
- [ ] Endpoint and move edits are undoable, abort cleanly, and avoid history entries for no-op gestures.
- [ ] Measurement Guide selection and mutation respect layer visibility, layer lock, and direct lock rules.
- [ ] Measurement Guides do not appear in Object Group, Object Stamp, Saved Object Stamp, Timeline, Budget, or Consortium workflows.
- [ ] Focused tests cover hit testing, move, endpoint drag, duplicate, copy/paste, deletion, locks, and out-of-scope workflow exclusion.
close_reason: Implemented Measurement Guide editing: select, move, endpoint drag with live labels, duplicate, copy/paste, delete, direct/layer lock handling, renderer selection/hover projection, and exclusion from grouping/object stamp/saved stamp affordances. Verified with npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, focused runtime/interaction/renderer/saved-stamp suites, and git diff --check.

title: Pin placed plant names on canvas
description:
## Slice Summary
Let users pin selected placed plant names so those names stay visible on the canvas and in saved designs.

## User Value
A designer can make important placed plant names persistently readable without turning them into Annotations or Design Object Locks.

## Out of Scope
Pinned-name legend behavior, Design Report export, and Measurement Guides.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add Pinned Plant Names for selected placed plants.

**Current behavior:**
Plant names can appear transiently through selection or hover presentation, but there is no persisted per-placed-plant presentation state that keeps names visible.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can use a Selection Action Toolbar button to pin or unpin the names of selected placed plants. The pinned state belongs to each individual placed plant and is saved with the Design. Pinned names remain visible during ordinary canvas use, update when the active language changes, and do not imply a Design Object Lock. If a selection includes plants plus other design objects, the action affects only the selected placed plants. If any selected placed plant is not pinned, the action pins all selected placed plants; if all selected placed plants are pinned, it unpins them. Duplicate preserves the pinned state; copy/paste, Object Stamp, Saved Object Stamp placement, and Plant Spacing create unpinned placed plants.

**Key interfaces:**
- Placed Plant persisted contract and file-format migration.
- Canvas selection and Selection Action Toolbar command surface.
- Canvas renderer and localized plant-name presentation cache.
- Duplicate, clipboard, Object Stamp, Saved Object Stamp, and Plant Spacing creation semantics.

**Out of scope:**
- Pinned-name legend behavior.
- Design Report export.
- Changing Design Object Lock behavior.
- Turning pinned names into Annotations or Timeline/Budget/Consortium targets.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A selected placed plant can be pinned and unpinned from the Selection Action Toolbar.
- [ ] Pinned Plant Name state is saved and reloaded with the Design.
- [ ] Pinned labels remain visible on the canvas and update after changing the app language.
- [ ] Mixed selections affect only selected placed plants and leave other selected design objects unchanged.
- [ ] The toggle pins all selected plants when any are unpinned and unpins all when all are already pinned.
- [ ] Duplicate preserves pinned-name state, while copy/paste, Object Stamp, Saved Object Stamp placement, and Plant Spacing create unpinned plants.
- [ ] Focused tests cover persistence, toggle behavior, locale refresh, and copy/duplicate/stamp/spacing semantics.
close_reason: Implemented pinned placed plant names with persisted v4 pinned_name state, Selection Action Toolbar toggle, localized always-visible labels, and copy/duplicate/stamp/spacing semantics. Tests: npm test; npx tsc --noEmit; npm run check:types; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Choose Design Report PDF renderer
description:
## Slice Summary
Choose the cross-platform renderer strategy for structured Design Report PDF export.

## User Value
Future implementation beads can build a consistent File menu export path instead of extending the current platform-stubbed canvas-snapshot PDF export.

## Out of Scope
Implementing the renderer, report UI, or report sections.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** decision
**Summary:** Choose the cross-platform PDF rendering strategy for structured Design Reports.

**Current behavior:**
Canopi has a native PDF export path shaped around a canvas snapshot and small layout metadata. That path is not a full structured Design Report, and platform support is uneven.

**Desired behavior:**
A human should choose the implementation strategy for generating structured, paginated Design Report PDFs consistently across supported desktop platforms. The decision should preserve the product rule that the report is built from structured report data: the canvas contributes a fitted white-background image that respects visible layers, while metadata, legends, Timeline, Budget, and Consortium content are structured PDF content with wrapping and pagination.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Report export command boundary from the File menu.
- Cross-platform PDF rendering dependency or platform service boundary.
- Structured report read model and canvas image input.
- Desktop packaging and build/release constraints.

**Out of scope:**
- Implementing the renderer or export command.
- Designing report section layouts beyond preserving the already-settled product decisions.
- Adding export-time layer override options.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A renderer strategy is selected and documented in the bead or a durable ADR.
- [ ] The decision explicitly covers Linux, macOS, and Windows behavior.
- [ ] The decision explains how structured text/tables and a fitted canvas image will be composed into one PDF.
- [ ] The decision states any accepted dependency, platform, packaging, or testing tradeoffs.
- [ ] Downstream implementation beads can proceed without another product or architecture decision about PDF generation.
close_reason: Selected printpdf as the shared Rust Design Report PDF renderer and documented the cross-platform strategy, rejected alternatives, structured composition boundary, font embedding requirement, and testing/packaging tradeoffs in docs/adr/0007-design-report-pdf-renderer.md. Tests skipped: docs-only decision.

title: Show pinned plant name legend
description:
## Slice Summary
Display a grouped legend for Pinned Plant Names when neutral plant display settings are active.

## User Value
A designer can read the symbol, color, and localized name for pinned plants without crowding the canvas legend.

## Out of Scope
Creating or persisting Pinned Plant Names and Design Report export.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Show a grouped legend for Pinned Plant Names under neutral display settings.

**Current behavior:**
The canvas legend appears for Color by modes. There is no legend for persistent pinned plant names.

**Desired behavior:**
When Display is Default, Color by is None, and at least one Pinned Plant Name exists, show a legend that groups pinned placed plants by localized display name, effective Plant Symbol, and effective plant color. Each row shows the symbol, color, localized name, and a count only when more than one pinned placed plant shares that exact visual entry. The legend updates dynamically as names are pinned/unpinned, colors or symbols change, plants are deleted, visibility changes, or the app language changes. If Color by is active, use the existing Color by legend instead of showing both legends.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas query surface for placed plants, localized names, effective plant color, effective Plant Symbol, and presentation mode.
- Existing canvas legend placement and scale-bar reservation.
- Plant Color and Plant Symbol presentation rules.

**Out of scope:**
- Creating or persisting Pinned Plant Names.
- Design Report legend rendering.
- Adding a full pinned-plants table.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The pinned-name legend appears only when Display is Default, Color by is None, and at least one pinned visible placed plant exists.
- [ ] Legend entries group by localized name, effective Plant Symbol, and effective plant color.
- [ ] Group counts appear only when the grouped count is greater than one.
- [ ] The legend updates when pinned names, colors, symbols, deleted plants, visibility, or locale changes.
- [ ] The existing Color by legend remains authoritative when Color by is active.
- [ ] Focused tests cover visibility conditions, grouping, counts, and locale updates.
close_reason: Implemented pinned plant name legend under neutral plant display settings with grouped localized name/symbol/color entries, dynamic counts, plant-layer visibility handling, locale updates, and Color By precedence. Tests: npm test -- display-legend.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Create persistent Measurement Guides
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a left-toolbar tool that creates saved Measurement Guides with dotted lines, end ticks, and always-visible distance labels.

## User Value
A designer can place durable measuring aids in a Design without misusing Linear Zones or Annotations.

## Out of Scope
Advanced Measurement Guide operations, grouping, stamping, and Design Report export.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a left-toolbar tool for creating persistent Measurement Guides.

**Current behavior:**
Canopi has Zone Measurements and temporary spacing/distance guides, but no saved straight measuring aid that is separate from Linear Zones.

**Desired behavior:**
Users can choose a left-toolbar Measurement Guide tool and drag from a start point to an endpoint to create a saved Measurement Guide. A Measurement Guide is a Design Object with two endpoints, a dotted line, small end ticks, and an always-visible distance label. It lives on its own fixed Measurement Guides layer, respects layer visibility and locking, participates in undo/redo and dirty state, and is saved/reloaded with the Design. Its distance label uses the same compact unit formatting rules as Zone Measurements and updates live while creating. Measurement Guides are not Zones, Spacing Guides, Annotations, planning targets, or ecological boundaries.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design file contract, generated bindings, and file-format migration.
- Scene state, history, dirty state, layers, and save/load composition.
- Canvas toolbar command graph and interaction tool modules.
- Renderer and measurement-label overlay behavior.

**Out of scope:**
- Post-creation endpoint editing and copy/duplicate/paste operations.
- Grouping, Object Stamp, Saved Object Stamp, Timeline/Budget/Consortium targeting.
- Design Report export.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A left-toolbar Measurement Guide tool creates a saved guide by dragging start to endpoint.
- [ ] Created guides render as dotted lines with end ticks and always-visible distance labels.
- [ ] Guide labels use the same distance unit formatting rules as Zone Measurements.
- [ ] Measurement Guides live on a fixed Measurement Guides layer and respect that layer's visibility and locking.
- [ ] Measurement Guides persist through save/load and migrate older Designs safely.
- [ ] Creation is undoable/redoable and marks the Design dirty only when a guide is actually created.
- [ ] Focused tests cover creation, rendering data, layer behavior, persistence, migration, and undo/redo.
close_reason: Implemented persistent Measurement Guides with toolbar command, scene persistence and v5 migration, Measurement Guides layer behavior, live and rendered distance labels, Canvas2D/Pixi rendering, undo/redo creation, generated bindings, and localized labels. Tests: npm test; npx tsc --noEmit; npm run check:types after staging; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Pack Consortium Entries into derived Consortium Lanes
description:
## Slice Summary
Make the Consortium tab pack Consortium Entries into derived Consortium Lanes so entries in the same Stratum can share a lane when their inclusive Succession Phase spans do not overlap.

## User Value
Designers can see more consortium planning in the same vertical space and can move a species-level Consortium Entry to a later phase without being forced into a separate lane when there is temporal room.

## Out of Scope
Do not persist lane numbers, add manual lane management, change the Consortium Time Model phase or Stratum definitions, or split Consortium Entries by individual Placed Plant.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Pack Consortium Entries into derived Consortium Lanes based on inclusive Succession Phase overlap.

**Current behavior:**
Consortium Entries in the same Stratum are presented as separate visual lanes even when their Succession Phase spans do not overlap. The visual lane effectively follows saved entry order, and same-Stratum vertical dragging can behave like manual lane reordering.

**Desired behavior:**
The Consortium tab should present derived Consortium Lanes. Within each Stratum, entries should use the fewest lanes that fit their inclusive Succession Phase spans. Entries whose spans do not overlap can share one lane. Entries whose spans overlap are presented in separate lanes. Saved consortium order remains a deterministic tie-breaker when multiple lane packings are possible, but lane numbers are not saved Design state and are not directly managed by designers.

Dragging a Consortium Entry vertically should choose the target Stratum, not a manual lane. Dragging or resizing should repack lanes during preview and after commit. Body dragging should preserve the entry phase-span length; resizing remains the way to change span length.

**Key interfaces:**
- Consortium planning projection: derives Consortium Entry presentation from Design consortiums, placed plants, localized names, and species colors.
- Consortium drag and resize interaction: previews non-canvas Design edits and commits them through Design Edit transactions.
- Consortium renderer and hit testing: consume derived lane layout without owning the Consortium Time Model or saved Design state.
- Consortium Time Model: owns Succession Phase order, Stratum order, clamping, and default entry timing.

**Out of scope:**
- Persisting lane IDs, lane indexes, or manual lane ordering in `.canopi` files.
- Changing Succession Phase labels, durations, Stratum order, or default Consortium Entry timing.
- Splitting Consortium Entries by individual Placed Plant.
- Adding a new list UI, popover, keyboard interaction, or explicit lane-management control.
- Changing unrelated Timeline, Budget, Target Presentation, or canvas scene behavior.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Non-overlapping Consortium Entries in the same Stratum share a derived Consortium Lane.
- [ ] Overlapping Consortium Entries in the same Stratum appear in separate derived Consortium Lanes.
- [ ] Phase-span overlap is treated as inclusive of both the start and end Succession Phase.
- [ ] Lane packing uses the fewest lanes per Stratum and uses saved consortium order only as a deterministic tie-breaker.
- [ ] Drag preview and commit repack lanes when a Consortium Entry moves between phases or Strata.
- [ ] Resize preview and commit repack lanes when a Consortium Entry span changes.
- [ ] Dragging vertically within the same Stratum no longer creates or preserves a manual lane assignment.
- [ ] No lane IDs, lane indexes, or manual lane ordering are added to saved Design state or shared contract types.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover projection packing, renderer row height or hit-test behavior where affected, and drag/resize repacking behavior.
- [ ] Required frontend gates pass: `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` plus focused Vitest coverage for the changed Consortium behavior.
close_reason: Implemented derived Consortium Lane packing in Planning Projection, removed manual same-Stratum lane reordering from Consortium drag previews, and added focused coverage for non-overlap sharing, inclusive overlap separation, deterministic tie-breaking, and drag/resize repacking. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- --run src/__tests__/consortium-renderer.test.ts src/__tests__/consortium-interaction.test.ts src/__tests__/frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test; git diff --check.

title: Make Plant Symbol menu a true two-row grid
description:
## Slice Summary
Make the Plant Symbol menu present the five Plant Habit Symbols on one row and the five abstract Plant Symbols on a second row, without wrapping either group into extra visual rows.

## User Value
A designer can scan the built-in Plant Symbol set as two coherent rows: habit symbols first, abstract symbols second. The menu feels intentional after adding groundcover and wave.

## Out of Scope
Changing the underlying Plant Symbol IDs, changing symbol artwork, changing canvas rendering, or changing Species Catalog growth-form data.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Make the Plant Symbol menu render as two intentional rows after the five-symbol habit and abstract sets.

**Current behavior:**
The Plant Symbol menu has five Plant Habit Symbols and five abstract Plant Symbols, but the visual grid still uses the older four-column sizing. Each semantic group can wrap into more than one physical row, making the popover feel accidental.

**Desired behavior:**
The menu presents the five Plant Habit Symbols in the first visual row and the five abstract Plant Symbols in the second visual row. The popover width, preview, spacing, and tests should all reflect the two-row layout without text overflow or awkward wrapping.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Symbol menu presentation and keyboard/pointer selection behavior.
- Plant Symbol glyph preview sizing inside the menu.
- Frontend tests that assert the menu offers the current built-in Plant Symbol set.
- Agent-facing frontend guidance if it documents the old row/column assumptions.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Plant Symbol IDs or defaults.
- Changing shrub, groundcover, or wave artwork.
- Changing canvas renderer behavior.
- Changing Species Catalog fields or filtering.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Plant Symbol menu renders exactly two visual symbol rows: five Plant Habit Symbols on the first row and five abstract Plant Symbols on the second row.
- [ ] The popover width, preview area, spacing, and focus states remain coherent with the existing Canopi field-notebook UI.
- [ ] Existing symbol selection behavior still works for both placed-plant overrides and species default symbol updates.
- [ ] Focused frontend coverage asserts the five-symbol row layout and the presence of groundcover and wave.
- [ ] Relevant agent docs are updated if they still describe the old four-by-two symbol set or four-column menu.
- [ ] Required frontend checks are run, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Implemented the true two-row Plant Symbol menu layout with five habit symbols and five abstract symbols, updated frontend agent docs, and verified with focused PlantSymbolMenu Vitest plus TypeScript check.

title: Land the chosen shrub and groundcover Plant Symbols
description:
## Slice Summary
Replace the shrub and groundcover Plant Symbol artwork with the agreed prototype direction: a filled puffy three-lobe shrub with a bottom line, and a groundcover made from three low curved runners from one bottom-center origin.

## User Value
A designer can choose shrub and groundcover symbols that are more recognizable on the canvas and coherent with the built-in Plant Symbol set.

## Out of Scope
Adding user-uploaded icons, changing placed-plant hit testing, changing the Visual Footprint boundary, changing plant-size behavior, or adding Species Catalog-derived symbol inference.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Replace shrub and groundcover built-in Plant Symbol artwork with the chosen prototype direction.

**Current behavior:**
The shrub symbol is an angular outline with a bottom line, and the groundcover symbol is a jagged low stroke. The symbols are functional but do not feel as coherent or recognizable as the rest of the built-in Plant Symbol set when shown at canvas scale.

**Desired behavior:**
The shrub symbol renders as a filled puffy three-lobe canopy with a bottom horizontal line, using fill behavior consistent with the tree symbol. The groundcover symbol renders as three low curved runners that start from the same bottom-center origin and spread left, slightly right/up, and right. Both symbols remain renderer-native Plant Symbol recipes inside the placed plant Visual Footprint.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Symbol recipe command contract.
- Plant Symbol glyph preview rendering.
- Pixi and Canvas2D canvas renderers for placed-plant Plant Symbols.
- Recipe bounds and renderer tests for Plant Symbols.
- ADR-0003: built-in Plant Symbols must remain renderer-native primitives without runtime SVG parsing, image textures, uploaded artwork, or a new icon dependency.

**Out of scope:**
- Runtime SVG parsing or storing raw SVG artwork in design data.
- Changing the stable Plant Symbol IDs.
- Changing circular hit testing, selection rings, stacking, bounds, zoom-to-fit behavior, or the Visual Footprint boundary.
- Changing Plant Symbol menu layout; that is tracked separately.
- Adding Species Catalog-derived symbol inference.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Shrub renders as the agreed filled puffy three-lobe canopy with a bottom horizontal line in the Plant Symbol menu, glyph preview, Pixi renderer, and Canvas2D renderer.
- [ ] Groundcover renders as three low curved runners from one bottom-center origin in the Plant Symbol menu, glyph preview, Pixi renderer, and Canvas2D renderer.
- [ ] The implementation uses renderer-native primitives and does not introduce runtime SVG parsing, image textures, uploaded icon support, or a new icon dependency.
- [ ] Plant Symbol recipe bounds stay within the approved Visual Footprint optical limit.
- [ ] Focused tests cover the updated recipes and any new recipe command support across SVG preview, Pixi, and Canvas2D behavior where local test seams exist.
- [ ] Required frontend checks are run, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Implemented the chosen shrub and groundcover Plant Symbol recipes with native curve-path rendering in SVG preview, Canvas2D, and Pixi, kept recipes inside the Visual Footprint, and verified with focused Plant Symbol tests, TypeScript check, and full frontend Vitest.

title: Make the Design name editable in the title bar
description:
## Slice Summary
Expose the Design name as editable title-bar text with localized fallback copy and standard text-edit confirmation behavior.

## User Value
A designer can double-click the title-bar Design name, get the whole visible name selected, type a new name, and confirm with Enter or by clicking elsewhere without seeing extra buttons.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change main Design open/save flows, file picker labels, recent file behavior, or document schema beyond the existing Design name value.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Make the Design name editable directly from the title bar.

**Current behavior:**
The title bar displays a non-editable name only for named Designs, hides the default name, and double-clicking the drag region can trigger window maximize behavior.

**Desired behavior:**
The title bar shows a localized visible fallback Design name when no user-owned name exists: English "Untitled Design" and French "Planche sans titre", with equivalent locale keys for all supported languages. Double-clicking the title text enters text edit mode and selects the complete visible name. Enter or blur confirms, Escape cancels, and no confirm/cancel buttons are shown. Confirming an unchanged fallback should not persist placeholder text or dirty the Design. Confirming a real changed name updates the Design name through the app-owned Design edit/session seam and marks the Design dirty as a normal non-canvas Design edit. Title text double-click must not trigger window maximize or drag behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Title bar Design name presentation
- Design Session name state
- Design dirty-state behavior
- Locale strings for visible fallback names

**Out of scope:**
- File picker behavior
- Recent files
- Main Design open/save command flow
- Native window title integration unless already owned by the existing title-bar seam
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The title bar shows a localized fallback Design name when no user-owned name is present.
- [ ] Double-clicking the title text enters edit mode and selects the full visible name.
- [ ] Enter and blur confirm; Escape cancels; no title-bar confirm/cancel buttons are shown.
- [ ] Confirming unchanged fallback text does not persist placeholder copy or dirty the Design.
- [ ] Confirming a changed real name updates the Design name and marks the Design dirty.
- [ ] Editing the Design name does not trigger title-bar drag or double-click maximize behavior.
- [ ] All user-visible fallback/edit labels are localized.
close_reason: Implemented editable title-bar Design name with localized fallback, select-all edit mode, no-button confirmation, unchanged fallback no-op, dirty real renames, and regression coverage in title-bar.test.tsx.

title: Add groundcover and wave Plant Symbols
description:
## Slice Summary
Add two stable built-in Plant Symbols: groundcover in the Plant Habit Symbol row and a compact tilde-like wave in the abstract row.

## User Value
A designer has more built-in marker shapes while keeping symbols visually consistent with existing renderer-native Plant Symbol recipes.

## Out of Scope
The wave symbol is not water, terrain, irrigation, or zone semantics. This slice does not add imported artwork, runtime SVG parsing, image textures, or new icon dependencies.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add groundcover and tilde-like wave as built-in Plant Symbols.

**Current behavior:**
The Plant Symbol popover offers four Plant Habit Symbols and four abstract symbols. There is no groundcover habit marker and no compact wave marker.

**Desired behavior:**
The Plant Symbol popover includes groundcover in the first Plant Habit Symbol row and a compact tilde-like wave in the abstract row below. Both symbols are stable built-in Plant Symbols with renderer-native recipes in every supported renderer path, localized labels/tooltips, and no semantic meaning beyond visual marker choice. The wave must look like a tilde-sized mark that fits the visual weight and grid rhythm of the existing symbols.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Symbol selection menu
- Built-in Plant Symbol identifiers and generated/shared contracts
- Plant Symbol renderer recipes
- Plant Symbol localization

**Out of scope:**
- Water, terrain, irrigation, or Zone semantics
- Imported artwork, runtime SVG parsing, or new icon dependencies
- Any change to Plant Symbol hit testing, Visual Footprint, grouping bounds, or canopy spread behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Groundcover appears in the Plant Habit Symbol row with localized labeling.
- [ ] Wave appears in the abstract Plant Symbol row as a compact tilde-like icon with localized labeling.
- [ ] Both symbols persist as stable built-in Plant Symbol identifiers.
- [ ] Both symbols render through native Plant Symbol recipes in all supported renderer paths.
- [ ] Existing Plant Symbol selection, species-default application, and mixed-selection behavior continue to work.
- [ ] Tests cover symbol availability and renderer recipe support.
