close_reason: Added stable groundcover and wave Plant Symbols through common-types, regenerated frontend known keys, added menu rows, native renderer recipes, localized labels, and focused menu/id/recipe tests.

title: Polish Saved Object Stamp rename mode
description:
## Slice Summary
Improve Saved Object Stamp inline rename so it behaves like a focused edit mode rather than a full row with every action still visible.

## User Value
A designer can rename a saved arrangement quickly: the current name is selected automatically, only confirm and cancel actions are shown during editing, blur or Enter confirms, Escape or cancel reverts, and the row spacing feels balanced with the reorder grip.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change Saved Object Stamp placement, export, import, delete confirmation, thumbnail previews, or Species favorites.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Make Saved Object Stamp rename mode feel focused and coherent with the Favorites side panel.

**Current behavior:**
Saved Object Stamp rename starts in place, but the user must manually select the existing name and row actions such as Place and Export remain visible while the name is being edited. The row spacing between the reorder grip and stamp name feels slightly loose.

**Desired behavior:**
When the designer starts renaming a Saved Object Stamp, the full current stamp name is selected automatically. During rename mode the row shows only confirm and cancel actions. Blur or Enter confirms a non-empty name, Escape or cancel reverts, and empty names still revert rather than persisting blank text. Confirm uses the approved localized green action affordance and cancel uses the approved localized red action affordance for this inline edit exception. The stamp name sits slightly closer to the six-dot reorder grip.

**Key interfaces:**
- Favorites side panel Saved Object Stamp rows
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench inline rename command
- User-visible Saved Object Stamp action strings

**Out of scope:**
- Saved Object Stamp placement behavior
- Saved Object Stamp import/export behavior
- Saved Object Stamp delete confirmation behavior
- Species favorites
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Starting rename selects the complete visible Saved Object Stamp name.
- [ ] Rename mode hides Place and Export and shows only confirm and cancel actions.
- [ ] Enter and blur confirm a non-empty rename; Escape and cancel revert.
- [ ] Confirm and cancel use the approved green/red inline-edit exception without changing unrelated UI chrome colors.
- [ ] Empty rename input reverts rather than saving a blank name.
- [ ] The stamp name/reorder grip spacing is visibly tighter and covered by a focused regression where practical.
close_reason: Implemented focused Saved Object Stamp rename mode: full-name auto-selection, rename-only confirm/cancel actions, green/red inline edit affordances, tighter grip spacing, and regression coverage in favorites-panel.test.tsx.

title: Make Saved Object Stamp dragging reorder live
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Saved Object Stamp manual ordering update visually while the designer drags the reorder grip, while committing persistence once when the drag completes.

## User Value
A designer can see the exact future order during drag instead of waiting until release to understand where the stamp will land.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change stamp body drag-to-place, canvas placement ghosts, import/export order semantics, or cross-panel drag behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Make Saved Object Stamp reorder dragging update visibly during the drag.

**Current behavior:**
Manual Saved Object Stamp ordering is only reflected after the drag completes, making the future drop position harder to read while the designer is dragging.

**Desired behavior:**
Dragging the reorder grip updates the visible Saved Object Stamp row order dynamically as the pointer moves across rows. The durable library order is committed once when the drag completes. Cancelling the drag or ending it outside a valid reorder target leaves the durable order unchanged or restored to its original order.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp reorder grip interaction
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench manual ordering command
- Favorites side panel visual row order

**Out of scope:**
- Dragging a stamp body to place it on the canvas
- Canvas placement ghosts
- Import/export semantics
- Cross-panel drag behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dragging a Saved Object Stamp reorder grip updates the visible row order before pointer release.
- [ ] The persistent Saved Object Stamp order is committed once when the drag completes.
- [ ] Cancelling or abandoning a reorder drag does not corrupt the saved order.
- [ ] Reorder behavior remains separate from stamp body drag-to-place behavior.
- [ ] Focused tests cover live preview order and final persistence behavior.
close_reason: Implemented live Saved Object Stamp reorder preview during drag with one workbench persistence call on drop, covered by favorites-panel.test.tsx.

title: Add Help > About Canopi
description:
## Slice Summary
Add an About Canopi command to the Help menu that opens an app-owned standard dialog with the Canopi icon, version number, license, and close behavior.

## User Value
A designer can find basic application identity and licensing information from the expected Help menu location.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not add diagnostic bundle behavior, release notes, updater behavior, native OS About integration, or external website links unless existing app patterns already provide them.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add an About Canopi command to the Help menu.

**Current behavior:**
The Help menu does not expose a standard About Canopi entry with application identity, version, icon, and license.

**Desired behavior:**
The Help menu includes an About Canopi command. Invoking it opens an app-owned standard dialog or window using existing app chrome patterns. The surface shows the Canopi icon, current application version, license, and a predictable close action. The command should fit the App Command Graph/menu architecture and keep user-visible strings localized.

**Key interfaces:**
- Help menu command projection
- App-owned dialog/window presentation
- Application version and license metadata
- Canopi icon asset

**Out of scope:**
- Release notes
- Updater behavior
- Diagnostic bundle behavior
- Native OS About integration unless already available through existing app infrastructure
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Help includes an About Canopi command.
- [ ] Activating About Canopi opens a standard app-owned dialog/window.
- [ ] The About surface shows the Canopi icon, version number, and license.
- [ ] The About surface has predictable close behavior and keyboard accessibility.
- [ ] User-visible About strings are localized.
- [ ] Tests cover command availability and dialog behavior.
close_reason: Added Help > About Canopi through the App Command Graph with an app-owned modal showing icon, version, license, and copyright, plus localized strings and command/dialog tests.

title: Keep Cancel from committing Saved Stamp rename drafts
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Saved Stamp inline rename flow so the Cancel rename action discards the current draft instead of letting input blur commit it.

## User Value
Designers can abandon an accidental Saved Stamp rename with the visible Cancel action without changing their library.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Saved Stamp placement, export, delete, import, or reorder behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** The Cancel rename action should discard Saved Stamp rename drafts.

**Current behavior:**
The rename input confirms on blur. When the dirty input has focus and the user activates the visible Cancel rename action, focus can leave the input first and the blur handler can send the draft to the rename command before the Cancel action runs.

**Desired behavior:**
Activating Cancel rename should discard the draft and keep the existing Saved Stamp name. Blur should still confirm ordinary non-empty edits, and Confirm/Enter should still save intentional edits.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Stamp row inline rename interaction
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench rename command
- Favorites panel behavior tests

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Save Selection, Import, Place, Export, Delete, or reorder behavior
- Changing Saved Object Stamp persistence or payload contracts
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A focused regression test proves the Cancel rename action discards a dirty draft without calling the rename command.
- [ ] Blur still confirms a non-empty rename draft in ordinary blur scenarios.
- [ ] Enter/Confirm still save non-empty rename drafts.
- [ ] Focused Favorites panel tests pass.
close_reason: Fixed Cancel rename so focus leaving the dirty input for the Cancel action discards the draft instead of blur-committing it; added focused FavoritesPanel regression coverage and verified with npm test -- favorites-panel.test.tsx.

title: Keep Escape from committing Saved Stamp rename drafts
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Saved Stamp inline rename flow so pressing Escape cancels the draft even when the input blurs as part of the key handler.

## User Value
Designers can safely abandon accidental Saved Stamp rename edits without persisting the draft.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Saved Stamp placement, export, delete, import, or reorder behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Escape should cancel Saved Stamp inline rename drafts without saving them.

**Current behavior:**
The rename input shares blur-to-confirm behavior with keyboard handling. Pressing Escape resets local state and then blurs the input, but the blur handler can still observe the stale non-empty draft and send a rename command.

**Desired behavior:**
Escape should leave the Saved Stamp name unchanged, exit rename mode, and never call the rename command for the discarded draft. Blur should continue to confirm non-empty drafts in ordinary blur scenarios, and Enter should continue to confirm.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Stamp row inline rename interaction
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench rename command
- Favorites panel behavior tests

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Save Selection, Import, Place, Export, Delete, or reorder behavior
- Changing the Saved Object Stamp persistence schema or payload format
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A focused regression test proves Escape cancels a dirty Saved Stamp rename draft without calling the rename command.
- [ ] Blur still confirms a non-empty rename draft.
- [ ] Enter still confirms a non-empty rename draft.
- [ ] Focused Favorites panel tests pass.
close_reason: Added focused FavoritesPanel regression coverage for Escape canceling Saved Stamp rename drafts; the current implementation already preserves the name and avoids the rename command, so no production code change was needed. Verified with npm test -- favorites-panel.test.tsx.

title: Clamp Saved Stamps frame after panel measurement changes
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Saved Stamps frame height projection so the frame remains within the Favorites panel when the panel height is known only after mount or changes later.

## User Value
The Favorites panel keeps both Plant favorites and Saved Stamps usable after initial render and panel resizing, instead of letting the Saved Stamps frame consume too much vertical space.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Saved Object Stamp library semantics, reorder behavior, import/export behavior, or unrelated panel layout.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Keep the Saved Stamps frame clamped to the measured Favorites panel height.

**Current behavior:**
The Saved Stamps frame height is resolved during render. On the initial render the panel element may not be available yet, and later panel height changes do not necessarily force a re-render. In those cases a persisted or default frame height can exceed the usable panel space and leave too little room for Plant favorites.

**Desired behavior:**
The Favorites panel should recompute the visible Saved Stamps frame height after the panel can be measured and whenever the panel size changes. The committed user preference should remain the user's requested frame height, while the rendered height is clamped so Plant favorites and Saved Stamps each keep their minimum usable height.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Stamps frame height preference in app settings state
- Favorites panel Saved Stamps frame rendering and resize behavior
- Existing Vitest coverage for Favorites panel frame resizing/clamping

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Saved Object Stamp placement, import/export, rename, delete, or reorder behavior
- Changing Species favorites data loading or presentation beyond the frame height interaction
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A regression test proves a Saved Stamps frame height is clamped after the Favorites panel becomes measurable or changes size.
- [ ] Drag resizing still previews the candidate height and commits the user preference only on drag completion.
- [ ] The rendered frame leaves at least the configured minimum height for both Plant favorites and Saved Stamps.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests for the Favorites panel pass.
close_reason: Fixed Favorites panel Saved Stamps frame clamping by observing panel size, subtracting non-frame chrome, and preserving the committed frame-height preference; verified with focused FavoritesPanel regression plus full frontend, typecheck, generated-binding, Rust check/clippy/fmt, and workspace tests.

title: Implement Saved Stamp spatial-signature thumbnail renderer
description:
## Slice Summary
Implement the recognition-thumbnail renderer that turns Saved Object Stamp payloads into compact spatial signatures optimized for recognition rather than literal downscaling.

## User Value
Dense Saved Stamps remain visually recognizable in the hover thumbnail without collapsing into unreadable dots.

## Out of Scope
Do not change exact placement ghost rendering or Saved Object Stamp payload persistence in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Render Saved Stamp recognition thumbnails as spatial signatures.

**Current behavior:** There is no reusable renderer that minifies Saved Object Stamp payloads into recognition-focused thumbnails.

**Desired behavior:** The renderer consumes a Saved Object Stamp payload and draws a compact spatial signature optimized for recognition, not literal downscaling. It preserves overall bounds and empty space, draws the largest two or three Zones, represents up to about 24 Plants as representative individual marks or clusters using dominant visual identity, and draws up to four Annotation strokes.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp payload parser/types
- Zone geometry helpers for oriented Zone shapes
- Plant symbol/color presentation helpers where practical
- Favorites thumbnail overlay from the blocker slice

**Out of scope:**
- Exact placement ghost rendering
- Saved Object Stamp payload persistence changes
- Storing precomputed thumbnail data
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Renderer derives thumbnails from Saved Object Stamp payload data at runtime; no persisted thumbnail data is introduced.
- [ ] Overall payload bounds and empty space are preserved in the thumbnail coordinate mapping.
- [ ] Largest two or three Zones are represented with rough shape/orientation.
- [ ] Dense Plant sets are capped at about 24 representative marks or cluster marks while preserving spatial spread and dominant visual identity.
- [ ] Annotations render as capped label strokes, not readable text.
- [ ] Sparse stamps still render individual objects recognizably.
- [ ] Focused tests cover dense plant payloads, sparse payloads, rotated Zones, annotation caps, and empty/invalid payload fallback.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck passes.
close_reason: Implemented runtime Saved Stamp spatial-signature thumbnails with tested sparse, dense, rotated-zone, annotation-cap, and fallback behavior. Checks: npm test -- saved-object-stamp-thumbnail-renderer.test.ts favorites-panel.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Implement dense Saved Stamp ledger rows
description:
## Slice Summary
Replace the existing Saved Stamp row/card layout with dense ledger rows that prioritize user-owned stamp names, use a six-dot reorder grip, and keep right-side icon actions visible.

## User Value
Designers can scan and manage Saved Stamps quickly without cramped names or a UI that feels detached from the rest of Favorites.

## Out of Scope
Do not add the hover recognition thumbnail renderer in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Implement dense Saved Stamp ledger rows.

**Current behavior:** Saved Stamp rows use text buttons and a layout that does not give enough priority to the stamp name or feel fully integrated with the Favorites panel.

**Desired behavior:** Saved Stamps render as dense ledger rows. The left affordance is a compact six-dot reorder grip that only reorders the library. The row body prioritizes the user-owned stamp name and puts composition counts in the secondary summary. Right-side icon actions are always visible in this order: Place, Export, Rename, Delete. Plain row clicks are inert; body drag starts drag/drop placement; the Place icon arms click-to-place mode.

**Key interfaces:**
- `FavoritesPanel`
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench commands
- Saved Object Stamp drag/drop source
- Existing rename/delete/reorder behaviors

**Out of scope:**
- Recognition thumbnail overlay
- Spatial-signature renderer
- Changing Saved Object Stamp payload shape
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Saved Stamp rows use a left six-dot reorder grip instead of generated codes such as `ST-01`.
- [ ] Reorder grip drag only reorders the library; row-body drag starts Saved Object Stamp drag/drop placement.
- [ ] Row name gets the dominant flexible track; composition counts appear in the secondary summary, not fixed metric columns.
- [ ] Right-side icon actions are always visible in order: Place, Export, Rename, Delete.
- [ ] Plain row click does not arm placement, select a stamp, or open details.
- [ ] Rename uses an in-place input; while renaming, Place and Export remain available, Rename becomes Confirm, Delete becomes Cancel, Enter confirms, Escape cancels, blur confirms non-empty names, and empty names revert.
- [ ] Delete uses inline two-step confirmation; while confirming, the row shows concise danger copy and only Confirm Delete / Cancel actions.
- [ ] Focused tests cover row actions, inert row click, drag/reorder separation, rename, delete confirmation, and export visibility.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck passes.
close_reason: Implemented dense Saved Stamp ledger rows with six-dot reorder grip, body-only placement drag, right-side icon actions, rename mode, and inline delete confirmation; covered behavior in Favorites panel tests and typecheck.

title: Persist Favorites split height
description:
## Slice Summary
Persist the Saved Stamps frame height as a per-user setting so the Favorites side panel can remember the split between Species favorites and Saved Stamps.

## User Value
Designers can resize the two Favorites frames once and have that layout return across app restarts without changing any Design file.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign Saved Stamp rows, add recognition thumbnails, or change Saved Object Stamp placement behavior in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Persist the Saved Stamps frame height as a user setting.

**Current behavior:** The Saved Stamps section uses a fixed max-height inside the Favorites panel and does not remember a user-adjusted split between Species favorites and Saved Stamps.

**Desired behavior:** The app stores the Saved Stamps frame height as a per-user settings preference. The default is 220px. At render time the Favorites panel clamps the value so Species favorites and Saved Stamps each keep at least 120px of usable height. Manual resize commits at drag end, not on every pointermove.

**Key interfaces:**
- Settings projection/persistence for user preferences
- Favorites side panel split state
- Resize handle behavior matching existing pointer-capture patterns

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning Saved Stamp rows
- Adding recognition thumbnails
- Changing `.canopi` Design save composition
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] User settings persist a Saved Stamps frame height preference; `.canopi` Design files do not gain this state.
- [ ] Default Saved Stamps frame height is 220px.
- [ ] Render-time clamping keeps both Species favorites and Saved Stamps at least 120px tall.
- [ ] Manual resize uses pointer capture, previews height during drag, and commits the preference only on pointerup/lost capture.
- [ ] Focused tests cover settings normalization/projection and Favorites split resize behavior.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck passes.
close_reason: Persisted Saved Stamps frame height in Settings, added the Favorites split resize interaction, and covered settings projection, panel resize, and Rust settings round-trip tests.

title: Add Saved Stamp recognition thumbnail overlay
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a fixed-size, visual-only recognition thumbnail overlay beside the Favorites panel, triggered from Saved Stamp row body hover or keyboard focus on row body/Place.

## User Value
Designers can recognize a Saved Stamp visually without placing it or crowding the dense ledger row.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the full spatial-signature minifying algorithm beyond a minimal usable payload-derived preview in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the Saved Stamp recognition thumbnail overlay owned by Favorites.

**Current behavior:** Saved Stamp hover/focus does not provide a visual recognition aid separate from placement ghosts.

**Desired behavior:** The Favorites panel owns a fixed-size, visual-only recognition thumbnail overlay beside the panel. It appears after delayed row-body hover or from keyboard focus on the row body/Place action. It does not appear from Export, Rename, Delete, inputs, delete-confirm controls, or the reorder grip. It does not duplicate row names, counts, metadata, or actions. The canvas runtime continues to own exact placement ghosts.

**Key interfaces:**
- Favorites panel UI state/lifecycle
- Saved Object Stamp payload parsing
- Overlay positioning and viewport clamping
- Saved Object Stamp exact placement ghost boundary

**Out of scope:**
- Full spatial-signature minifying algorithm
- Exact placement ghost changes
- Persisted thumbnail data
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Row-body hover shows a fixed-size visual-only thumbnail beside the Favorites panel after a short delay.
- [ ] Keyboard focus on row body or Place action shows the same recognition affordance.
- [ ] Export, Rename, Delete, inputs, delete confirmation controls, and reorder grip do not trigger the thumbnail.
- [ ] Thumbnail frame is roughly 180px by 150px and clamps to the viewport.
- [ ] Thumbnail does not duplicate stamp name, counts, metadata, or actions.
- [ ] Favorites panel owns the overlay lifecycle; canvas runtime exact placement ghost behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Focused tests cover trigger/hide behavior and viewport clamping.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck passes.
close_reason: Added a Favorites-owned Saved Stamp recognition thumbnail overlay with delayed row-body hover, Place focus trigger, viewport clamping, visual-only SVG preview, and tests for trigger/hide behavior.

title: Update Saved Stamp library copy and i18n
description:
## Slice Summary
Update user-visible Saved Object Stamp library labels, tooltips, and status text for the new Favorites frames and ledger row actions across all supported locales.

## User Value
The redesigned UI is fully localized, with French using planche/Planches for the saved arrangement library while preserving tampon for stamping tools.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Plant Stamp or Object Stamp tool semantics in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Localize the redesigned Saved Stamp library UI.

**Current behavior:** Saved Object Stamp copy predates the final Favorites-frame and ledger-row terminology, and French uses tampon language for the saved library.

**Desired behavior:** All user-visible strings for the redesigned Saved Stamps frame, row icons, rename/delete states, tooltips, empty/loading states, and errors go through i18n. French Saved Object Stamp library copy uses planche/Planches. Plant Stamp and Object Stamp tool interactions continue to use tampon.

**Key interfaces:**
- `desktop/web/src/i18n/*.json`
- Saved Object Stamp UI components
- Existing i18n completeness test

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Plant Stamp/Object Stamp tool behavior
- Redesigning row layout beyond copy wiring
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New or changed Saved Stamp frame/row strings are i18n keys, not inline literals.
- [ ] All 11 locale files contain the new keys and pass i18n completeness checks.
- [ ] French Saved Object Stamp library copy uses planche/Planches terminology where user-facing.
- [ ] French Plant Stamp and Object Stamp tool copy keeps tampon terminology.
- [ ] Focused tests or snapshots cover the key UI labels/tooltips that changed.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck and i18n completeness tests pass.
close_reason: Localized the redesigned Saved Stamp library row actions, resize label, and library copy across all 11 locales; French library copy now uses Planches/planche while Plant/Object Stamp tools keep Tampon. Checks: npm test -- favorites-panel.test.tsx i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Rework Favorites panel into sibling frames
description:
## Slice Summary
Turn the Favorites side panel into two coherent sibling frames, Plants and Stamps, separated by the persisted vertical split.

## User Value
The panel feels like one Favorites workspace rather than Species favorites with a pasted-on Saved Stamps widget.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement the final Saved Stamp ledger row actions or recognition thumbnail overlay in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Rework Favorites into two sibling frames: Plants and Stamps.

**Current behavior:** Species favorites own the Favorites panel body, and Saved Stamps read visually as a lower add-on section.

**Desired behavior:** The Favorites side panel presents Species favorites and Saved Stamps as sibling frames in one coherent Favorites workspace. The panel/navigation concept remains Favorites. The top frame is Plants, the lower frame is Stamps/Planches, separated by the persisted resize split. Favorite plant rows emphasize botanical name plus climate zone and life cycle for planning context.

**Key interfaces:**
- `FavoritesPanel`
- Species Catalog Workbench favorite rows
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench library view
- Per-user split-height preference from the blocker slice

**Out of scope:**
- Final Saved Stamp ledger row actions
- Recognition thumbnail overlay
- Changing Species Catalog search/result pages outside Favorites
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Favorites side panel renders Species favorites and Saved Stamps as sibling frames with coherent header rhythm, ruled borders, dense spacing, and field-notebook chrome.
- [ ] The panel/nav remains named Favorites, not Library.
- [ ] The split uses the persisted Saved Stamps frame height preference and honors min-height clamps.
- [ ] Favorite plant rows show botanical name as secondary text and climate zone plus life cycle as the primary scan metadata, not edibility ratings or USDA hardiness shorthand.
- [ ] If Species favorite row data lacks climate zone/life cycle fields, the slice extends the necessary frontend/backend contract and regenerates bindings in the same change.
- [ ] Focused Favorites panel tests cover frame structure, split sizing, and plant-row metadata.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck passes.
close_reason: Reworked Favorites into sibling Plants and Saved Stamps frames, routed plant favorites through a Favorites row variant, extended SpeciesListItem with climate/life-cycle metadata, and covered the UI plus species projection tests.

title: Document Saved Stamp thumbnail sizing decision
description: Capture the agreed Saved Object Stamp recognition thumbnail sizing and initial spatial-signature caps in agent docs.
acceptance_criteria: Frontend guidance records fixed-size recognition thumbnail defaults and initial minifying caps; user-owned dirty files remain untouched.
close_reason: Documented the agreed Saved Object Stamp recognition thumbnail default: fixed-size visual-only frame around 180px by 150px, viewport-clamped, with initial caps of largest two or three Zones, about 24 representative Plants or cluster marks, and up to four Annotation strokes. Checks: git diff --check.

title: Use climate and life cycle in Favorites plant rows
description: Refine the standalone Favorites/Saved Stamps prototype so favorite plant rows show climate zone and life cycle instead of edibility and USDA zone shorthand.
acceptance_criteria: Static HTML prototype opens directly from disk; Favorite plant rows show botanical name plus climate zone/life cycle; docs guidance captures the plant-row content decision; user-owned dirty files remain untouched.
close_reason: Updated the standalone Favorites/Saved Stamps prototype so favorite plant rows show botanical name plus climate zone and life cycle, replacing edibility and USDA hardiness shorthand. Added frontend guidance for Favorites plant rows. Checks: git diff --check; static HTML scan for stale edibility/USDA labels and banned font-weight 500/rgba/debug markers.

title: Give Saved Stamp ledger names more room in prototype
description: Refine the standalone Saved Stamp ledger prototype so stamp names get more horizontal space while keeping right-side actions and composition summary.
acceptance_criteria: Static HTML prototype opens directly from disk; Saved Stamp ledger rows allocate more room to stamp names; docs guidance reflects the updated row structure if needed; user-owned dirty files remain untouched.
close_reason: Refined the standalone Saved Stamp ledger prototype so stamp names have the dominant row track: removed fixed metric columns, moved composition counts into the secondary summary, kept six-dot grip and right-side icon actions. Updated frontend guidance to prioritize user-owned stamp names over fixed count columns. Checks: git diff --check; static HTML style scan for banned font-weight 500/rgba/debug markers.

title: Update static Saved Stamp prototype to agreed ledger design
description: Update the standalone Saved Object Stamp HTML prototype to the agreed single-direction design: Favorites sibling frames, B-style ledger rows, six-dot reorder grip, right-side icons including export, and visual-only recognition thumbnail.
acceptance_criteria: Static HTML prototype opens directly from disk and shows the agreed design direction only; docs decisions remain captured; user-owned dirty files stay untouched.
close_reason: Updated the standalone Saved Stamp prototype to the agreed ledger direction: Favorites has sibling Plantes/Planches frames with a resizable divider, B-style dense rows, six-dot reorder grips, always-visible Place/Export/Rename/Delete icon actions, inline rename/delete states, and a visual-only recognition thumbnail. Captured the resulting interaction decisions in docs/agent/frontend-patterns.md and docs/agent/canvas-runtime.md. Checks: git diff --check; rg scan on static HTML for banned font-weight 500/rgba/debug markers.

title: Add standalone Saved Stamp UI prototype HTML
description: Create a simple standalone HTML/CSS file showing the Saved Object Stamp UI prototype without requiring the full app shell or dev server.
acceptance_criteria: A static HTML file can be opened directly from disk and shows only the stamp UI prototype; it uses Canopi field-notebook styling and avoids app runtime dependencies; user-owned dirty files stay untouched.
close_reason: Added a standalone static HTML/CSS Saved Object Stamp UI prototype at desktop/web/prototypes/stamp-ui-prototype.html. It opens directly from disk, shows only the stamp UI variants, and uses right-side icon actions with Canopi field-notebook styling. Checks: git diff --check; rg style scan for banned font-weight 500/rgba/debug markers. No app tests run because this is static throwaway HTML.

title: Prototype Saved Object Stamp row UI variants
description: Explore bolder Saved Object Stamp row layouts inside the existing Favorites panel context. The prototype should make right-side icon actions, stronger stamp identity, and coherent field-notebook chrome easy to compare before production implementation.
acceptance_criteria: A throwaway UI prototype near the Favorites/Saved Stamps panel provides multiple structurally different variants switchable by URL/search-param controls; the prototype can be run locally with the existing frontend dev command; user-owned work remains untouched.
close_reason: Delivered a throwaway Saved Object Stamp UI prototype in the Favorites side panel with three URL-switchable variants: Rail row, Ledger strip, and Shelf label. The prototype uses local-only interaction state, right-side icon actions, and the existing frontend dev command. Checks: npx tsc --noEmit; npm test -- --run src/__tests__/favorites-panel.test.tsx src/__tests__/app-sidebar-width.test.tsx. Dev server: npm run dev, view ?stampPrototype=A|B|C.

title: Preserve effective Plant Symbols in Saved Object Stamps
description:
## Slice Summary
Saved Object Stamps should capture the Plant Symbol a designer actually sees, including symbols inherited from Design-level species defaults, so later placement/import/export does not depend on the target Design's defaults.

## User Value
Designers can reuse saved arrangements without Plant Symbols changing or falling back when the source used species-level Plant Symbol defaults.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Plant Symbol editing semantics, the Saved Object Stamp payload schema shape beyond stored values, or Species Catalog data.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Saved Object Stamps must preserve effective Plant Symbols, including species defaults.

**Current behavior:** Saved Object Stamp payloads store only a placed plant's explicit Plant Symbol. If the visible symbol comes from a Design-level species default, saving/importing/exporting the stamp stores `null`, so later placement can fall back to `round` or inherit a different target Design default.

**Desired behavior:** Saving or importing a Saved Object Stamp should capture the effective Plant Symbol visible on the source placed plant. Later placement should use that captured symbol explicitly so Saved Object Stamps are portable across Designs with different species defaults.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench payload normalization
- Saved Object Stamp `.canopi` import path
- Plant Symbol resolution between placed-plant explicit symbols and species defaults

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Plant Symbol edit commands or default-clearing semantics
- Changing Species Catalog data
- Adding new custom/uploaded symbols
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Saving a selected placed plant with no explicit symbol but a species default captures the effective Plant Symbol.
- [ ] Importing a `.canopi` file as a Saved Object Stamp captures effective Plant Symbols from `plant_species_symbols` when placed plants omit explicit symbols.
- [ ] Captured symbols remain explicit on later stamp placement/export.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover selection save and `.canopi` import behavior.
close_reason: Fixed Saved Object Stamp payload normalization to preserve effective Plant Symbols inherited from species defaults during selection save and .canopi import. Verified with focused Saved Object Stamp tests, TypeScript, Rust cargo check, and related Rust unit tests.

title: Import Saved Object Stamp anchors from Elliptical Zone bounds
description:
## Slice Summary
Importing a `.canopi` file as a Saved Object Stamp should derive the stamp anchor from visible design object geometry, treating an Elliptical Zone's second point as radii rather than a world coordinate.

## User Value
Designers can import arrangements containing Elliptical Zones and place them at the expected cursor/drop location.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Zone rendering, Zone editing, or the persisted Zone data format.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Saved Object Stamp import must derive anchors from visible design object geometry for Elliptical Zones.

**Current behavior:** Importing a `.canopi` file as a Saved Object Stamp flattens all Zone points into anchor calculation. For Elliptical Zones, the second point represents radii, not a world point, so the anchor can be pulled away from the visible geometry.

**Desired behavior:** Imported Saved Object Stamp anchors should be based on visible geometry bounds. Elliptical Zones should contribute their actual world-space bounds or center/radii-derived bounds, so placing the imported stamp aligns the visible arrangement with the cursor/drop anchor.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp import from `.canopi`
- Zone geometry semantics for Elliptical Zones
- Saved Object Stamp placement delta calculation

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the persisted Zone representation
- Changing Zone rendering or editing tools
- Changing Object Stamp sampling behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Importing a stamp with only an Elliptical Zone derives an anchor from the ellipse's visible bounds, not its radii point as a coordinate.
- [ ] Mixed imports with plants, annotations, and Elliptical Zones derive an anchor from combined visible geometry.
- [ ] Existing non-elliptical Zone import behavior remains intact.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover Elliptical Zone import anchor behavior.
close_reason: Fixed Saved Object Stamp .canopi import anchors for Elliptical Zones by deriving anchors from visible Zone bounds instead of treating radii as coordinates. Verified with focused Saved Object Stamp file tests, TypeScript, Rust cargo check, and related Rust unit tests.

title: Export Saved Object Stamp groups with valid zone member ids
description:
## Slice Summary
Exported Saved Object Stamp `.canopi` files must contain Object Groups whose typed Zone members point at the exported Zone names used by the file schema.

## User Value
Designers can export and re-import grouped Saved Object Stamps without losing Zone membership or creating stale Object Group references.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the Saved Object Stamp payload schema or Object Group semantics outside export composition.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Saved Object Stamp export must write Object Group Zone members using the Zone identifiers valid in the exported `.canopi` file.

**Current behavior:** Saved Object Stamp payloads carry an internal Zone id separate from the Zone display/name. Export composition writes Zones under their `name` field but leaves Object Group members pointing at the payload-internal Zone id. The resulting `.canopi` file can contain stale Object Group references.

**Desired behavior:** Exported Object Groups should preserve only members that resolve to exported objects, and Zone members should reference the exported Zone name used by the `.canopi` schema. A Saved Object Stamp exported and imported again should preserve valid captured Object Groups involving Zones.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp `.canopi` file composition
- Typed Object Group member contract for Placed Plants, Zones, and Annotations
- Saved Object Stamp import path from `.canopi`

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the Saved Object Stamp payload schema
- Changing Object Group flat typed membership semantics
- Changing normal Design save/load behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Exported `.canopi` Object Group Zone members reference exported Zone names, not payload-internal Zone ids.
- [ ] Exporting and importing a grouped Saved Object Stamp involving a Zone preserves valid Object Group membership.
- [ ] Invalid or missing members are still dropped rather than written as stale references.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover grouped Zone export/import round-tripping.
close_reason: Fixed Saved Object Stamp .canopi export so Object Group Zone members reference exported Zone names and round-trip through import. Verified with focused Saved Object Stamp file tests, TypeScript, Rust cargo check, and related Rust unit tests.

title: Fix Saved Object Stamp save availability for live and locked selections
description:
## Slice Summary
Saving a Saved Object Stamp should be available from the Saved Stamps panel and contextual canvas commands whenever the current design object selection is eligible, including directly locked design objects.

## User Value
Designers can save reusable arrangements immediately after selecting design objects, and directly locked source objects can still be captured as unlocked Saved Object Stamp copies as intended.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Saved Object Stamp storage, import/export, or placement behavior beyond selection availability.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Saved Object Stamp save availability must track live design object selection and allow directly locked source objects.

**Current behavior:** The Saved Stamps panel derives its save button state from a computed view that does not react to selection-only changes. Its save path also treats any blocked selection target as structural, so directly locked design objects that appear in the selection read model can be offered by contextual commands but then fail to save.

**Desired behavior:** The Saved Stamps panel updates when the canvas selection changes without requiring a scene edit. Saving should be allowed for editable design objects and directly locked design objects when there are no structural blockers. Locked sources must still be captured without lock state and placed later as unlocked design objects.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench selection view and save-current-selection behavior
- Canvas Runtime Surface selection query/read model
- Selection Action Toolbar and Canvas Context Menu Saved Object Stamp command availability

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Saved Object Stamp storage or payload schema
- Changing Design Object Lock semantics
- Adding new user-facing error handling for failed saves
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Saved Stamps panel save-selection state recomputes after a selection-only canvas interaction.
- [ ] Directly locked design objects are eligible Saved Object Stamp sources when no structural blockers are present.
- [ ] Contextual save commands and the workbench save path agree on eligibility for editable, locked, empty, and structurally blocked selections.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover live selection refresh and locked-source saving.
close_reason: Fixed Saved Object Stamp save availability so live selection changes refresh the panel state and directly locked design objects can be captured when no structural blockers are present. Verified with focused Saved Object Stamp Vitest coverage, TypeScript, Rust cargo check, and related Rust unit tests.

title: Fix Clippy len-zero baseline in user DB test
description: Clippy with -D warnings fails on a pre-existing len() > 0 assertion in desktop/src/db/user_db.rs. Repair it separately from feature beads so CI-parity gates can run cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: Clippy no longer reports clippy::len_zero for the user DB test assertion.
close_reason: Replaced pre-existing len() > 0 test assertion with is_empty() so CI Clippy -D warnings passes.

title: Export Saved Object Stamp as canopi file
description:
## Slice Summary
A designer can export one Saved Object Stamp as a valid `.canopi` file containing only the visible canvas objects and captured Object Groups needed for that stamp.

## User Value
Saved arrangements can be backed up or shared without inventing a new file format.

## Out of Scope
Importing stamps, exporting the full library as a bundle, or opening the exported file as part of Design Session save flows.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Export one Saved Object Stamp as a valid `.canopi` file.

**Current behavior:**
Saved Object Stamps are personal user DB entries with no portable export path.

**Desired behavior:**
Each Saved Object Stamp row/card exposes an export action. The frontend uses a save dialog for `.canopi`; Rust writes a valid minimal Design file containing only the stamp's visible placed plants, zones, annotations, and captured Object Groups, with safe defaults for required Design fields. Export must not route through Design Session save behavior, must not affect dirty state, and must not record Recent Designs.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench export command.
- Frontend save dialog and Rust file write boundary.
- CanopiFile composition for a minimal stamp export.
- Existing `.canopi` format serialization.

**Out of scope:**
- Importing stamps.
- Full-library bundle export.
- Design Session save/save-as integration.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Each Saved Object Stamp has an export action using a `.canopi` frontend save dialog.
- [ ] Exported file is a valid `.canopi` Design with safe defaults and only the stamp's visible canvas objects/groups.
- [ ] Export excludes Location, Budget, Timeline, Consortiums, description, lock state, notes, planted dates, quantities, and other non-visual metadata.
- [ ] Export does not mark the current Design dirty and does not record Recent Designs.
- [ ] Exported files can be imported back as Saved Object Stamps.
- [ ] Focused tests cover file composition, metadata exclusion, and no Design Session side effects.
close_reason: Shipped row-level Saved Object Stamp export to .canopi through a stamp-specific frontend dialog and Rust write command, with minimal file composition, metadata stripping, no Design Session/Recent Designs side effects, and focused frontend/Rust coverage.

title: Place Saved Stamp by armed click placement
description:
## Slice Summary
A designer can arm a Saved Object Stamp from the panel and click the canvas to place it with full ghost preview, one undoable Scene Edit, unlocked copies, and final selection of the newly placed objects.

## User Value
Saved arrangements become reusable on the canvas through an accessible click placement path that does not require native drag/drop.

## Out of Scope
Dragging from the panel to the canvas and `.canopi` import/export.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Place a Saved Object Stamp through an armed click placement mode.

**Current behavior:**
The library can store saved visible arrangements, but the canvas has no placement path for those entries.

**Desired behavior:**
A designer can click a place action on a Saved Object Stamp row/card to arm placement. While armed, the canvas shows full ghost copies of the stamp's plants, zones, annotations, and captured Object Groups in their real relative positions, anchored by the combined visual-bounds center. Clicking the canvas commits one Scene Edit transaction, creates unlocked ordinary Design Objects, preserves captured Object Groups with remapped members, selects the newly placed top-level objects, and returns to Select. Placement is all-or-nothing if any required target Layer is hidden or locked.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench placement intent.
- Canvas runtime command/tool surface for placement and preview.
- Scene Edit transaction and selection behavior.
- Layer visibility/lock guard behavior.

**Out of scope:**
- Native drag/drop from the panel.
- Import/export.
- Manual reorder behavior beyond not conflicting with placement.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Place action arms a Saved Object Stamp placement mode without requiring native drag/drop.
- [ ] Full translucent ghost copies preview the stamp at the snapped pointer/click location.
- [ ] Placement uses the combined visual-bounds center as the anchor and preserves relative geometry, colors, symbols, annotation text, Zone geometry, and captured group names.
- [ ] Placement is one undoable Scene Edit; undo removes all newly created objects and restores prior state.
- [ ] Newly placed objects are selected without creating a new Object Group, while captured Object Groups remain grouped.
- [ ] Placement is blocked all-or-nothing when any required target Layer is hidden or locked.
- [ ] Focused canvas interaction tests cover preview lifecycle, commit, undo, selection, grouping, and Layer guards.
close_reason: Implemented armed Saved Object Stamp placement from the panel with full ghost previews, one undoable Scene Edit, unlocked ordinary copies, preserved captured groups, final selection, and all-or-nothing Layer guards. Verified with focused interaction/runtime/workbench/panel/i18n tests, TypeScript, boundary tests, and full frontend Vitest.

title: Import canopi file as Saved Object Stamp
description:
## Slice Summary
A designer can import a `.canopi` file into the personal Saved Stamps library without opening or replacing the current Design Session.

## User Value
Saved Object Stamps are portable between installs and users while staying separate from whole-design import workflows.

## Out of Scope
Exporting stamps, opening imported files as Designs, or bulk library import.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Import a `.canopi` file as a Saved Object Stamp library entry.

**Current behavior:**
Canopi can open `.canopi` files as Designs and import Design Templates as whole Designs, but there is no way to add a `.canopi` file to the Saved Object Stamp library.

**Desired behavior:**
The Saved Stamps section exposes an import action that uses a frontend file dialog for `.canopi` files. Importing reads the file through a stamp-specific or lower-level format path that does not record Recent Designs, extracts only visible canvas objects and valid captured Object Groups, strips non-visual metadata and Design-level state, generates an initial library name from the Design name or default naming fallback, and adds a personal Saved Object Stamp entry. It must not open or replace the current Design Session, mark the current Design dirty, or trigger dirty-design guards.

**Key interfaces:**
- Frontend dialog boundary for file selection.
- Rust format loading without Recent Designs side effects.
- Saved Object Stamp payload normalization and user DB insert.
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench import status and error presentation.

**Out of scope:**
- Exporting stamps.
- Opening imported files as Designs.
- Bulk import or library merging UI.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Saved Stamps section has an import action using a `.canopi` frontend file dialog.
- [ ] Import creates a Saved Object Stamp library entry without replacing/opening the current Design Session and without marking the current Design dirty.
- [ ] Import does not record the file in Recent Designs.
- [ ] Imported payload includes only visible plants, zones, annotations, and valid captured Object Groups, with non-visual metadata stripped.
- [ ] Import name comes from the Design name with agreed fallback behavior.
- [ ] Tests cover successful import, invalid/empty import, no Recent Designs side effect, and no Design Session side effect.
close_reason: Shipped Saved Object Stamp import from .canopi through a frontend open dialog and no-recents Rust load command, with visible-only payload normalization, metadata stripping, Design Session side-effect guards, invalid/empty handling, and full frontend/Rust test coverage.

title: Manage Saved Stamp names deletion and order
description:
## Slice Summary
Add library management for existing Saved Object Stamps: inline rename, two-step delete, and manual order persistence through a dotted reorder grip.

## User Value
Designers can keep their personal Saved Stamps library tidy and ordered without losing entries accidentally.

## Out of Scope
Creating stamps, placing stamps, importing or exporting `.canopi` stamp files.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add rename, delete, and manual ordering to the Saved Object Stamp library.

**Current behavior:**
Saved Object Stamp entries can be created and shown, but the personal library has no management affordances.

**Desired behavior:**
The Saved Stamps section lets a designer rename an entry inline, delete an entry through a compact two-step confirmation state, and manually reorder entries with a dotted grip handle. The order persists in the user DB and is not changed as a side effect of placing a stamp. The dotted grip is only for panel reordering; dragging the stamp body remains reserved for canvas placement once that feature exists.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench for library state and commands.
- User DB/IPC update, delete, and reorder operations.
- Favorites side panel UI and i18n.

**Out of scope:**
- Saving new stamps.
- Canvas placement.
- Import/export.
- Library-level undo stack.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Saved Stamps can be renamed inline and persist after reload.
- [ ] Delete uses an inline two-step confirmation with cancel and does not use `window.confirm()`.
- [ ] Dotted grip reorder persists manual order in the user DB.
- [ ] Reordering uses a dedicated handle and does not start canvas placement.
- [ ] Placing or selecting a stamp does not reorder the library.
- [ ] Focused tests cover rename, delete confirmation, reorder persistence, and i18n-visible labels.
close_reason: Implemented Saved Stamp inline rename, two-step delete, persisted manual order APIs, draggable reorder grip, and focused Rust/frontend/i18n tests.

title: Drag Saved Stamp from panel to canvas
description:
## Slice Summary
A designer can drag a Saved Object Stamp body from the Saved Stamps section onto the canvas, see the full ghost preview during drag, and drop to place using the same semantics as armed click placement.

## User Value
The library feels direct and spatial: arrangements can be dragged from the right panel into the design.

## Out of Scope
Panel reorder drag behavior except preserving the existing dedicated reorder grip boundary.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Drag a Saved Object Stamp from the panel onto the canvas.

**Current behavior:**
Saved Object Stamp placement can be armed through an explicit place action, but the panel body cannot be dragged to place arrangements.

**Desired behavior:**
A designer can drag the body of a Saved Object Stamp row/card from the Saved Stamps section onto the canvas. The full ghost preview follows the canvas pointer using the same placement semantics as armed click placement, and dropping commits the same all-or-nothing Scene Edit. The dotted reorder grip remains dedicated to panel reordering and must not start canvas placement.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp drag payload serialization/parsing.
- Favorites side panel drag source behavior.
- Canvas dragover/drop handling and Saved Object Stamp placement command.
- Existing click-placement preview and commit behavior.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Species Plant Stamp drag/drop behavior.
- Reworking reorder mechanics beyond keeping drag responsibilities separate.
- Import/export.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dragging a stamp body starts a Saved Object Stamp canvas drag payload.
- [ ] Canvas dragover shows the full ghost preview and reports blocked state when placement cannot occur.
- [ ] Drop commits using the same semantics as armed click placement, including one undoable Scene Edit and final selection.
- [ ] The dotted reorder grip never starts canvas placement.
- [ ] Existing Plant Stamp drag/drop behavior remains covered and unchanged.
- [ ] Tests cover drag payload parsing, drag preview, blocked drop, successful drop, and reorder/placement drag separation.
close_reason: Shipped panel-body Saved Object Stamp drag/drop with full ghost preview, locked-layer blocking, placement selection semantics, and focused/full frontend test coverage.

title: Save selection into Saved Stamps section
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the first end-to-end Saved Object Stamp path: a user can save an eligible canvas selection into a personal library entry and see it in the Saved Stamps section below Species favorites.

## User Value
Designers can preserve a reusable visible arrangement of placed plants, zones, annotations, and captured Object Groups without treating it as a whole Design Template.

## Out of Scope
Inline rename/delete/reorder, canvas placement, drag/drop placement, import/export, and canvas toolbar/context-menu affordances beyond what is necessary to save from the panel.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Create the first end-to-end Saved Object Stamp save path and panel section.

**Current behavior:**
Canopi has Species favorites in the right Favorites side panel and transient Object Stamp/clipboard reuse on the canvas, but there is no personal library entry for saving a selected visible canvas arrangement for later reuse.

**Desired behavior:**
A designer can save an eligible canvas selection as a named Saved Object Stamp and see it in a Saved Stamps section below Species favorites. The entry is stored in the user DB, not the plant DB, settings JSON, or the current Design. The saved payload preserves only visible arrangement details for placed plants, zones, annotations, and captured Object Groups; it strips non-visual planning metadata and lock state. Directly locked selected Design Objects may be captured, but structural blockers such as hidden Layers, locked Layers, missing targets, and grouped members selected outside their Object Group are not eligible.

Default names are generated once at save time. Use the three most frequent selected plant species names at most, using localized Common Name with Canonical Name fallback; ties follow first appearance in the saved arrangement. If there are no plants, use Zone and Annotation counts.

**Key interfaces:**
- Saved Object Stamp domain term and ADR for personal user DB storage plus `.canopi` portability.
- User DB migration and CRUD IPC/service surface for a personal Saved Object Stamp library.
- Canvas query/selection surfaces for eligibility, selected object data, localized names, and visible arrangement normalization.
- Favorites side panel and a Saved Object Stamp Workbench for panel state.

**Out of scope:**
- Inline rename, delete, manual reorder, import/export, click placement, drag/drop placement.
- Adding a new PanelBar route.
- Embedding Saved Object Stamps into normal `.canopi` Design save composition.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] User DB migration creates dedicated Saved Object Stamp storage with stable id, name, payload JSON, sort order, created timestamp, and updated timestamp.
- [ ] Frontend can save the current eligible selection into the Saved Stamps section below Species favorites.
- [ ] Saved payload includes visible plants, zones, annotations, and captured Object Groups with remapped internal references, and excludes lock state, plant notes, planted dates, plant quantities, Zone notes, and Design-level state.
- [ ] Directly locked selected Design Objects can be saved as unlocked payload objects; structural blockers remain ineligible.
- [ ] Default names follow the agreed plant-name/count rules and do not auto-change after creation.
- [ ] Focused Rust and frontend tests cover persistence, eligibility, payload normalization, default naming, and panel rendering.
close_reason: Implemented Saved Object Stamp save path with user DB storage, IPC, generated contract, Saved Stamps panel section, selection normalization, and focused Rust/frontend tests.

title: Expose Save as Saved Object Stamp in canvas affordances
description:
## Slice Summary
Add Save as Saved Object Stamp to canvas-contextual affordances so users can save a selected arrangement from the Selection Action Toolbar and Canvas Context Menu.

## User Value
The feature is discoverable at the moment a reusable arrangement is selected, not only after the user opens the Favorites side panel.

## Out of Scope
Creating the underlying library persistence, panel list, or placement behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add Save as Saved Object Stamp to canvas contextual affordances.

**Current behavior:**
Users can save a selection only through the Saved Stamps panel once the base library exists, so the feature is not discoverable at the moment an arrangement is selected.

**Desired behavior:**
The Selection Action Toolbar and Canvas Context Menu expose Save as Saved Object Stamp for eligible selections. The action uses the same eligibility rules, default naming, payload normalization, and user DB save path as the panel save action. It is available for directly locked selected Design Objects that can be captured as unlocked copies, but remains unavailable for structural blockers such as hidden Layers, locked Layers, missing targets, and grouped members selected outside their Object Group.

**Key interfaces:**
- App Command Graph or canvas command surface used for contextual actions.
- Selection Action Toolbar.
- Canvas Context Menu.
- Saved Object Stamp Workbench save command.

**Out of scope:**
- Creating the base Saved Stamps section or persistence.
- Placement behavior.
- New global shortcuts unless explicitly added later.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Selection Action Toolbar exposes Save as Saved Object Stamp for eligible selections.
- [ ] Canvas Context Menu exposes Save as Saved Object Stamp with the same eligibility and disabled behavior.
- [ ] Both affordances call the same save path as the panel action.
- [ ] Directly locked selected Design Objects can be captured; structural blockers remain ineligible.
- [ ] Existing toolbar/context-menu actions retain their current behavior.
- [ ] Focused tests cover action visibility, disabled states, save dispatch, and no duplicated eligibility logic.
close_reason: Added Save as Saved Object Stamp to the Selection Action Toolbar and Canvas Context Menu through a shared contextual eligibility helper and runtime app-adapter command, preserving direct locked-object capture while disabling structural blockers. Verified with focused interaction tests, TypeScript, i18n/boundary tests, and full frontend Vitest.

title: Limit Plant Drag Distance Guides to two
description: While dragging a placed plant, show at most two nearest Plant Drag Distance Guides instead of three. Preserve current nearest-neighbor behavior, continuous recalculation during drag, and exclusion of dragged plants.
acceptance_criteria: Dragging a placed plant displays no more than two distance labels/lines to the nearest non-dragged placed plants, existing snapped drag behavior still works, and glossary language distinguishes Plant Drag Distance Guides from Plant Spacing Interval.
close_reason: Limited Plant Drag Distance Guides to the two nearest non-dragged placed plants during drag, preserving continuous nearest-neighbor recalculation and existing snapped drag behavior. Added glossary language distinguishing Plant Drag Distance Guides from Plant Spacing Interval. Tests: npm test -- --run src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'shows the two nearest non-dragged Placed Plant distances'; npm test -- --run src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Keep Elliptical Zone boundary hit tolerance screen-stable
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix Elliptical Zone boundary hit testing so the selectable and hoverable boundary remains within the intended screen-space tolerance across large ellipse sizes and high viewport scales.

## User Value
Users can reliably select, hover, and drag large Elliptical Zone boundaries at high zoom without boundary clicks missing because of a coarse polygon approximation.

## Out of Scope
Do not change rendered ellipse geometry, Zone selection priority, filled-interior selection behavior, or broad non-ellipse hit-testing semantics.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Keep Elliptical Zone boundary hit testing screen-space stable for large high-zoom ellipses.

**Current behavior:**
Elliptical Zone boundary hit testing approximates the ellipse with a fixed number of polygon segments. For large Elliptical Zones at high viewport scale, the approximation can drift farther from the rendered boundary than the intended screen-space hit tolerance, so clicks on visible boundary arcs can miss selection, hover, or whole-Zone drag start.

**Desired behavior:**
Elliptical Zone boundary hits should honor the same screen-space-stable tolerance as other Zone boundary hits regardless of ellipse size or zoom. A click on or near the rendered Elliptical Zone boundary should be considered a boundary hit within the configured tolerance, while interior clicks away from the boundary should continue to pass through according to existing Zone selection rules.

**Key interfaces:**
- Zone boundary hit testing
- Elliptical Zone geometry and rotation semantics
- Selection, hover, and whole-Zone drag intent that consume boundary hit results

**Out of scope:**
- Changing rendered ellipse geometry
- Changing the 6 px-style boundary tolerance contract for non-ellipse Zones
- Reintroducing filled-interior Elliptical Zone selection
- Broad hit-testing priority changes between Plants, Annotations, and Zones
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Large Elliptical Zone boundary hits at high viewport scale succeed for points on visible boundary arcs between coarse fixed polygon sample vertices.
- [ ] Elliptical Zone interior clicks away from the boundary do not become Zone hits.
- [ ] Rotated Elliptical Zone boundary hits continue to respect the ellipse orientation.
- [ ] Existing Plant and Annotation priority over Zone boundary hits is preserved.
- [ ] Focused regression coverage exercises the large high-zoom Elliptical Zone boundary miss through the public hit-testing or scene interaction path.
close_reason: Fixed Elliptical Zone boundary hit testing by adapting ellipse boundary polygon density to viewport scale and radius so chord sag stays under the screen-space tolerance. Added high-zoom large-ellipse regression coverage. Gates: cd desktop/web && npm test -- hit-testing.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Prevent no-op Zone Control Point taps from mutating zones
description:
## Slice Summary
Ensure pressing and releasing a Zone Control Point without meaningful drag movement does not reshape the Zone, update measurements, or create an undoable edit.

## User Value
Users can inspect or tap Zone Control Points without accidentally changing Zone geometry, even when snapping is enabled or the pointer lands inside the handle hit target rather than exactly on the visual mark.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign Zone Control Point affordances, snapping behavior during real drags, or Zone geometry editing beyond preserving no-op tap semantics.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Preserve no-op semantics when a user taps a Zone Control Point without dragging.

**Current behavior:**
A Zone Control Point press followed by release can apply the pointer-up position as a shape edit even when the pointer never moved past the drag threshold. With snapping enabled, or when the pointer lands inside the handle hit target but away from the visual point, the Zone can resize or move and an undoable edit can be committed from what should be a no-op tap.

**Desired behavior:**
Zone geometry, live measurements, and the undo stack must remain unchanged unless the pointer movement crosses the drag threshold for an actual Zone Control Point drag. Once a real drag starts, existing dynamic geometry and measurement updates should continue to work.

**Key interfaces:**
- Zone Control Point pointer gesture lifecycle
- Zone geometry mutation and commit behavior
- Dynamic Zone measurements during control-point drags
- Undoable scene edit transaction boundary

**Out of scope:**
- Changing which Zone Control Points are shown
- Changing snapping behavior for real drags
- Broad Zone editing architecture refactors
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Pressing and releasing a Zone Control Point without crossing the drag threshold leaves Zone geometry unchanged.
- [ ] A no-op Zone Control Point tap does not create an undoable scene edit.
- [ ] The no-op behavior holds when snapping is enabled and when the pointer starts inside the handle hit target but away from the visual mark.
- [ ] Real Zone Control Point drags still update geometry and measurements dynamically and still commit one undoable edit.
- [ ] Focused regression coverage exercises no-op taps through the public scene interaction path.
close_reason: Fixed Zone Control Point no-op taps by applying final geometry only after pointer movement crosses the drag threshold. Added public scene interaction regression coverage. Gates: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Add Elliptical Zone radius-handle dragging
description:
## Slice Summary
Single selected editable Elliptical Zones should expose four oriented cardinal Zone Control Points that resize width or height while preserving ellipse semantics.

## User Value
Designers can modify Elliptical Zone dimensions directly and see measurements update while dragging.

## Out of Scope
Sampled perimeter handles and rotation changes are out of scope.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Let designers resize Elliptical Zones with oriented cardinal radius Zone Control Points.

**Current behavior:**
Elliptical Zones can be selected, moved, rotated, and measured, but users cannot directly adjust their width or height from the canvas.

**Desired behavior:**
When exactly one editable top-level Elliptical Zone is selected in the Select tool, four oriented cardinal Zone Control Points appear at east, west, north, and south in the ellipse's local rotated space. Dragging east or west changes width; dragging north or south changes height; the opposite local side stays anchored; center and radius vector are derived from the dragged and anchored sides; rotation remains a Rotation Handle concern. Live Zone Measurements update from snapped preview geometry and the drag commits one undoable Scene Edit when geometry changed.

**Key interfaces:**
- Zone Control Point foundation
- Oriented Elliptical Zone geometry helpers
- Zone Measurement presentation
- Scene Edit transaction lifecycle

**Out of scope:**
- Sampled perimeter point editing
- Rotation changes through radius handles
- Converting Elliptical Zones to Polygonal Zones
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A single selected editable Elliptical Zone shows four oriented cardinal Zone Control Points.
- [ ] East/west handles resize width, north/south handles resize height, and the opposite local side stays anchored.
- [ ] Dragging handles preserves Elliptical Zone type and does not change rotation.
- [ ] Snapped preview geometry drives live width, height, and area Zone Measurements.
- [ ] Degenerate radii are clamped, rejected, or aborted rather than committed.
- [ ] The drag produces one undoable Scene Edit when geometry changed and no edit for no-op movement.
- [ ] Eligibility, overlay suppression, pointer priority, and cancellation behavior match the Zone Control Point foundation.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover unrotated and rotated ellipse resizing, live measurements, invalid geometry, and undo.
close_reason: Shipped Elliptical Zone cardinal Zone Control Point dragging while preserving ellipse semantics and live width/height/area measurements. Gates: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Make Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle Select-tool-only
description:
## Slice Summary
Selection overlays should appear only when they support Select-tool work and should clear stale tooltip state whenever hidden or refreshed.

## User Value
Designers can edit Annotations and use non-select tools without Selection Action Toolbar or Rotation Handle affordances blocking or confusing the workflow.

## Out of Scope
Zone Control Point implementation and Annotation double-click recognition are separate slices.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle visible only when they support Select-tool selection work.

**Current behavior:**
Selection overlays are driven by selection state alone. They can remain visible while the user is modifying an Annotation or using a non-select tool, and toolbar button tooltips can remain visible after prior hover or focus state.

**Desired behavior:**
The Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle are Select-tool-only selection affordances. They hide during Annotation inline editing, Canvas Context Menu interaction, active Design Object drag, active rotation drag, active Zone Control Point drag, and any non-select tool. Toolbar tooltip visibility is derived only from current hover or focus and is cleared whenever the toolbar hides, re-renders, the active tool changes, the selection changes, a command runs, or the toolbar is disposed.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame selection-dependent overlay lifecycle
- Selection Action Toolbar controller
- Rotation Handle controller
- CanvasQuerySurface design object selection read model

**Out of scope:**
- Implementing Zone Control Points
- Changing command availability semantics beyond overlay visibility and tooltip cleanup
- Changing Annotation text editing behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle are hidden for every non-select tool while preserving the current selection.
- [ ] Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle are hidden while Annotation inline editing is active and reappear only after commit/cancel when the Select tool and selection are eligible.
- [ ] Starting or canceling active selection-related drags hides/restores overlays through the shared selection refresh lifecycle.
- [ ] Toolbar button tooltips disappear on toolbar hide, re-render, blur, pointerleave, pointerdown/click, command dispatch, active tool change, selection change, and disposal.
- [ ] Focused keyboard activation still works for enabled toolbar buttons when the toolbar is visible.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover overlay visibility and stale tooltip cleanup.
close_reason: Shipped Select-tool-only Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle behavior, including Annotation edit/non-select hiding and stale tooltip cleanup. Gates: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts, npm test -- hit-testing.test.ts, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add Rectangular Zone corner dragging
description:
## Slice Summary
Single selected editable Rectangular Zones should expose corner Zone Control Points that resize the Zone while preserving rectangular geometry and rotation semantics.

## User Value
Designers can resize Rectangular Zones directly without converting them to free polygons.

## Out of Scope
Rotation remains owned by the Rotation Handle.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Let designers resize Rectangular Zones from corner Zone Control Points while preserving rectangular geometry.

**Current behavior:**
Rectangular Zones can be selected, moved, rotated, and measured, but their corners cannot be dragged to resize the Zone directly.

**Desired behavior:**
When exactly one editable top-level Rectangular Zone is selected in the Select tool, each corner appears as a Zone Control Point. Dragging a corner resizes the rectangle from the opposite corner in the Zone's local rotated space, preserves the Rectangular Zone type, keeps rotation as a Rotation Handle concern, updates dimensions and area live from snapped preview geometry, and commits one undoable Scene Edit when geometry changed.

**Key interfaces:**
- Zone Control Point foundation
- Oriented Rectangular Zone geometry helpers
- Zone Measurement presentation
- Scene Edit transaction lifecycle

**Out of scope:**
- Freeform polygon conversion
- Rotation changes through corner dragging
- Adding arbitrary rectangle side handles unless required for the corner behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A single selected editable Rectangular Zone shows corner Zone Control Points.
- [ ] Dragging a corner resizes from the opposite corner while preserving the Rectangular Zone type.
- [ ] Rotated Rectangular Zones resize in local rotated space without changing rotation.
- [ ] Snapped preview geometry drives live width, height, edge-length, and area Zone Measurements.
- [ ] Degenerate width or height is clamped, rejected, or aborted rather than committed.
- [ ] The drag produces one undoable Scene Edit when geometry changed and no edit for no-op movement.
- [ ] Eligibility, overlay suppression, pointer priority, and cancellation behavior match the Zone Control Point foundation.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover unrotated and rotated rectangle resizing, live measurements, invalid geometry, and undo.
close_reason: Shipped Rectangular Zone corner Zone Control Point dragging while preserving rectangular geometry and live measurements. Gates: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add Polygonal Zone vertex dragging
description:
## Slice Summary
Single selected editable Polygonal Zones should expose existing vertex Zone Control Points that can be dragged to reshape the Zone with live measurements.

## User Value
Designers can refine existing Polygonal Zone boundaries without redrawing the Zone.

## Out of Scope
Adding or deleting Polygonal Zone vertices is future behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Let designers reshape Polygonal Zones by dragging existing vertex Zone Control Points.

**Current behavior:**
Polygonal Zones can be selected and measured, but their existing vertices cannot be dragged to refine the boundary.

**Desired behavior:**
When exactly one editable top-level Polygonal Zone is selected in the Select tool, each existing vertex appears as a Zone Control Point. Dragging a vertex updates the Zone boundary with existing snapping rules, keeps the Zone polygonal, updates edge-length and area Zone Measurements live from snapped preview geometry, and commits one undoable Scene Edit when the drag changes geometry. Adding and deleting vertices remain out of scope.

**Key interfaces:**
- Zone Control Point foundation
- Polygonal Zone geometry and measurements
- Scene Edit transaction lifecycle

**Out of scope:**
- Adding vertices
- Deleting vertices
- Converting between Zone types
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A single selected editable Polygonal Zone shows Zone Control Points for existing vertices only.
- [ ] Dragging a vertex reshapes the Polygonal Zone without adding, deleting, or reordering vertices.
- [ ] Snapped preview geometry drives live edge-length and area Zone Measurements during the drag.
- [ ] Invalid or degenerate polygon geometry is clamped, rejected, or aborted rather than committed.
- [ ] The drag produces one undoable Scene Edit when geometry changed and no edit for no-op movement.
- [ ] Eligibility, overlay suppression, pointer priority, and cancellation behavior match the Zone Control Point foundation.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover vertex dragging, live measurements, invalid geometry, undo, and out-of-scope add/delete behavior.
close_reason: Shipped Polygonal Zone existing-vertex Zone Control Point dragging with live edge and area measurements. Gates: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Make Annotation double-click inline edit cross-platform
