description:
## Slice Summary
Existing text Annotations should enter inline editing from a deterministic Select-tool double-click recognizer instead of platform-dependent pointer detail behavior.

## User Value
Designers can reliably double-click editable Annotations to modify text on every supported platform.

## Out of Scope
Creating new Annotations and Zone reshaping are separate interactions.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Replace platform-dependent Annotation double-click editing with a deterministic recognizer.

**Current behavior:**
Existing text Annotation inline editing relies on pointer event detail to detect double-clicks. This can fail on some platforms, so double-click modify behavior is not reliable everywhere.

**Desired behavior:**
In the Select tool, a primary-button double-click on the same editable text Annotation within a short time and small movement threshold selects that Annotation and starts inline editing. The recognizer ignores additive selection gestures, drag/pan/rotation/control-point gestures, overlay interactions, locked or structurally blocked Annotations, hidden Layers, locked Layers, grouped members, and non-text Annotations. Enter and F2 remain keyboard edit entry points for a single selected editable text Annotation.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame pointer gesture lifecycle
- Annotation inline editor
- Design Object selection read model
- Hit testing for editable top-level Annotations

**Out of scope:**
- Creating new Annotations
- Editing locked or grouped Annotations
- Changing text commit/delete semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Double-clicking an editable text Annotation in the Select tool selects only that Annotation and opens the inline editor.
- [ ] Double-click recognition does not depend solely on PointerEvent.detail.
- [ ] Additive modifier double-clicks do not enter inline editing.
- [ ] Pointer movement beyond the drag threshold prevents inline editing.
- [ ] Locked, hidden, locked-Layer, grouped, blocked, and non-text Annotations do not enter inline editing.
- [ ] Enter and F2 still open inline editing for one selected editable text Annotation.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover cross-platform double-click recognition and blocked cases.
close_reason: Shipped cross-platform existing text Annotation inline edit activation with a deterministic two-click recognizer while preserving keyboard edit entry. Gates: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Select Zones by boundary proximity instead of filled interior
description:
## Slice Summary
Single-click Zone selection and hover should target Zone boundaries, not filled interiors, while preserving area-based band selection.

## User Value
Designers can select Placed Plants and Annotations inside Zones without the Zone stealing clicks, while Zones remain easy to select from their edges.

## Out of Scope
Zone Control Point reshaping is a separate slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make single-click Zone selection and hover boundary-based rather than fill-based.

**Current behavior:**
Rectangular, Polygonal, and Elliptical Zones can be selected from their filled interiors. This causes clicks intended for objects inside a Zone to select the Zone instead.

**Desired behavior:**
Single-click Zone hit testing uses screen-space boundary proximity. Rectangular, Polygonal, and Elliptical Zones are selectable only when the pointer is on or near their Zone boundary; Linear Zones continue to use near-line hit testing. Direct hits on Placed Plants and Annotations have priority over Zone boundary hits. Zone hover mirrors the same priority and does not highlight filled interiors. Band selection remains area/intersection-based for Zones.

**Key interfaces:**
- Top-level canvas hit testing
- Hover presentation for Design Objects
- Band selection geometry
- Zone geometry helpers for oriented Rectangular and Elliptical Zones

**Out of scope:**
- Zone Control Point reshaping
- Changing Zone rendering fill opacity or saved Zone geometry
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Interior clicks inside Rectangular, Polygonal, and Elliptical Zones no longer select the Zone when no boundary is nearby.
- [ ] Boundary-proximity clicks select Rectangular, Polygonal, Elliptical, and Linear Zones using a stable screen-space tolerance around 6 px.
- [ ] When a Placed Plant or Annotation overlaps a Zone boundary, the direct Design Object receives hover, selection, and drag priority.
- [ ] Whole-Zone dragging starts from boundary-proximity hits, not filled interiors.
- [ ] Band selection continues to select Zones by area/intersection.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover interior pass-through, boundary selection, object-over-boundary priority, hover, and band selection.
close_reason: Shipped boundary-proximity Zone hit testing with object-over-Zone priority and area-based band selection preserved. Gates: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts, npm test -- hit-testing.test.ts, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add Zone Control Point foundation for Linear Zones
description:
## Slice Summary
Single selected editable Linear Zones should expose endpoint Zone Control Points that can be dragged to reshape the Zone with live measurements and one undoable Scene Edit.

## User Value
Designers can modify existing Linear Zones directly instead of recreating them.

## Out of Scope
Polygonal, Rectangular, and Elliptical Zone-specific control points are follow-on slices.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the Zone Control Point interaction foundation through Linear Zone endpoint editing.

**Current behavior:**
Existing Zones can be selected and moved, but users cannot directly reshape a selected Zone by dragging its important points.

**Desired behavior:**
When exactly one editable top-level Linear Zone is selected in the Select tool, its endpoints appear as visually quiet Zone Control Points with touch-friendly hit targets. Dragging an endpoint reshapes the Linear Zone using existing snapping rules, updates Zone Measurements live from the snapped preview geometry, hides Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle during the drag, commits one undoable Scene Edit on pointerup when geometry changed, and aborts cleanly on Escape, tool switch, document replacement, disposal, or no-op movement.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame pointer gesture lifecycle
- Zone geometry and Zone Measurement presentation
- Scene Edit transaction lifecycle
- Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle overlay lifecycle

**Out of scope:**
- Polygonal, Rectangular, and Elliptical Zone-specific control point geometry
- Adding or deleting Zone points
- Changing Zone creation behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A single selected editable Linear Zone in the Select tool shows endpoint Zone Control Points immediately.
- [ ] Zone Control Points are not shown for multi-selection, Object Group selection, locked Zones, locked Layers, hidden Layers, grouped members, blocked selections, or non-select tools.
- [ ] Endpoint drags use existing grid/guide snapping and update the Linear Zone length measurement live.
- [ ] Endpoint drags commit one undoable Scene Edit only when geometry changed and abort cleanly on cancellation paths.
- [ ] Selection Action Toolbar, Rotation Handle, passive Hover Tooltip, hovered entity projection, and locked affordances hide during active Control Point drags.
- [ ] Zone Control Points stack above Zone Measurements, and measurements remain pointer-transparent.
- [ ] Mouse, pen, and touch pointer paths share the same behavior with usable invisible hit targets.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover visibility eligibility, dragging, live measurement updates, snapping, undo behavior, cancellation, and overlay suppression.
close_reason: Shipped Zone Control Point foundation through Linear Zone endpoint dragging with live Zone Measurements and one undoable Scene Edit. Gates: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Preserve flat typed Object Groups through reuse commands
description:
## Slice Summary
Make canvas reuse and transform commands preserve flat typed Object Groups end to end. Duplicate, copy/paste, delete, reorder, rotate, and Object Stamp should operate on cross-Layer groups by using typed concrete members and member-derived geometry/Layer rules.

## User Value
After a user creates a mixed Object Group, common canvas commands keep behaving predictably and do not corrupt membership, lose members, or revive legacy group-level Layer/transform authority.

## Out of Scope
Creating new grouping affordances or changing the basic grouping/merge eligibility rules already covered by earlier slices.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Preserve flat typed Object Groups through canvas reuse and transform commands.

**Current behavior:**
Reuse and transform commands were built around legacy group membership and group-level metadata assumptions. Mixed cross-Layer groups need those commands to use typed concrete members and member-derived geometry/Layer rules consistently.

**Desired behavior:**
Duplicate, copy/paste, delete, reorder, rotate, and Object Stamp operate on flat typed Object Groups without losing member identity, creating nested groups, relying on group-level Layer/position/rotation, or bypassing member Layer and lock rules. Cloned or stamped groups remap typed concrete member references to cloned members and keep group membership flat.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas clipboard and paste behavior
- Duplicate, delete, bring-forward, and send-backward Scene Edit commands
- Selection rotation behavior
- Object Stamp source sampling and placement
- Member-derived group bounds and Layer/lock eligibility

**Out of scope:**
- Creating basic mixed Object Groups
- Group merge behavior for selected existing groups
- New UI beyond existing reuse and transform commands
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Duplicate and copy/paste clone mixed Object Groups with remapped typed concrete members and no nested group membership.
- [ ] Delete, bring-forward, and send-backward operate on mixed Object Groups using resolved concrete members and existing scene order semantics.
- [ ] Rotation uses member-derived bounds and mutates concrete member geometry without relying on group-level rotation.
- [ ] Object Stamp samples and places mixed Object Groups with remapped typed concrete members while respecting member Layer and lock rules.
- [ ] Focused tests cover duplicate, copy/paste, delete, reorder, rotate, Object Stamp, and blocked source/target cases for cross-Layer groups.
close_reason: Implemented flat typed Object Group preservation through reuse and transform commands: copy/paste, duplicate, delete, reorder, rotation, Object Stamp, drag, hover, species edit guards, and render projections now use typed concrete members and member-derived bounds/Layers without group-level transforms. Covered by scene-runtime, scene-interaction, mutation, selection, full frontend, and Rust gates.

title: Derive Object Group Layer behavior from members
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Object Group visibility, selection eligibility, and mutation eligibility derive from the Layers of their concrete members. Cross-Layer Object Groups are editable only when every member Layer is visible and unlocked.

## User Value
Users can trust that grouped content is edited as a whole; Canopi does not allow partial group edits when some member Layers are hidden or locked.

## Out of Scope
Changing the persisted Object Group schema, adding new toolbar actions, or implementing merge behavior for selected Object Groups.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Derive Object Group visibility, selection, and mutation eligibility from member Layers.

**Current behavior:**
Object Group behavior can rely on a standalone group Layer. That model breaks down for cross-Layer groups and can make a group appear editable when some member Layers are hidden or locked.

**Desired behavior:**
Object Groups derive Layer behavior from their concrete members. A group is editable only when every existing member is on a visible, unlocked Layer and no member Design Object is locked. If any member Layer is hidden, the group should not be selected from canvas hit testing, and visible members remain structurally blocked from individual selection so users cannot edit part of a group. If any member Layer is locked, group mutation commands are unavailable and the group is treated as a structural blocker rather than an object-level Unlock target.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Query Surface Design Object selection model
- Runtime hit testing and band selection
- Scene Edit command eligibility
- Design Object lock and Layer lock rules

**Out of scope:**
- Persisted Object Group schema changes
- Adding new Group or Ungroup affordances
- Existing group merge behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Cross-Layer Object Group selection eligibility is derived from all existing concrete member Layers.
- [ ] A group with a hidden member Layer is not selected as a group from canvas hit testing, and visible members remain structurally blocked from individual selection.
- [ ] A group with a locked member Layer cannot be moved, rotated, deleted, duplicated, copied as a mutation source, ungrouped, or otherwise mutated.
- [ ] Groups blocked by locked member Layers do not show object-level Unlock affordances.
- [ ] Focused runtime tests cover visible/unlocked, hidden-member-Layer, locked-member-Layer, and locked-member-Design-Object cases.
close_reason: Implemented member-derived Object Group Layer behavior: selection, hit testing, hover, select-all, mutation eligibility, and lock handling now resolve group Layers from typed concrete members. Hidden member Layers block group selection while members stay structurally grouped; locked member Layers block mutation without object-level unlock affordances. Covered by selection, interaction, runtime, and full frontend tests plus Rust gates.

title: Group mixed Design Objects from the Selection Action Toolbar
description:
## Slice Summary
Allow editable top-level Placed Plant, Zone, and Annotation selections to become one flat Object Group from the Selection Action Toolbar, shortcut, and command graph. Group/Ungroup availability should come from the same flattened typed member rules.

## User Value
A user can group the real set of Design Objects they selected, instead of being limited to plants or same-Layer selections.

## Out of Scope
Merging selected existing Object Groups into one survivor, and reuse-command preservation such as Object Stamp or copy/paste.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Allow mixed Placed Plant, Zone, and Annotation selections to be grouped from normal selection actions.

**Current behavior:**
Grouping is exposed through commands and the Selection Action Toolbar, but current eligibility is constrained around plant or same-Layer behavior rather than the user's selected Design Objects.

**Desired behavior:**
The Selection Action Toolbar shows Group for editable top-level multi-selections whose flattened typed concrete member set has at least two members and whose member Layers are all visible and unlocked. The Group command creates a flat Object Group containing selected Placed Plants, Zones, and Annotations, and Ungroup selects the resolved concrete former members that are visible and editable. Shortcuts, menus, and toolbar actions dispatch through the same Scene Edit command behavior and use the same eligibility rules.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection Action Toolbar
- App Command Graph Group and Ungroup commands
- Canvas Scene Edit command surface
- Canvas Query Surface Design Object selection model

**Out of scope:**
- Merging selected existing Object Groups into one survivor
- Object Stamp, copy/paste, duplicate, rotate, or reorder behavior for mixed groups
- New group naming UI
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Selection Action Toolbar shows Group for eligible mixed Placed Plant, Zone, and Annotation selections.
- [ ] The Group command creates one flat Object Group with typed concrete members and selects the new group.
- [ ] Ungroup removes the selected Object Group and selects its resolved visible editable former members.
- [ ] Group/Ungroup toolbar actions, shortcuts, and command graph paths share the same command eligibility and no-op behavior.
- [ ] Focused frontend/runtime tests cover mixed selection grouping, blocked Layer eligibility, toolbar availability, shortcut dispatch, and ungroup selection.
close_reason: Implemented mixed Design Object grouping: the Selection Action Toolbar now exposes Group for unblocked mixed editable selections, and the Group/Ungroup command paths create/remove flat typed Object Groups across plants, zones, and annotations. Ungroup selects resolved visible editable former members. Covered by mutation, toolbar interaction, runtime, and full frontend tests.

title: Migrate Object Group persistence to typed concrete members
description:
## Slice Summary
Change the shared Design file contract so Object Groups store typed concrete members and no standalone Layer, position, or rotation authority. Legacy raw member IDs are migrated only when they resolve unambiguously to one Placed Plant, Zone, or Annotation.

## User Value
Designs with unambiguous groups continue to load, newly saved designs carry the healthier group model, and future grouping behavior is not blocked by ID collisions.

## Out of Scope
Toolbar behavior, group merge UX, reuse commands, and broader canvas interaction changes beyond what is needed to round-trip the new contract.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Move Object Group persistence to typed concrete members and remove standalone group Layer/transform authority.

**Current behavior:**
Object Groups persist raw member IDs plus group-level Layer, position, and rotation fields. Raw member IDs can collide across Placed Plant IDs, Zone names, and Annotation IDs, and the group-level fields can be mistaken for runtime authority even though group behavior should be member-derived.

**Desired behavior:**
Object Groups persist identity, optional name, lock state, and typed concrete members only. Legacy groups with raw member IDs load through a one-way migration that resolves a raw ID only when it matches exactly one Placed Plant, Zone, or Annotation; ambiguous and missing members are dropped, and groups with fewer than two resolved members are dropped. Newly saved Designs emit the new group shape.

**Key interfaces:**
- Shared Design file contract and generated bindings
- Object Group hydration and serialization
- Document load problem reporting for migration issues
- Scene snapshot and Design Session save/load round-trips

**Out of scope:**
- Selection Action Toolbar behavior
- Grouping existing groups from a selection
- Reuse commands such as Object Stamp, duplicate, copy, and paste
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Shared Rust and generated TypeScript Object Group contracts represent typed concrete members and no longer expose group-level Layer, position, or rotation as authoritative persisted fields.
- [ ] Loading legacy groups with raw member IDs resolves only unambiguous Placed Plant, Zone, or Annotation matches.
- [ ] Ambiguous or missing legacy group members are dropped, migration issues are reported through the document-load problem surface, and migrated groups with fewer than two members are dropped.
- [ ] Saving a Design with Object Groups writes typed concrete members and does not write standalone group Layer, position, or rotation fields.
- [ ] Focused Rust and frontend coverage verifies legacy migration, new round-trips, generated binding checks, and ambiguous ID handling.
close_reason: Implemented typed concrete Object Group persistence and migration: Rust CanopiFile now serializes groups as typed members only, legacy raw member IDs resolve only unambiguous concrete objects, dropped migration issues are logged for diagnostics, generated TypeScript bindings updated, and scene codec round-trips the new shape. Gates run: cargo test -p common-types legacy_object_groups_migrate_to_typed_concrete_members; cargo test -p canopi-desktop legacy_object_group_migration_report_identifies_dropped_members_and_groups; npm test; npx tsc --noEmit; cargo fmt --all -- --check; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace; CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test --workspace.

title: Merge existing Object Groups through Group
description:
## Slice Summary
When Group is invoked on a selection containing existing Object Groups, expand them into concrete members, deduplicate by typed identity, and merge into one flat Object Group. Preserve the single selected group identity when only one group participates; with multiple selected groups, keep the topmost selected group's identity.

## User Value
Users can add objects to existing groups and combine groups without ungrouping first or creating nested group structures.

## Out of Scope
Changing the underlying Object Group persistence contract or implementing unrelated group naming/renaming UI.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Let Group add objects to existing Object Groups by merging into flat membership.

**Current behavior:**
Grouping an existing Object Group with additional objects is either blocked or risks implying nested groups. Users have to ungroup and regroup to add objects, which is poor UX and can lose group identity.

**Desired behavior:**
When Group is invoked on a selection containing Object Groups, selected groups are expanded into concrete members, selected concrete Design Objects are added, and members are deduplicated by typed identity. If exactly one Object Group participates, preserve that group's identity, name, direct lock state, and selection. If multiple Object Groups participate, the topmost selected Object Group in scene order survives with its identity, name, and direct lock state; other selected group records are removed. The command proceeds only when the flattened concrete member set has at least two members.

**Key interfaces:**
- Group Scene Edit command
- Scene order for selected Object Groups
- Canvas Query Surface Design Object selection model
- Object Group membership and lock rules

**Out of scope:**
- Persisted Object Group schema migration
- New group naming or inspector UI
- Reuse command behavior beyond preserving valid merged group membership
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Grouping one selected Object Group with additional editable Design Objects adds the objects to that group while preserving the group's ID, name, direct lock state, and selection.
- [ ] Grouping multiple selected Object Groups merges all concrete members into the topmost selected group in scene order and removes the other selected group records.
- [ ] Grouping deduplicates concrete members by typed identity and no-ops when fewer than two concrete members remain.
- [ ] Grouping existing groups never persists nested Object Group members.
- [ ] Focused tests cover single-group merge, multi-group merge survivor selection, duplicate member selection, and blocked member Layer/lock cases.
close_reason: Implemented existing-group merge behavior: Group expands selected Object Groups into concrete typed members, deduplicates by typed identity, preserves the single selected group identity/name/lock state, and merges multiple selected groups into the topmost selected group while removing the others. Covered by mutation and toolbar interaction tests plus full frontend and Rust gates.

title: Deepen Canvas Runtime Surface construction
description: Architecture review found Canvas Runtime Surface is deep for app callers, but construction remains concentrated in SceneCanvasRuntime: store, camera, history, rendering, presentation, document lifecycle, mutations, interaction, and role surfaces are wired in one hub. Deepen construction and lifecycle wiring so role modules can evolve without routing new facts through a large constructor.
design: Keep CanvasCommandSurface, CanvasQuerySurface, and CanvasDocumentSurface stable for app callers. Move construction order, disposal order, and role-surface dependency assembly behind a dedicated runtime construction module. SceneCanvasRuntime may remain a compatibility shell during migration, but new behavior should not expand it as the knowledge hub.
acceptance_criteria: Runtime construction wiring is concentrated behind a smaller construction seam. Command, query, and document surfaces remain app-facing stable interfaces. Disposal ordering remains explicit and testable. Existing canvas runtime tests continue to build realistic runtime slices without casting raw SceneCanvasRuntime as role surfaces. Relevant agent docs are updated if the runtime construction contract changes.
close_reason: Shipped runtime construction seam behind canvas/runtime/scene-runtime/construction.ts while preserving command/query/document surfaces and explicit renderer/effect/document disposal wiring. Gates: cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit; cd desktop/web && npm test -- canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts planning-canvas-interaction-frame.test.ts timeline-interaction-frame.test.ts consortium-interaction.test.ts consortium-chart-hover.test.tsx; cd desktop/web && npm test -- frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npm test.

title: Deepen Planning Canvas Interaction Frame
description: Architecture review found the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame is still shallow: Timeline Action Canvas and Consortium adapters retain too much gesture lifetime, commit/abort ordering, stale identity cleanup, and Target Presentation sequencing. Deepen the frame so shared planning-canvas interaction behavior has one test surface while Timeline and Consortium keep surface-specific geometry and rendering.
design: The Planning Canvas Interaction Frame should own document listener lifetime, pointer/keyboard cleanup ordering, stale visible-item cleanup, Target Presentation hover/selection writes, and drag commit/abort timing through adapter-provided delegates. It must not own Timeline geometry, Consortium geometry, popover content, Canvas2D rendering, or raw Design Edit mutation details.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline Action Canvas and Consortium route shared planning gesture lifetime through the frame; adapters provide geometry and mutation delegates only. Existing Target Presentation semantics are preserved. Focused frontend tests cover listener cleanup, stale visible-item cleanup, and commit/abort ordering through the frame seam. Relevant agent docs are updated if the seam contract changes.
close_reason: Deepened the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame so active drag finish/abort ordering, stale cleanup, and Target Presentation cleanup live behind the shared frame seam. Timeline Action Canvas and Consortium now supply drag lifecycle delegates instead of owning active drag state. Verified with focused Vitest planning/timeline/consortium interaction coverage and cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Hide canvas selection overlays during Design Object drag
description:
## Slice Summary
When a Select-tool Design Object drag starts, hide selection transform overlays and clear passive hover presentation until the drag ends.

## User Value
Designers can drag selected Design Objects without the Selection Action Toolbar, Rotation Handle, or stale plant Hover Tooltip competing with the active drag feedback.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Rotation Handle drag behavior, band selection, panning, creation-tool drags, Selection Action Toolbar placement rules, or Plant Drag Distance Overlay behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Hide selection overlays and passive hover presentation while a Design Object drag is active.

**Current behavior:**
When a selected Design Object is clicked and dragged with the Select tool, the Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle can remain visible during the move. A plant Hover Tooltip can also remain visible after the user starts dragging a Placed Plant, competing with active drag feedback.

**Desired behavior:**
When a Select-tool Design Object drag is armed, immediately hide the Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle until the drag ends or is canceled. Restore those overlays only after scene and selection-dependent measurements refresh so their positions reflect the final geometry. At drag start, clear passive hover presentation, including Hover Tooltip, hovered entity projection, and locked-object affordance. Do not force the Hover Tooltip to reappear on pointer-up; it should return only on the next passive hover movement.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame pointer gesture lifecycle
- Shared Select-tool Design Object drag behavior
- Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle runtime overlays
- Hover Tooltip and hovered entity presentation
- Plant Drag Distance Overlay active drag feedback

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Rotation Handle drag behavior or its live rotation readout
- Changing band selection, panning, or creation-tool drag contracts
- Changing Selection Action Toolbar or Rotation Handle placement rules
- Changing Plant Drag Distance Overlay behavior
- Changing Design Object selection, locking, or mutation semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Select-tool dragging of a selected editable Design Object hides the Selection Action Toolbar as soon as the drag is armed.
- [ ] Select-tool dragging of a selected rotatable Design Object hides the Rotation Handle as soon as the drag is armed.
- [ ] Pointer-up after a moved Design Object restores the Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle using the final selection geometry.
- [ ] No-op pointer-up and cancel paths restore the Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle without creating extra Scene Edit history.
- [ ] Starting a Placed Plant drag hides the Hover Tooltip, clears hovered entity projection, and hides locked-object affordance.
- [ ] Hover Tooltip does not immediately reappear on pointer-up; it waits for the next passive hover movement.
- [ ] Rotation Handle drags, band selection, panning, creation-tool drags, and Plant Drag Distance Overlay behavior remain unchanged.
- [ ] Focused scene interaction tests cover overlay hiding/restoration and hover cleanup.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused scene interaction tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Shipped Select-tool Design Object drag presentation cleanup: Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle hide during active drags and restore on pointer-up/no-op/cancel, while Plant Hover Tooltip and passive hover projection clear at drag start. Verified with focused scene interaction tests, npx tsc --noEmit, full npm test, and git diff --check.

title: Scope Annotation inline-edit shortcuts to canvas focus
description:
## Slice Summary
Annotation inline-edit keyboard shortcuts should only run when the canvas interaction context has focus or the event target is part of the canvas surface.

## User Value
Designers can use Enter and F2 on app chrome, side panels, and Layer Panel controls without unexpectedly opening an Annotation editor on the canvas.

## Out of Scope
Do not remove keyboard access to inline Annotation editing, change Annotation selection rules, or alter unrelated global shortcut behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Restrict Annotation inline-edit keyboard shortcuts to canvas focus.

**Current behavior:**
When a text Annotation is selected, the window-level keyboard handler can treat Enter or F2 from any non-editable target as an Annotation inline-edit shortcut. This can intercept ordinary focused buttons or controls in app chrome, side panels, or the Layer Panel.

**Desired behavior:**
Enter and F2 should open the selected Annotation inline editor only when the keyboard event belongs to the canvas interaction context. Focused interactive controls outside the canvas should keep their normal activation behavior and should not be default-prevented by the canvas Annotation editor shortcut.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction keyboard event handling
- Annotation inline editor activation
- Canvas container focus and event target scoping
- App chrome and panel keyboard interaction behavior
- Focused interaction tests

**Out of scope:**
- Removing keyboard access to Annotation inline editing
- Changing Annotation selection, locking, or text commit behavior
- Redesigning global shortcut management
- Changing unrelated panel or toolbar controls
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Enter and F2 open inline editing for exactly one editable selected text Annotation when the canvas interaction context has focus.
- [ ] Enter and F2 from focused buttons or interactive controls outside the canvas do not open the canvas Annotation editor.
- [ ] The outside-control keyboard event is not default-prevented by the canvas Annotation editor shortcut.
- [ ] Existing canvas Space/pan and active text-editor keyboard behavior remain unchanged.
- [ ] Focused interaction tests cover canvas-scoped shortcut activation and outside-control non-interception.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused scene interaction tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Shipped the plant symbol preview token fix, scoped annotation edit shortcuts to canvas focus, and guarded inline annotation edit commits against current lock/layer/visibility editability. Verified with focused Vitest coverage, full npm test, and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Recheck Annotation editability before inline-edit commit
description:
## Slice Summary
An already-open Annotation inline editor must recheck editability before committing text changes or empty-text deletion.

## User Value
Designers can lock or hide an Annotation or its Layer while an editor is open without the pending editor bypassing those guards during blur/click-away commit.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the Annotation domain model, file schema, text rendering model, or editing grouped/locked Annotations.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Recheck Annotation editability before committing inline-editor changes.

**Current behavior:**
An Annotation inline editor verifies the target exists before commit, but does not re-derive whether the target is still editable. If the Annotation or its Layer becomes locked, hidden, grouped, or otherwise blocked while the editor is open, a blur/click-away commit can still rewrite or delete the Annotation.

**Desired behavior:**
Inline-editor commit should recheck the current Design Object editability at the mutation boundary before changing or deleting Annotation text. If the target is no longer editable, the editor should clean up without rewriting or deleting the Annotation and without creating a Scene Edit history entry.

**Key interfaces:**
- Annotation inline editor commit path
- Design Object selection/read model editability rules
- Scene Edit transaction boundaries
- Layer lock/visibility and Design Object lock guards
- Focused interaction tests

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the Annotation schema or rendering model
- Allowing edits to locked, hidden, grouped, or structurally blocked Annotations
- Changing normal editable Annotation commit, empty-delete, cancel, or no-op semantics
- Changing Layer Panel lock/visibility behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] If an open inline editor's Annotation becomes locked before commit, the pending text change is not applied and no Scene Edit history entry is created.
- [ ] If the Annotations Layer becomes locked before commit, the pending text change is not applied and no Scene Edit history entry is created.
- [ ] If the Annotations Layer becomes hidden before commit, the pending text change is not applied and no Scene Edit history entry is created.
- [ ] Empty-text deletion also respects the same current editability recheck.
- [ ] Normal editable Annotation commit, empty-delete, cancel, and unchanged no-op behavior remain covered and passing.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused scene interaction tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Shipped the plant symbol preview token fix, scoped annotation edit shortcuts to canvas focus, and guarded inline annotation edit commits against current lock/layer/visibility editability. Verified with focused Vitest coverage, full npm test, and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Keep Selection Action Toolbar clear of Rotation Handle near bottom edge
description:
## Slice Summary
Rotatable selections near the bottom edge of the canvas should keep both the Selection Action Toolbar and Rotation Handle visible and usable.

## User Value
Designers can rotate and use selection actions on Zones, Annotations, Object Groups, and other rotatable selections near canvas edges without one overlay hiding the other.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new selection actions, change command semantics, change Rotation Handle behavior, or redesign the Selection Action Toolbar beyond placement.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Keep the Selection Action Toolbar from covering the Rotation Handle for rotatable selections near the canvas bottom edge.

**Current behavior:**
When a rotatable selection is close enough to the canvas bottom edge that the toolbar cannot fit below the selected bounds, toolbar placement flips above the selection using only the normal toolbar gap. The Rotation Handle is also placed above the selection, so the toolbar can overlap and visually hide the handle.

**Desired behavior:**
For rotatable selections, toolbar placement must reserve enough screen-space clearance for the Rotation Handle whenever the toolbar is placed above the selected bounds. If the toolbar cannot fit below, it should flip or clamp within the canvas margin without covering the handle. Existing non-rotatable selection placement, edge clamping, command availability, focus behavior, and z-index ordering should remain unchanged.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection Action Toolbar placement
- Rotation Handle placement
- Design Object selection read model
- Scene Interaction overlay refresh
- Focused canvas interaction tests

**Out of scope:**
- Adding, removing, or renaming selection actions
- Changing command semantics, lock/group eligibility, or selection rules
- Redesigning toolbar visuals beyond placement
- Changing Rotation Handle geometry or behavior except as needed to preserve clearance
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rotatable selections near the bottom canvas edge do not place the Selection Action Toolbar over the Rotation Handle.
- [ ] Toolbar placement still keeps the full toolbar inside the visible canvas margin.
- [ ] Normal rotatable selections with space below still place the toolbar below while keeping the Rotation Handle above.
- [ ] Single non-rotatable selections, including Placed Plants, keep their existing balanced close-above placement.
- [ ] Existing top, left, right, and bottom edge clamping behavior remains covered and passing.
- [ ] Focused scene interaction tests cover the bottom-edge rotatable overlap regression.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused canvas interaction tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Fixed rotatable selection toolbar placement so bottom-edge fallback reserves Rotation Handle clearance; verified with focused scene interaction tests, cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit, and cd desktop/web && npm test (150 files, 1046 tests).

title: Show nearest Placed Plant distances while dragging
description:
## Slice Summary
Dragging a Placed Plant should show live center-to-center spacing to up to three nearest visible non-dragged Placed Plants.

## User Value
Designers can adjust plant placement with immediate spacing feedback without switching tools.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Plant Spacing tool behavior, persist distance guides, use canopy-edge distances, or add measurement overlays for non-plant objects.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Show live center-to-center distances to nearby Placed Plants while dragging a Placed Plant.

**Current behavior:**
Dragging a Placed Plant updates its position live, but the user does not receive dynamic spacing feedback to nearby Placed Plants unless they switch to a dedicated spacing workflow.

**Desired behavior:**
While dragging one or more Placed Plants, show a transient overlay for the actively dragged Placed Plant position with up to three nearest visible non-dragged Placed Plants. Distances are center-to-center in design meters, recomputed live as the drag moves and after snapping is applied. Use unobtrusive dashed connector lines and compact metric labels at connector midpoints, reusing existing metric distance formatting. Exclude every currently dragged Placed Plant from nearest-neighbor candidates. Hide the overlay when there are no other visible Placed Plants and clean it up on pointer-up, cancel, tool change, document replacement, or runtime disposal.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction shared drag gestures
- Placed Plant presentation and visibility
- Metric distance formatting
- Runtime-owned DOM overlay lifecycle
- Scene Edit transaction preview during drag

**Out of scope:**
- Persisted guides or saved measurements
- Canopy-edge distances or canopy overlap analysis
- Non-plant Design Object measurements
- Changing Plant Spacing tool behavior or Plant Spacing Interval settings
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Dragging a Placed Plant shows center-to-center metric distances to up to three nearest visible non-dragged Placed Plants.
- [ ] Distance candidates exclude all Placed Plants included in the current drag selection.
- [ ] Distances update live after the same snapped drag delta that moves the plant.
- [ ] Overlay uses transient connector lines and labels that do not create Scene Edit history or persisted data.
- [ ] Overlay hides when there are no eligible nearby Placed Plants and cleans up on pointer-up, cancel, tool change, document replacement, and dispose.
- [ ] Existing drag behavior, commit/abort semantics, snapping, and selection behavior remain unchanged.
- [ ] Focused interaction tests cover one neighbor, more than three neighbors, hidden/dragged exclusions, snapping updates, and cleanup.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused canvas interaction tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Implemented the canvas UX slices with focused regression coverage; verified with focused Vitest suites, cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit, and cd desktop/web && npm test (150 files, 1045 tests).

title: Increase canvas zoom cap for precision adjustment
description:
## Slice Summary
The canvas should allow deeper zoom for precise placement and adjustment while preserving existing camera behavior.

## User Value
Designers can make millimeter-scale visual adjustments when detailed placement matters.

## Out of Scope
Do not change default zoom, zoom gestures, zoom-to-fit behavior, ruler semantics, or map projection authority.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Raise the canvas camera zoom cap to support precision adjustment.

**Current behavior:**
The canvas camera clamps zoom at 200 px/m, which limits precise visual adjustment once the user needs very fine placement.

**Desired behavior:**
Raise the maximum canvas zoom clamp to 1000 px/m. Existing zoom-in, wheel zoom, zoom-out, pan, viewport invalidation, and zoom-to-fit behavior should otherwise remain unchanged. The MapLibre bridge should continue to sync through the existing projection and MapLibre zoom clamp model.

**Key interfaces:**
- CameraController viewport scale clamp
- Zoom controls and wheel zoom
- Canvas Map Surface camera sync
- Viewport revision/render invalidation

**Out of scope:**
- Changing default viewport scale
- Changing zoom factor or zoom shortcuts
- Changing zoom-to-fit padding or fit behavior
- Changing MapLibre source max zoom, basemap tiles, or projection math
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Camera viewport scale clamps at 1000 px/m instead of 200 px/m.
- [ ] Zoom-in and wheel zoom can reach the new cap but do not exceed it.
- [ ] Zoom-out minimum, zoom factor, default initialization, pan behavior, and zoom-to-fit behavior remain unchanged except for the higher cap when relevant.
- [ ] Canvas Map Surface sync still receives a valid clamped MapLibre zoom at high canvas scale.
- [ ] Focused camera tests cover the new maximum clamp and unchanged minimum clamp.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused camera/projection tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Implemented the canvas UX slices with focused regression coverage; verified with focused Vitest suites, cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit, and cd desktop/web && npm test (150 files, 1045 tests).

title: Add Design Location buttons to map layer frames
description:
## Slice Summary
Basemap, contour lines, and hillshading layer details should offer a direct Design Location button when map layers need a saved Location.

## User Value
Designers can recover from missing map readiness directly from the relevant layer frame instead of reading a passive message.

## Out of Scope
Do not duplicate Location Workbench UI in the layer panel, change map layer settings authority, or change MapLibre rendering.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Map and terrain layer details should offer a direct Design Location button when no Location is saved.

**Current behavior:**
The basemap layer detail shows a sentence-style message telling the user to set a design location first. Contour lines and hillshading layer details do not expose the same direct route to the Location Workbench.

**Desired behavior:**
When a Design has no saved Location, the basemap, contour lines, and hillshading layer detail frames each show a compact secondary button labeled for the Design Location action. Clicking the button navigates to the existing Location Workbench. Remove the passive basemap message. Do not duplicate location search or map editing UI in the Layer Panel. Once a Location exists, layer details return to their normal controls and saved-location presentation.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Layer Presentation read model
- Layer Panel map and terrain layer details
- App Command Graph or shell navigation for Location Workbench
- Location Workbench presentation

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Location data authority or Location Workbench behavior
- Changing MapLibre rendering, basemap style, contour interval semantics, or hillshade settings
- Adding inline geocoding to the Layer Panel
- Changing scene Layer visibility or locking semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Basemap detail no longer shows the sentence-style missing-location message.
- [ ] Basemap, contour lines, and hillshading details each show a Design Location button when no saved Location exists.
- [ ] Each Design Location button navigates to the existing Location Workbench and closes any incompatible side-panel state as normal navigation does.
- [ ] Existing map layer opacity, contour interval, and hillshade controls continue to work when a Location exists.
- [ ] Layer Panel does not import raw Design Session location state directly; it uses existing presentation/navigation seams.
- [ ] User-visible strings are added or reused across all locale files as needed.
- [ ] Focused component/workbench tests cover no-location and has-location detail states.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused Layer Panel tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Implemented the canvas UX slices with focused regression coverage; verified with focused Vitest suites, cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit, and cd desktop/web && npm test (150 files, 1045 tests).

title: Inline edit existing canvas Annotations
description:
## Slice Summary
Existing canvas Annotations should be editable in place with standard design-tool interactions, without introducing a new text object type.

## User Value
Designers can correct or expand text notes already placed in a Design without deleting and recreating them.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the Annotation domain term, file schema, text rendering model, rotation behavior, or non-text Design Objects.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Existing canvas Annotations should be editable in place with standard design-tool interactions.

**Current behavior:**
The Text tool can create a new text Annotation through a textarea overlay. Existing Annotations can be selected, moved, rotated, copied, pasted, and deleted, but their text cannot be edited in place.

**Desired behavior:**
In the Select tool, double-clicking a visible editable top-level Annotation opens an inline textarea editor for that Annotation. If exactly one editable Annotation is selected, Enter and F2 also open editing for keyboard access. Editing reuses the familiar textarea overlay interaction. Enter commits, Shift+Enter inserts a newline, blur or clicking elsewhere commits, and Escape cancels and restores the original text. Committing unchanged text creates no Scene Edit history entry. Committing empty or whitespace-only text deletes the Annotation through an undoable Scene Edit. Locked, locked-Layer, grouped, hidden, or otherwise structurally blocked Annotations do not enter text editing.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame
- Annotation Text tool behavior
- Scene Edit transaction and undo/redo history
- Design Object selection read model
- Annotation rendering and hit testing

**Out of scope:**
- New Design Object types or new file schema fields
- Rich text, font controls, text wrapping controls, or Annotation resizing
- Editing grouped or locked Annotations
- Changing Zone Measurements or Timeline Action notes
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Double-clicking a visible editable top-level Annotation in the Select tool opens an inline textarea editor for that Annotation.
- [ ] Enter and F2 open the editor when exactly one editable Annotation is selected.
- [ ] Enter commits the edit; Shift+Enter inserts a newline; blur/click-away commits; Escape cancels and restores original text.
- [ ] Unchanged committed text creates no Scene Edit history entry.
- [ ] Empty or whitespace-only committed text deletes the Annotation through an undoable Scene Edit and clears selection appropriately.
- [ ] Locked, locked-Layer, hidden, grouped, or structurally blocked Annotations cannot be edited.

title: Keep Selection Action Toolbar balanced and visible
description:
## Slice Summary
The Selection Action Toolbar should feel visually attached to the selected Design Object and stay fully visible near canvas edges.

## User Value
Designers can use selection actions without the toolbar floating too far from the object or disappearing outside the visible canvas.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new selection actions, change command semantics, change locking rules, or replace the runtime-owned toolbar model.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** The Selection Action Toolbar should stay close to selected Design Objects and fully visible near canvas edges.

**Current behavior:**
The Selection Action Toolbar can appear too high above the selected Design Object, especially for small Placed Plants. Near canvas edges, placement should remain fully visible and feel attached to the selection.

**Desired behavior:**
The Selection Action Toolbar stays inside the visible canvas with an 8px margin and flips when close to an edge before hard-clamping. Single non-rotatable selections, including single Placed Plants, use a close above-selection placement with a balanced gap. Rotatable selections keep the Rotation Handle above the object and place the toolbar close below by default. Toolbar availability, command behavior, focus preservation, and runtime-owned overlay lifecycle remain unchanged.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection Action Toolbar overlay
- Rotation Handle overlay
- Design Object selection read model
- Scene Interaction Frame overlay ordering

**Out of scope:**
- New toolbar actions or command behavior changes
- Changing selection eligibility, lock behavior, grouping, or shortcut semantics
- Moving the toolbar out of runtime-owned overlays
- Redesigning toolbar visual styling beyond placement
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Single Placed Plant selections show the Selection Action Toolbar close enough to feel attached and not excessively high.
- [ ] Single non-rotatable selections use close above-selection placement when space allows.
- [ ] Rotatable selections keep the Rotation Handle above and place the toolbar close below by default.
- [ ] Toolbar placement flips or clamps so the full toolbar remains inside the visible canvas with an 8px margin on all edges.

title: Make Timeline Action notes multiline and scrollable
description:

title: Balance Placed Plant selection labels below glyphs
description:
## Slice Summary

## User Value

## Out of Scope

design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Selected Placed Plant labels should sit below Plant Symbol glyphs without overlap.

**Current behavior:**
Selected Placed Plant labels are anchored at the selected species centroid. For a single selected Placed Plant this means the name starts at the plant center, so it can overlap the Plant Symbol glyph.

**Desired behavior:**
Selection labels for Placed Plants should use a screen-space downward offset based on the selected plant Visual Footprint before overlap resolution. Use a balanced adaptive offset: plant visual radius plus about 2px, clamped to roughly 5px minimum and 8px maximum. The label should remain readable across zoom levels without changing Plant Symbol geometry, hit testing, selection rings, bounds, or zoom-to-fit behavior.

title: Localize Plant Color and Plant Symbol popover names
description: Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers can show stale placed-plant Common Names after switching the app language because selected plant presentation contexts use the stored placed-plant common name instead of the active localized Common Name.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers should use active localized Common Names after language changes.

**Current behavior:**
The selected plant presentation contexts used by Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers derive the displayed species label from the placed plant's stored Common Name. That stored name can remain in the language used when the placed plant was created or loaded, so switching the app language can leave the popover title and species-wide action label stale even after localized Common Names are available elsewhere in the canvas runtime.

**Desired behavior:**
When the active language changes and localized Common Names are loaded for the selected species, both Plant Color and Plant Symbol selected plant contexts should prefer the active localized Common Name. If no localized name exists, they should fall back to the placed plant Common Name and then the Canonical Name as existing UI behavior allows. Open popovers should be able to observe the updated selected plant context through the existing query/runtime refresh seams.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas runtime selected plant color context
- Canvas runtime selected plant symbol context
- Canvas runtime localized Common Name presentation cache
- Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers
- Runtime query revision for plant name changes

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Species Catalog data or Common Name storage
- Rewriting placed plant persisted Common Names on locale changes
- Changing Plant Symbol IDs, color palettes, or species default semantics
- Changing unrelated canvas labels, tooltips, or planning projections
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Selected Plant Color context uses the active localized Common Name when one is available.
- [ ] Selected Plant Symbol context uses the active localized Common Name when one is available.
- [ ] Both contexts fall back to the placed plant Common Name when no localized Common Name is available.
- [ ] Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers can update displayed plant names after language changes.
- [ ] Focused regression coverage reproduces the stale Common Name behavior and passes after the fix.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including the focused regression and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Diagnosed stale Plant Color and Plant Symbol popover Common Names after language changes. Fixed selected plant presentation contexts to prefer runtime localized Common Names over persisted placed-plant names and made both popovers observe plant-name query revisions. Added runtime and component regressions. Verified focused RED/GREEN repros, relevant frontend suites, TypeScript, full frontend Vitest, and diff whitespace check.

title: Simplify Plant Color and Plant Symbol popover actions
description:
## Slice Summary
Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers should end at their two explicit set actions, with no species-default hint text or clear actions below those buttons.

## User Value
Designers get a cleaner, symmetric Plant Color and Plant Symbol tool surface focused on choosing a value and applying it either to the current placed plant selection or to the selected species default.

## Out of Scope
Do not remove the underlying scene edit commands, command-surface methods, runtime behavior, or i18n keys for clearing per-plant overrides or species defaults. Do not change palette choices, symbol choices, species default semantics, or canvas rendering.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers should stop after their two explicit set actions.

**Current behavior:**
The Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers include two explicit set actions, followed by species-default hint text and clear actions for per-plant overrides or species defaults. This makes the popovers longer and mixes the primary apply workflow with secondary clearing workflows.

**Desired behavior:**
Each popover should keep the chooser UI and the two explicit apply actions: apply to the current placed plant selection, and apply to all placed plants of the selected species when a single species is selected. Nothing should appear below those two set buttons. The underlying clear commands and translations should remain available in the codebase for future UI placement or command exposure; this slice only removes the current popover exposure.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Color popover
- Plant Symbol popover
- Canvas plant presentation command surface
- Plant presentation component tests
- Frontend agent UI guidance for Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers

**Out of scope:**
- Removing or weakening clear scene edit commands
- Removing i18n keys for clear actions
- Changing color palette, advanced color picker, Plant Symbol choices, or species default semantics
- Changing canvas rendering or document persistence
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plant Color popover renders no content below the visible set action buttons.
- [ ] Plant Symbol popover renders no content below the visible set action buttons.
- [ ] Species-default hint text is removed from both popovers.
- [ ] Clear color, clear symbol, and clear species-default buttons are removed from both popovers.
- [ ] Existing set-selection and set-species-default actions still work for Plant Color and Plant Symbol.
- [ ] Underlying clear command-surface methods and runtime mutation behavior remain intact.
- [ ] Focused component tests cover the simplified popover action surfaces.

- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused popover tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.

title: Fit Plant Symbol glyphs inside popover controls
description:

## Out of Scope

- [ ] Plant Symbol preview glyph renders within a padded frame that does not crop recipe strokes.
- [ ] Glyphs remain centered and visually balanced inside their controls.
- [ ] Symbol selection and apply behavior remain unchanged.

title: Disable Context Menu edits for topmost blocked hits
description:
## Slice Summary
Canvas Context Menu retargeting should treat the topmost visible Design Object under a right-click as authoritative before considering editable objects behind it.

## User Value
Designers can right-click an overlapped structurally blocked Design Object without accidentally selecting, copying, or deleting an editable Design Object behind the clicked target.

## Out of Scope
Do not change empty-canvas context paste behavior, editable target selection rules, directly locked target behavior, normal Paste or Duplicate offsets, or the Canvas Context Menu command set.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Canvas Context Menu retargeting must disable edit commands when the topmost visible right-click target is structurally blocked, even if an editable object is behind it.

**Current behavior:**
The context-menu retargeting path can run an editable-only hit test before resolving the topmost visible hit. When a visible structurally blocked Design Object overlaps an editable Design Object behind it, the editable-only hit test skips the blocked object and returns the behind object. The Canvas Context Menu can then select or preserve that behind editable object and enable Copy/Delete, even though the user clicked the blocked target.

**Desired behavior:**
Right-clicking the canvas should first resolve the topmost visible Design Object at the clicked point. If that target is structurally blocked, such as by a locked Layer or Object Group lock policy, the Canvas Context Menu for that click should keep Copy and Delete disabled and must not retarget to an editable object behind it. Paste should remain driven by clipboard availability and should continue using the clicked canvas point. Empty-canvas, selected editable target, unselected editable target, and directly locked target behavior should remain unchanged.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Context Menu target retargeting
- Top-level visible hit testing versus editable hit testing
- Design Object lock and Layer lock policy
- Canvas Design Object selection read model
- Canvas scene edit command surface for Copy, Delete, and Paste

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Canvas Context Menu visual design or command set
- Changing normal Paste, Duplicate, or Context Paste placement rules
- Changing Design Object lock semantics, Layer lock semantics, or Object Group lock policy
- Adding operating-system clipboard integration
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Context-clicking an overlapped topmost structurally blocked Design Object keeps Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete disabled.
- [ ] The menu does not select, copy, or delete an editable Design Object behind the clicked blocked target.
- [ ] Paste availability remains clipboard-driven and Context Paste still uses the clicked canvas point.
- [ ] Empty-canvas right-click still preserves the current selection and existing Paste behavior.
- [ ] Editable target, selected editable target, and directly locked target context-menu behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Focused interaction regression covers a topmost locked-Layer or structurally blocked target overlapping an editable target behind it.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including the focused context-menu regression and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Resolved Canvas Context Menu retargeting so the topmost visible structurally blocked hit disables Copy/Delete before editable-only hit fallback can select an object behind it. Added focused overlapped locked-layer regression and updated canvas runtime docs. Verified focused RED/GREEN regression, full scene-interaction Vitest, TypeScript, full frontend Vitest, and diff whitespace check.

title: Disable Canvas Context Menu edits on blocked hits
description:
## Slice Summary
Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete should not remain enabled from an unrelated previous editable selection when the menu is opened over a visible but structurally blocked Design Object.

## User Value
Designers can right-click blocked Design Objects without accidentally copying or deleting a different selected object because the menu context fell through as if it were empty canvas.

## Out of Scope
Do not change empty-canvas context paste behavior, editable target selection rules, locked-object selection/unlock behavior, menu entries beyond Copy/Paste/Delete, or operating-system clipboard integration.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Canvas Context Menu hits on structurally blocked visible Design Objects must produce a disabled edit context instead of preserving an unrelated editable selection.

**Current behavior:**
The Canvas Context Menu retargeting path can fall through when the right-click hits a visible Design Object that is structurally blocked rather than editable. Examples include an Object Group that is non-editable because a member is locked, or an object on a locked Layer skipped by editable-only hit testing. If an older editable selection is still active, the menu opens over the blocked object while Copy/Delete availability is derived from that old selection, so Delete can mutate the wrong Design Object.

**Desired behavior:**
Right-clicking a structurally blocked visible target should distinguish that target from empty canvas. The menu may preserve the visual selection if that best matches the runtime model, but the menu context must make Copy and Delete disabled for that right-click. Paste should continue to depend on the internal canvas clipboard and use the clicked canvas point. Empty-canvas right-click should continue preserving the current selection and existing paste behavior. Editable-object, selected-editable-object, and directly locked-object behavior should remain unchanged.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction contextmenu handling and target retargeting
- Canvas Context Menu command availability
- Design Object selection read model and blocked target reporting
- Canvas scene edit command surface for Copy/Delete/Paste
- Hit testing for editable, locked, and structurally blocked visible Design Objects

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Canvas Context Menu command set or visual design
- Changing normal Paste, Duplicate, or Context Paste placement behavior
- Changing Design Object lock semantics, Layer lock semantics, or group lock policy
- Adding operating-system clipboard integration
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Context-clicking a structurally blocked visible Design Object disables Copy and Delete for that Canvas Context Menu instance.
- [ ] A previous editable selection is not copied or deleted from a menu opened over a blocked target.
- [ ] Paste availability remains clipboard-driven, and Context Paste still uses the clicked canvas point.
- [ ] Empty-canvas right-click still preserves the current selection and existing Paste behavior.
- [ ] Editable target, selected editable target, and directly locked target context-menu behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Focused interaction regression covers a blocked target such as a locked-Layer object or Object Group blocked by a locked member while another editable object was previously selected.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including the focused context-menu regression and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Disabled Canvas Context Menu Copy/Delete for visible structurally blocked right-click hits without mutating a previous editable selection, while keeping Paste clipboard-driven at the clicked point. Added focused interaction regression and updated canvas runtime docs. Verified focused regression, full scene-interaction Vitest, TypeScript, full frontend Vitest, and diff whitespace check.

title: Preserve elliptical Zone radii during paste
description:
## Slice Summary
Copy/Paste, Duplicate, and Canvas Context Paste should translate elliptical Zones without treating their radius vector as a world point.

## User Value
Designers can copy or duplicate elliptical Zones without changing their size or distorting their geometry, including when context-pasting far from the source.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Paste or Duplicate offset policy, Context Paste anchoring rules, non-elliptical Zone geometry, Zone serialization, object identity cloning, or Scene Edit history semantics.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Pasting elliptical Zones must offset only the ellipse center while preserving the radius vector.

**Current behavior:**
The canvas clipboard offsets every `SceneZoneEntity.points` entry when cloning a Zone. That is correct for linear, polygonal, and rectangular Zones whose points are world coordinates, but it is wrong for elliptical Zones: `points[0]` is the center and `points[1]` is the radius vector. Offsetting both entries changes the ellipse radii. Normal Paste can resize the ellipse, and Canvas Context Paste far from the source can create a large distorted ellipse.

**Desired behavior:**
When cloning an elliptical Zone from the internal canvas clipboard, translate only `points[0]` by the paste offset and leave all radius/vector entries unchanged. Normal Paste and Duplicate should still place the ellipse one meter to the right of the source. Canvas Context Paste should still use the copied selection's current visual-bounds center as the clicked-point anchor, but the cloned ellipse must preserve the source radii. Non-elliptical Zone clone behavior should remain unchanged.

**Key interfaces:**
- Internal canvas clipboard payload and `pasteClipboardPayload`
- Zone geometry model for elliptical versus point-based Zone types
- Normal Paste, Duplicate, and Canvas Context Paste scene edit command surfaces
- Scene Edit history, selection update, dirty-state, and undo/redo behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Changing normal Paste or Duplicate offset distance
- Changing Context Paste anchoring or context menu behavior
- Changing Zone serialization or the ellipse file/runtime data model
- Changing object identity cloning, Object Group behavior, or undo/redo semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Normal Paste of an elliptical Zone offsets only the center one meter to the right and preserves the radius vector.
- [ ] Duplicate of an elliptical Zone uses the same one-meter-right behavior and preserves the radius vector.
- [ ] Canvas Context Paste of an elliptical Zone anchors the pasted selection's current visual center at the clicked canvas point without changing the ellipse radii.
- [ ] Linear, polygonal, rectangular, and existing non-ellipse Zone paste behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Pasted Zones still get fresh names, update selection to the clone, mark the scene dirty, and preserve undo/redo behavior.
- [ ] Focused regression coverage exercises elliptical Zone paste geometry through the public canvas scene edit surface.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including the focused regression and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Preserved elliptical Zone radii during normal Paste, Duplicate, and Canvas Context Paste by translating only the ellipse center in clipboard clones. Added public runtime regression coverage and updated canvas runtime docs. Verified focused regression, scene-runtime suite, TypeScript, full frontend Vitest, and diff whitespace check.

title: Recompute Canvas Context Paste anchor after viewport changes
description:
## Slice Summary
Canvas Context Paste should anchor pasted Design Objects using visual bounds computed for the current viewport scale, not a source center cached when Copy happened.

## User Value
Designers can copy a selection, zoom, and context-paste at a canvas point without the pasted selection landing off-center because annotation or symbolic plant visual bounds changed with zoom.

## Out of Scope
Do not change normal Paste or Duplicate offsets, context-menu selection rules, operating-system clipboard behavior, object identity cloning, or Scene Edit history semantics.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Canvas Context Paste should recompute the copied selection anchor with the current viewport scale before placing clones.

**Current behavior:**
Context Paste stores the copied selection's visual-bounds center at Copy time. Some Design Object visual bounds depend on viewport scale, including annotations and symbolic plant presentation. If the user zooms between Copy and Canvas Context Menu Paste, the cached anchor can be stale, so the pasted selection's current visual center does not land on the clicked canvas point.

**Desired behavior:**
Canvas Context Paste should translate copied Design Objects so their current visual-bounds center lands on the clicked canvas point, even when viewport scale changes after Copy. The anchor used for context paste should be derived from the copied payload and the current selection/read-model presentation context at paste time. Normal Paste and Duplicate should keep their existing one-meter-right offset behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Internal canvas clipboard payload
- Canvas Context Menu Paste command surface
- Canvas Design Object visual bounds and selection read model
- Viewport-scale-dependent annotation and plant presentation bounds
- Scene Edit history and selection update behavior for pasted clones

**Out of scope:**
- Changing normal Paste or Duplicate offset behavior
- Changing Canvas Context Menu target selection rules
- Adding operating-system clipboard integration
- Changing Design Object cloning identities, group membership, or undo/redo semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canvas Context Paste lands the pasted selection's current visual center on the clicked canvas point after the viewport scale changes between Copy and Paste.
- [ ] Regression coverage includes at least one viewport-scale-dependent copied selection, such as an annotation or symbolic plant selection.
- [ ] Normal Paste and Duplicate continue to use the one-meter-right offset behavior.
- [ ] Pasted Design Objects still preserve relative geometry, fresh identities or names, Object Group membership, selection updates, dirty state, and undo/redo behavior.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including focused context-paste regression coverage and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Recomputed Canvas Context Paste anchors from the copied payload using the current viewport/presentation context at paste time, with annotation zoom regression coverage. Verified focused regression, full scene-runtime Vitest, full frontend Vitest, and TypeScript.

title: Disable Canvas Context Menu edits for blocked selections
description:
## Slice Summary
Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete should stay disabled whenever the current Design Object selection contains locked or otherwise blocked targets, even if the same selection also contains editable targets.

## User Value
Designers do not accidentally run partial edit actions from the context menu when their visible selection includes protected or blocked Design Objects.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the Selection Action Toolbar behavior, object lock semantics, layer lock semantics, normal Paste placement, context Paste anchoring, or operating-system clipboard integration.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete should be disabled for any selection that includes locked or blocked Design Objects.

**Current behavior:**
The Canvas Context Menu enables Copy and Delete when the selection has at least one editable Design Object. In a mixed selection, such as one editable plant plus one directly locked plant, the menu can dispatch commands that operate only on the editable subset while the locked object remains selected. This diverges from the Selection Action Toolbar, which treats locked or blocked selection state as non-mutable for these actions.

**Desired behavior:**
Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete availability should require an editable selection with no locked targets and no blocked targets. Mixed editable plus locked or blocked selections should keep Copy and Delete visible but disabled. Paste availability should continue to depend only on internal canvas clipboard content. Existing empty-canvas, editable-only selection, directly locked target, and context paste behavior should remain unchanged.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Design Object selection read model
- Canvas Context Menu command availability
- Canvas scene edit command surface for Copy and Delete
- Selection Action Toolbar locked/blocked selection policy

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Selection Action Toolbar behavior
- Changing object lock or Layer lock semantics
- Changing normal Paste or context Paste placement
- Adding menu commands beyond Copy, Paste, and Delete
- Operating-system clipboard integration
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete are disabled when the selection contains any locked targets.
- [ ] Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete are disabled when the selection contains any blocked targets.
- [ ] Canvas Context Menu Copy and Delete remain enabled for editable-only Design Object selections.
- [ ] Paste availability continues to depend on internal canvas clipboard content, not selection mutability.
- [ ] Focused regression coverage exercises a mixed editable plus locked or blocked selection from the context-menu surface.
- [ ] Required frontend checks pass, including the focused context-menu interaction test and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Aligned Canvas Context Menu Copy/Delete availability with blocked-selection policy so mixed editable plus locked/blocked selections keep mutation commands disabled while Paste remains clipboard-driven. Verified with focused context-menu regressions, full scene-interaction Vitest, TypeScript, and full frontend Vitest.

title: Correct canvas Paste and Duplicate offset behavior
description:
## Slice Summary
Normal canvas Paste and Duplicate should clone copied Design Objects with the internal canvas clipboard and place the first copy one meter to the right of the copied selection. Repeated normal Paste or Duplicate advances one additional meter to the right each time.

## User Value
Designers can duplicate or paste selected Design Objects without the copies jumping far away, and repeated copy workflows remain predictable.

## Out of Scope
The Canvas Context Menu, operating-system clipboard integration, cross-window clipboard behavior, and additional edit commands are out of scope.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Normal canvas Paste and Duplicate should place copied Design Objects one meter to the right and advance repeated copies predictably.

**Current behavior:**
Normal Paste and Duplicate place copied Design Objects too far from the original selection. The behavior does not match the desired one-meter canvas editing affordance.

**Desired behavior:**
Normal Paste and Duplicate use Canopi's internal canvas clipboard only. The first normal Paste after Copy places the copied Design Object selection one meter to the right. Repeated normal Paste from the same clipboard advances one additional meter to the right each time. Duplicate uses the same one-meter-right behavior for consistency, so repeated duplicate actions advance predictably. All copied Design Object types preserve their editable geometry, fresh identities, and object group membership, and each Paste or Duplicate remains one undoable Scene Edit.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas scene edit command surface for Copy, Paste, and Duplicate
- Canvas internal clipboard payload and paste placement behavior
- Design Object selection read model and editable-target filtering
- Scene Edit history, dirty-state, selection, and undo/redo behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Canvas Context Menu behavior
