- Operating-system clipboard integration
- Cross-window or cross-design clipboard behavior
- New edit commands beyond Copy, Paste, and Duplicate
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Normal Paste after copying a single placed plant at `(10, 20)` creates the first clone at `(11, 20)` and the second normal paste from the same clipboard at `(12, 20)`.
- [ ] Duplicate selected Design Objects uses the same one-meter-right default offset as normal Paste, and repeated Duplicate advances one meter right each time.
- [ ] Multi-selection and Object Group Paste/Duplicate preserve relative positions, fresh identities or names, group membership, and zone or annotation geometry.
- [ ] Locked, hidden, Layer-locked, grouped-member, and otherwise blocked Design Objects remain excluded from editable copy sources according to the existing selection model.
- [ ] Each Paste or Duplicate records one undoable Scene Edit, updates selection to the cloned Design Objects, and preserves undo/redo and dirty-state behavior.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover single-object, repeated, and grouped or mixed-selection offset behavior.
close_reason: Implemented normal Paste and Duplicate as one-meter-right clipboard clones with repeated paste/duplicate advancement; covered single-object, repeated, and Object Group behavior. Verified with full frontend Vitest and TypeScript.

title: Add Canvas Context Menu for Copy Paste Delete
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a canvas-scoped context menu with Copy, Paste, and Delete. The menu should use the current Design Object selection or the clicked canvas point according to the agreed context rules, keep unavailable commands visible but disabled, and remain visible inside the canvas at any right-click location.

## User Value
Designers can use familiar right-click edit commands directly on the canvas, including anchored paste at the clicked canvas point.

## Out of Scope
Operating-system clipboard integration, menu entries beyond Copy/Paste/Delete, broad app-wide context menus, and changing the normal Paste/Duplicate offset behavior are out of scope.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a canvas-scoped context menu for Copy, Paste, and Delete with anchored context paste.

**Current behavior:**
The canvas suppresses the native browser context menu on canvas interaction surfaces but does not provide a Canopi Canvas Context Menu. Designers must use keyboard shortcuts, app menus, or the Selection Action Toolbar for common edit commands.

**Desired behavior:**
Right-clicking the canvas opens a compact Canvas Context Menu containing Copy, Paste, and Delete. All three commands stay visible; unavailable commands are disabled. Right-clicking an unselected editable top-level Design Object selects only that object before opening the menu. Right-clicking a Design Object already inside the current editable selection preserves that selection. Right-clicking empty canvas preserves selection and uses the clicked canvas point for context paste. Right-clicking a directly locked Design Object may select it as a locked target, but Copy and Delete remain disabled because locked objects are not editable mutation sources. Context Paste uses the copied selection's combined visual-bounds center as the anchor and translates copied Design Objects so that center lands on the clicked canvas point. The menu positions itself dynamically inside the visible canvas so viewport edges or app chrome do not hide it.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Context Menu runtime overlay
- Scene Interaction Frame contextmenu handling
- Canvas scene edit command surface for Copy, Paste, and Delete
- Canvas Design Object selection read model
- Internal canvas clipboard and context paste placement
- Interface design system context-menu pattern

**Out of scope:**
- Operating-system clipboard integration
- Menu entries beyond Copy, Paste, and Delete
- Broad app-wide or panel context menus
- Changing the normal Paste/Duplicate offset behavior
- Replacing the Selection Action Toolbar
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Right-clicking the canvas opens a compact Canvas Context Menu with Copy, Paste, and Delete styled according to the interface design system context-menu pattern.
- [ ] The menu clamps or flips inside the visible canvas at all canvas edges and corners, appears above canvas overlays, and does not hide behind app chrome.
- [ ] Native context menus remain available for editable controls, existing menus, dialogs, and non-canvas panels.
- [ ] Copy and Delete are disabled when there is no editable Design Object selection or when the context is a locked-only selection; Paste is disabled until the internal canvas clipboard has content.
- [ ] Right-clicking an unselected editable Design Object selects only that object; right-clicking inside the current editable selection preserves the selection; right-clicking empty canvas preserves the selection.
- [ ] Right-clicking a directly locked Design Object selects it as a locked target while leaving Copy and Delete disabled.
- [ ] Context Paste anchors the copied selection's combined visual-bounds center to the clicked canvas point while preserving relative geometry and Object Group membership.
- [ ] Copy, Paste, and Delete dispatch through canvas scene-edit command surfaces and preserve Scene Edit history, selection updates, dirty state, and undo/redo behavior.
- [ ] Focused interaction tests cover menu visibility, disabled states, selection rules, locked-object behavior, and anchored context paste.
close_reason: Added runtime-owned Canvas Context Menu with Copy, Paste, and Delete; commands remain visible with disabled states, clamp inside the canvas, retarget selection on right-click, support locked targets, and anchor context paste at the clicked canvas point. Verified with full frontend Vitest and TypeScript.

title: Keep location notice behind plant styling popovers
description: The bottom-left canvas Location Notice can paint above the Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers because the notice and toolbar share the same top-level stacking level. Fix the layering so plant styling popovers stay above the location message.
acceptance_criteria: When Plant Color or Plant Symbol is open, the bottom-left Location Notice paints behind the popover. Focused frontend regression coverage verifies the stacking contract.
close_reason: Lowered the Location Notice stacking level below the toolbar-hosted Plant Color and Plant Symbol popovers while keeping it above ruler/scale-bar chrome. Verified with focused canvas chrome layering regression, existing canvas notice feedback/layout tests, and frontend TypeScript check.

title: Normalize action bar icon weight
description: The action bar icons appear too bold compared with the surrounding UI chrome. Diagnose the mounted action bar styling and reduce icon weight without changing unrelated toolbar behavior.
close_reason: Normalized mounted action bar icon strokes to 1.5 and added focused regression assertions. Tests: npm test -- src/__tests__/panel-bar.test.tsx src/__tests__/scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Apply approved Option A Plant Symbol glyph proportions
description:
## Slice Summary
Update the built-in Plant Symbol glyph recipes from the current balance pass to the approved updated Option A direction: larger, more minimalist glyphs with balanced optical footprint across tree, shrub, herbaceous, climber, round, square, triangle, and cross.

## User Value
Users get Plant Symbols that are easier to recognize at picker and canvas-marker sizes while retaining the same Visual Footprint, hit testing, selection, and zoom behavior.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new Plant Symbol IDs, uploaded artwork, external icon libraries, runtime SVG parsing, automatic Species Catalog habit inference, or file-format changes.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Replace the current Plant Symbol glyph proportions with the user-approved updated Option A direction.

**Current behavior:**
The current Plant Symbol recipes are more evocative than the original built-in set, but the glyphs still read a little small and detailed. The climber mark in particular can dominate when scaled up, while the tree needed a slightly larger canopy during design review.

**Desired behavior:**
Plant Symbols should use the approved updated Option A visual direction: larger, simpler, and optically balanced across the full eight-symbol set. Keep the existing Visual Footprint as the interaction and layout boundary; only glyph artwork proportions should change.

Validated target geometry:
- `round`: filled/stroked circle at center with radius `1`.
- `square`: filled/stroked square from `-0.78` to `0.78` on both axes.
- `triangle`: filled/stroked triangle with points `[0, -0.98]`, `[0.93, 0.78]`, `[-0.93, 0.78]`.
- `cross`: line cross with stroke width `0.24` and segments `[-0.96, 0] -> [0.96, 0]` and `[0, -0.96] -> [0, 0.96]`.
- `tree`: one filled/stroked canopy circle at `[0, -0.25]` with radius `0.78`, plus trunk line `[0, 0.47] -> [0, 0.98]` with stroke width `0.22`.
- `shrub`: one minimalist mound path through `[-0.94, 0.54]`, `[-0.74, -0.13]`, `[-0.28, -0.5]`, `[0.28, -0.5]`, `[0.74, -0.13]`, `[0.94, 0.54]` with stroke width `0.24`, plus baseline `[-0.86, 0.56] -> [0.86, 0.56]` with stroke width `0.22`.
- `herbaceous`: three-line plant mark with stroke width `0.24` and segments `[0, 0.95] -> [0, -0.84]`, `[0, 0.15] -> [-0.74, -0.32]`, and `[0, -0.13] -> [0.74, -0.57]`.
- `climber`: one slightly smaller vine mark with stroke width `0.24` and segments `[-0.36, 0.88] -> [0.25, 0.35]`, `[0.25, 0.35] -> [-0.22, -0.14]`, `[-0.22, -0.14] -> [0.27, -0.63]`, and `[0.27, -0.63] -> [-0.04, -0.9]`.

**Key interfaces:**
- Built-in Plant Symbol recipe contract
- Plant Symbol picker glyph rendering
- Canvas2D and Pixi renderer-native Plant Symbol drawing
- Plant presentation Visual Footprint, hit testing, selection rings, hover rings, stack badges, and low-zoom fallback

**Out of scope:**
- New symbol IDs or renamed symbols
- Uploaded/custom artwork, icon libraries, images, DOM overlays, or runtime SVG parsing
- Automatic mapping from Species Catalog growth form or habit data
- Changes to hit testing, band selection, bounds, zoom-to-fit, selection/hover/locked rings, stack badges, or persisted file format
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Built-in Plant Symbol recipes match the approved updated Option A target proportions for all eight symbols.
- [ ] Glyphs are larger and more minimalist than the current balance pass while remaining inside the existing Visual Footprint boundary.
- [ ] Picker glyphs, Canvas2D, and Pixi continue to consume the same recipe source so symbol identity stays consistent across UI and canvas.
- [ ] Existing Plant Symbol IDs and persisted file behavior remain unchanged.
- [ ] Visual Footprint behavior remains unchanged for hit testing, band selection, bounds, zoom-to-fit, selection/hover/locked rings, stack badges, and low-zoom dot fallback.
- [ ] Focused tests cover the updated recipe contract and unchanged Visual Footprint behavior.
- [ ] Required frontend gates pass, including focused Plant Symbol tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Applied approved Option A Plant Symbol proportions, kept Visual Footprint behavior unchanged, and verified with focused Plant Symbol tests, TypeScript, and full frontend Vitest.

title: Refine Plant Symbol glyph visual balance
description:
## Slice Summary
Update the built-in Plant Symbol glyph recipes to the validated Option B direction from the glyph design session: habit symbols should be more evocative while staying minimalist, and abstract symbols should use balanced optical footprints instead of filling the entire circular marker area.

## User Value
Users can distinguish tree, shrub, herbaceous, climber, round, square, triangle, and cross symbols more easily in the picker and on the canvas without changing placement, hit testing, selection, or zoom behavior.

## Out of Scope
Do not add uploaded icons, external icon libraries, runtime SVG parsing, new Plant Symbol IDs, automatic Species Catalog habit inference, or changes to the Design file format.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Replace the current Plant Symbol glyph proportions with the user-validated Option B direction from the glyph study.

**Current behavior:**
Plant Symbol recipes technically fit inside the circular Visual Footprint, but the visual weight is uneven. Some abstract glyphs fill the marker too literally, while the habit glyphs are less evocative and can feel visually imbalanced at picker and canvas-marker sizes.

**Desired behavior:**
Plant Symbols preserve the existing circular Visual Footprint for interaction geometry while using optically balanced glyph recipes. The validated direction is Option B from the design session: more evocative but still minimalist habit glyphs for tree, shrub, herbaceous, and climber, plus balanced abstract glyphs for round, square, triangle, and cross. The glyph may use an inset optical footprint; it does not need to touch the Visual Footprint edge.

**Key interfaces:**
- Built-in Plant Symbol recipe contract
- Canvas renderer-native primitive drawing for Plant Symbols
- Plant Symbol picker glyph rendering
- Plant presentation Visual Footprint, hit testing, selection, stack badges, and zoom behavior
- Canvas runtime and Plant Symbol agent documentation

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new symbol IDs or renaming existing symbol IDs
- Uploaded/custom artwork, SVG assets, icon libraries, images, DOM overlays, or runtime SVG parsing
- Automatic mapping from Species Catalog growth form or habit data
- Changing hit testing, band selection, bounds, zoom-to-fit, selection rings, hover rings, stack badges, or file format
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The built-in Plant Symbol recipes match the validated Option B direction: habit symbols are more evocative and minimalist, and abstract symbols use balanced optical footprints.
- [ ] Picker glyphs and both canvas renderers continue to consume the same recipe source, so the same symbol identity looks consistent across UI and canvas.
- [ ] Existing Plant Symbol IDs and persisted file behavior remain unchanged.
- [ ] Visual Footprint behavior remains unchanged for hit testing, selection/hover/locked rings, band selection, bounds, zoom-to-fit, stack badges, and low-zoom dot fallback.
- [ ] Tests are updated or added to cover the recipe change and unchanged Visual Footprint behavior.
- [ ] Canvas runtime/Plant Symbol docs are updated to clarify that Visual Footprint is interaction geometry and glyph recipes should be optically balanced rather than edge-filling.
- [ ] Required frontend gates pass, including focused Plant Symbol renderer/presentation tests and `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Refined Plant Symbol glyph recipes to the approved Option B visual balance, kept Visual Footprint behavior unchanged, updated Plant Symbol docs, and verified with focused frontend tests, TypeScript, and full frontend Vitest.

title: Use inherited effective Plant Symbols in the symbol picker
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Plant Symbol picker so multi-species selections with no explicit selected symbols initialize and describe the picker from the shared effective symbol state instead of defaulting to round.

## User Value
Users can open the Plant Symbol picker on selections that inherit species defaults without accidentally overwriting those inherited symbols with round.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Plant Symbol rendering, species-default mutation semantics, or the icon set.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Use selected plants' shared effective Plant Symbol when the picker has no explicit current symbol.

**Current behavior:**
When selected placed plants span multiple species and none has an explicit `symbol`, the Plant Symbol context can have `sharedCurrentSymbol: null`, `inheritedSymbol: null`, and a meaningful `sharedEffectiveSymbol` such as `tree` or `mixed` from species defaults. The picker currently falls back to `round`, so pressing the selection apply action can overwrite inherited effective symbols with `round`. The status text can also report inherited round even when the effective inherited state is different or mixed.

**Desired behavior:**
For selections with no explicit shared current symbol, the Plant Symbol picker should initialize from `sharedEffectiveSymbol` when it is a concrete symbol, and should choose a neutral safe default only when the effective state is mixed. Status text should describe inherited effective symbols accurately for multi-species inherited selections.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selected Plant Symbol context
- Plant Symbol picker initialization and status text
- Plant Symbol selection apply command
- Plant Symbol menu tests

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Plant Symbol rendering or icon recipes
- Changing species-default set/clear mutation behavior
- Changing the stored file format
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A multi-species selection with `sharedCurrentSymbol: null`, `inheritedSymbol: null`, and concrete `sharedEffectiveSymbol` initializes the active picker symbol to that effective symbol.
- [ ] Pressing the selection apply action in that state applies the concrete shared effective symbol, not `round`.
- [ ] The status text describes the inherited effective symbol accurately instead of saying inherited round.
- [ ] A multi-species selection with mixed effective symbols still initializes to a safe default without pretending the inherited state is round.
- [ ] Focused Plant Symbol menu tests cover the inherited concrete and mixed-effective cases.
- [ ] Required frontend gates pass, including `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` and focused Vitest coverage.
close_reason: Fixed Plant Symbol menu inherited effective symbol handling; focused Vitest and TypeScript gates passed.

title: Prevent Plant Symbol species defaults from changing locked plants
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix species-wide Plant Symbol default set/clear behavior so locked, layer-locked, or group-locked plants do not visibly change via fallback symbol resolution.

## User Value
Locks remain trustworthy: species-default symbol edits affect only editable placed plants and do not bypass lock rules through inherited rendering.

## Out of Scope
Do not change general lock semantics, selection behavior, or Plant Color species-default behavior unless a matching bug is found and filed separately.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Prevent species-wide Plant Symbol defaults from changing locked plants through fallback rendering.

**Current behavior:**
Species-wide Plant Symbol default edits skip locked placed plants when deciding which plant entities to rewrite. However, locked plants with no explicit `symbol` still resolve their effective rendered symbol through the shared species default map, so setting or clearing a species default can visibly change locked, layer-locked, or group-locked plants.

**Desired behavior:**
Species-wide Plant Symbol set and clear actions must not visibly change locked or otherwise non-editable placed plants. Editable plants of the species may be rewritten to the chosen explicit symbol, and the species default may be updated for future placements, but locked plants that would otherwise inherit the changed default need to retain their previous effective symbol.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Symbol resolution for placed plants and species defaults
- Species-wide Plant Symbol mutation commands
- Design Object, Layer, and Object Group lock semantics
- Scene edit history, dirty state, and serialization

**Out of scope:**
- Changing general Design Object lock semantics
- Changing Plant Color species-default behavior unless a separate matching bug is filed
- Adding automatic Species Catalog habit inference
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Setting a Plant Symbol species default does not change the effective symbol of locked, layer-locked, or group-locked plants.
- [ ] Clearing a Plant Symbol species default does not change the effective symbol of locked, layer-locked, or group-locked plants.
- [ ] Editable placed plants of the target species still update to the selected explicit symbol when setting the species default.
- [ ] Future Plant Stamp placements still use the species default explicitly.
- [ ] Undo/redo, dirty state, and serialization remain correct for species-default symbol edits.
- [ ] Focused runtime tests cover locked plant fallback behavior for set and clear, plus the relevant frontend gates pass.
close_reason: Prevented species-level Plant Symbol set/clear from changing locked or group-locked plants by materializing their previous inherited symbol before default changes, with focused runtime tests and TypeScript check passing.

title: Render built-in Plant Symbols in the canvas
description:
## Slice Summary
Render the built-in Plant Symbol set in default symbolic marker mode using the same Visual Footprint as the current round marker. Rendering must work through both Pixi and Canvas2D and preserve existing interaction geometry.

## User Value
A Design can visually distinguish placed plants with tree, shrub, herbaceous, climber, round, square, triangle, and cross symbols without making the canvas slower or harder to select.

## Out of Scope
Do not add symbol editing UI or species default commands in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Render the built-in Plant Symbol set inside the existing placed-plant Visual Footprint.

**Current behavior:**
Default symbolic plant rendering draws round markers using the shared Visual Footprint. Hit testing, selection rings, bounds, stack badges, and zoom-to-fit all depend on that same circular footprint.

**Desired behavior:**
The canvas renders `tree`, `shrub`, `herbaceous`, `climber`, `round`, `square`, `triangle`, and `cross` symbols as built-in marker recipes inside the existing circular Visual Footprint. Pixi and Canvas2D renderers use native primitives rather than runtime SVG parsing, image textures, DOM overlays, or a new icon dependency. At low zoom where symbols are not readable, all symbols collapse to the same round/dot marker. Canopy spread mode remains physical circle geometry when canopy spread is known; missing-canopy symbolic fallback uses the resolved Plant Symbol.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant presentation context and entries
- Pixi scene renderer and Canvas2D scene renderer
- Visual Footprint, hit testing, selection, bounds, stack badge, and zoom-to-fit behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Editing or picker UI for symbols
- File-format migration work
- Per-symbol hit testing or selection outlines
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Both Pixi and Canvas2D render all eight built-in symbols without SVG/image/icon-library dependencies.
- [ ] Symbol glyphs fit inside the existing round marker edge and keep the same Visual Footprint scaling rules as current round markers.
- [ ] Hit testing, selection/hover/locked rings, band selection, stack badge placement, grouping bounds, and zoom-to-fit continue to use the existing circular Visual Footprint.
- [ ] Low-zoom rendering collapses unreadable symbols to a simple round/dot marker.
- [ ] Canopy spread mode keeps physical canopy circles when canopy spread is known and uses symbol fallback only when canopy spread is missing.
- [ ] Focused renderer/presentation tests cover symbol resolution, fallback, and unchanged interaction geometry.
close_reason: Implemented built-in Plant Symbol rendering for Pixi and Canvas2D using shared native primitive recipes, low-zoom dot fallback, canopy-circle preservation, and focused presentation/renderer/geometry tests passing.

title: Persist Plant Symbol IDs in v3 Design files
description:
## Slice Summary
Add Plant Symbol persistence to the Design file contract as a v3 scene-owned format change. Old Designs should migrate without visual changes, and new Designs should round-trip species symbol defaults and placed-plant symbol overrides.

## User Value
Users can save, reopen, autosave, and share Designs that contain Plant Symbol choices without losing appearance state.

## Out of Scope
Do not add the toolbar picker, canvas glyph rendering beyond fallback compatibility, or automatic Species Catalog inference.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Add Plant Symbol persistence to the Design file contract as a v3 scene-owned format change.

**Current behavior:**
Design files persist placed plants and per-species color defaults, but there is no Plant Symbol contract. Old files render every symbolic plant marker as the round glyph.

**Desired behavior:**
Design files have a v3 scene-owned Plant Symbol contract. A top-level species-default map stores stable symbol IDs keyed by canonical species name, and placed plants may carry an optional explicit symbol override. Missing, null, or unknown symbol values resolve to `round`. Old files migrate to v3 with an empty species-default symbol map and no placed-plant symbol overrides, preserving their existing appearance.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design file version migration and save/load format contract
- Shared Rust/TypeScript `CanopiFile` and `PlacedPlant` contracts
- Scene hydrate/serialize/defaults and generated bindings
- Document field ownership metadata for scene-owned top-level fields

**Out of scope:**
- Symbol picker UI
- Canvas drawing beyond fallback compatibility
- Automatic inference from Species Catalog growth form data
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Design file current version is bumped to v3 with a migration from v2 that adds an empty Plant Symbol species-default map.
- [ ] The shared contract exposes stable Plant Symbol IDs and adds scene-owned top-level species defaults plus optional placed-plant overrides.
- [ ] Generated frontend bindings and known field ownership metadata are regenerated and committed.
- [ ] Scene defaults, hydration, serialization, clone, and round-trip tests preserve Plant Symbol fields and fallback old/missing values to `round`.
- [ ] Required gates for shared contract and persistence changes are run or documented if blocked.
close_reason: Implemented v3 Plant Symbol persistence with migration, generated bindings, scene round-trip/fallback tests, TypeScript, Rust format, and focused Rust/frontend tests passing.

title: Apply Plant Symbols to selected placed plants
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a separate Plant Symbol toolbar control and popover for applying a symbol to selected editable placed plants. The picker should be icon-only with tooltips, use explicit apply and clear actions, and preserve color as an independent presentation axis.

## User Value
Users can choose symbols for selected placed plants the same way they already customize plant color, while keeping color and symbol controls separate.

## Out of Scope
Do not add species default editing or automatic habit inference in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a separate Plant Symbol toolbar control for applying symbols to selected editable placed plants.

**Current behavior:**
The left canvas toolbar has a Plant Color control for selected placed plants, but no Plant Symbol control. Symbols can exist only once earlier slices add persistence/rendering.

**Desired behavior:**
The toolbar has a separate icon-only Plant Symbol button placed next to Plant Color, with Plant Color first and Plant Symbol second. The popover shows an icon-only grid with tooltips, grouped as habit symbols and abstract symbols, plus explicit apply and clear actions for the selected editable placed plants. The picker reports current, mixed, and inherited states. The symbol grid uses neutral UI ink, with a separate preview showing the chosen symbol in the effective plant color. Symbol edits are Scene Edits: they respect Design Object locks and Layer locks, create undo/redo history, mark the Design dirty, and serialize through the scene.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas command/query surfaces for plant presentation edits and selected plant context
- Canvas toolbar and popover chrome
- Scene edit transactions, locks, layers, dirty state, and history
- i18n strings for labels and tooltips

**Out of scope:**
- Species-default symbol editing
- Automatic habit inference
- Combining Plant Color and Plant Symbol into one popover
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A separate Plant Symbol toolbar button appears next to Plant Color and is enabled only when selected editable placed plants exist.
- [ ] The popover provides icon-only symbol choices with tooltips, grouped as habit and abstract symbols, with explicit apply-to-selection and clear-selection actions.
- [ ] The picker represents current, mixed, and inherited selected-plant states without exposing storage details.
- [ ] Applying or clearing a selected plant symbol respects editable selection, Design Object locks, Layer locks, undo/redo, dirty state, and serialization.
- [ ] Toolbar status shows the shared current symbol when selected editable plants agree and a generic/mixed affordance otherwise.
- [ ] Focused frontend/runtime tests cover selected-symbol context, apply/clear behavior, locks/layers, history, dirty state, and i18n.
close_reason: Implemented separate Plant Symbol toolbar and icon-only popover for selected editable plants, including current/mixed/inherited context, apply/clear Scene Edits through the plant-presentation seam, lock/layer-respecting selection behavior, translated i18n strings, and focused runtime/UI/i18n/typecheck coverage.

title: Set Plant Symbol defaults for a species
description:
## Slice Summary
Allow users to set or clear a Plant Symbol default for a single selected species and have future placements use that default. Existing editable placed plants of that species should update immediately when the species default is set, matching Plant Color behavior.

## User Value
Users can standardize a species' symbol across a Design without editing each placed plant by hand.

## Out of Scope
Do not infer defaults from Species Catalog growth form data or add global app-wide symbol defaults.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add single-species Plant Symbol defaults that affect future placement and can update existing editable plants.

**Current behavior:**
Plant Color can set a per-species default and immediately update editable placed plants of that species. Plant Symbols have no equivalent species-default command until earlier slices add selected-plant symbol editing.

**Desired behavior:**
When selected editable placed plants all share one canonical species, the Plant Symbol popover offers a species-default action. Setting a species default stores the chosen stable symbol ID in the Design's species-default symbol map and writes the chosen symbol to existing editable placed plants of that species, matching Plant Color behavior. `round` is an explicit valid default; clearing the default removes the species-default entry. New Plant Stamp placements use the species default by writing the symbol explicitly at creation time. Copy/paste, Object Stamp, and Plant Spacing preserve explicit symbols from their source plants.

**Key interfaces:**
- Single-species selected plant context
- Plant Stamp source placement and other plant duplication/reuse workflows
- Scene edit transactions for species-scoped edits
- Species-default symbol map serialization

**Out of scope:**
- Multi-species bulk default editing
- Global app-wide symbol defaults
- Automatic Species Catalog growth-form inference
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The species-default action is available only when selected editable placed plants share one canonical species.
- [ ] Setting a species default stores the chosen symbol in the Design-level species-default map and updates existing editable placed plants of that species while leaving locked/non-editable plants unchanged.
- [ ] Clearing a species default removes the species-default entry without silently rewriting existing placed plants.
- [ ] `round` is treated as an explicit valid species default distinct from clearing.
- [ ] New Plant Stamp placements with a species default store that symbol explicitly; copy/paste, Object Stamp, and Plant Spacing preserve explicit source symbols.
- [ ] Focused tests cover species-default set/clear, future placement defaults, existing-plant updates, locks/layers, undo/redo, dirty state, and serialization.
close_reason: Implemented species-level Plant Symbol defaults with explicit round support, selected-species popover actions, undoable scene diffs for plantSpeciesSymbols, future Plant Stamp defaults, explicit symbol preservation for copy/paste, Object Stamp, and Plant Spacing, plus focused and full frontend gates passing.

title: Reject generic text drags as plant drop targets
description:
## Slice Summary
Plant dragover feedback should only accept Canopi plant drags when browser drag data is protected. Generic text drags must not be presented as valid plant drops.

## User Value
Users get accurate drop feedback: plant rows/cards can still be dragged onto the canvas, while unrelated selected text or text snippets do not show plant placement previews or copy cursors.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign Plant Stamp drag serialization, remove the legacy text/plain drop parser, or change locked Plants Layer rejection semantics.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Protected dragover detection should not treat generic text/plain drags as plant drops.

**Current behavior:**
When browser drag data is protected, the canvas can only inspect DataTransfer types during dragover. The current plant drag probe treats text/plain in the type list as sufficient evidence of a plant drag. Ordinary selected text also exposes text/plain, so the canvas may set copy feedback and show a plant placement preview even though the eventual drop payload is rejected.

**Desired behavior:**
Protected dragover should trust the Canopi-specific plant MIME type for plant-drop feedback. The legacy text/plain fallback may still be parsed on the actual drop event, where payload bytes are available, but text/plain must not be accepted by type alone during protected dragover. Invalid or unrelated drags should leave dropEffect as none and hide the plant preview.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant Stamp drag data serialization and detection
- Scene canvas dragover/drop feedback path
- DataTransfer protected-mode behavior during dragover
- Locked Plants Layer creation guard

**Out of scope:**
- Removing legacy text/plain drop payload support
- Changing Plant Stamp click-to-place behavior
- Changing plant card/row/favorites visual design
- Changing drag/drop behavior for non-plant canvas tools
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Protected dragover accepts current Canopi plant drags that advertise the custom plant MIME type.
- [ ] Protected dragover does not accept text/plain by type alone.
- [ ] Ordinary text drags keep dropEffect as none and do not show the plant placement preview.
- [ ] Actual drop still parses valid legacy text/plain plant payloads when payload bytes are available.
- [ ] Locked Plants Layer drops remain rejected.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover both valid plant protected dragover and unrelated text protected dragover.
close_reason: Fixed protected dragover detection so only the custom Canopi plant MIME type is trusted by type alone; legacy text/plain plant payloads are still parsed when readable on drop. Tests: npm test -- plant-stamp-source.test.ts; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'drag-and-drop|ordinary text drags|locked Plants Layer'; npm test -- plant-stamp-source.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Keep active canvas gestures alive across overlays
description:
## Slice Summary
Active canvas pointer gestures must continue processing pointer movement when the cursor crosses runtime-owned overlays such as the Selection Action Toolbar or locked affordance.

## User Value
A user dragging or rotating a Design Object can move across contextual overlays without the transform freezing, skipping live updates, or committing stale pointer state.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not redesign overlay hit targets, toolbar command behavior, or non-active hover suppression policy.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Active canvas gestures must process pointer movement before overlay ignore rules.

**Current behavior:**
Pointer movement for active canvas gestures is handled at document/window scope. When the cursor crosses a runtime-owned overlay, such as the Selection Action Toolbar or locked affordance, the pointer event target can be that overlay. Overlay ignore checks can return before active shared drags, active Design Object Rotation, or active tool drags receive the movement. This can freeze live Rotation Handle updates or make pointer-up commit stale drag deltas.

**Desired behavior:**
Once a canvas pointer gesture is active, pointer movement should continue through the active gesture path regardless of whether the event target is a runtime-owned overlay. Overlay ignore checks should only suppress no-active-gesture hover and tool preview behavior, or should otherwise be ordered so active gestures always receive movement and cleanup. Existing overlay click/hover behavior should remain coherent when no canvas gesture is active.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame pointer gesture lifetime
- Shared design object dragging
- Design Object Rotation and Rotation Handle updates
- Selection Action Toolbar and locked affordance overlays
- Tool drag update routing

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning toolbar or locked affordance commands
- Changing pointer-up commit semantics beyond fixing stale movement
- Changing non-canvas panel pointer behavior
- Reworking overlay ownership or styling
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Active shared Design Object dragging continues to update while the pointer event target is the Selection Action Toolbar or locked affordance.
- [ ] Active Design Object Rotation continues to update while the pointer event target is the Selection Action Toolbar or locked affordance.
- [ ] Active tool drags are not skipped solely because the cursor crosses a runtime-owned overlay.
- [ ] No-active-gesture hover and preview behavior still ignores runtime-owned overlays as intended.
- [ ] Regression tests cover stale/frozen pointer movement across overlays for at least shared dragging and rotation.
close_reason: Fixed Scene Interaction pointermove routing so active shared drags, Rotation Handle drags, and tool drags continue processing before runtime overlay ignore checks. Verified with npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts pixi-scene.test.ts, npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, and git diff --check.

title: Respect Object Group locks in species-wide Plant color edits
description:
## Slice Summary
Species-wide Plant color edits must not mutate Plants that are members of locked Object Groups or groups that are effectively locked by locked members.

## User Value
A user can lock an Object Group and trust that broad species color actions cannot mutate any Plant protected by that group lock.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not redesign Plant Color Menu wording, group membership semantics, or non-Plant color workflows.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Species-wide Plant color edits must respect Object Group locks.

**Current behavior:**
Species-wide Plant color edits can filter out directly locked Plants and locked Plants Layers, but a Plant inside a locked Object Group, or inside a group that is effectively locked by another locked member, can still be recolored through a broad same-species command.

**Desired behavior:**
A Plant protected by Object Group lock semantics must not be mutated by species-wide color edits. The editable target filter for broad Plant color changes should match the same lock invariant used by selection and other mutation paths: direct Plant locks, Layer locks, locked Object Groups, and Object Groups locked by members all block per-Plant color writes. Default-only species color writes should keep working when there are no placed Plants of that species.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant color context and species-wide Plant color command
- Scene Design Object lock and Object Group effective lock semantics
- Scene Edit transaction boundary for Plant color mutation
- Editable target filtering for broad species scopes

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning Object Group lock semantics
- Changing Plant Color Menu wording or layout
- Changing non-Plant color workflows
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Species-wide Plant color edits leave Plants inside locked Object Groups unchanged.
- [ ] Species-wide Plant color edits leave Plants inside groups locked by another locked member unchanged.
- [ ] Editable unlocked Plants of the species still receive the color update.
- [ ] Species default-only color writes still work when no placed Plants of that species exist.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover direct group lock and effective group lock cases.
close_reason: Fixed species-wide Plant color edits to exclude Plants inside locked Object Groups, including groups effectively locked by another locked member. Verified with npm test -- scene-runtime/mutations.test.ts, focused combined regression suites, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, and git diff --check.

title: Use rotated annotation geometry for band selection
description:
## Slice Summary
Band selection must use the visible rotated Annotation text geometry instead of selecting by the axis-aligned bounding box around that geometry.

## User Value
A user dragging a rectangular selection near a rotated label only selects the Annotation when the band intersects the visible rotated text frame.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not redesign Annotation layout, text editing, point hit testing, or selection toolbar behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Band selection must use rotated Annotation geometry, not only the rotated frame AABB.

**Current behavior:**
Rotated Annotation layout exposes an axis-aligned bounding box around rotated text. Rectangular band selection can use that box and select an Annotation when the band touches an empty corner of the bounding box, even though point hit testing would reject the same empty area.

**Desired behavior:**
Band selection for rotated Annotations should match the visible rotated text frame. A selection rectangle should select a rotated Annotation only when it intersects the rotated text polygon or contains visible rotated text geometry. Empty AABB corners around non-right-angle rotated labels should not count as hits.

**Key interfaces:**
- Annotation layout geometry helpers
- Canvas hit testing and band selection query behavior
- Selection read model and renderer-visible Annotation bounds
- Regression tests for rotated Annotation selection geometry

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning Annotation text measurement or editing UI
- Changing point hit testing except to keep it consistent with shared geometry
- Changing non-Annotation band selection behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A tiny band selection in an empty corner of a 45-degree Annotation bounding box does not select the Annotation.
- [ ] A band selection that intersects the visible rotated Annotation frame does select it.
- [ ] Existing point hit testing remains consistent with rotated Annotation geometry.
- [ ] Non-rotated Annotation band selection behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover rotated Annotation band selection.
close_reason: Fixed annotation band selection to intersect rotated annotation geometry instead of selecting empty AABB corners. Verified with npm test -- hit-testing.test.ts, focused combined regression suites, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, and git diff --check.

title: Project rotated Zones correctly in Target map overlays
description:
## Slice Summary
Panel Target map overlays must project rotated Zone geometry consistently with the Zone shape shown on the canvas.

## User Value
A user selecting or hovering a panel Target for a rotated Zone sees the same oriented Zone footprint on MapLibre that the canvas displays.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not redesign Zone editing, map camera behavior, or non-Zone Target projection.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Target map overlays must project rotated Zone geometry consistently with the canvas.

**Current behavior:**
Rotated Zone orientation is hydrated into scene state and rendered on the canvas, but panel Target map overlays can still project raw stored Zone points. For rotated rectangular or elliptical Zones, this can draw an unrotated footprint on MapLibre while the canvas shows the oriented shape.

**Desired behavior:**
Target map projection for Zones should use the same effective Zone geometry that canvas rendering and hit testing use. A rotated Zone highlighted from a panel Target should appear on the map with the same orientation and footprint as the canvas Zone. Non-rotated Zone projection should remain unchanged.

**Key interfaces:**
- Zone geometry helpers and scene Zone orientation state
- Target map projection from panel Targets to map overlay coordinates
- Canvas-to-map projection for Zone points or polygons
- Regression tests for rotated and non-rotated Zone Target overlays

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning Zone editing or rotation controls
- Changing map camera synchronization
- Changing Plant, Annotation, or non-Zone Target projection behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A rotated rectangular Zone Target projects to the rotated footprint in map overlay geometry.
- [ ] A rotated elliptical Zone Target projects consistently with the canvas Zone geometry or the shared Zone geometry representation used by overlays.
- [ ] Non-rotated Zone Target projection remains unchanged.
- [ ] Panel Target hover/selection overlays use the corrected Zone geometry without changing canvas scene state.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover rotated Zone map projection.
close_reason: Fixed Target map overlays to project rotated rectangular and elliptical Zone targets from effective Zone geometry rather than raw persisted points. Verified with npm test -- panel-target-map-projection.test.ts, focused combined regression suites, npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, and git diff --check.

title: Exclude locked plants from species-wide color edits
description:
## Slice Summary
Species-wide Plant color edits must not mutate locked Plant entities.

## User Value
A user can lock an individual Plant and trust that both direct selection color edits and species-wide color actions respect that lock.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change unlocked species-wide color behavior, Plant Color Menu wording, or non-Plant color workflows.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Species-wide Plant color edits must respect Plant locks.

**Current behavior:**
Plant color context can expose a single selected species from editable selected Plants. The Set for all species color command then applies to every Plant with that canonical name, including locked Plants of the same species that were not editable targets.

**Desired behavior:**
Species-wide Plant color mutations must exclude locked Plants and other non-editable Plant targets. The menu may still offer the species-wide action when there are editable Plants of that species, but the mutation must never recolor locked Plants. If no editable Plant would be affected, no scene edit or dirty/history change should be created.

**Key interfaces:**
- Plant color context and species-wide color command
- Editable selection model and Design Object lock state
- Scene Edit transaction boundary for Plant color mutation
- Layer visibility and lock filtering for editable Plant targets

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Plant Color Menu wording or layout
- Changing direct selected Plant color behavior beyond preserving existing lock filtering
- Changing non-Plant color workflows
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Species-wide color edits leave locked Plants of the same species unchanged.
- [ ] Species-wide color edits still recolor editable unlocked Plants of the selected species.
- [ ] No scene edit is created when no editable Plant of the species can be recolored.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover locked and mixed species-wide color paths.
- [ ] Direct selected Plant color lock filtering remains covered.
close_reason: Fixed species-wide Plant color edits so locked Plants and locked Plants Layers are excluded from per-Plant color mutations while preserving default-only species color writes. Tests: npm test -- scene-runtime/mutations.test.ts -t 'does not recolor locked Plants through species-wide color edits'; npm test -- scene-runtime/mutations.test.ts serializer-plant-species-colors.test.ts; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime/mutations.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts serializer-plant-species-colors.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: Clamp selection action toolbar using rendered width
description:
## Slice Summary
The Selection Action Toolbar must use its actual rendered width when clamping placement near canvas edges.

## User Value
Users can reach all visible toolbar actions even when the selected object is near the right edge of the canvas.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not redesign the Selection Action Toolbar action set, tooltip behavior, or rotation handle placement.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** The Selection Action Toolbar must clamp placement using its real rendered width.

**Current behavior:**
Toolbar placement clamps horizontal position with a fixed width estimate that is smaller than the actual toolbar for normal selections with several visible actions. Near the right canvas edge, the toolbar can extend beyond the canvas and hide action buttons.

**Desired behavior:**
Toolbar placement should use the rendered toolbar width, or an equivalent action-count based width, before clamping to the canvas bounds. All visible actions should remain reachable near canvas edges, and placement should remain stable for locked-selection and normal-selection toolbar variants.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection Action Toolbar DOM overlay placement
- Canvas bounds used for overlay clamping
- Available selection action projection
- Tooltip and rotation handle overlay layering

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning toolbar actions or icon sizes
- Changing tooltip placement policy beyond preserving current visibility
- Changing rotation handle geometry
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The toolbar stays fully within the canvas when a normal multi-action selection is near the right edge.
- [ ] Placement uses the actual rendered width or a correct equivalent width for the available actions.
- [ ] Locked-selection toolbar placement remains coherent.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover the edge-clamping behavior.
- [ ] Existing tooltip-over-rotation behavior remains intact.
close_reason: Fixed Selection Action Toolbar placement so clamping uses the rendered toolbar width with a design-token fallback, keeping all actions visible near the canvas edge. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'keeps the Selection Action Toolbar inside the right canvas edge'; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts -t 'Selection Action Toolbar'; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime/mutations.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts serializer-plant-species-colors.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: Block plant creation on locked Plant layers
description:
## Slice Summary
When the Plants scene Layer is locked, Plant creation paths must refuse to add new Plant entities to that locked Layer.

## User Value
A user can lock the Plants Layer and trust that Plant Stamp and drop-to-canvas workflows cannot modify it by adding new Plants.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change existing Plant selection, Plant movement, Plant color edits, or Zone/Annotation creation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Plant creation paths must respect the Plants Layer lock.

**Current behavior:**
Layer locks are visible to users and block editing existing objects, but Plant creation can still append new Plant entities to the Plants Layer while that Layer is locked.

**Desired behavior:**
When the Plants Layer is locked, all Plant creation entry points must refuse to create new Plant entities. This includes Plant Stamp placement and drop-to-canvas Plant creation. The guard must be enforced at the mutation path, not only by disabling visible UI, so shortcuts, drag/drop, and direct tool entry points cannot bypass it. The user-facing tool state should stay coherent with the rest of the app by making locked-layer creation unavailable or no-op without creating document history.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Layer lock state
- Plant creation commands and tool adapters
- Scene Edit transaction boundary for Plant creation
- Canvas render and selection refresh behavior after refused creation

**Out of scope:**
- Changing existing Plant edit behavior on unlocked Layers
- Changing Zone or Annotation creation guards
- Redesigning the Layer Panel UI
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plant Stamp cannot create a Plant while the Plants Layer is locked.
- [ ] Drop-to-canvas Plant creation cannot create a Plant while the Plants Layer is locked.
- [ ] Refused Plant creation does not add history, mark the document dirty, or leave stale preview/selection state.
- [ ] Plant creation still works normally when the Plants Layer is unlocked.
- [ ] Regression tests cover locked and unlocked Plants Layer creation paths.
close_reason: Blocked Plant Stamp and drop-to-canvas Plant creation when the Plants Layer is hidden or locked, without creating scene edits. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Exclude locked plants from plant color edits
description:
## Slice Summary
Locked Plant selections must not enable or receive plant color edits. Selecting a locked Plant for the Unlock affordance should not make color controls treat that Plant as editable.

## User Value
A user can lock an individual Plant and trust that all mutation paths respect the lock until the Plant is explicitly unlocked.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change non-Plant color behavior, Layer lock behavior, or selection toolbar Unlock behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Locked Plants selected for Unlock must be excluded from plant color context and color mutations.

**Current behavior:**
Selecting a directly locked Plant stores the locked Plant id in the canvas selection so the user can access Unlock. Plant color controls still derive their enabled/context state from the raw selected Plant ids and the color mutation path still applies to those ids, so a locked Plant can be recolored before it is unlocked.

**Desired behavior:**
Locked Plants may remain selected for the Unlock workflow, but they must not count as editable Plants for plant color controls. Color context should ignore locked Plant targets, and color mutations must not modify locked Plants even if their ids are present in the raw selection. If all selected Plants are locked, the color action should be unavailable or disabled in the same way it is when there is no editable Plant color target.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas selection read model for editable versus locked Design Object targets
- Plant color context read path
- Plant color mutation command
- Design Object lock state on scene Plant entities

**Out of scope:**
- Changing how locked Plant selection exposes Unlock
- Changing Layer lock behavior
- Changing non-Plant color workflows
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Selecting a locked Plant does not enable plant color actions as an editable color target.
- [ ] Applying a plant color while a locked Plant id is selected does not mutate that locked Plant.
- [ ] Mixed selections color only editable unlocked Plants and leave locked Plants unchanged.
- [ ] Focused regression tests cover locked-only and mixed locked/unlocked Plant color selections.
- [ ] Existing Unlock selection behavior for locked Plants remains intact.
close_reason: Excluded locked Plants from plant color context and selected color mutations while preserving locked selection for Unlock. Tests: npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts; npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Block zone creation on locked Zone layers
description:
## Slice Summary
When the Zones scene Layer is locked, Zone drawing tools must refuse to create new Zone entities on that locked Layer.

## User Value
A user can lock the Zones Layer and trust that drawing tools cannot modify it by adding Rectangular, Elliptical, Linear, or Polygonal Zones.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change existing Zone selection, Zone movement, Zone rotation, or Plant/Annotation creation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Zone drawing tools must respect the Zones Layer lock.

**Current behavior:**
Layer locks are visible to users and block editing existing objects, but Zone drawing tools can still append new Zone entities to the Zones Layer while that Layer is locked.

**Desired behavior:**
When the Zones Layer is locked, all Zone creation entry points must refuse to create new Zone entities. This includes Rectangular, Elliptical, Linear, and Polygonal Zone drawing. The guard must be enforced at the mutation path, not only by disabling visible UI, so pointer flows and tool internals cannot bypass it. Refused creation should cancel or suppress transient drawing state coherently and must not create document history.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Layer lock state
- Zone drawing tool state machines
- Scene Edit transaction boundary for Zone creation
- Canvas transient preview cleanup

**Out of scope:**
- Changing existing Zone edit behavior on unlocked Layers
- Changing Plant or Annotation creation guards
- Changing Zone geometry semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rectangular Zone creation is blocked while the Zones Layer is locked.
- [ ] Elliptical Zone creation is blocked while the Zones Layer is locked.
- [ ] Linear Zone creation is blocked while the Zones Layer is locked.
- [ ] Polygonal Zone creation is blocked while the Zones Layer is locked.
- [ ] Refused Zone creation does not add history, mark the document dirty, or leave stale drawing previews.
- [ ] Zone creation still works normally when the Zones Layer is unlocked.
- [ ] Regression tests cover locked and unlocked Zones Layer creation paths.
close_reason: Blocked rectangle, ellipse, line, and polygon Zone creation when the Zones Layer is hidden or locked, including polygon draft cleanup. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Block annotation creation on locked Annotation layers
description:
## Slice Summary
When the Annotations scene Layer is locked, text annotation creation must refuse to add new Annotation entities to that locked Layer.

## User Value
A user can lock the Annotations Layer and trust that text creation cannot modify it by adding new annotations.

## Out of Scope
This slice does not change existing Annotation selection, Annotation movement, Annotation rotation, or Plant/Zone creation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Text annotation creation must respect the Annotations Layer lock.

**Current behavior:**
Layer locks are visible to users and block editing existing objects, but text annotation creation can still append new Annotation entities to the Annotations Layer while that Layer is locked.

**Desired behavior:**
When the Annotations Layer is locked, text annotation creation must refuse to create new Annotation entities. The guard must be enforced at the mutation path, not only by disabling visible UI, so direct text-tool flows cannot bypass it. Refused creation should avoid document history and should not leave stale text input overlays or transient selection state.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Layer lock state
- Text annotation tool creation flow
- Scene Edit transaction boundary for Annotation creation
- Text input overlay lifecycle

**Out of scope:**
- Changing existing Annotation edit behavior on unlocked Layers
- Changing Plant or Zone creation guards
- Changing annotation rotation, rendering, or hit testing behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Text annotation creation is blocked while the Annotations Layer is locked.
- [ ] Refused Annotation creation does not add history, mark the document dirty, or leave stale text input overlays.
- [ ] Annotation creation still works normally when the Annotations Layer is unlocked.
- [ ] Existing Annotation selection and Unlock behavior remains unchanged.
- [ ] Regression tests cover locked and unlocked Annotations Layer creation paths.
close_reason: Blocked text Annotation creation when the Annotations Layer is hidden or locked and prevented stale text editors or scene edits. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Keep selection action tooltips above rotation controls
description: Selection Action Toolbar hover tooltips can be obscured by the Rotation Handle because both overlays occupy the space above a selected canvas object. Users should be able to read action tooltips on hover; rotation controls must not cover them.
close_reason: Fixed Selection Action Toolbar tooltip layering by opening action tooltips above the toolbar and keeping the toolbar stacking layer above the Rotation Handle. Added a canvas interaction regression test. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Allow selected locked objects to unlock from action bar
description: Locked canvas Design Objects currently cannot be selected, so users only get a locked tooltip and cannot reach the action bar to unlock the object. The desired behavior is that clicking a locked object creates a locked selection, shows the Selection Action Toolbar with Unlock active and other mutation buttons disabled, then after Unlock the object becomes an editable selection with normal actions and Lock shown again.
close_reason: Fixed locked Design Object UX by allowing direct locked hits to become selected locked targets, showing the Selection Action Toolbar with Unlock enabled and other primary mutation actions disabled, and preserving non-editable semantics until unlock. Tests: npm test -- scene-runtime/selection.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts; npm test -- scene-runtime.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: Honor annotation rotation across canvas runtime
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the canvas rotation behavior for text annotations so annotation rotation is consumed consistently anywhere annotations are displayed or selected. Rotating a selected annotation, or a mixed selection containing annotations, should visibly rotate the annotation instead of only translating its unrotated anchor.

## User Value
Users can trust the Rotation Handle for annotations and mixed selections because the visible canvas result matches the saved design object transform.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign annotation editing, text layout controls, or add new annotation manipulation UI. Do not broaden this into unrelated selection or rotation behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make annotation rotation an end-to-end canvas runtime behavior instead of write-only state.

**Current behavior:**
The Rotation Handle can write a new angle to a text annotation while moving its anchor around the selection pivot. Other canvas runtime paths still treat annotations as unrotated, so the visible text, selection bounds, and hit testing can disagree with the saved `rotationDeg` value.

**Desired behavior:**
When an annotation has a non-zero rotation, the canvas displays the text at that rotation and every runtime surface that reasons about the annotation uses the same oriented geometry. This includes rendering, hit testing, selection/read-model bounds, selection overlays, and mixed-selection rotation previews or commits. Rotating a standalone annotation should visibly rotate around the intended pivot. Rotating a mixed selection containing annotations should keep the annotation visually aligned with the committed transform.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Design Object annotation data, including annotation position and rotation angle.
- Scene runtime rendering surfaces for annotations.
- Scene runtime target projection, hit testing, and selection/read-model bounds for annotations.
- Selection Rotation Handle transaction behavior for single and multi-selection rotation.

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning annotation authoring, inline editing, typography, wrapping, or text measurement controls.
- Adding new annotation rotation UI beyond making the existing Rotation Handle correct.
- Changing plant, zone, layer, lock, or Object Group semantics except where needed to keep existing mixed-selection rotation tests valid.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A selected annotation with a non-zero rotation renders its text visibly rotated by that angle.
- [ ] Annotation hit testing and selection/read-model bounds honor annotation rotation instead of using only unrotated text bounds.
- [ ] Dragging the Rotation Handle on a standalone annotation rotates the visible annotation and persists the new angle.
- [ ] Dragging the Rotation Handle on a mixed selection containing an annotation rotates the annotation visibly around the shared pivot while preserving existing plant, zone, lock, and Object Group behavior.
- [ ] Focused frontend regression tests cover annotation rendering/bounds/hit testing or the closest available runtime seams for rotated annotations.
- [ ] Required frontend quality gates pass, including the focused test file and `npx tsc --noEmit`.
close_reason: Implemented annotation rotation as visible runtime geometry: renderers rotate text and highlights, hit testing uses oriented text geometry, and selection/read-model bounds use rotated annotation bounds. Tests: npm test -- hit-testing.test.ts scene-runtime/selection.test.ts pixi-scene.test.ts canvas2d-scene.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts camera-controller.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: Add Species Selection
description:
## Slice Summary
Add Select-tool Species Selection: double-clicking a visible editable top-level Placed Plant selects all visible editable top-level Placed Plants with the same Canonical Name across the design, and Shift+double-click toggles that Species set. Also expose the command in the Selection Action Toolbar when the current plant selection has one clear reference Species.

## User Value
Users can quickly work with all placed instances of a Species without manually building a multi-selection.

## Out of Scope
Do not add equivalent select-same behavior for Zones, Annotations, or Object Groups. Do not apply Species Selection in non-Select tools or through planning Target Presentation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add Select-tool Species Selection by double-click and toolbar command.

**Current behavior:**
Users must manually select multiple Placed Plants of the same Species. The canvas has Species identity through Canonical Name, but no direct selection gesture for all visible editable same-Species placed plants.

**Desired behavior:**
With the Select tool active, double-clicking a visible editable top-level Placed Plant selects every visible editable top-level Placed Plant in the design with the same Canonical Name. Shift+double-click toggles that Species set against the current selection. The Selection Action Toolbar exposes Select Same Species when the current plant selection has one clear reference Canonical Name. Species Selection does not mutate Design data, Target Presentation, history, or dirty state.

**Key interfaces:**
- Select tool pointer handling
- Selection model eligibility
- Canonical Name identity
- Selection Action Toolbar command filtering
- Canvas Query/Command selection surfaces
- Selection labels and presentation invalidation

**Out of scope:**
- Similar selection for Zones, Annotations, or Object Groups
- Non-Select tool double-click behavior
- Planning Target Presentation changes
- Selecting grouped members or locked/hidden/Layer-locked plants
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Plain double-click with the Select tool on an eligible Placed Plant replaces selection with visible editable top-level Placed Plants sharing its Canonical Name.
- [ ] Shift+double-click toggles that Species set against the current selection.
- [ ] Species Selection excludes grouped members, locked Design Objects, hidden Layers, and locked Layers.
- [ ] Species Selection applies across the whole design, not only the current viewport.
- [ ] The Selection Action Toolbar exposes Select Same Species only when there is one clear reference Species.
- [ ] Species Selection updates highlights, selection labels, and toolbar placement immediately without dirtying the document or adding history.
- [ ] Focused tests cover double-click, Shift+double-click, Canonical Name identity, exclusions, Select-tool-only behavior, toolbar command availability, and no history/dirty mutation.
close_reason: Shipped Select-tool Species Selection by double-click and Shift double-click, Select Same Species toolbar action, command/read-model support, and non-dirty selection behavior. Tests: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime/selection.test.ts scene-runtime/mutations.test.ts canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: Make Design Object Locks discoverable
description:
## Slice Summary
Expose Design Object Lock behavior through visible canvas and selection affordances. Locked Design Objects remain hover-discoverable but not selectable; editable selections can lock/unlock through the toolbar; locked objects can show a direct unlock affordance; the legacy no-selection unlock-all shortcut behavior is removed.

## User Value
Users understand why an object cannot be edited and can unlock it without knowing a hidden shortcut.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement Layer lock row controls here. Do not introduce a special locked selection state or unlock-all behavior unless it is a separate explicit command.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Make Design Object Locks visible, discoverable, and directly usable.

**Current behavior:**
Design Object Locks exist in persisted state and runtime commands, but the UX is mostly hidden. Locked objects are not selected or dragged, and the legacy shortcut behavior can unlock all objects when nothing is selected.

**Desired behavior:**
Locked Design Objects remain visible and hover-discoverable without becoming editable selections. Hover reveals a lock cue/action. Editable selections can lock/unlock through the Selection Action Toolbar. Direct unlock is possible from the locked-object affordance. `Cmd/Ctrl+L` affects only the current editable selection, and unlock-all is not triggered by an empty selection.

**Key interfaces:**
- Design Object Lock state
- Selection model eligibility and locked-object hover state
- Selection Action Toolbar command slots
- Shortcut command handling
- Scene Edit history and serialization

**Out of scope:**
- Layer lock controls
- Special locked selection state
- Implicit unlock-all behavior
- Custom context menus
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Locked Design Objects can be hovered/discovered without becoming editable selections.
- [ ] A locked-object affordance communicates the locked state and offers direct unlock where appropriate.
- [ ] Editable selections expose Lock/Unlock through the Selection Action Toolbar when valid.
- [ ] `Cmd/Ctrl+L` no longer unlocks all Design Objects when no editable selection is present.
- [ ] Lock/unlock mutations remain Scene Edits with correct undo/redo, dirty state, and serialization behavior.
- [ ] Mixed or stale locked/unlocked selection states are refreshed or refused instead of partially mutating silently.
- [ ] Focused tests cover locked hover discovery, direct unlock, toolbar lock/unlock, shortcut behavior, and no special locked selection.
close_reason: Shipped discoverable Design Object lock UX: Lock action in Selection Action Toolbar, direct locked-object hover unlock affordance, no special locked selection, removed no-selection unlock-all behavior, docs/i18n updates, and frontend gates passing: npm test; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Support oriented shaped Zones
description:
## Slice Summary
Add first-class orientation support for Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones while preserving backward compatibility for existing designs. Oriented shaped Zones should load, save, render, hit-test, and report Zone Measurements as the same Zone kind instead of degrading into Polygonal Zones.

## User Value
Users can later rotate Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones without losing shape-specific behavior or measurements.

## Out of Scope
Do not add the Rotation Handle or general selection rotation in this slice. Do not rewrite all Zone geometry to a new center/width/height representation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Add backward-compatible orientation support for Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones.

**Current behavior:**
Rectangular and Elliptical Zones are effectively axis-aligned. Elliptical Zones store center plus radii, while Rectangular Zones store points, and neither has an explicit orientation concept that shaped-zone rotation can preserve.

**Desired behavior:**
Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones can carry optional orientation in degrees. Missing orientation loads as zero degrees. New saves preserve orientation while keeping the Zone kind. Rendering, hit testing, Zone Measurements, bounds, and file compatibility treat oriented shaped Zones as shaped Zones, not as generic Polygonal Zones.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canopi file Zone contract and generated bindings
- Scene hydration/serialization
- Zone renderer projections
- Zone hit testing and bounds
- Zone Measurements
- Document format round-trip behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Adding user-facing Rotation Handle behavior
- Rewriting all shaped Zone geometry to a center/width/height model
- Converting shaped Zones into Polygonal Zones
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Existing designs without Zone orientation load with zero-degree shaped-zone orientation.
- [ ] New saves preserve explicit orientation for Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones without changing their Zone kind.
- [ ] Shared Rust/TypeScript contracts and generated bindings include the orientation field as needed.
- [ ] Renderers draw oriented Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones consistently in light and dark themes.
- [ ] Hit testing and selection/bounds logic respect oriented shaped Zone geometry.
- [ ] Zone Measurements describe oriented shaped Zone geometry, not axis-aligned bounds.
- [ ] Focused file-format, contract, renderer, hit-testing, and measurement tests cover backward compatibility and oriented shaped Zones.
close_reason: Implemented backward-compatible Zone orientation support: Zone.rotation contract and generated bindings, SceneZoneEntity.rotationDeg hydration/serialization, oriented shaped Zone rendering, hit testing, bounds, object stamp previews, and measurements. Verified with npm test, npx tsc --noEmit, npm run check:types, cargo fmt --all -- --check, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo check --workspace, CANOPI_SKIP_BUNDLED_DB=1 cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Add single-selection Rotation Handle
description:
## Slice Summary
Add runtime-owned Rotation Handle behavior for one eligible visibly rotatable Design Object. Users can drag the handle to rotate a single annotation or Zone with live angle readout, Shift snapping, Escape abort, off-canvas continuation, and one Scene Edit on commit.

## User Value
Users can orient individual labels, beds, paths, and boundaries directly on the canvas.

## Out of Scope
Do not support multi-selection or Object Group rotation in this slice. Do not add numeric rotation input, custom pivots, or multi-touch rotation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add a runtime-owned Rotation Handle for one eligible selected Design Object.

**Current behavior:**
Selected Design Objects can be moved and edited in some ways, but users cannot rotate a single annotation, Linear Zone, Rectangular Zone, Elliptical Zone, or Polygonal Zone through a visible canvas affordance.

**Desired behavior:**
When one eligible visibly rotatable Design Object is selected, the canvas shows a screen-space Rotation Handle above the selection bounds. Dragging it rotates the object around the default Rotation Pivot, shows a compact signed delta readout, supports Shift snapping to 15-degree increments, aborts on Escape, commits on pointer-up including off-canvas pointer-up, and creates one Scene Edit only when the final angle crosses the no-op threshold.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection model bounds and eligibility
- Scene Interaction Frame/shared gesture lifetime
- Scene Edit transactions and undo/redo history
- Rotation geometry helpers
- Zone orientation support
- Runtime-owned DOM overlays

**Out of scope:**
- Multi-selection rotation
- Object Group rotation
- Numeric rotation input
- Custom Rotation Pivot editing
- Multi-touch rotation
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Eligible single selections show a Rotation Handle; single Placed Plants do not show one.
- [ ] Dragging the Rotation Handle updates geometry/orientation live and displays signed clockwise delta degrees.
- [ ] Holding Shift snaps the displayed and applied delta to 15-degree increments.
- [ ] Escape aborts the active rotation and restores starting geometry without a history entry.
- [ ] Pointer-up commits one Scene Edit when the angle delta exceeds the threshold, including pointer-up outside the canvas.
- [ ] Tiny/no-op angle deltas abort without dirtying the document or creating undo history.
- [ ] Rotation respects Design Object Locks, hidden Layers, and locked Layers.
- [ ] Focused interaction tests cover drag lifecycle, snapping, abort, commit, off-canvas behavior, and no-op threshold.
close_reason: Implemented runtime-owned single-selection Rotation Handle with live signed readout, Shift snapping, Escape/no-op aborts, off-canvas pointer-up commit, lock/layer suppression, and oriented Zone selection bounds. Verified with npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime/selection.test.ts i18n-completeness.test.ts, npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, npm run check:types, and git diff --check.

title: Rotate multi-selection and Object Groups
description:
## Slice Summary
Extend Design Object Rotation to multi-object selections, mixed selections, plant arrangements, and Object Groups using one shared Rotation Pivot. Object Groups should rotate by mutating member geometry as the visible source of truth.

## User Value
Users can turn whole arrangements, grouped clusters, and mixed canvas selections without rebuilding their layouts manually.

## Out of Scope
Do not add group editing, custom pivots, or per-plant visual orientation. Do not change same-Layer Object Group creation rules.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Extend Design Object Rotation to multi-selection and Object Groups.

**Current behavior:**
Rotation is not available for multi-object arrangements. Object Groups move by mutating member geometry, and future rotation needs to preserve that visible-source-of-truth model.

**Desired behavior:**
