Editable multi-selections rotate around one shared Rotation Pivot derived from combined visual bounds. Placed Plants move around the pivot without gaining individual orientation. Annotations move and rotate visible text. Linear and Polygonal Zones rotate points. Rectangular and Elliptical Zones move centers and increment orientation. Object Groups rotate by mutating member geometry rather than applying a hidden wrapper transform.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection model combined bounds
- Single-selection rotation gesture and transaction flow
- Scene Edit transactions
- Object Group membership and lock semantics
- Rotation geometry helpers
- Renderer and measurement invalidation

**Out of scope:**
- Group member editing or nested group editing
- Cross-Layer Object Group creation
- Per-plant directional symbols or plant orientation display
- Custom Rotation Pivot editing
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Multi-selection uses one shared Rotation Pivot at the combined visual bounds center.
- [ ] Multi-plant selections rotate plant positions without adding visible plant orientation.
- [ ] Mixed selections rotate plants, annotations, Zones, and Object Groups coherently in one Scene Edit.
- [ ] Object Group rotation mutates member geometry as the visible source of truth and preserves group membership.
- [ ] Rotation refuses or aborts when selected objects become locked, hidden, or Layer-locked during the gesture.
- [ ] Selection labels and relevant overlays refresh during or immediately after rotation without stale presentation.
- [ ] Focused tests cover multi-selection, mixed selection, Object Groups, locked members, and undo/redo behavior.
close_reason: Implemented multi-selection and Object Group rotation through the runtime Rotation Handle: shared pivot, plant position rotation without plant orientation, mixed selection transforms, group member geometry mutation, locked-member suppression, and docs update. Verified with npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime/selection.test.ts, npx tsc --noEmit, npm test, and git diff --check.

title: Strengthen canvas selection highlights
description:
## Slice Summary
Make canvas selected, hover, locked-object hover, and locked-Layer hover states visibly distinct and consistent with the field-notebook design system. The selected state should be stronger and easier to read across light/dark themes and renderer backends.

## User Value
Users can clearly see which Design Objects are selected or blocked before they transform or act on them.

## Out of Scope
Do not add lock actions, the Selection Action Toolbar, or the Rotation Handle. Do not introduce green UI chrome.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Strengthen and differentiate canvas selection highlight states.

**Current behavior:**
Canvas selection highlights are too subtle, especially for the rotation and lock UX planned in the PRD. Hover, selected, locked-object hover, and locked-Layer hover are not yet clearly differentiated as one coherent visual language.

**Desired behavior:**
Selected Design Objects are clearly visible across plants, Zones, annotations, themes, and renderer backends. Hover states remain lighter than selected states. Locked Design Object hover and locked-Layer hover are visibly distinct without creating green UI chrome or clutter. Styling follows the field-notebook design system and remains screen-readable across zoom.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas theme tokens and selection colors
- Renderer selection/highlight projections
- Runtime hover state
- Selection model output
- Layer and Design Object lock states

**Out of scope:**
- Adding lock/unlock actions
- Adding the Selection Action Toolbar
- Adding the Rotation Handle
- Changing plant color semantics
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Selected Design Objects are visibly stronger than hover states in both light and dark themes.
- [ ] Plants, Zones, and annotations receive coherent selected and hover treatment across renderer backends.
- [ ] Locked Design Object hover and locked-Layer hover have distinct visual cues that do not imply editable selection.
- [ ] Highlight stroke/halo weight remains screen-readable across zoom without scaling into heavy world geometry.
- [ ] No green UI chrome is introduced for selection controls or highlights.
- [ ] Focused renderer/theme tests or equivalent visual assertions cover the updated highlight states.
close_reason: Strengthened canvas selection/hover visuals across Pixi and Canvas2D: selected strokes are stronger than hover, locked Design Object and locked Layer hover states get distinct non-green cues, visible-hover hit testing explains locked targets without making them editable, and rgba canvas tokens now parse correctly in Pixi. Verified with npm test and npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add Selection Action Toolbar shell
description:
## Slice Summary
Add the runtime-owned Selection Action Toolbar surface for editable selections. The toolbar should position itself around selection bounds, avoid the Rotation Handle, expose icon-only command slots with tooltips, avoid autofocus, preserve explicit keyboard accessibility, and suppress only canvas-scoped native defaults that interfere with canvas work.

## User Value
Users get a coherent place for object commands without memorizing hidden shortcuts or using the left Canvas Toolbar for selection actions.

## Out of Scope
Do not implement lock badge behavior, Layer lock controls, Species Selection, or new rotation precision inputs in this slice. Do not move plant color into the toolbar.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Add the runtime-owned Selection Action Toolbar shell for editable selections.

**Current behavior:**
Common object commands exist through shortcuts or menus, but there is no compact contextual canvas toolbar for the current editable Design Object selection.

**Desired behavior:**
Editable selections automatically show a compact Selection Action Toolbar that positions near selection bounds, avoids the Rotation Handle, does not take focus automatically, uses icon-only buttons with tooltips, preserves explicit keyboard activation when focused, and dispatches existing object commands through the command surface. Canvas-native defaults such as context menu are suppressed only on canvas interaction surfaces and overlays.

**Key interfaces:**
- Selection model bounds and eligibility
- Runtime-owned DOM overlay lifecycle
- App command graph / canvas command surfaces
- Button tooltip and icon patterns
- Canvas scoped default suppression

**Out of scope:**
- Implementing lock badge behavior
- Adding Species Selection
- Moving plant color into the toolbar
- Adding custom context menus
- Adding numeric rotation input
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Editable selections show a compact Selection Action Toolbar without stealing focus.
- [ ] Toolbar placement avoids selected geometry where possible and does not overlap the Rotation Handle.
- [ ] Toolbar buttons are icon-only with tooltips and shortcut labels where commands have shortcuts.
- [ ] Toolbar buttons are keyboard-focusable and activatable only when the user explicitly focuses them.
- [ ] Invalid commands are hidden unless a blocked disabled state is clearer for the user.
- [ ] Existing object commands dispatch through the established command surfaces rather than duplicating mutation logic.
- [ ] Native context menu suppression is scoped to canvas interaction surfaces/overlays and does not affect inputs, menus, dialogs, or non-canvas panels.
- [ ] Focused component/interaction tests cover visibility, placement, focus behavior, command dispatch, and default suppression.
close_reason: Shipped runtime-owned Selection Action Toolbar shell with focus-preserving icon actions, command-surface dispatch, canvas-scoped contextmenu suppression, i18n labels, docs update, and frontend tests/typecheck passing: npm test; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Deepen Design Object selection bounds and eligibility
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the shared selection read model for editable Design Object eligibility and combined visual bounds. The slice should let callers ask which selected objects are editable, why visible objects are excluded, and where selection overlays should anchor without duplicating lock, Layer, Object Group, Visual Footprint, Zone, or Annotation rules.

## User Value
Users get more predictable selection behavior as later toolbar, lock, Species Selection, and rotation slices all use the same eligibility and bounds rules.

## Out of Scope
Do not add the Rotation Handle, Selection Action Toolbar, lock badges, or Species Selection gesture in this slice. Do not change user-visible selection styling beyond what is necessary to prove the read model.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Deepen the shared selection model for editable Design Object eligibility and visual bounds.

**Current behavior:**
Selection, hit testing, grouping, locking, Visual Footprint bounds, annotation bounds, Layer visibility, and Layer locks are resolved in several nearby runtime paths. Future rotation, toolbar, lock, and Species Selection work needs one stable read model instead of duplicating these rules.

**Desired behavior:**
Runtime callers can ask for the current editable top-level Design Object selection, excluded/blocked objects, and combined visual bounds using one shared selection model. The model respects Design Object Locks, Layer visibility, Layer locks, Object Group boundaries, plant Visual Footprints, annotation readable bounds, and Zone geometry. It produces enough information for Rotation Pivot calculation, Rotation Handle placement, Selection Action Toolbar placement, and Species Selection eligibility.

**Key interfaces:**
- SceneStore persisted/session state
- Canvas Query Surface selection reads
- Scene Interaction Frame/shared selection behavior
- Plant Visual Footprint and annotation bounds helpers
- Layer visibility and lock projections
- Object Group top-level selection semantics

**Out of scope:**
- Rendering stronger highlights
- Adding Rotation Handle behavior
- Adding Selection Action Toolbar UI
- Adding lock badges or Species Selection gestures
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A shared selection model returns editable top-level Design Object selections without duplicating lock, Layer, Object Group, plant, annotation, and Zone rules in callers.
- [ ] Combined visual bounds include plant Visual Footprints, annotation readable bounds, Zone geometry, and Object Group member geometry as users perceive them.
- [ ] Hidden Layers, locked Layers, locked Design Objects, and grouped members are excluded from editable selection outputs according to the PRD.
- [ ] The model exposes enough structured reason/state for later locked-object and locked-Layer hover affordances without making locked objects editable selections.
- [ ] Focused tests cover plants, annotations, Zones, Object Groups, hidden Layers, locked Layers, Design Object Locks, and mixed selections.
- [ ] Existing selection, drag, grouping, and plant color behavior remains unchanged except where the shared model intentionally centralizes existing rules.
close_reason: Implemented CanvasQuerySurface.getDesignObjectSelection() as the shared editable Design Object selection read model, centralized mutation commands on it, documented the seam, and verified with npx tsc --noEmit plus full frontend npm test.

title: Expose Object Group commands
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Group and Ungroup visible through selection actions when valid. The behavior should preserve current Object Group semantics, including same-Layer grouping and top-level group selection, while making the commands discoverable outside shortcuts.

## User Value
Users can create and break apart Object Groups as an intentional canvas workflow rather than a hidden shortcut feature.

## Out of Scope
Do not add nested group editing, member editing, cross-Layer Object Groups, or group style changes.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Expose Object Group and Ungroup commands through selection actions.

**Current behavior:**
Object Groups are persisted and used by selection, movement, deletion, duplication, and Object Stamp, but Group/Ungroup are effectively shortcut-only and hard to discover.

**Desired behavior:**
The Selection Action Toolbar exposes Group when the editable selection can form an Object Group and Ungroup when the selection contains eligible Object Groups. Same-Layer grouping semantics remain unchanged. Grouped members remain top-level-inaccessible through ordinary selection, and Object Groups continue to move/transform as one arrangement.

**Key interfaces:**
- Object Group scene mutations
- Selection Action Toolbar command filtering
- Selection model eligibility and Layer checks
- App command graph / canvas command surface
- Object Stamp source behavior

**Out of scope:**
- Cross-Layer Object Groups
- Nested group editing or member editing
- Group naming or visual styling
- Changing Object Stamp behavior except where command discoverability requires it
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Group appears only for editable selections that satisfy current Object Group creation rules.
- [ ] Ungroup appears only for selected Object Groups that can be ungrouped.
- [ ] Toolbar Group/Ungroup dispatch through existing command surfaces and preserve undo/redo, dirty state, and serialization behavior.
- [ ] Same-Layer grouping semantics remain intact.
- [ ] Locked Design Objects, locked Layers, hidden Layers, and stale invalid selections block Group/Ungroup clearly.
- [ ] Focused tests cover command availability, grouping, ungrouping, same-Layer restrictions, locks, and toolbar dispatch.
close_reason: Shipped discoverable Group/Ungroup Selection Action Toolbar commands with selection-model availability filtering, command-surface dispatch, i18n labels, docs update, and frontend gates passing: npm test; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Expose Layer lock UX
description:
## Slice Summary
Make Layer locks visible and usable in the Layer Panel and reflect Layer-locked object state on canvas hover. Layer lock cues should be distinct from Design Object Lock cues and should point users toward the Layer Panel authority.

## User Value
Users can distinguish object-level locks from Layer-level edit restrictions and control Layer locks from the natural Layer surface.

## Out of Scope
Do not add arbitrary user-created Layers or a full Layer management system. Do not add object-level unlock actions for Layer-locked objects.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** feature
**Summary:** Expose Layer lock controls and distinct locked-Layer canvas cues.

**Current behavior:**
Layers are fixed visibility/locking groups and Layer lock state exists in the presentation model and command surface, but the visible Layer Panel does not clearly expose lock controls and canvas cues do not distinguish Layer locks from Design Object Locks.

**Desired behavior:**
Scene Layers show clear lock controls in the Layer Panel. Objects on locked Layers remain non-editable and show a distinct locked-Layer hover cue that points users toward the Layer Panel authority rather than object-level unlock. Hidden Layers remain non-interactive.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Layer Presentation
- Layer Panel row controls
- Layer command surface
- Selection model eligibility and hover cues
- Scene interaction hit testing

**Out of scope:**
- Arbitrary user-created Layers
- Full Layer management/reordering
- Object-level unlock actions for Layer-locked objects
- Map/terrain Layer locking
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Scene Layer rows expose visible lock controls that write through Canvas Layer Presentation.
- [ ] Layer lock controls follow the field-notebook design system and are accessible.
- [ ] Objects on locked Layers are not selectable, transformable, deletable, groupable, or stampable.
- [ ] Hovering an object on a locked Layer shows a distinct locked-Layer cue, not a Design Object Lock cue.
- [ ] Hidden Layers suppress hit testing, hover cues, selection, toolbar actions, and Rotation Handles.
- [ ] Focused tests cover Layer Panel lock controls, authority-correct writes, canvas hover cues, and locked/hidden Layer interactivity.
close_reason: Shipped visible Layer Panel lock controls through Canvas Layer Presentation, distinct locked-Layer affordance coverage, and hidden-Layer hit-test coverage. Tests: npm test -- layer-panel.test.tsx canvas-layer-presentation.test.ts hit-testing.test.ts scene-runtime/presentation.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.

title: PRD: Canvas Selection Actions and Rotation
description:
## Problem Statement

Permaculture designers need to rotate selected Design Objects on the canvas so they can orient beds, paths, annotations, groups, and plant arrangements to match real site conditions. Today the canvas has movement, selection, grouping, locking, layer visibility, and hidden shortcut commands, but the transform UX is fragmented: rotation is not exposed, selection highlights are too subtle, Design Object Locks and Object Groups are difficult to discover, Layer locks exist in the model but are not clearly controlled, and selecting all placed plants of the same Species requires manual multi-selection.

The result is that users cannot confidently manipulate a selected arrangement, and they cannot reliably understand why some visible objects are editable while others are locked, grouped, hidden by Layers, or unavailable for transformation.

## Solution

Add a coherent canvas selection UX centered on editable Design Object selections. Users can rotate visible editable Design Objects with an on-canvas Rotation Handle, use a compact Selection Action Toolbar for common object commands, see stronger selection highlights, discover and manage Design Object Locks and Layer locks, use visible Group/Ungroup affordances, and double-click a Placed Plant to select visible editable Placed Plants of the same Species.

Rotation uses a shared Rotation Pivot for multi-selection and preserves shape intent for Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones. The UX follows the field-notebook design system: ochre canvas affordances, precise icon controls, fixed screen-space overlay sizing, no green UI chrome, no bulky floating cards, and no automatic focus changes.

## Success Criteria

- Users can rotate supported editable Design Object selections with a discoverable on-canvas Rotation Handle, including single annotations, single zones, Object Groups, and multi-object arrangements.
- Rotation preserves shape intent for Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones, creates one undoable Scene Edit per committed drag, and does not dirty the document for no-op drags or selection-only gestures.
- Selection highlights, Rotation Handle placement, live angle readout, and Selection Action Toolbar are clear in light and dark themes without violating the field-notebook design system.
- Design Object Locks, Layer locks, and Object Groups are discoverable through visible canvas or panel affordances, while preserving the invariant that editable selection excludes locked or hidden objects.
- Species Selection lets users double-click a Placed Plant in Select tool mode to select all visible editable top-level Placed Plants with the same Canonical Name.
- Existing canvas authority boundaries remain intact: SceneStore stays the canvas state authority, Scene Edits own canvas mutations, and renderers remain projections rather than interaction owners.

## User Stories

1. As a designer, I want to rotate one selected annotation, zone, or linear feature, so that labels, beds, paths, and boundaries can match the orientation of my site.
2. As a designer, I want to rotate multiple selected Design Objects around one shared Rotation Pivot, so that a whole arrangement keeps its relative layout while turning together.
3. As a designer, I want Object Groups to rotate like one arrangement, so that grouped clusters and stamped patterns remain easy to manipulate.
4. As a designer, I want Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones to remain their original Zone kinds after rotation, so that shape-specific editing and Zone Measurements still make sense.
5. As a designer, I want selection highlights and rotation affordances to be clearly visible, so that I can tell what will be transformed before I drag.
6. As a designer, I want Shift-drag rotation snapping and a live angle readout, so that I can make precise turns without opening a separate panel.
7. As a designer, I want rotation drags to behave like other canvas drags, so that Escape cancels, pointer-up commits, and off-canvas movement remains predictable.
8. As a designer, I want a Selection Action Toolbar for common commands, so that Delete, Duplicate, ordering, Lock/Unlock, Group/Ungroup, and Select Same Species are discoverable without memorizing shortcuts.
9. As a designer, I want locked Design Objects to be visible and discoverable without becoming editable selections, so that I understand why an object cannot be moved, rotated, deleted, grouped, or stamped.
10. As a designer, I want an obvious way to unlock a locked Design Object from the canvas, so that I do not need to know a hidden shortcut.
11. As a designer, I want Layer locks to be visible in the Layer Panel and reflected on canvas hover, so that I can distinguish object-level locks from Layer-level edit restrictions.
12. As a designer, I want Object Group commands to be visible when valid, so that grouping is a coherent canvas feature rather than a hidden shortcut.
13. As a designer, I want double-clicking a Placed Plant to select all visible editable Placed Plants of the same Species, so that I can quickly recolor, duplicate, delete, group, or inspect a species placement pattern.
14. As a keyboard user, I want toolbar actions to be keyboard-accessible only when I intentionally focus the toolbar, so that visual highlights on canvas do not unexpectedly become keyboard targets.

## Implementation Decisions

- Use the project term Design Object, not element, shape, or canvas object, for placed plants, zones, annotations, and object groups.
- Treat rotation as a Scene Edit because it mutates canvas-owned Design state and must participate in undo/redo, dirty state, serialization, and render invalidation.
- The primary rotation affordance is an on-canvas Rotation Handle shown when rotation has visible meaning. It appears above the combined selection bounds, with the Rotation Pivot at the center of those same bounds.
- Do not show the Rotation Handle for a single selected Placed Plant, because its current circular symbol has no visible orientation. Show it for multi-plant selections, mixed selections, annotations, linear zones, rectangular zones, elliptical zones, polygonal zones, and object groups.
- Compute multi-selection Rotation Pivot and Rotation Handle placement from combined axis-aligned visual bounds. Plant bounds use current Visual Footprint. Annotation bounds use readable text bounds. Zone bounds use current visible geometry.
- Rotation Handle, live rotation readout, lock badges, and Selection Action Toolbar are runtime-owned DOM overlays coordinated by scene interaction behavior. They are not renderer-owned projections and not outer Preact chrome reaching into runtime internals.
- Rotation overlays use fixed screen-space sizing and follow the interface design system: ochre selection color, thin ruled outlines/stems, compact icon controls, fixed readable stroke weights, tokenized sizes, no green chrome, and no decorative card treatment.
- Rotation starts on pointer-down on the Rotation Handle, updates live during pointer movement, commits as one undoable Scene Edit on pointer-up if the final delta crosses a small angular threshold, and aborts without history when the delta is effectively zero.
- Escape during rotation aborts the active rotation transaction, restores starting geometry, hides the live angle readout, and leaves selection unchanged.
- Pointer release outside the canvas commits rotation, and off-canvas movement is edge-clamped consistently with existing canvas drawing and movement expectations.
- Shift during rotation snaps to 15-degree increments. Rotation does not snap to grid or guides in this scope.
- The drag readout shows signed clockwise delta degrees normalized to `-180` through `180`. Stored orientation values use degrees and are normalized to `0 <= degrees < 360` where orientation fields exist.
- Add optional Zone orientation support for file compatibility. Missing orientation means `0` degrees. The optional field may exist on Zone records for schema simplicity, but only Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones interpret it.
- Preserve shaped Zone kind during rotation. Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones keep explicit orientation instead of being converted to Polygonal Zones. This follows the accepted architecture decision for shaped-zone rotation.
- Rotate Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones by moving their centers around the active Rotation Pivot and incrementing orientation while preserving intrinsic size. Rotate Linear Zones and Polygonal Zones by directly rotating their saved points.
- Placed Plants rotate by moving their positions around the Rotation Pivot. Do not assign individual plant orientation until plant symbols have visible direction.
- Annotations rotate by moving their positions and rotating visible text orientation.
- Object Group rotation mutates member geometry as the visible source of truth. The group wrapper is not a hidden transform surface. Mixed selections containing groups and standalone Design Objects rotate as one selected arrangement.
- Rotation respects Design Object Locks and Layer locks. Locked objects and objects on locked Layers are not editable selections and cannot be transformed. If lock state changes during an active rotation, the gesture should abort rather than partially commit.
- Hidden Layers suppress object hit testing, selection, hover discovery, lock badges, Rotation Handles, and toolbar actions for those objects.
- Strengthen selection highlights as part of this work. Selected state should be visibly stronger than hover state across plants, zones, annotations, light/dark themes, and renderer backends.
- Locked Design Objects remain hover-discoverable but not selectable. Hover can show a lock badge/action. Clicking a locked object must not create a special locked selection.
- Layer-locked objects show a distinct locked-Layer cue that points users toward the Layer Panel, not an object-level unlock.
- `Cmd/Ctrl+L` should affect the current editable selection only. Legacy unlock-all-when-no-selection behavior should not be preserved. Any unlock-all command must be explicit and clearly labeled.
- Keep fixed Layers as a long-term product concept. Improve Layer Panel discoverability and add visible lock controls for scene Layers. Do not add arbitrary user-created Layer management in this scope.
- Keep Object Groups as a long-term product concept. Expose Group/Ungroup affordances, while preserving current same-Layer Object Group semantics unless a future model change deliberately revisits group layering.
- The Selection Action Toolbar appears automatically for editable selections, does not take focus automatically, and avoids covering selected geometry or the Rotation Handle.
- The Selection Action Toolbar uses icon-only buttons with tooltips and shortcuts. Mostly hide invalid commands rather than rendering a disabled command set. Show disabled states only when a command is conceptually relevant but blocked by a clear condition.
- Include common existing object commands in the Selection Action Toolbar: Delete, Duplicate, Bring Forward, Send Back, Lock/Unlock, Group/Ungroup, and Select Same Species when applicable. Precision rotation may be added there later.
- Keep the existing plant color affordance for now. It may eventually move into selection actions, but that is not required for the first coherent rotation/lock/group pass.
- Selection Action Toolbar buttons remain keyboard-focusable and activatable with Enter/Space only when users explicitly tab or focus into the toolbar. Canvas selection must not auto-focus the toolbar.
- The Rotation Handle itself is not keyboard-focusable in the first version. Keyboard rotation should come through explicit shortcuts or future numeric rotation input.
- Disable native context menu behavior on canvas interaction surfaces and selection overlays only. Do not suppress normal defaults in inputs, textareas, menus, dropdowns, dialogs, Species Catalog surfaces, or other non-canvas UI.
- Species Selection is a Select-tool gesture. Plain double-click on a visible editable top-level Placed Plant selects all visible editable top-level Placed Plants with the same Canonical Name across the whole design. Shift+double-click toggles that Species set against the current selection.
- Species Selection excludes grouped members, locked Design Objects, hidden Layers, and locked Layers. It uses Canonical Name identity, not Common Name. It does not apply in Plant Stamp, Object Stamp, Plant Spacing, Zone drawing, Text, or other non-Select tools.
- Species Selection does not create document history or dirty state. Selection highlights, selection labels, and Selection Action Toolbar placement update immediately after the selection changes.
- Double-click behavior for zones, annotations, and Object Groups is out of scope for this first pass.

## Testing Decisions

- Test behavior through public runtime interaction seams and user-equivalent pointer/keyboard events, not by calling private handlers.
- Add focused geometry tests for rotation math as a deep module: pivot calculation from visual bounds, degree normalization, Shift snapping, threshold handling, point rotation, shaped-zone orientation updates, group member transforms, and annotation orientation changes.
- Add file-format and contract tests for optional Zone orientation, including old files loading with `0` degree orientation and new saves preserving shaped-zone orientation.
- Add renderer/backend behavior tests or visual snapshot-oriented assertions for stronger selection highlights and oriented Rectangular/Elliptical Zone rendering where existing renderer tests already cover scene projections.
- Add hit-testing tests for oriented Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones, locked Design Object hover discovery, locked Layer hover cues, and hidden Layer non-interactivity.
- Add interaction-frame tests for Rotation Handle drag lifecycle: live update, Shift snapping, Escape abort, pointer-up commit, off-canvas commit, edge-clamped movement, no-op threshold abort, and one Scene Edit per drag.
- Add toolbar interaction tests for Selection Action Toolbar visibility, valid command filtering, non-autofocus behavior, explicit keyboard activation, and command dispatch through the app command graph.
- Add Layer Panel tests for visible Layer lock controls and authority-correct lock writes through Canvas Layer Presentation.
- Add lock UX tests for object-level lock badges/actions, no special locked selection state, and removal of legacy unlock-all shortcut behavior.
- Add Object Group command tests showing Group/Ungroup affordances are available only when valid and preserve same-Layer semantics.
- Add Species Selection tests for plain double-click replacement selection, Shift+double-click toggling, Canonical Name identity, whole-design selection, grouped member exclusion, lock/Layer exclusion, Select-tool-only behavior, and no dirty/history mutation.
- Existing prior art includes scene interaction tests, runtime mutation tests, hit-testing tests, renderer tests, command shortcut tests, canvas layer presentation tests, file-format round-trip tests, and frontend boundary tests.

## Out of Scope

- Arbitrary user-created Layers or a full Layer management system.
- Custom Rotation Pivot editing or persistent pivot placement.
- Multi-touch two-finger rotation.
- Custom right-click context menus.
- Group editing, nested group editing, or selecting individual members inside Object Groups.
- Per-plant orientation until plant symbols have visible direction.
- Moving plant color into the Selection Action Toolbar unless a later slice deliberately includes it.
- Numeric rotation inspector, absolute orientation inspector, or keyboard rotation shortcuts beyond the existing shortcut architecture.
- Equivalent double-click "select same" behavior for zones, annotations, or Object Groups.
- Broad global suppression of browser/OS defaults outside canvas interaction surfaces.

## Further Notes

- This PRD is intentionally broad enough to become an epic. Delivery should be split into slices with clear dependencies: selection highlight tuning, shaped-zone orientation schema/geometry, rotation interaction overlays, Selection Action Toolbar, lock/layer UX, Object Group command discoverability, and Species Selection.
- The shaped-zone orientation decision is recorded in the architecture decision record for preserving Rectangular Zones and Elliptical Zones during rotation. Implementation should not replace those shaped zones with polygon approximations.
- Runtime ownership of selection transform overlays is deliberate. Adding outer component seams before the runtime exposes stable read models would likely create leaky authority boundaries.
- Layer lock and Design Object Lock cues must remain visually related but semantically distinct.
- The first implementation should prefer deep geometry and selection-command modules with narrow, testable interfaces so renderer and interaction code can call shared behavior instead of duplicating rotation math or selection eligibility rules.
close_reason: Closed after all 10 child beads shipped: selection read model, oriented shaped Zones, stronger highlights, toolbar, groups, object locks, layer locks, species selection, single-selection rotation, and multi-selection/Object Group rotation. Final child gates included focused interaction/read-model tests, full frontend npm test, TypeScript check, generated binding check on the stack, and whitespace checks.

title: Project Linear Zone targets to map overlays
description:
## Slice Summary
Linear Zones are persisted as two-point Zones and can be used as Budget, Timeline, or Consortium targets. Target map projection currently skips any zone with fewer than three points, so targeted Linear Zones do not render in map hover/selection overlays.

## User Value
When a user hovers or selects a panel item targeting a Linear Zone, map-backed target overlays render that Linear Zone instead of silently omitting it.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Linear Zone persistence, drawing behavior, or target identity resolution. Do not repair the unrelated Location/MapLibre boundary test tracked by canopi-6xlg.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Linear Zone targets project to map-renderable line features when a Location is present.
- [ ] Area Zone targets continue to project as closed polygon features.
- [ ] Invalid non-linear zones with fewer than three points remain skipped.
- [ ] MapLibre panel-target overlay contracts include a line layer that can render Linear Zone features without filling them.
- [ ] Focused target projection and overlay tests pass.
- [ ] TypeScript check passes.
close_reason: Projected Linear Zone targets as GeoJSON LineString features, constrained overlay fill to polygon zones, and covered projection/overlay behavior. Tests: npm test -- --run src/__tests__/panel-target-map-projection.test.ts src/__tests__/panel-target-map-overlays.test.ts src/__tests__/canvas-map-surface-overlays.test.ts src/__tests__/canvas-map-surface-lifecycle.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

acceptance_criteria: Line tool creates saved linear zones with snapped drag endpoints; toolbar and command graph expose the Line tool with shortcut L and reordered groups; Linear Zones render, select by stroke proximity, show length measurements, and preserve existing zone behaviors; focused frontend tests pass.
