Full cd desktop/web && npm test was run and failed only on pre-existing location-route-boundary.test.ts expecting ../maplibre/host; follow-up canopi-6xlg filed.

title: Clear Polygonal Zone draft redo after redo-only cancellation
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Polygonal Zone Zone Draft cancellation path where Undo of the only draft vertex leaves redoable draft state even though the visible draft is gone. Pressing Escape after that redo-only state should cancel the draft redo state so Redo cannot restore the cancelled vertex.

## User Value
A user who starts a Polygonal Zone, undoes the only draft vertex, and then cancels with Escape gets a truly cancelled Zone Draft instead of stale Redo restoring the old vertex.

## Out of Scope
Do not change committed Scene Edit history semantics. Do not remove the intentional ability to Redo the one undone draft vertex before explicit cancellation. Do not add transient redo support for other tools.

## Source
Follow-up review finding from canopi-kjxk.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Clear Polygonal Zone Zone Draft redo state when a redo-only draft is explicitly cancelled.

**Current behavior:**
After a user places one Polygonal Zone draft vertex and runs Undo, the visible Zone Draft is cleared but the transient draft redo stack can still hold the undone vertex. Because some cancellation paths treat an empty draft vertex list as no active draft, pressing Escape in that redo-only state can fail to clear the redo stack. Redo can then restore a vertex after the user tried to cancel the draft.

**Desired behavior:**
A redo-only Polygonal Zone Zone Draft state is still cancellable transient tool state. After placing one draft vertex, running Undo, and pressing Escape, draft Redo must no longer be available and must not restore the cancelled vertex. Before Escape or another explicit cancellation path, Redo should still restore the one undone draft vertex.

**Key interfaces:**
- Polygonal Zone Zone Draft transient Undo/Redo behavior
- Scene Interaction Frame keyboard cancellation handling
- Canvas Runtime Surface history command availability

**Out of scope:**
- Changing committed Scene Edit history behavior
- Removing one-vertex draft Redo before explicit cancellation
- Generalizing transient history for other tools
- Addressing unrelated full-suite Location/MapLibre boundary failures
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A focused regression test covers: place one Polygonal Zone draft vertex, Undo, press Escape, then Redo does not restore the vertex.
- [ ] Redo remains available immediately after the one-vertex Undo before the user cancels.
- [ ] Escape or equivalent transient cancellation clears redo-only Polygonal Zone draft state even when no draft vertices are currently visible.
- [ ] Normal committed Scene Edit Undo/Redo behavior is unchanged.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests for the changed behavior pass.
close_reason: Fixed redo-only Polygonal Zone Zone Draft cancellation by letting Escape clear transient redo state even when no draft vertices are visible. Passing checks: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Make Polygonal Zone draft Undo/Redo step through vertices
description:
## Slice Summary
When a user is drawing a Polygonal Zone, Undo and Redo should operate on the active Zone Draft before falling through to committed Scene Edit history. Undo removes the most recent placed draft vertex; Redo restores draft vertices while the same unbranched draft remains active.

## User Value
A user can correct the latest Zone Edge while building a Polygonal Zone without losing the whole draft, and can restore an accidentally undone draft edge. Toolbar, menu, command palette, and keyboard command availability should match the actual draft/history state.

## Out of Scope
Do not add redo support for other transient tool drafts. Do not change persisted Zone geometry or Scene Edit history semantics after a Polygonal Zone is committed. Do not introduce a generalized app-wide transient history framework unless the local canvas seams require it.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Make active Polygonal Zone Zone Draft Undo/Redo step through draft vertices before committed Scene Edit history.

**Current behavior:**
While drawing a Polygonal Zone, Backspace can remove the most recent draft vertex, but Undo is routed to committed Scene Edit history. If the latest committed edit is a completed Polygonal Zone, Undo removes the whole committed Zone rather than stepping back through the active Zone Draft. Command availability is tied to committed history only, so toolbar/menu/palette Undo may be disabled even when a Zone Draft has vertices that can be undone.

**Desired behavior:**
When a Polygonal Zone Zone Draft is active, every Undo entry point should first remove the most recent placed draft vertex. If the draft has one vertex, Undo clears the draft. Redo should restore draft vertices while the same unbranched draft remains active. If no draft undo/redo is available, Undo/Redo should fall through to committed Scene Edit history. Undo/Redo command availability should include draft undo/redo availability. Native text undo/redo must continue to win when focus is inside editable text controls.

Draft Redo should clear whenever the user branches the draft by placing a new vertex after undoing, commits/closes the Polygonal Zone, cancels the draft, switches tools, disposes/replaces the design, or otherwise clears transient tool state. Committing after draft Undo should create the Polygonal Zone from only the currently visible draft vertices.

**Key interfaces:**
- App Command Graph Undo/Redo commands and their disabled-state projections
- Canvas Runtime Surface history command surface
- Scene Interaction Frame and Polygonal Zone tool state
- Scene Edit history for committed canvas changes

**Out of scope:**
- Redo behavior for other transient tools
- Persisting Zone Draft history into the design
- Changing committed Scene Edit undo/redo semantics
- Changing native editable-field undo/redo behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] While drawing a Polygonal Zone with multiple draft vertices, Undo removes only the most recent draft vertex and does not commit or undo a Scene Edit.
- [ ] Undo with one draft vertex clears the Zone Draft without dirtying the design or creating a Zone.
- [ ] Redo restores draft vertices after draft Undo while the same unbranched Zone Draft remains active.
- [ ] Placing a new draft vertex after draft Undo clears the draft Redo stack.
- [ ] Closing/committing a Polygonal Zone after draft Undo uses only the currently visible draft vertices.
- [ ] Escape cancellation, tool switching, disposal, and transient cleanup clear draft undo/redo state.
- [ ] Toolbar, menu, command palette, and keyboard Undo/Redo route through draft state before committed Scene Edit history.
- [ ] Undo/Redo disabled states are enabled when draft undo/redo is available, even if committed Scene Edit history is empty.
- [ ] Editable text controls keep native Undo/Redo behavior.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests cover draft Undo, draft Redo, command availability, and fallthrough to committed Scene Edit history.
- [ ] Relevant agent docs remain aligned with the final behavior.
close_reason: Implemented Polygonal Zone Zone Draft Undo/Redo through transient draft vertices before committed Scene Edit history. Passing checks: cd desktop/web && npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts commands-registry.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit. Full cd desktop/web && npm test was run and failed on unrelated location-route-boundary.test.ts expecting ../maplibre/host in the Location map editing source.

title: Preserve canonical-name Species list fallbacks
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Species Catalog canonical-name list projection used by favorites and recently viewed so it preserves the documented Common Name lookup order while still sharing the `SpeciesListItem` read projection.

## User Value
Favorites and recently viewed Species continue to show the same Common Name display as before the list-projection refactor, including primary English `species_common_names` fallback behavior and correct fallback flags.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign Species Catalog search ranking, detail Common Name hydration, or favorite/recently viewed ordering.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Preserve canonical-name Species list Common Name fallback behavior after the shared list-projection refactor.

**Current behavior:**
Canonical-name Species list hydration for favorites and recently viewed can now differ from the previous lookup contract when a Species has no `best_common_names` row. Primary English `species_common_names` entries may be skipped in favor of the storage fallback name, and English rows without a best-name row can inherit the search projection fallback flag.

**Desired behavior:**
Favorites and recently viewed hydration should keep the documented Common Name lookup order while still sharing the `SpeciesListItem` row projection where practical. Locale best names should win, English best names should remain the fallback, primary English `species_common_names` should still be considered before the storage fallback name, and English canonical-name hydration should not mark a row as a fallback solely because a best-name row is missing.

**Key interfaces:**
- Species Catalog read projection
- `SpeciesListItem` list display fields
- Common Name lookup order
- Favorites and recently viewed Species hydration

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning Species Catalog search ranking
- Changing Species detail Common Name hydration
- Changing favorite or recently viewed ordering
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Favorites/recently viewed canonical-name hydration considers primary English `species_common_names` before `species.common_name` when no `best_common_names` row exists.
- [ ] English canonical-name hydration without a best-name row does not incorrectly mark `is_name_fallback`.
- [ ] Search list behavior remains covered and unchanged for locale best names, secondary Common Names, and fallback flags.
- [ ] Focused backend regression tests cover the missing `best_common_names` cases.
close_reason: Fixed canonical-name Species list hydration to preserve primary English species_common_names fallbacks and English non-fallback flags through the shared SpeciesListItem projection. Verified red/green regression plus focused Species Catalog and Plant Browser tests, fmt check, workspace check, Clippy, and cargo test --workspace.

title: Remove unrelated MapLibre bead closure from Species branch
description:
## Slice Summary
Clean up the Species Catalog list-projection branch metadata so it does not mark the unrelated MapLibre surface-adapter bead complete unless the branch also carries the MapLibre implementation.

## User Value
The issue tracker remains truthful across review branches, and future agents do not lose visibility into unrelated MapLibre work because a Species-only branch closed it.

## Out of Scope
Do not merge MapLibre adapter code into the Species Catalog branch. Do not alter the actual MapLibre implementation branch except if a maintainer explicitly requests branch reconciliation.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Keep the Species Catalog list-projection branch from closing unrelated MapLibre work.

**Current behavior:**
The Species Catalog list-projection branch metadata records the MapLibre surface-adapter bead as closed even though that branch does not contain the corresponding MapLibre implementation. If merged as-is, the tracker can hide unrelated MapLibre work from future agents.

**Desired behavior:**
The Species Catalog branch should carry only Species Catalog list-projection bead metadata unless it also carries the matching MapLibre implementation. The MapLibre surface-adapter bead should remain represented only on the branch or commit that contains the MapLibre implementation, or otherwise remain visible as unfinished work.

**Key interfaces:**
- bd exported issue metadata
- Species Catalog list-projection review branch
- MapLibre surface-adapter bead state

**Out of scope:**
- Merging MapLibre adapter code into the Species Catalog branch
- Reworking the MapLibre surface-adapter implementation
- Changing unrelated bead states
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The Species Catalog list-projection branch no longer adds a closed MapLibre surface-adapter bead without the matching implementation.
- [ ] The Species Catalog projection bead metadata remains present and accurate.
- [ ] No unrelated bead state changes are introduced while repairing the branch metadata.
- [ ] Final branch diff makes the issue-tracker metadata match the code carried by the branch.
close_reason: Removed the unrelated MapLibre surface-adapter closure from the Species list-projection branch export while preserving the Species projection bead metadata. Verified the branch issue diff carries the Species/review beads and no canopi-ae86 line.

title: Deepen Species Catalog list projection
description: Give Species Catalog list rows one shared read projection so search, favorites, recently viewed, and future list callers share SELECT shape, named row mapping, localized Common Name hydration, and list item defaults instead of splitting row-order mapping across query_builder/search/list_items.
close_reason: Added a shared SpeciesListItem list projection mapper, routed search and canonical-name hydration through the same Common Name projection, removed obsolete duplicate hydration helpers, documented the boundary, and verified focused tests plus Rust CI gates.

title: Deepen MapLibre surface adapters
description: Create a deeper MapLibre surface adapter seam so Canvas Map Surface, Location Workbench map editing, and Design Template world map callers stop hand-rolling host choreography, typed casts, event cleanup, and create-error handling. Preserve current behavior while concentrating typed map lifetime and event ownership behind a reusable module.
close_reason: Added a typed MapLibre Surface Adapter above the host, migrated Canvas Map, Location, and Design Template world map surfaces to it, documented the boundary, and verified with focused MapLibre surface/lifecycle tests plus TypeScript.

title: Guard and document Planning Canvas Interaction Frame ownership
description:
## Slice Summary
Remove duplicated planning canvas listener and cleanup ownership, add source guard coverage, and update agent guidance for the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame seam.

## User Value
Future planning surfaces have a clear interaction seam, and regressions around hover, drag cleanup, stale selection cleanup, and Target Presentation ordering are easier to catch.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Scene Interaction Frame behavior for canvas runtime tools. Do not convert this seam into a generic Canvas2D input module.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Guard and document Planning Canvas Interaction Frame ownership.

**Current behavior:**
Planning canvas interaction ownership is split between Timeline Action Canvas and Consortium planning, and docs do not yet describe the new Planning Canvas Interaction Frame seam.

**Desired behavior:**
Agent guidance and source guard tests describe Planning Canvas Interaction Frame as the planning-specific interaction lifetime seam. Timeline Action Canvas and Consortium planning use adapters, and production code does not reintroduce duplicated document listener and cleanup ownership for planning canvases.

**Key interfaces:**
- Planning Canvas Interaction Frame ownership rules
- Timeline Action Canvas adapter
- Consortium planning adapter
- Frontend source guard tests and agent guides

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Scene Interaction Frame behavior for canvas runtime tools
- Turning the frame into a generic Canvas2D input module
- Adding new planning product behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Agent guidance names Planning Canvas Interaction Frame as the shared planning-canvas interaction lifetime seam and distinguishes it from Scene Interaction Frame.
- [ ] Source guard tests prevent Timeline Action Canvas and Consortium planning from reintroducing duplicated production document listener and cleanup ownership outside the frame adapter path.
- [ ] Documentation preserves ownership of geometry, rendering, popover details, and Design Edit commands in surface-specific adapters.
- [ ] Timeline Action Canvas and Consortium planning both use the Planning Canvas Interaction Frame adapters.
- [ ] Frontend source guard tests and type checks pass.
close_reason: Added Planning Canvas Interaction Frame ownership guards, documented its separation from Scene Interaction Frame, made wheel listener installation optional for non-wheel planning surfaces, and verified focused Vitest coverage plus npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Guard and document MapLibre Host ownership
description:
## Slice Summary
Remove remaining direct production MapLibre ownership outside the MapLibre Host and update agent guidance so future map-backed surfaces use host adapters.

## User Value
The MapLibre Host seam stays durable after migration, and future changes are less likely to reintroduce duplicate resource ownership.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new map-backed product behavior. Do not change tile, terrain, or projection contracts beyond what the migration already requires.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Guard and document MapLibre Host ownership after all map-backed surfaces are migrated.

**Current behavior:**
Agent guidance still reflects a transitional state where full-screen map surfaces may own MapLibre locally. Source guards only partially enforce MapLibre ownership.

**Desired behavior:**
Agent docs and source guard tests describe the MapLibre Host as the production resource-lifetime seam for map-backed surfaces. Production code has no direct MapLibre ownership outside the host and low-level maplibre modules.

**Key interfaces:**
- MapLibre Host ownership rules
- Canvas Map Surface, Location Workbench map, and Design Template world map adapters
- Frontend source guard tests
- MapLibre agent guide

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new map-backed product behavior
- Changing projection, terrain, tile, or Target Presentation contracts
- Reworking non-MapLibre canvas rendering
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Agent guidance names MapLibre Host as the production MapLibre resource-lifetime seam and removes stale permission for direct local ownership in migrated surfaces.
- [ ] Source guard tests allow MapLibre imports/construction only in the host and low-level maplibre modules, plus tests where appropriate.
- [ ] Canvas Map Surface, Location Workbench map editing, and Design Template world map all use host adapters.
- [ ] The documentation clearly distinguishes MapLibre Host from Canvas Map Surface, Location Workbench, and Design Template authority.
- [ ] Frontend source guard tests and type checks pass.
close_reason: Shipped MapLibre Host ownership guards and docs: added a broad frontend source-boundary test that keeps production app/components code from importing maplibre-gl, constructing basemap styles directly, or constructing MapLibre classes outside low-level maplibre modules; updated docs/agent/maplibre.md to name MapLibre Host as the production resource-lifetime seam and removed stale component-local ownership guidance. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts maplibre-host.test.ts world-map-surface.test.tsx location-map-editing-host.test.tsx canvas-map-surface-lifecycle.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Migrate Design Template world map to MapLibre Host
description:
## Slice Summary
Route the Design Template world map through the MapLibre Host while preserving template marker rendering, marker selection, fit-to-bounds, and selected-template fly-to behavior.

## User Value
Design Template browsing keeps the same map behavior while sharing MapLibre loading, resize, rebuild, teardown, and preserved-view behavior with the rest of the app.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Design Template catalog state, template download/import workflow, marker visual design, or Canvas Map Surface behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Migrate the Design Template world map to the MapLibre Host.

**Current behavior:**
The Design Template world map directly owns MapLibre creation, controls, resize observation, marker lifetime, preserved view state, style rebuilds, and teardown.

**Desired behavior:**
The Design Template world map uses a MapLibre Host adapter. The adapter preserves marker rendering, marker selection, fit-to-bounds when template layout changes, selected-template fly-to behavior, and preserved view state while MapLibre resource lifetime moves behind the host seam.

**Key interfaces:**
- MapLibre Host interface for interactive map lifetime and preserved view state
- Design Template map marker adapter
- Design Template catalog selection and preview behavior
- World map tests

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Design Template catalog state or import workflows
- Changing marker visual design
- Changing Canvas Map Surface or Location Workbench behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Design Template world map no longer directly imports or constructs MapLibre outside the MapLibre Host adapter path.
- [ ] Template marker rendering, marker selection, fit-to-bounds behavior, selected-template fly-to behavior, style rebuild behavior, and preserved view state are preserved.
- [ ] Focused tests cover marker synchronization and host adapter lifecycle for the Design Template world map.
- [ ] Source guard coverage prevents direct production MapLibre ownership from returning to the Design Template world map.
- [ ] Frontend type checks and focused world-map tests pass.
close_reason: Shipped the Design Template world map migration to MapLibre Host: WorldMapSurface now requests map lifetime through maplibre/host.ts, with MapLibre map/marker/bounds/control constructors isolated in maplibre/world-map.ts; preserved marker rendering, click selection, selected marker classes, fit-to-bounds, selected-template fly-to, host resize, and preserved view across basemap rebuilds; added world-map behavior tests and source guards, and updated docs/agent/maplibre.md. Tests: cd desktop/web && npm test -- world-map-surface.test.tsx frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts maplibre-host.test.ts; cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Move Canvas Document Surface behavior behind its role module
description:
## Slice Summary
Move Canvas Document Surface lifecycle behavior behind the document role module, including document load, replace, serialization, saved checkpoints, chrome attachment, resize, and teardown.

## User Value
Document-session code gets a focused lifecycle surface that can be tested and evolved without reaching through the broad canvas runtime implementation.

## Out of Scope
Do not change the saved file format, alter non-canvas document authority, or migrate command/query behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Move document lifecycle behavior behind the Canvas Document Surface role module.

**Current behavior:**
Document lifecycle operations are exposed through the Canvas Document Surface, but the concrete scene runtime still owns much of the behavior for loading, replacing, serializing, saving checkpoints, canvas chrome, resizing, and destruction.

**Desired behavior:**
The document role module owns the lifecycle behavior behind the Canvas Document Surface. Document-session callers should depend on the document surface rather than broad runtime internals. Save composition should keep canvas scene state under scene authority and non-canvas document state under the document-session authority.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Document Surface
- Document session load, replace, and save workflows
- Scene document serialization and hydration contracts
- Saved-checkpoint and history baseline behavior
- Runtime resize, chrome attachment, and teardown lifecycle

**Out of scope:**
- Changing the `.canopi` file format
- Moving command or query behavior
- Changing document-session ownership of non-canvas fields
- Rewriting renderer or interaction internals
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Document load and replace behavior is owned behind the document role module.
- [ ] Canvas document serialization goes through the document role module and preserves existing save composition semantics.
- [ ] Saved checkpoints, history clearing, and dirty-baseline behavior are preserved.
- [ ] Canvas chrome attachment, viewport initialization, resize, and destroy behavior remain lifecycle-owned and covered through the document surface.
- [ ] Document-session callers do not need the broad concrete runtime for document lifecycle operations.
- [ ] Focused tests cover load, replace, serialize, mark-saved, resize, chrome attachment, and teardown behavior.
- [ ] Canvas runtime agent documentation is updated if lifecycle ownership guidance changes.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Moved Canvas Document Surface lifecycle behavior into the document role module with narrow runtime dependencies; SceneCanvasRuntime compatibility methods now delegate to the role; preserved runtime-shaped fake compatibility in surface composition; updated canvas runtime guidance. Verification: npm test -- canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts document-session-lifecycle.test.ts document-session-transition.test.ts use-canvas-document-session.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Retire obsolete canvas runtime mirror paths
description:
## Slice Summary
Remove or demote obsolete canvas runtime mirror revision and plant-presentation paths once runtime reads are off mirror signals, and add guardrails against reintroducing runtime mirror imports.

## User Value
Canvas runtime code has a clear authority boundary: mirrors may exist only as app chrome projections, not production runtime inputs.

## Out of Scope
Do not remove legitimate UI projection signals or broaden this into unrelated signal cleanup.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Retire obsolete runtime mirror paths and guard against reintroduction.

**Current behavior:**
After runtime guide and metadata reads move to authoritative seams, some canvas mirror modules or revision paths may remain as compatibility scaffolding. Without deletion or demotion, future runtime code can accidentally depend on app chrome projection signals again.

**Desired behavior:**
Obsolete runtime mirror revision and plant-presentation paths are removed or demoted to explicit UI projection adapters. Runtime implementation code must not import mirror modules except through documented adapters that only serve app chrome projection. Query revisions should cover app freshness needs that previously depended on mirrors.

**Key interfaces:**
- Runtime mirror modules
- Canvas Query Surface revisions and read models
- App chrome projection adapters
- Source-boundary tests for canvas runtime imports
- Canvas runtime agent documentation

**Out of scope:**
- Removing legitimate UI projection state
- Reworking unrelated app signals
- Changing user-facing canvas behavior
- Broad frontend state-management refactors
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Obsolete runtime mirror revision and plant-presentation paths are deleted or demoted to explicit UI projection adapters.
- [ ] Production canvas runtime implementation no longer imports mirror modules for runtime behavior.
- [ ] App chrome projections that still need signals have clear adapter ownership and documentation.
- [ ] Query revisions or equivalent read models cover freshness needs previously served by runtime mirrors.
- [ ] Source-boundary tests forbid runtime implementation imports of retired mirror modules, with only documented UI projection adapter exceptions.
- [ ] Focused tests cover app chrome freshness and runtime behavior after mirror removal.
- [ ] Canvas runtime and frontend pattern agent documentation are updated if mirror-state guidance changes.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Deleted obsolete canvas/runtime-mirror-state.ts, strengthened source-boundary tests so runtime mirror revision signals cannot be reintroduced, and documented Canvas Query Surface revisions as the replacement freshness seam. Verification: npm test -- frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts canvas-map-surface-snapshot.test.ts maplibre-canvas-surface.test.tsx canvas-panel-feedback.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Move runtime guide and metadata reads off mirror signals
description:
## Slice Summary
Replace production canvas runtime reads of guide and north-bearing mirror signals with reads from scene authority, query surfaces, or the design-session metadata seam.

## User Value
Runtime behavior follows the authoritative canvas and document seams instead of coupling scene logic to app-level mirror signals.

## Out of Scope
Do not delete all mirror modules yet, change saved metadata shape, or rewrite MapLibre camera synchronization.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Move production runtime guide and metadata reads off mirror signals.

**Current behavior:**
Some production canvas runtime paths still read app-level mirror signals for guide and metadata-like facts. That couples scene behavior to app projection state and makes it harder to reason about authority for guides, plant presentation, and north bearing.

**Desired behavior:**
Production runtime behavior should read authoritative canvas facts from Scene Store or Canvas Query Surface, and metadata-owned facts from the design-session metadata/read seam. Guides should be sourced from scene authority for snapping. Plant size and plant color presentation should be session-owned behind runtime/query surfaces. North bearing should remain design metadata and should be consumed through the design-session metadata/read seam rather than mirror state.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Store persisted guide authority
- Canvas Query Surface freshness and presentation reads
- Canvas Command Surface plant-presentation writes
- Design-session metadata/read seam
- Canvas Map Surface projection inputs
- Runtime mirror modules used by app chrome projections

**Out of scope:**
- Deleting every mirror module
- Changing saved metadata names or file shape
- Rewriting MapLibre camera synchronization
- Changing user-facing snapping or map orientation behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Production runtime snapping reads guides from scene authority rather than root mirror signals.
- [ ] Runtime plant size mode and plant color attribute behavior uses session-owned command/query surfaces rather than root mirror signal reads.
- [ ] North-bearing consumers in canvas/map runtime paths read through the design-session metadata/read seam rather than scene metadata mirror state.
- [ ] Existing snapping, guide visibility, plant presentation, and map-orientation behavior is preserved.
- [ ] Focused tests cover guide snapping, plant-presentation query freshness, and north-bearing projection inputs after the authority change.
- [ ] Source-boundary tests fail if production canvas runtime implementation imports retired mirror-state modules.
- [ ] Canvas runtime and document-lifecycle agent documentation are updated if authority wording changes.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Moved production runtime guide reads to SceneStore persisted guides for chrome rendering and snapping; moved Canvas Map Surface north-bearing reads to the Design Session metadata seam; removed production runtime imports of mirror revision signals; updated source-boundary tests and guide docs. Verification: npm test -- frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts canvas-map-surface-snapshot.test.ts canvas-map-surface-reconciliation.test.ts maplibre-canvas-surface.test.tsx canvas-panel-feedback.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Canvas Runtime Surface role-module tracer
description:
## Slice Summary
Establish internal role modules behind Canvas Runtime Surfaces and move one representative command, query, and document path through them while keeping app-facing behavior stable.

## User Value
Future agents can deepen the runtime behind smaller surfaces without re-opening every canvas consumer or widening the existing runtime interface.

## Out of Scope
Do not migrate every runtime method, remove the concrete runtime class, or retire mirror signals in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Establish internal role modules behind Canvas Runtime Surfaces.

**Current behavior:**
Canvas Runtime Surfaces provide command, query, and document-facing types, but the concrete scene runtime still owns most behavior directly. Existing surfaces are not yet deep enough to make the concrete runtime a narrow lifecycle and construction coordinator.

**Desired behavior:**
Canvas Runtime Surfaces remain the app-facing seam, while command, query, and document behavior begins moving behind internal role modules. This slice should prove the architecture with one representative command path, one representative query path, and one representative document path. The concrete scene runtime may still construct and wire the modules, but callers should continue receiving the same command, query, and document surfaces.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Runtime Surfaces
- Canvas Command Surface
- Canvas Query Surface
- Canvas Document Surface
- Scene runtime construction and lifecycle wiring
- Source-boundary tests for canvas runtime imports and surface access

**Out of scope:**
- Migrating every method on every surface
- Removing the concrete scene runtime class
- Retiring canvas mirror signals
- Changing saved document shape or user-facing canvas behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Internal role modules exist for command, query, and document surface behavior.
- [ ] At least one representative command path is owned by the command role module.
- [ ] At least one representative query path is owned by the query role module.
- [ ] At least one representative document path is owned by the document role module.
- [ ] App-facing Canvas Runtime Surfaces remain stable for existing consumers.
- [ ] Source-boundary tests guard the intended surface/module import direction.
- [ ] Canvas runtime agent documentation reflects the role-module direction.
- [ ] Focused frontend tests for the migrated tracer paths pass.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Established command, query, and document role modules behind createCanvasRuntimeSurfaces; added source-boundary and behavioral tracer coverage for the role composition; updated canvas runtime agent guidance. Verification: npm test -- canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Add Scene Interaction Frame event-test harness
description:
## Slice Summary
Add a small user-equivalent DOM event harness for Scene Interaction Frame tests and migrate representative select, drag, pan, wheel, and tool-drag interactions through it.

## User Value
Interaction regressions are tested at the same surface users exercise, while private runtime handler calls become less necessary.

## Out of Scope
Do not migrate the full interaction test suite or rewrite individual tool state machines in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Add a Scene Interaction Frame DOM event test harness and migrate representative interactions.

**Current behavior:**
Some Scene Interaction tests exercise private pointer, keyboard, or wheel handlers directly. This makes tests overly coupled to runtime internals and weakens confidence that frame-level event lifetime, listener cleanup, pointer capture, and cached coordinate handling work through the mounted event surface.

**Desired behavior:**
A small test harness dispatches user-equivalent DOM events through the Scene Interaction Frame surface. The harness should model pointer id, screen coordinates, modifiers, wheel input, space-held pan state, cached element rect, listener attachment/detachment, and cleanup ordering closely enough for interaction tests. Representative select/drag, pan, wheel, and one tool-drag flow should migrate to the harness.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame event surface
- Pointer, keyboard, and wheel event lifetime
- Coordinate projection and cached canvas bounds
- Tool module adapters
- Runtime cleanup and listener disposal
- Vitest DOM/event test utilities

**Out of scope:**
- Migrating every interaction test
- Rewriting tool-specific state machines
- Changing user-facing interaction behavior
- Replacing renderer visual tests
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A reusable Scene Interaction Frame test harness dispatches user-equivalent pointer, keyboard, and wheel events.
- [ ] The harness covers pointer id, screen/world coordinate projection, modifier keys, space-held pan state, cached element bounds, and listener cleanup.
- [ ] Representative select/drag, pan, wheel, and one tool-drag interaction test path uses the harness instead of private handler calls.
- [ ] Tests verify event listener attach/detach and cleanup ordering for at least one completed and one cancelled interaction.
- [ ] Existing interaction behavior remains unchanged for selection, dragging, panning, wheel zoom, and the migrated tool drag.
- [ ] Canvas runtime agent documentation describes Scene Interaction Frame as the preferred interaction test surface.
- [ ] Focused Scene Interaction Frame tests pass.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Added a reusable Scene Interaction Frame DOM event harness, covered pointer/key/wheel dispatch with cached bounds and listener cleanup, migrated representative select-drag, pan, wheel, and rectangle tool-drag tests through it, and documented the harness as the preferred interaction test surface. Verification: npm test -- scene-interaction-frame.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Move Canvas Command Surface behavior behind its role module
description:
## Slice Summary
Move Canvas Command Surface behavior behind the command role module, covering tool, viewport, history, scene edit, chrome, layer, and plant-presentation commands.

## User Value
Toolbar, shortcut, and workflow callers keep using the command surface while runtime mutation behavior becomes easier to localize and guard with tests.

## Out of Scope
Do not change tool semantics, history behavior, interaction gestures, or query/document APIs.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Move mutation behavior behind the Canvas Command Surface role module.

**Current behavior:**
Tool commands, viewport commands, history commands, scene edits, chrome commands, layer commands, and plant-presentation commands are exposed through the Canvas Command Surface, but much of the behavior still lives directly on the concrete scene runtime.

**Desired behavior:**
The command role module owns runtime mutation behavior behind the Canvas Command Surface. Toolbar, shortcut, workflow, and controller callers should continue to use command-surface methods without seeing query or document lifecycle capabilities. Existing edit transactions, history checkpoints, layer visibility/lock behavior, and plant-presentation behavior should be preserved.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Command Surface
- Tool selection and viewport commands
- Scene edit transaction coordination
- History and saved-checkpoint behavior
- Layer visibility, lock, ordering, and selection commands
- Plant size and color presentation commands

**Out of scope:**
- Changing tool semantics or keyboard shortcuts
- Moving query or document lifecycle behavior
- Rewriting Scene Interaction Frame gestures
- Changing persisted scene data shape
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tool, viewport, history, scene-edit, chrome, layer, and plant-presentation commands are owned behind the command role module.
- [ ] Existing command consumers can depend on Canvas Command Surface without broad runtime access.
- [ ] Undo, redo, copy, paste, duplicate, delete, grouping, locking, ordering, and layer commands preserve existing behavior.
- [ ] Plant size mode and plant color attribute commands update the authoritative session/presentation state expected by query consumers.
- [ ] Command tests cover representative toolbar/shortcut/workflow paths through the command surface.
- [ ] Source-boundary tests prevent command consumers from importing query/document-only runtime internals where a command surface is enough.
- [ ] Canvas runtime agent documentation is updated if command ownership guidance changes.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Moved Canvas Command Surface behavior into the command role module with scene store, camera, history, document, mutation, scene edit, settings, and presentation dependencies; SceneCanvasRuntime compatibility methods now delegate to the role; preserved runtime-shaped fake compatibility in surface composition; updated canvas runtime guidance. Verification: npm test -- canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts canvas-actions.test.ts commands-registry.test.ts canvas-toolbar-plant-color.test.tsx canvas-layer-presentation.test.ts canvas-panel-feedback.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Move Canvas Query Surface behavior behind its role module
description:
## Slice Summary
Move Canvas Query Surface read behavior behind the query role module, covering snapshots, revisions, selection reads, placed plants, localized names, and plant-presentation context.

## User Value
Panels, map projection code, and tests can depend on read-only canvas facts without seeing mutation or document lifecycle capabilities.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new panel features, change persisted scene data, or migrate command/document behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Move read behavior behind the Canvas Query Surface role module.

**Current behavior:**
Panels, map projection code, and other read-only consumers can use Canvas Query Surface methods, but the concrete scene runtime still owns most query behavior directly. Some freshness and presentation reads still rely on runtime mirror paths that should become replaceable after query ownership deepens.

**Desired behavior:**
The query role module owns read-only behavior behind the Canvas Query Surface, including scene snapshots, viewport revisions, selection reads, placed plants, localized common names, and selected plant presentation context. Read-only consumers should not need command or document lifecycle capabilities.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Query Surface
- Scene snapshot and viewport revision read models
- Selection read model
- Placed plant and localized common-name reads
- Plant size and color presentation context
- Panel and map projection read consumers

**Out of scope:**
- Changing mutation behavior
- Changing document lifecycle behavior
- Deleting mirror modules before replacement query freshness is in place
- Adding new panel UI features
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Scene snapshot, viewport, viewport revision, and screen-size reads are owned behind the query role module.
- [ ] Selection, placed-plant, localized-name, and plant-presentation-context reads are owned behind the query role module.
- [ ] Read-only consumers can use Canvas Query Surface without command or document lifecycle access.
- [ ] Query revisions provide the freshness guarantees needed by existing panel and map consumers.
- [ ] Focused tests cover query-surface reads for scene state, selection, viewport, placed plants, localized names, and plant presentation context.
- [ ] Source-boundary tests guard read-only consumers against broad runtime imports where a query surface is enough.
- [ ] Canvas runtime agent documentation is updated if query ownership guidance changes.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Moved Canvas Query Surface read behavior into the query role module with scene store, camera, mutation, presentation, and revision dependencies; SceneCanvasRuntime compatibility methods now delegate to the role; preserved runtime-shaped fake compatibility in surface composition; updated canvas runtime guidance. Verification: npm test -- canvas-runtime-surfaces.test.ts canvas-session.test.ts canvas-session-selection.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts canvas-map-surface-snapshot.test.ts canvas-map-surface-reconciliation.test.ts canvas-panel-feedback.test.tsx maplibre-canvas-surface.test.tsx; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Migrate Scene Interaction tests off private handlers
description:
## Slice Summary
Migrate the broad Scene Interaction test surface away from direct private handler calls and add a guardrail preventing new private-handler test access.

## User Value
Scene interaction tests become more resilient to internal runtime refactors because they drive the public frame-level event surface.

## Out of Scope
Do not change user-facing interaction behavior, delete coverage without replacement, or expand this into renderer visual refactors.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** chore
**Summary:** Migrate Scene Interaction tests off private handlers and add a guardrail.

**Current behavior:**
The interaction test suite still contains direct calls to private runtime handlers for pointer, keyboard, or wheel behavior. These tests can pass while the actual event surface regresses, and they block internal refactors that would otherwise preserve user behavior.

**Desired behavior:**
Scene Interaction tests should drive the frame-level event surface through the shared test harness. Private handler access should be removed except for any explicitly documented low-level unit tests that cannot be expressed as user-equivalent events. A source-boundary or test guard should prevent new private-handler test access.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame test harness
- Scene Interaction Frame event surface
- Existing interaction behavior coverage
- Source-boundary or lint-style test guard for private handler access
- Canvas runtime agent documentation

**Out of scope:**
- Reducing interaction coverage
- Changing user-facing canvas interaction behavior
- Rewriting renderer output tests
- Broadly renaming runtime internals beyond what the migration requires
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Broad Scene Interaction tests no longer call private pointer, keyboard, or wheel handlers directly.
- [ ] Existing behavior coverage for selection, dragging, panning, wheel zoom, tool cancellation, overlays, and cleanup is preserved through frame-level event tests.
- [ ] Any remaining private-handler unit access is explicitly justified and isolated from user-equivalent interaction coverage.
- [ ] A source-boundary or lint-style test fails when new broad interaction tests call private handlers directly.
- [ ] Canvas runtime agent documentation states that Scene Interaction Frame is the default test surface for interaction behavior.
- [ ] Focused interaction tests and the private-handler guard test pass.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Migrated broad Scene Interaction and related runtime integration tests from private pointer/key/wheel handler calls to the Scene Interaction Frame event harness, added a source guard against reintroducing private handler calls, and routed map north-bearing reads through the Location Workbench presentation seam to keep existing boundaries green. Verification: npm test -- scene-interaction.test.ts scene-interaction-tool-boundary.test.ts scene-interaction-frame.test.ts scene-runtime.test.ts; npm test -- location-route-boundary.test.ts canvas-map-surface-snapshot.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test.

title: Keep MapLibre host out of location barrel
description:
## Slice Summary
Prevent the shared Location barrel from re-exporting the MapLibre-backed map editing host while preserving lightweight saved-location read model imports.

## User Value
Canvas-side and other lightweight consumers can import saved Location presentation helpers without eagerly linking MapLibre-backed editing code outside the intended map-loading seam.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign Location Workbench behavior, change map editing UX, alter MapLibre setup semantics, or introduce new lazy-loading infrastructure.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Keep the MapLibre-backed Location map editing host out of the shared Location barrel.

**Current behavior:**
The shared Location barrel exposes the map editing host alongside lightweight Location read models. Consumers that only need saved-location presentation helpers can therefore statically link the MapLibre-backed editing module through the barrel.

**Desired behavior:**
Lightweight saved-location consumers continue to import the Location barrel without pulling in the MapLibre-backed editing host. Components and tests that need map editing import that hook directly from the map editing module. The LocationPanel/map-loading seam remains the only path that should link map editing behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Location barrel exports for controller, notice, search, and workbench helpers
- Location map editing host hook
- Saved Location presentation/read model imports used by canvas-side modules
- Source-boundary tests for Location and MapLibre loading boundaries

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Location Workbench behavior
- Changing Location map editing UX or MapLibre setup behavior
- Reworking MapLibre lazy-loading infrastructure
- Moving saved Location read model ownership
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] The shared Location barrel no longer re-exports the MapLibre-backed map editing host module.
- [ ] Existing consumers that need map editing import the hook directly from the map editing module.
- [ ] Lightweight saved-location consumers continue importing read models from the Location barrel without API changes.
- [ ] A source-boundary test prevents the Location barrel from re-exporting or importing MapLibre-backed editing code.
- [ ] Focused Location/MapLibre boundary tests pass.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Removed the MapLibre-backed map editing host from the shared Location barrel, moved LocationTab and map-editing host tests to direct map-editing imports, and added a source-boundary regression that keeps lightweight saved-location barrel imports free of map-editing. Verification: npm test -- location-route-boundary.test.ts location-map-editing-host.test.tsx location-tab-map-failure.test.tsx location-workbench.test.tsx location-search-controller.test.ts location-notice-read-model.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Move shared selection gestures into Scene Interaction Frame
description:
## Slice Summary
Move shared panning, band selection, Design Object dragging, snapping, and selection cleanup behavior into the Scene Interaction Frame while preserving existing canvas interaction semantics.

## User Value
Selection and drag regressions become easier to localize because shared gestures sit behind one runtime interaction seam.

## Out of Scope
Do not rewrite tool-specific Plant Stamp, Object Stamp, Plant Spacing, Annotation, or Zone drawing behavior in this slice.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Move shared selection gestures into Scene Interaction Frame.

**Current behavior:**
Shared panning, band selection, Design Object dragging, snap-adjusted drag deltas, selection writes, and selection-dependent refresh behavior are implemented alongside tool dispatch and other interaction concerns.

**Desired behavior:**
Scene Interaction Frame owns shared gesture behavior for panning, band selection, top-level Design Object dragging, snapping, selection writes, and cleanup. Tool-specific Scene Edit modules remain internal adapters. Existing behavior for off-canvas drag continuation, additive selection, locked Design Objects, hidden/locked Layers, and render invalidation is preserved.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame shared gesture behavior
- Runtime-owned selection seam
- Scene Edit transaction coordinator
- Hit testing and Design Object lock checks
- Snapping and guide reads

**Out of scope:**
- Rewriting Plant Stamp, Object Stamp, Plant Spacing, Annotation, or Zone drawing state machines
- Changing lock or visibility semantics
- Changing canvas rendering or history checkpoint behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Panning, band selection, and shared top-level Design Object dragging are owned by Scene Interaction Frame.
- [ ] Additive selection, empty-area band selection, locked Design Object exclusion, and grouped Design Object behavior are preserved.
- [ ] Snap-to-grid and snap-to-guides behavior for shared dragging is preserved.
- [ ] Drag transactions commit only when movement mutates scene state and abort on no-op drags.
- [ ] Viewport and scene invalidation remain correctly separated for pan/zoom versus scene edits.
- [ ] Focused tests cover panning, band selection, shared dragging, snapping, no-op abort, lock exclusion, and cleanup after pointer release.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Moved shared panning, band selection, top-level Design Object dragging, snap-adjusted drag deltas, selection writes, transaction commit/abort, and shared cleanup into the Scene Interaction Frame shared-gestures seam while preserving existing tool dispatch. Verification: npm test -- scene-interaction-frame.test.ts scene-interaction-tool-boundary.test.ts scene-interaction.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Route map visibility through Canvas Layer Presentation
description:
## Slice Summary
Move map-active visibility logic and Canvas Map Surface layer visibility inputs behind Canvas Layer Presentation so map readiness and visible-layer decisions are not duplicated in canvas shell and map snapshot code.

## User Value
Basemap, contours, and hillshading continue to show and hide the same way, with one test surface for deciding whether map layers are active.

## Out of Scope
Do not change MapLibre camera sync, terrain source loading, Target Presentation overlays, or Location draft/search behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Route map visibility and Canvas Map Surface layer inputs through Canvas Layer Presentation.

**Current behavior:**
Map visibility decisions are duplicated between canvas shell logic and map snapshot/reconciliation logic. Callers can inspect raw map and terrain visibility state directly to decide whether the in-canvas map should be active.

**Desired behavior:**
Canvas Layer Presentation exposes map-layer visibility facts that canvas shell and Canvas Map Surface callers can consume. Basemap, contours, and hillshading remain settings-backed, but callers no longer duplicate visible-map rules. Canvas Map Surface receives layer visibility/opacity inputs derived through the presentation seam.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Layer Presentation map visibility read model
- Canvas Map Surface snapshot interface
- Location Notice read model input
- Settings projection for map and terrain layer preferences

**Out of scope:**
- Changing MapLibre camera sync
- Changing terrain source loading or paint synchronization
- Changing Target Presentation overlays
- Changing Location draft or search behavior
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canvas shell map-active logic consumes Canvas Layer Presentation instead of duplicating raw map/terrain visibility checks.
- [ ] Canvas Map Surface snapshot assembly consumes Canvas Layer Presentation for map layer visibility and opacity facts.
- [ ] Basemap, contours, and hillshading visibility/opacity behavior is preserved.
- [ ] Location Notice visibility continues to reflect saved Location readiness plus map layer visibility.

title: Route tool modules through Scene Interaction Frame cleanup
description:
## Slice Summary
Route Scene Edit tool modules through the Scene Interaction Frame cleanup contract for tool changes, Escape cancellation, document replacement, pointer release, overlays, hover, and drop placement.

## User Value
Tool lifecycle behavior remains unchanged while stale overlays, listeners, selections, previews, and pointer capture are guarded by one shared cleanup interface.

## Out of Scope
Do not add new tools, change tool shortcuts, change Scene Edit history semantics, or alter canvas rendering.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task

**Current behavior:**
Scene Edit tool modules own tool-specific state, while shared interaction cleanup still has to coordinate pointer release, tool changes, Escape cancellation, document replacement, overlays, hover presentation, and drop placement across the broad interaction module.

**Desired behavior:**
Scene Edit tool modules plug into the Scene Interaction Frame cleanup contract. The frame owns cross-tool ordering and guarantees that tool changes, Escape cancellation, disposal, document replacement, pointer release, overlays, hover state, previews, and pointer capture leave no stale runtime state. Tool modules continue to own only tool-specific state machines.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame cleanup contract
- Scene Edit tool module adapter interface
- Runtime hover and Target Presentation adapter
- Overlay and transient UI cleanup seams
- Scene Edit transaction coordinator

**Out of scope:**
- Adding new tools
- Changing tool shortcuts
- Changing Scene Edit history semantics
- Changing renderer output or visual styling
acceptance_criteria:

title: Deepen Location Workbench search lifecycle
description:

title: Move Location map editing behind Location Workbench
description:

title: Introduce Scene Interaction Frame seam
description:
## Slice Summary
Establish Scene Interaction Frame as the canvas runtime seam for gesture lifetime without changing visible canvas behavior. The seam should make setup, teardown, mode transitions, and cross-tool cleanup testable directly.

## User Value
Canvas interaction behavior remains unchanged while future interaction work has a deeper interface for pointer and keyboard lifecycle.

## Out of Scope
Do not move every interaction branch in this slice, change Scene Edit behavior, change tool-specific state machines, or alter renderer output.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Summary:** Introduce Scene Interaction Frame as the runtime gesture seam.

**Current behavior:**
Canvas runtime interaction behavior is concentrated in a broad interaction module. It owns listener setup, pointer capture, key handling, mode transitions, panning, hover, shared selection, shared dragging, tool dispatch, and transient cleanup as one implementation surface.

**Desired behavior:**
Scene Interaction Frame becomes the explicit seam for gesture lifetime. This tracer slice should establish the frame interface and route setup, teardown, mode naming, and cross-tool cleanup through it without changing user-visible canvas behavior. It should create a test surface for lifecycle and transition behavior before moving more gesture implementation behind the seam.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Interaction Frame interface
- Scene runtime interaction lifecycle
- Scene Edit tool module adapters
- Runtime-owned selection and render invalidation seams

**Out of scope:**
- Moving all selection or drag behavior in this slice
- Changing Scene Edit history semantics
- Changing tool-specific state machines
- Changing renderer output
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] Existing listener setup, teardown, and tool transition behavior remain unchanged from a user perspective.

- [ ] The public Canvas Runtime Surface does not grow unless a role-specific interface is justified by callers.

title: Route LayerPanel through Canvas Layer Presentation
description:
## Slice Summary
Make LayerPanel consume the Canvas Layer Presentation read model and commands instead of assembling Layer rows and map or terrain special cases itself.

## User Value
The visible Layer panel behavior stays intact while the UI becomes a rendering adapter over a deeper Layer presentation interface.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign the Layer panel, add new controls, alter Location editing, or change Canvas Map Surface rendering behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Route LayerPanel through Canvas Layer Presentation.

**Current behavior:**
LayerPanel renders layer rows while also knowing map-backed and terrain-backed special cases. It decides labels, visibility toggles, active layer behavior, Location readiness presentation, opacity controls, and terrain controls locally.

**Desired behavior:**
LayerPanel consumes Canvas Layer Presentation read models and calls Canvas Layer Presentation commands. LayerPanel remains responsible for rendering layout and local UI events only. It does not know which rows are settings-backed, scene-owned, map-backed, or terrain-backed beyond what the read model tells it.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Layer Presentation read model and command interface
- LayerPanel rendering contract
- Location Workbench saved Location presentation through Canvas Layer Presentation
- Canvas Runtime Surface layer commands through Canvas Layer Presentation

**Out of scope:**
- Redesigning LayerPanel
- Adding or removing visible layer rows
- Changing Location editing behavior
- Changing Canvas Map Surface lifecycle
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] LayerPanel renders rows from Canvas Layer Presentation instead of assembling the visible Layer catalog itself.
- [ ] LayerPanel dispatches layer row actions through Canvas Layer Presentation commands.
- [ ] Existing row behavior is preserved for scene Layers, base map, contours, and hillshading.
- [ ] Existing Location-required and Location-present presentation for map-backed rows is preserved.
- [ ] Focused tests cover LayerPanel behavior through Canvas Layer Presentation rather than direct map/settings/runtime special cases.
- [ ] Import-boundary or source tests guard against reintroducing direct hard-coded map/terrain Layer authority in LayerPanel.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

title: Canvas Layer Presentation catalog and commands
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the Canvas Layer Presentation seam for the visible Layer catalog and layer-related commands. The slice should cover scene Layers, map-backed layers, terrain-backed layers, active layer selection, visibility, opacity, and authority-correct writes.

## User Value
Layer chrome keeps the same behavior while future map, terrain, and scene Layer changes have one caller-facing seam instead of spreading special cases across UI and map modules.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Layer visual styling, add new Layer kinds, change Canvas Map Surface lifecycle, or change saved Design schema.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Create the Canvas Layer Presentation seam for visible Layer read models and layer commands.

**Current behavior:**
Layer chrome, map-backed layer settings, terrain-backed layer settings, active layer state, and scene Layer writes are assembled across separate callers. Callers need to know which layer names are settings-backed, which are scene-owned, and which Location or terrain facts affect controls.

**Desired behavior:**
Canvas Layer Presentation owns the visible layer catalog and exposes a caller-ready read model plus commands. It includes scene Layers, map-backed layers, and terrain-backed layers. Reads combine Layer state, settings-backed map and terrain preferences, active layer selection, and saved Location readiness. Writes route to the correct authority without callers knowing whether a row is settings-backed or scene-owned.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Layer Presentation read model and command interface
- Canvas Runtime Surface layer commands
- Settings projection for map and terrain layer preferences
- Location Workbench saved Location presentation
- Canvas Map Surface layer visibility inputs

**Out of scope:**
- Changing saved Design schema
- Adding new Layer kinds
- Changing map or terrain rendering behavior
- Changing Layer panel visual design
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Canvas Layer Presentation exposes one visible Layer catalog that includes scene Layers, base map, contours, and hillshading.
- [ ] The read model includes labels, active state, visibility, opacity, lock/edit affordance where applicable, terrain controls where applicable, and Location readiness where applicable.
- [ ] Commands route visibility, opacity, locking, active layer, basemap style, contour interval, and hillshade changes to the correct authority.
- [ ] Callers can use the interface without checking hard-coded layer names for `base`, `contours`, or `hillshading`.
- [ ] Focused tests cover catalog construction, authority-specific command routing, Location readiness for map-backed layers, and no-op/invalid input handling.
- [ ] Relevant agent docs are updated if the Canvas Layer Presentation seam changes subsystem guidance.
- [ ] `cd desktop/web && npx tsc --noEmit` passes.
close_reason: Introduced Canvas Layer Presentation as the visible Layer catalog and authority-correct command seam for scene layers, basemap, contours, and hillshading; moved canvas-settings layer actions to delegate through it; added focused seam tests, boundary coverage, and agent docs. Verification: npm test -- canvas-layer-presentation.test.ts canvas-actions.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit.

title: Preserve document-owned fields in detached runtime serialization
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the detached Canvas Runtime App Adapter document composer so default SceneCanvasRuntime serialization preserves document-owned fields from the loaded Design file while still applying canvas-owned scene output.

## User Value
Runtime tests and detached/default runtime callers can serialize a loaded Design without silently dropping timeline, budget, consortiums, unknown extra fields, or original timestamps.

## Out of Scope
Do not change production attached Design Session composition, save format ownership metadata, or unrelated canvas serialization behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Preserve document-owned Design fields when the detached Canvas Runtime App Adapter composes serialized output.

**Current behavior:**
When the scene runtime is constructed without an injected app adapter, its detached document composer builds the serialized Design mostly from the canvas scene output. That canvas output contains placeholders for document-owned sections, so detached/default serialization can lose loaded timeline, budget, consortiums, unknown extra data, and original timestamps.

**Desired behavior:**
Detached/default runtime serialization should keep document-owned fields from the original loaded Design while applying canvas-owned scene state from the runtime. Metadata overrides such as name, description, location, and north bearing should continue to behave as expected.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Runtime App Adapter document composition contract
- Detached scene runtime serialization path
- Canvas-owned versus document-owned Design field authority
- Scene runtime serialization tests

**Out of scope:**
- Do not change production attached Design Session composition.
- Do not revise shared Design file ownership metadata.
- Do not refactor unrelated serializer or persistence paths.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A regression test covers default/detached `SceneCanvasRuntime.serializeDocument()` preserving loaded document-owned fields such as timeline, budget, consortiums, unknown `extra`, and original timestamps.
- [ ] The detached composer preserves document-owned fields from the provided document while applying canvas-owned scene output and metadata overrides.
- [ ] Existing attached production composition behavior remains covered and unchanged.
- [ ] Focused canvas runtime serialization tests and frontend typecheck pass.
close_reason: Fixed detached runtime document composition to preserve loaded document-owned Design fields while applying canvas-owned scene output; added a regression test for default SceneCanvasRuntime serialization preserving description, location, north bearing, consortiums, timeline, budget, budget currency, created_at, unknown extra, and canvas plant color changes. Gates: npm test -- canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts; npm test -- canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts document-format-contract.test.ts design-session-persistence.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Enforce the Canvas Runtime Surface seam
description:

## User Value

## Out of Scope
Do not add new runtime capabilities or refactor unrelated canvas behavior.
design:
## Agent Brief

**Summary:** Add guardrails for the Canvas Runtime Surface seam.

**Current behavior:**
Canvas Runtime Surface and Canvas Runtime App Adapter are now named concepts, but future runtime-core changes could reintroduce direct app imports unless source guardrails and agent docs enforce the seam.

**Desired behavior:**
Source boundary tests prevent runtime-core modules from importing app modules directly, except narrowly documented test adapters or app-owned adapter modules. Agent docs describe where Canvas Runtime Surface and Canvas Runtime App Adapter code belongs, which modules own the seam, and how tests should fake adapter behavior.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Runtime Surface command, query, and Design Session document roles
- Canvas Runtime App Adapter production and test adapters
- Source boundary tests
- Agent docs for canvas runtime and document lifecycle

**Out of scope:**
- Do not refactor additional runtime behavior after the seam has been enforced.
- Do not add unrelated architecture rules.
- Do not weaken existing source boundary tests.
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] Agent docs describe Canvas Runtime Surface and Canvas Runtime App Adapter ownership using glossary vocabulary.
- [ ] Existing canvas runtime, Design Session, and frontend boundary tests still pass.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck passes.
- [ ] No unrelated architecture rules or broad rewrites are included.
close_reason: Added a broad source-boundary guard that fails when production canvas runtime core files import app modules directly, documented CanvasRuntimeSurfaces and CanvasRuntimeAppAdapter ownership, and left runtime behavior unchanged. title: Introduce Canvas Runtime Surface host
description:
## Slice Summary
Create the host seam that lets the Design Session lifecycle initialize canvas runtime behavior and receive Canvas Runtime Surfaces without treating the concrete runtime implementation as the app-facing interface.

## User Value
Agents and maintainers can verify that app modules consume command, query, and document surfaces rather than raw runtime implementation details.

## Out of Scope
Do not move individual app-owned dependencies out of runtime core in this slice except where needed to establish the host seam.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Establish Canvas Runtime Surface host creation as the app-facing seam.

**Current behavior:**
The Design Session lifecycle can construct and publish canvas runtime surfaces, but the concrete runtime implementation is still directly named and treated as the object being hosted. This makes the Canvas Runtime Surface seam partly shallow because app code still has to know how runtime implementation is created.

**Desired behavior:**
The app initializes canvas runtime behavior through a host/factory seam that returns command, query, and Design Session document surfaces. App modules reason about Canvas Runtime Surface roles, not concrete runtime implementation internals. The concrete runtime may still exist behind the host, but callers should not need its type unless they are inside the runtime implementation or focused runtime tests.

**Key interfaces:**
- Canvas Runtime Surface roles: command, query, and Design Session document surfaces
- Design Session lifecycle startup, resize, autosave, teardown, and surface publication
- Runtime host or factory seam that owns concrete runtime construction

**Out of scope:**
- Do not remove every runtime-core app dependency in this slice.
- Do not change user-visible canvas behavior.
- Do not change Scene Edit semantics, renderer behavior, or save format.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Design Session lifecycle initializes canvas through a host/factory seam that returns Canvas Runtime Surfaces.
- [ ] App-facing lifecycle code no longer needs to name the concrete runtime implementation type except through the new host seam.
- [ ] Existing canvas startup, resize, queued Design load, autosave, and teardown behavior are covered by focused tests.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck passes.
close_reason: Introduced CanvasRuntimeHost and app runtime host factory so Design Session lifecycle initializes, publishes, and destroys Canvas Runtime Surfaces without naming SceneCanvasRuntime; added lifecycle and boundary coverage. Gates: npm test -- document-session-lifecycle.test.ts use-canvas-document-session.test.tsx frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Route canvas clean-state through Canvas Runtime App Adapter
description:
## Slice Summary
Route canvas clean-state updates through a Canvas Runtime App Adapter so Scene Edit history can report clean or dirty state without runtime core importing Design Session state.

## User Value
Undo, redo, saved checkpoints, and dirty indicators remain observable while canvas runtime core becomes less coupled to Design Session implementation.

## Out of Scope
Do not redesign dirty-state semantics or autosave policy.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** task
**Summary:** Move canvas clean-state reporting behind a Canvas Runtime App Adapter.

**Current behavior:**
Scene Edit history reports clean or dirty state by reaching directly into Design Session state. This leaks Design Session ownership into canvas runtime core and makes the history module harder to reuse or test through the Canvas Runtime Surface interface.

**Desired behavior:**
Canvas runtime core reports clean-state changes through an injected Canvas Runtime App Adapter. Production adapters update Design Session dirty baselines; tests can provide a small fake adapter and assert clean-state transitions without importing Design Session state.

**Key interfaces:**
- Scene Edit history saved-position semantics
- Canvas Runtime App Adapter clean-state callback
- Canvas command undo, redo, save checkpoint, and history clearing behavior
- Design Session dirty-state projections

**Out of scope:**
- Do not alter when Scene Edits enter history.
- Do not change autosave scheduling or non-canvas dirty-state tracking.
- Do not refactor unrelated Design Session state.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Runtime core history no longer imports Design Session state directly.
- [ ] Production Canvas Runtime App Adapter updates canvas clean-state exactly where current dirty indicators expect it.
- [ ] Undo, redo, mark-saved, clear-history, and history truncation behavior retain saved-position semantics.
- [ ] Focused tests cover clean-state reporting through an adapter fake.
- [ ] Frontend typecheck and focused canvas runtime tests pass.
close_reason: Routed SceneHistory clean-state reporting through CanvasRuntimeAppAdapter, wired production app adapter to Design Session setCanvasClean, migrated runtime tests to adapter probes, and updated boundary/docs. Gates: npm test -- canvas/runtime/scene-runtime.test.ts dirty-state.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; git diff --check.

title: Avoid Timeline redraws on tooltip-only mouse moves
description:
## Slice Summary
Fix the Timeline Action Canvas hover path so moving the pointer within the same hovered Timeline Action can update tooltip coordinates without forcing a full canvas redraw when geometry, scroll, zoom, row data, selection, and hovered action identity are unchanged.

## User Value
Timeline hover remains responsive on dense plans because overlay-only pointer movement does not repaint the entire Timeline canvas at mousemove frequency.

## Out of Scope
Do not change Timeline Action editing semantics, drag behavior, Planning Projection grouping, or introduce new Timeline ruler controls.
design:
## Agent Brief
**Category:** bug
**Summary:** Stabilize Timeline Action Canvas geometry and render dependencies so tooltip-only pointer movement does not repaint the canvas.

**Current behavior:**
Timeline hover updates can re-render the host model for tooltip coordinate changes even when the hovered Timeline Action identity has not changed. The canvas renderer dependency set observes a freshly-created geometry object, so overlay-only renders can schedule a full Timeline canvas redraw at mousemove frequency.

**Desired behavior:**
The Timeline canvas should redraw only when canvas-visible inputs change, such as rows, layout, origin, zoom, scroll, selection, hovered action identity, locale/theme, or species colors. Pointer movement that only changes tooltip overlay coordinates for the same hovered Timeline Action should update the overlay without creating new renderer dependencies or redrawing the canvas.

**Key interfaces:**
- Timeline Action Canvas host model renderer dependency contract.
- Timeline Action Canvas geometry snapshot used by rendering and interaction hit testing.
- Shared Canvas2D renderer hook dependency behavior.

**Out of scope:**
- Changing Timeline Action drag, resize, add, edit, or delete behavior.
- Reintroducing hidden Timeline ruler-control hit testing.
- Changing Planning Projection grouping or lane assignment.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tooltip-only mouse moves over the same Timeline Action do not create a new canvas renderer dependency or force a Timeline canvas redraw.
- [ ] Canvas redraws still happen when geometry or visible canvas inputs change, including rows/layout, origin, zoom, scroll, selection, hovered action identity, theme, locale, and species colors.
- [ ] Focused tests cover the tooltip-only no-redraw behavior through the public Timeline Action Canvas host/render seam or the closest stable app seam.
- [ ] Existing Timeline Action Canvas interaction-frame tests and frontend typecheck pass.
notes: Implemented controller-side Timeline Action Canvas geometry caching so geometry identity changes only when bounds inputs change: rows, layout, origin date, px/day, or scroll. Added a mounted InteractiveTimeline regression test proving first hover identity changes renderer deps while tooltip-coordinate-only movement over the same action keeps every renderer dependency entry stable. Gates: npm test -- interactive-timeline-hover.test.tsx; npm test -- interactive-timeline-hover.test.tsx timeline-canvas-geometry.test.ts timeline-interaction-frame.test.ts timeline-renderer.test.ts timeline-interaction.test.ts frontend-boundaries-sources.test.ts; npx tsc --noEmit; npm test; git diff --check.
close_reason: Fixed Timeline Action Canvas tooltip-only redraws by caching geometry until bounds inputs change, added mounted host regression coverage for renderer dependency stability, and passed focused Timeline tests, npx tsc --noEmit, full npm test, and git diff --check.

title: Refactor Timeline Action Canvas geometry
description: Deepen Timeline Action Canvas internals behind the existing app/timeline/canvas seam. Renderer geometry, hit testing, row offsets, canvas height, sidebar and ruler dimensions, and hidden ruler-control hit testing are currently split across renderer, interaction, host-model, and frame code. Make geometry the shared source for renderer and interaction so future timeline controls and drag behaviour have one test surface.
